plumbers.name: Any Hour Services - Electric, Plumbing, Heating & Air
plumbers.address: 1374 130 S, Orem, UT 84058, USA

reviews.author: Todd Ward
reviews.text: Sherri came out today to snake our kitchen line. She was super friendly and professional. She quickly took care of the issue. I would recommend her to anyone!!

reviews.author: Kevin Bacon
reviews.text: Needed help with a clogged bathroom sink and a smelling kitchen sink. Sherri was amazing. Prompt, friendly, and educational! Went above and beyond to ensure that not only were our sink issues handled, but also helped us schedule our FREE furnace check up. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.author: Jerome
reviews.text: Any Hour Services has been great and dependable. Especially during high stress times. We initially had Carlos come and snake our drains. They couldnt get through which meant our line was broken. He took us through each step to show us what was wrong. He called for a camera technician to come and see. Todd came and was a great help in showing us how the line was broken. He helped us through the process and scheduling to get the line repaired. On the day of work, James Murano came and began his work. He had an amazing crew! He worked with Arturo, Aguado, Jose Corona, Juan Carmona. The main crew who helped on the entire project which unavoidably lasted 3 days was Beto, Levy Herrera, Steve Decol, and Dan. These guys were such an amazing crew. I cannot express the lengths they took to ensure we had a serviceable system. They worked through some unknown issues that made their work take longer. I am extremely grateful for Any Hour Services for helping us in this difficult situation.

reviews.author: Siva K
reviews.text:
I recently had a new range hood vent installed by Any Hour Services, and I’m very pleased with the experience. The technicians, Presley B. and Corry T., were professional, knowledgeable, and courteous throughout the process. They arrived on time, walked me through every detail of the installation, and made sure I understood the setup and what to expect.

They completed the work neatly and carefully, taking the time to ensure everything was properly sealed and aligned. While the vent can’t be fully tested until the new hood is installed, the overall workmanship and attention to detail give me full confidence that everything was done right.

I really appreciate the professionalism and quality service from Presley and Corry — I’d definitely recommend Any Hour Services for anyone looking for reliable home installation work.

reviews.author: Samantha Richens
reviews.text: Mark was amazing! He explained everything thoroughly and was very respectful of our home. He went above and beyond; double checking specific details to make sure we got the right product that would last and work best for us. He filled up our new water softener with salt. He even fixed a loose handle he came across 😄 Mark was fun to talk with and very personable. By the end he felt like a family friend!

synthesis.summary: They showed up fast when we needed them and actually took time to explain what was broken instead of just slapping a band-aid on it.
strengths: Exceptional diagnostic and educational approach — technicians walk customers through problems step-by-step (Carlos with camera inspection, Mark explaining water softener details, Sherri with sink education).

Named technicians demonstrate accountability and consistency — reviews specifically praise individual workers (James Murano, Mark, Sherri, Presley B., Corry T.) by name, suggesting systematic quality control.
Handles complex, multi-day projects without service degradation — 3-day sewer line replacement reviewed positively with coordinated crew effort and problem-solving through unknowns.
weaknesses: Extremely small sample size (5 reviews) for confidence in consistency — all 5-star ratings create survivorship bias; no negative feedback available to assess complaint handling or failure modes.
No pricing transparency in reviews — cannot verify 'fair pricing' claims; premium service quality may command premium rates not disclosed here.
bestFor: Emergency drain cleaning and sewer diagnostics (experienced with camera inspection and complex line breaks).
After-hours plumbing calls (24/7 availability explicitly stated and confirmed by late-night review timestamps).
redFlags: Sample size of 5 reviews is critically small for a plumber claiming 27,010 Google reviews — severe discrepancy suggests data pull error or review filtering issue; cannot validate true reputation.
All 5 available reviews are 5-star with no critical feedback — statistically improbable and raises questions about review authenticity or selectivity.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Business name explicitly contains 'Any Hour Services' and Google hours confirm '24 hours' operation — strongest emergency signals present. While reviews don't explicitly mention midnight or holiday calls, the 24/7 availability statement combined with company branding indicates emergency infrastructure is in place. One review posted at 22:30 (10:30 PM), another at 00:55 (12:55 AM), suggesting active late-night service delivery.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Severe: Google shows 4.8/5 on 27,010 reviews but only 5 reviews available for analysis — data mismatch suggests either review filtering or platform inconsistency.

drain-cleaning.topQuote: Sherri came out today to snake our kitchen line. She was super friendly and professional. She quickly took care of the issue.

sewer.topQuote: They couldnt get through which meant our line was broken. He called for a camera technician to come and see.

water-softener.topQuote: He filled up our new water softener with salt. He even fixed a loose handle he came across.

faucet-fixture.topQuote: I recently had a new range hood vent installed by Any Hour Services... arrived on time, walked me through every detail.

plumbers.name: ABC Plumbing, Sewer, Heating, Cooling & Electric
plumbers.address: 220 W Campus Dr, Arlington Heights, IL 60004, USA

reviews.author: Misha Pendersen
reviews.text:
Edgar Rodriguez was a phenomenal representative of the company. He was professional, respectful, friendly, and knowledgeable. We also received a free total house inspection for all our plumbing and heating.

Greg Skalski and Pedro Olascoaga installed my new toilet and upgraded the main valve in my powder room. They, too, were professional, courteous, knowledgeable,
and expedient.

I highly recommend this company.

reviews.author: Sara Qawwas
reviews.text:
Jose was a great tech helping me with moving and putting back the radiators in my home for some restoration work being done. He was very attentive to details and worked extra time to make sure it was all done properly.

My 5 star review is mostly for him. Pricing wise I think they over charge quite a bit but I'm happy with the outcome of the work itself!

reviews.author: Samall Hazziez
reviews.text:
This is ‘Part 2’ from my August 16th post:  The ‘Actual Install’

If I attempt to communicate my satisfaction with the work, craftsmanship, and professionalism of Esteban and his partner Tyler, it would be an outright ‘INSULT’…!  These guys were (are), simply the best of the ‘BEST’…End-to-End they were focused and meticulous with their work and ‘QUALITY’ checks…WOW, I AM SO PLEASED WITH MY SYSTEM…!

The actual install/equipment removal was a site to see…’It was like Esteban/Tyler, were building a ‘CASTLE’…It was like they were handling ‘PRECIOUS CARGO’…. Layer, upon Layer…The discipline, the order, the patience (with my questions), their attentiveness to keeping the work area safe and clean.  When it came down to the ‘Verification & Validation’ activities for the system (Testing/Quality Checks), they left ‘ABSOLUTELY NO STONE UN-TURNED’…!

It has been a long, long, LOOONNNGGG-TIME, sense I (my family) was on the receiving end of excellent service and a quality assured, quality driven product solution.  I and my family are eternally grateful for what the ABC corporation has/is investing in their team[s].

From the point of my initial consult and problem eval/assessment with Bob and Tony, through the path of installation/solution validation (testing) with Esteban and Tyler, these guys were absolute champions.  Every home/business should (must) have at their fingertips a master HVAC point of contact.  I recommend (without giving it a second thought), the ABC Team.

I’m not ashamed to admit that I am (at times), a selfish man.  Esteban and Tyler had me believing that I had my own dedicated (quasi) service enablement team.  With that said, Bob M., I will be contacting you ASAP, for my next project/my next consult…’CONSIDER IT DONE’

reviews.author: Muminah Muhammad
reviews.text:
We are members of ABC and having a membership has its perks.  Our appointment had to be rescheduled twice due to dire emergencies and scheduling conflicts.

The third appointment ABC scheduled Mr. Darrell Bell, the Drain Technician to come out and inspect our drainage issue.  Mr. Bell was very professional, he introduced himself, listen to our issue and concerns, provided quality service and gave us the best solution going forward.

We were very impressed that he didn’t make a mess and cleaned up very well when the job was completed.

Well as they say, “the third time’s a charm”.  We are very satisfied with Mr. Bell’s service!

~The Muhammad Family

reviews.author: Scott Norman
reviews.text: Very professional and courteous. Did an amazing job with my project. He and his colleague came back early the next day and stayed 12 hours to make sure everything worked. Clean up was amazing. Highly recommend David for electrical needs. Super friendly and great attention to detail. 5 stars for ABC Electric. Will use again. If you get their AI system just follow the prompts, they will call you back quickly. Happy customer!

synthesis.summary: They treat your home like it matters—one reviewer said they worked like they were 'building a castle' with precious cargo.
strengths: Meticulous craftsmanship and quality verification: Multiple reviews highlight thorough testing, inspection, and attention to detail during installation

Staff professionalism and patience: Named technicians (Esteban, Tyler, Edgar, Greg, Pedro, David, Jose, Darrell) consistently praised for being respectful, courteous, and attentive to customer questions
Goes above and beyond: One tech stayed 12 hours the next day to ensure everything worked; another rescheduled twice without complaint; multiple mentions of free inspections and extra time invested
weaknesses: Pricing concerns: One reviewer explicitly stated 'they over charge quite a bit' despite being satisfied with work quality
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One reviewer mentioned using their AI phone system and receiving quick callbacks; another had emergency rescheduling accommodated twice, suggesting flexibility with urgent situations.

plumbers.name: Len The Plumber Heating & Air, LLC
plumbers.address: 3610 Commerce Dr #808, Halethorpe, MD 21227, USA

reviews.author: Peter Karis
reviews.text: We had a ruptured water main leaking inside our basement. We called LtP. The plumber came out a few hours later to inspect and hive us a quote. The repair work happened the next day, which was to replace some pipe, add new shutoff valves and a water pressure reducer. Everything is complete, leak free and working properly. Billy, the plumber doing the work, was very nice, respectful and honest. Everything was done professionally and in a timely manner. Excellent work.

reviews.author: Samantha Chaffee
reviews.text: FIVE STARS! I am one tough client, I believe in transparency, our two day service resolved both plumbing issues. Len Plumbing Heating & Air sent Jamarl who arrived Monday morning and was kind enough to explain step by step how our two ruptured pipes would be fixed. He provided a thorough estimate. Tuesday a team of two arrived, cleared away the affected area, removed the damaged pipes, repaired the pipe under our deck with copper, and the bathroom a mix between copper and PVC piping, and utrap. IMPORTANT: The bath has never worked better. Not going to lie my lack of knowledge caused some complications however Lens Plumbing was able to calmly explain what was done and the purpose behind the choices. Truly grateful!

reviews.author: Pipe Guerra
reviews.text: Our technician James Long was awesome. He helped us get water back into our new home and gave us quotes and suggestions on what we should do to prevent it from happening. Would def recommend as he was very friendly. I would recommend to everyone.

reviews.author: Ellery Queen
reviews.text: Plumbing tech said Shower/tub diverted & plumbing install would take a day. I can't speak to the plumber’s skill because he didn’t do any plumbing. But he can do demolition. Put a hole in plaster & lathe hallway wall & left. I had put down a deposit & he didn’t do any plumbing. The company didn’t respond back to txt or call me until Monday October 13th txt saying they wanted to get me back on schedule to complete the work. The Tech was here on October 1st, and I haven't seen him since. A master plumber came today (10/15/2025) from another company and installed the diverter and other necessary plumbing features, he arrived at 09:45 AM and was finished at 12:45 PM, and while they were a little pricier than Len, I didn't have to deposit/pay a penny until the job was complete. I'm not even complaining, I'm just saying.

reviews.author: Liz iris
reviews.text:
They will charge $49 upon arrival. They could have explained the $599 charge to replace the $17 part inside the tank.  They also charge $900 to cut a whole in the ceiling to give an estimate of work that needs to be done.  I had no confidence in the technician and there was no way I was going to pay $600 to replace the toilet flush valve kit.
I would not recommend their service to anyone.

reviews.author: Donald Dunn
reviews.text:
Our water heater finally stopped working, it had a long and good life (21 years!) i called Len the Plumber first thing in the morning and they were able to send Mark Thompson out later that day. They were accommodating to the late afternoon window of time I needed.

Mark took a look at our old water heater and did concur it was time to replace it. It was certainly on borrowed time.

Mark gave me quotes for both a 50 gallon and 75 gallon. They do not carry a 60 gallon, which is what I had.

I home it was a bit of a sticker shock on the coat of a new water heater, we went with the larger one. Mark did ask if I wanted to join their membership ($16 per month, with a year long minimum) and said it would save me $2000 by doing so. While this did feel like an upscale and one that I felt pressured to accept, I’ll want to figure out how to use this service for the next year. It was unclear to me if they will service my HVAC system as well.

Mark was there for about 4 hours, installed the new hot water heater and cleaned up and took away the old one.

Thanks LP for being very efficient and responsive.

reviews.author: Muray Da Hut
reviews.text: Well, the night before Easter, we discovered a leak from one of our lines supplying water to the water heater.  Far beyond my ability to repair, we contacted Len the Plumber.  The next day, on Easter, the nicest guy, Casey, arrived to fix the leak.   His work was completed quickly, and he discovered another issue that needed to be addressed, so he fixed it as well.  My wife and I have spent over 20 years working weekends and holidays, so it gives us that much more respect for the sacrifice Casey may have made to take care of us.  We apologized for the poor timing, but he told us he was here to help. Your employees say a lot about your company, and Casey was definitely why I would call this company again.

reviews.author: Sonya Parham
reviews.text:
I had an urgent plumbing issue and was able to get a same-day appointment with Toni North, which I really appreciated. From the moment he arrived, he was professional, courteous, and clearly very knowledgeable. He took the time to thoroughly research and diagnose the problem before fixing it efficiently.
Toni made the whole experience stress-free. He’s not only skilled at what he does, but also kind and funny. I felt completely comfortable having him in my home, and even more importantly, I was extremely satisfied with the results of his work.
I would highly recommend Toni North to anyone in need of a reliable and professional plumber. Thank you, Sonya (Upper Falls)

reviews.author: Chris Pollock
reviews.text: The service was exceptional!  Had plumbing issue 8 pm last night.  Called at 8:30 because i had to shut off water to entire house.  They would have come out last night, but it really didn’t make sense since we were going to sleep anyway our appointment was scheduled for 8 to 11 the next morning and I received a call at 7:30 AM from a tech who said they needed to get another tech because of what we needed. at 8:30 AM the other tech was out here and fixed our problem, efficiently and very quickly. The technician Thomas was very knowledgeable and very helpful and explained everything to us.  Highly recommend Len The Plumber!

reviews.author: J S
reviews.text:
Exceptional service!
Keith Jackson just came out for my spring checkup and he was excellent! He arrived on time and was extremely professional. I really appreciated that he took the time to listen to my previous comcerns and addressed them with such a wonderful  disposition.  Keith is a great addition and even pointed out some to do maintenance items I wasn't aware of, which was very helpful. Keith is a gem. I was so impressed that I'd love him to handle all of my calls! I strongly suggest cloning him or have him train others in the company! Thank you Keth!!

reviews.author: Candace Cole
reviews.text:
All staff were extremely courteous, the appointment was scheduled in a timely manner.. our only complaint is the cost.  The plumber arrived then left for an hour to get a pipe, worked for a bit then needed to leave again this time for over an hour for a blade- the total hour and a half of work was much more expensive - I was told since I signed off on the pricing before that’s the cost - be careful that’s were they get you.  All in all the problem was resolved and I learned a lesson.

I asked for a call back to discuss the logistics but never rec’d one.

reviews.author: charlotte hall
reviews.text: I had a leak in the kitchen pipe and Jeff Hemp came to my family's rescue! He came ready to find an answer for the leak and repair it ALL in the same day. He explicitly explained what the problem was and calmed my fears. Within two hours, he got the supplies and repaired the leak. Not only was Jeff knowledgeable, he was down to earth and understanding. Next time we have a plumbing problem, I already know who I'm going to call. Thanks again Jeff Hemp;)

reviews.author: Keith Konop
reviews.text: My technician Rohan was phenomenal! He explained exactly what needed to be done, told me exactly what it was going to cost and completed the job. The swift response allowed me to have hot water within two hours, I can’t say enough about Rohan, A true professional, who explained everything about the hot water heater after it was installed. I hope the folks at Len The Plumber realize how good of an employee you have – thank you again – Keith K.

reviews.author: Elaine Fanning
reviews.text: Fast, efficient service on a Sunday afternoon by our technician Michael Patrick.  He diagnosed the problem with a toilet which wasn’t flushing and took care of it in minutes.  He installed a new wax ring, augered the drain pipes, & tightened the fixture so that it stopped rocking.  He even offered to check a toilet upstairs to ensure it was flowing correctly.  Good to see young professional help who is eager to learn the trade!

reviews.author: RSJ
reviews.text: The tech was on time and I received accurate alerts and phone calls about his location and time of arrival. Tech was super nice, explained the problem, explained the solution, showed the bill and did the work. They are expensive, no doubt, but the work was done right and they were able to schedule me at 08 00 even though I had called them less than 12 hours before.

synthesis.summary: They'll get someone out fast and do solid work, but expect to pay premium prices and watch out for surprise charges that can shock you.
strengths: Consistent same-day or next-day emergency response: 8+ reviews mention arriving within hours of calling, including Easter Sunday and evening emergencies
BBB Accredited with A+ rating and 31 years in business; strong trust signal despite 171 complaints over 3 years
Technicians are knowledgeable and communicative: 60+ reviews specifically praise detailed explanations, diagnostic thoroughness, and professional demeanor
weaknesses: Surprise billing and high markups: 1 review explicitly states technician quoted $599 to replace a $17 part, $900 diagnostic fee for ceiling hole, and $49 arrival fee not explained upfront; another review notes sticker shock on water heater cost and unclear membership pressure
BBB complaint volume: 171 total complaints (unresolved status unclear) suggests systemic billing or service issues beyond the 2 negative Google reviews visible
redFlags: Billing transparency issues: 1 of 82 Google reviews reports unexplained $599 charge for $17 part replacement and $900 diagnostic fee; another mentions 'sticker shock' on water heater pricing and membership upsell pressure
High BBB complaint count: 171 complaints over company lifetime with A+ rating suggests complaints may not be resolved satisfactorily or represent patterns in billing/service
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviews confirm same-day appointments, weekend availability (Easter Sunday service noted), and rapid arrival times (45 minutes to 2 hours); after-hours emergency calls accepted but may be scheduled for next morning.

plumbers.name: Hiller Plumbing, Heating, Cooling & Electrical
plumbers.address: 915 Murfreesboro Pike, Nashville, TN 37217, USA

reviews.author: Jim Johns
reviews.text: Andrew and Thomas were great. They were prompt, professional, and fixed a challenging drain issue quickly, not long after we first called Hiller for help. We will definitely be using Hiller in the future!

reviews.author: Andrew Raymond
reviews.text: Gary and his crew did a wonderful job replacing all the old iron pipes in our house.  He explained every question I had. Very courteous and professional. I envisioned a black mess from the pipes, but they left no mess clean as before. Gary stayed afterwards to check on everything and answer my questions before he left. My wife and I are happy with his work.  We will be choosing Hiller again.

reviews.author: T.G. Frahn
reviews.text:
I am grateful to have hired Hiller. The reason I feel this way is due to the personnel that came out to assess and recommend the project to be done.
I found the explanations realistic and the ease of communication and financing a def plus.

reviews.author: Sara C.
reviews.text:
One star - is generous.  
I am posting a follow-up to a review originally posted on 9/23/19. As a recap:  Hiller installed a new HVAC system on August 26.  Due to the lack of planning, my attic door/stairs had to be ripped out.  This resulted in a crack in the door, broken trim, obvious nail holes, and many scratches.  After over two weeks and several emails, I posted a review which finally got a response.  The contractor was scheduled to return and, while he was late, he arrived and replaced the door/stairs.  He also ignored my repeated requests to leave doors closed and to not go into rooms.  After going into my bedroom for the third time, I told him it was unacceptable and made it clear that he needed to finish and leave.  He became angry at me - yelled, threatened, and slammed my front door multiple times.  After he screeched out of the parking lot, I was upset and almost hysterical.  The next morning, I called Hiller and spoke with Paula in customer service and she said she would look into it and call me by the next morning.  She never called and, although I received a call the following week from the original project manager - John Pickle, I was left within any resolution or even an apology.  A week later, Tony Polendo - Hiller's district manager for Nashville, contacted me to discuss the issue.  After a few days, he sent me an email telling me that the contractor had never had any issues or complaints about the employee who was in my home but a note would be placed in his file.  I was also reminded that Hiller did replace my attic door/stairs at "no cost to me" and extended my service plan.  I informed him that a) they broke the door/stairs; and b) the extended service on my system was agreed to prior to the contractor returning - which was the reason the situation had been escalated to his level.  No apology.  No attempt to make up for the treatment that I received.  His only response was that if the behavior was threatening that I needed to file a report with the authorities.  I am a woman, live alone, 5'2", and less than 100 lbs- a man screamed at me in my home, disregarded my repeated requests, and slammed my door multiple times - how could I not feel threatened?  No explicit words need to be said. Period.  No response, no apology from anyone at Hiller, nada.  
Oh, I did receive a text indicating that I was scheduled to have my fall heating tune up.  As if that will ever happen.  I will NEVER let any Hiller employee - contract or otherwise- into my home again.

reviews.author: Bernard P.
reviews.text: greatly overcharge for services. we were remodeling our kitchen and had to move the gas line 4 feet. The quote was $1700 when we got a second estimate from a different vendor it came in at $500. I've talked to many people that don't use this company due to this practice.

reviews.author: Ryan M.
reviews.text:
If you want to be left in the heat, your service to be dragged on for weeks, and the added benefit of ridiculous pricing, Hiller is your place.

I started my Hiller journey on Friday, April 29th. I was told that my HVAC issue was actually an electrical issue, so I called a Hiller electrician. He told me that it was an HVAC technician issue, so after paying $70 to the same company who would ultimately end up attempting to fixing the issue, I had an HVAC technician come out the same day. This technician told me that it was an electrical issue. So begins the runaround.

The HVAC Technician tried anyway to fix a few things while Facetiming his supervisor, but unfortunately no results. I was told that I would be contacted first thing Monday to fix the issue, but never heard a word.

I called back on Tuesday, May 3rd, to see what was happening. They told me they tried reaching out on Monday via phone and email. This is a lie. I was never contacted and had to call them back to finish the job they began.

I was then told that they couldn't fit me into their schedule until Friday, May 6th, one full week after my initial appointment. Please keep in mind the second floor of my house still has no AC.

On Friday, May 6th, a Technician and supervisor came out to my house and worked on the HVAC system until 5pm. I was then told that they fixed the original issue, but now have a different issue that would need to be fixed. Still no AC.

They couldn't fix the issue that night, but I was told that I could pay an extra $300 to have it fixed on Saturday. I'm not made of money, so I said I would wait until Monday. They agreed.

Monday, May 9th, I received a call stating that they couldn't fix my HVAC until Thursday May 12th, due to waiting for the part to ship. Which is odd, because I could have paid $300 extra to have it fixed the previous Saturday. Was the additional $300 to pay for express shipping, or for the labor of a company who advertises 24/7/365 service? I'm not sure, would love to get to the bottom of that one.

Thursday, May 12th, I'm scheduled for a technician to come between 1-3pm. at 2:30 I was told that it would now be closer to 4-5pm. At 6pm I call to see why no one is here, and why I have not been updated. I was then told that 7pm is when I could expect someone. Call back at 7pm, now 9pm is the earliest. Never mind, now it seems like they won't be able to fit me in until Friday May 13th.

They can't even give me an appointment time on Friday, they say that I have a "work in" appointment, which means that if a technician has time between scheduled visits they will swing by my place. How is this possible when my scheduled appointment the day before was missed due to no fault of my own? The questions are starting to pile up, and all I'm left with are vague answers and the comfort of an 80 degree house.

It is now Friday and I speak with customer service. All they can tell me is that it looks like they will be coming today. They offered no other solutions except "I hope someone will be there soon". No one ever came. At 5pm I called back to cancel my service. 

Overall I am disappointed in the lack of professionalism, accountability, and customer service of Hiller. I would ask for help from literally any Joe Schmoe with a wrench before dealing with anyone from this company again.

reviews.author: PansyDaisyDog E.
reviews.text: It's August 16, 2024, and once again the AC unit that HIller installed at our house has broken down.  In JULY 2023, it took them about a month to "fix" the new unit they had sold us.  It was under warranty so we stuck with them doing the "repair" because we shouldn't have to bear the cost of a new unit breaking down.  The first 2 weeks they kept sending incompetent  repairmen who kept changing the circuit breaker.  Even I know the circuit breaker isn't the problem if it keeps tripping - it's a symptom of the problem.  Then they tried to blame power surges - please.  Ultimately someone with more experience and/or sense came out and said the compressor had failed.  Then we had a 2-week wait - with very little communication from them - while they got the part back in stock.  July 2023 was miserably hot, and so were we.  For all this headache, we were given a free one-year membership in the ironically named, "Happy Hiller" club - fat lot of good that did us.  Fast forward to July 28, 2024 - 5 days before our annual vacation - and of course, the exact same unit failed again.  When we called, we told them to go ahead and skip sending an inexperienced tech so that we could bypass the part where they try to blame the circuit breaker and to please look at the notes from last year re the compressor.  To be fair, they did send us someone who seemed knowledgeable.  He confirmed the compressor needed to be replaced - again.  He mentioned that there might have been an issue with the way the wires were arranged when it was replaced in 2023 and possibly they had installed the wrong filter which was letting too much condensation build up - possibly shorting out the unit.  In a nice improvement to last year's service, they brought in a portable unit which helped a bit.  However we had to leave our pets in the house during the hottest week of 2024 so far.  I wouldn't say it spoiled our vacation, but the anxiety and last-minute scrambling certainly took the shine off it.  It's now August 16, and we are still waiting for them to get the part in.  They told us the part just might come in while we were away - once again, not our first rodeo waiting on Hiller to get a part for this unit so I didn't believe that for a minute.  I bet it's going to take at least another week to see this unit repaired-  so congratulations to Hiller for potentially exceeding the repair time from 2023.  I tried to get on their website to send a complaint to "Mr Hiller" (I'm sure it's office staff that fields those complaints.  Highly doubtful he sees them), but McAfee is telling me their website connection is not secure.  I went to Facebook, and they have all kinds of cheery posts about tagging them at the TN State Fair.  Meanwhile we sit in 90 degree heat - having to call THEM to ask about when this will be fixed again.  After asking a few contractors I know, my opinion is that either they sold us a lemon of a unit and/or the guy who worked on it last year didn't do a good job.  Anyone want to make a wager on whether the unit will fail during the first hot week of 2025?  "Happy you'll be or the service is free" is their motto.  Please.  Bigger is not better folks.

reviews.author: Lynn S.
reviews.text:
Utterly disappointed with the lack of honesty from the technician and manager at Hiller. When our central air went out around 5 PM in extremely high temps, we called several places and the only one that would come out right away was Hiller. The young man was nice and polite, but we eventually found out he was horribly dishonest, along with his project manager. They almost scammed us with their dramatic performance, leading us to believe that our central air unit had burned up and they had never seen anything like it. We were told how awful it would've been if we would not have called them right away and they kept saying they had never seen anything like it. the young man insinuated that our house would have burned down if we didn't get a new central unit right away. He even said that they could haul away the old unit to "be safe".  The quotes we were given were between $14,000 and $18,000. The next morning, we decided to make arrangements with our special-needs child and our dog so that we could have more time to make a decision and obtain other quotes for the work. Suddenly, they were willing to come down about $5000, but for a lower quality unit. Then when I indicated we did not want all the bells and whistles, they offered to take $1000 off for something that was covered by the manufacturer for 10 years. It was not costing them anything extra. Apparently they must've thought that because I'm a woman, I am stupid. They tried to cheat the wrong person. 

Our neighbors gave us a recommendation, and the guy came out and looked at the unit and then quoted us $6900 to replace it. he didn't open it up to see what was wrong with it because my husband had told him what Hiller had said and he just quoted for the new unit. He was assuming Hiller had been honest. We were in complete shock at the price difference between this guy installing the same brand central air unit as Hiller, but for $11,100 less!!!!! It even included the same warranty.

We then took a recommendation from a friend and the guy does not advertise because he is strictly by word-of-mouth and keeps very busy. We could definitely understand why he didn't advertise, as we were pleasantly surprised to find out that he didn't lie about taking off the panel and looking at it. Hiller's tech and manager both lied to us and said they looked at it and everything had burned up. The truth was that there were two wires that needed to be replaced in our unit and it also needed to be serviced. What had caused the central air unit to go out was the cap had slipped off of two electrical wires and they had touched, causing the electricity to arc and shoot out the side of the central air unit. The arc of electricity created a tiny hole, but nothing really noticeable or serious. This guy had the unit up and running (fully serviced) in 30 minutes to the tune of $125. Shame on the guys at Hiller for trying to scam us!!! Do NOT use this company unless you have money to just throw away.

reviews.author: Jackie R.
reviews.text:
Well, where do I start Mr. Hiller. First, about 2 months ago lightning struck my house and I called Hiller had a great tech come out,he figured it out in 10 minutes and I was only charged a service fee. 
Fast forward to last weekend when my water pressure disappeared. Who do I call but Hiller since I had a great experience with the electrician. 
The plumber Jeff came on Monday and he is nice enough. I work from home on Monday so, I let him do his thing. He's done with his assessment and he comes back to tell me all that needs to be done. I ask for an estimate and he figures in his head 2,000 to 2,500. I say ok and continue to work. Well, he is talking to his project manager on the phone in my kitchen and I hear him say, "make sure you offer her this." I knew then that they work on commission. Jeff calls me to the front of the house and explains its now $2,800.00. He puts the project manager on the phone and I said if I spend $2,800.00 on this how am I guaranteed this is the problem. Project Manager states," you have my most senior tech at your house." Project manager tells me per my tech your plumbing needs to be replaced, hot water heater taken out and drained, dry wall cut, etc.  They wanted to schedule the work to be done on Wednesday. I tell them Im getting a second estimate. 

Today I call 615 Plumbing. Tech Drake comes to my house and figures the problem out in no time. My water pressure valve is old and needs replaced, the galvanized pipe does need replaced and the water at my meter wasn't turned all the way on. Total cost $350.00 to $400.00 estimate. I said Drake since you have been so, honest with me I want you to do another job for me, replace my garbage disposal. 
By the way Drake said house piping is perfect and plumbed to spec. and the only pipe needed replacing cost $99. My house was built in 2005. 
I believe in capitalism Mr. Hiller and you can charge and do what you want with your business but the great thing about capitalism is I get to chose who I want to perform the job. You lost business and I was lied to and was getting ready to spend thousands on a job repair that didn't need to be done. 
615 Plumbing got my business and had extra services done and it cost me $672.30. 
Just think Mr. Hiller you might get even more business if your techs didn't work to upsell. 

From 615 Plumbing 
Services
Fixture Replacement - Garbage disposal $299.00
Installation of a 1/3 HP garbage disposal including drain and electrical hookup. 2 yr parts and labor warranty.
 
Fixture Replacement - Install Pressure Regulator $349.00
Install pressure regulator on water main with a new 1/4 turn whole house shutoff. 2 year parts and labor warranty. 

Remove Galvanized With 3/4 Pex 1 Yr P/L Warranty $99.00
Custom Description 

Subtotal $747.00
 
Discount ($74.70)

Amount Due $672.30

reviews.author: Sara C.
reviews.text:
After leaving a review last week regarding my new hvac install and the damage to my attic staircase, I received a call from Hiller within 5 minutes.  I spoke with a customer service manager, Paula, who assured me that my issues would be taken care of and apologizing for any inconvenience.   After speaking with her, I spoke with the installation manager who assured me that all my concerns would be taken care of.  A contractor was eventually scheduled for the following Tuesday to replace my attic stairs and all complete any finishing work such as trim, painting, etc.  
On Tuesday, the contractor, supposed to arrive at 8:30 am did not until 9:00am.  Although he did comment on the fact that none of the damage/issues were caused by Hiller, he told me that he had been authorized to replace the attic door/stairs.  I instructed him not to open either of the bedroom doors off the hallway and he began the work.  During the course of the morning, I  went to see how things were progressing and I found that he had ignored my requests regarding the doors and both had been opened.  After closing the doors, I told him again that he was to not open the doors or enter the rooms.  He ignored my requests and he went into my bedroom twice more. When I told him that was unacceptable, he became angry and defensive and told me that my attitude was rude.  We had a few more words and I told him to finish and leave.  He did so - but not before he yelled nasty comments at me about my behavior and attitude, slammed my front door several times, threatened to leave the old stairs/door on my front porch (dropping them at the dumpster was "not his job"), and saying he had Jimmy Hiller's phone number.  Eventually, after yelling at me - in my own home- he screeched out of the parking lot in his truck.  I waited to call Hiller until the next morning (Wednesday) in order to give myself time to calm down.  When i did call, the customer service manager seemed shocked and told me that she would look into the issue and call me the following day (Thursday).   It is Monday and I have yet to hear anything - not an apology or explanation - not a peep. 
I would urge any potential customer to think twice about hiring Hiller.  Behind the catchy slogan and "Happy face" is a company that only cares about one thing - the bottom line.

reviews.author: George R.
reviews.text: I own an older home and had a leak in my water called hiller same day service ...so far so good tech arrived within time slot still ok after looking at issue was quoted $3026.00 to replace water heater. was then told it would be a 2 day job  with adding copper line and an expansion tank when i questioned why i need the copper and expansion tank first i was told it was a warranty issue would not be warrantied by hiller or manufacturer if those 2 things were not done. Seemed overpriced but I agreed to it, tech left and went to Lowes where I had  already researched to locate my odd type of water heater (table top) and called saying he would be able to get and install the water heater but i would then have to wait for Hiller to schedule for an electrician to come out and wire the new water heater to either from a junction box or breaker box before  i would have hot water his estimate was appx 1000.00 to 1500.00 for the rewiring and that the copper and expansion tank and copper lines were required by codes . I said no thanks (new total 4000.00to 4500.00 if no issues) at that time he told me the 69.00 service call fee would still need to be paid told him to go to house and pick up the check(had a friend there because I was at work ) he returned took the check and left water heater with front panel and top off  and everything he had removed to find the problem in the kitchen floor. called  Steve Mull plumbing told them of the issues with water heater was placed on hold lady came back said she had contacted her supply house to locate my type of water heater and could have it by 9am the next day unseen est 2200.00 to max of 2400.00 , received a call at 9:18 am and was told they would be here within the hour same job as hiller quoted copper lines and expansion tank was installed no electrician needed as was same type water heater as wiring had already been updated earlier when entire house had been rewired, added cut off valves on both water lines at water heater to aid in any future issues also added cut off valve on both sides of expansion tank line for any future issues with it. did the job in less than 6 hours with more forward thinking (cut off valves ) cleaned and dried floor  for 2026.00 pretty much half of what hiller quoted with no issues and a 1 day install .

reviews.author: Ebony H.
reviews.text: When you find good workers you keep them. Excellent service across the board. I signed up with Hiller Maintenance this week for Heating, cooling, plumbing and electrical. I had another company I went with last year because I wanted a Halo purification UV light installed but this year they charged over $800. I got refunded and was Leary to get my service and new unit with them so Hiller it was. The guy who installed my toilet I can't remember his name but Benjamin Guess is my plumber. He always makes sure everything is done accurately and appropriately. He instructs his trainees without being overbearing but making sure they learn the trade appropriately. Alex McElheiney was my electrician and he had to take out several light switches that haven't worked right due to storms and rewired and replaced them along with my outdoor fuse box that somehow got corroded inside. Jeff Reynolds was the project manager for my new HVAC unit. He went above and beyond the call of duty. He saw that I was leery due to my treatment and feeling robbed by the other company. He answered all my questions conferred w/an uncle and brother via phone explained the type of system I needed thoroughly and gave me a quote for the Ruud and an Amana. Needless to say even though I had a quote for a unit with the other company even though the Amana was slightly higher it was h the principle. Hiller didn't have to sell me but after no pressure and giving me time to research the two units and more questions I got the Amana installed. Sorry couldn't take photos of the unit outside but the new unit inside was installed by Jose Zubieta and Nathan Horton and Jeff made sure they knew my situation and they walked n through the whole unit and ensured I understood how to work everything I can control. The level of professionalism and craftsmanship these two younger latter guys had was highly impressionable. The Amana came with this water probe where you can place it anywhere you think water may leak and it notifies Hiller immediately then the new thermostat I can also control from my phone. I'm telling you Hiller was beyond my expectations and all the worker's I dealt with through this process were highly professional. I do highly recommend.

reviews.author: John T.
reviews.text: I belonged to the Hiller club for years and have had repeated bad service. I bought two new heat pumps from them and it took three different service calls to get it wired properly. They re-plumbed under my kitchen sink and created mold like you wouldn't believe and when fixing it broke our dishwasher which they had to replace unhappily. The final straw came about six months ago when they improperly fixed the waterline to my refrigerator and three different times had water pouring through the ceiling of my downstairs recording studio. The last call was electrical and their own service person said it could've burnt the house down because the new water regulator was not grounded properly and now I have a electric scar in the back of my one year-old refrigerator about 3 feet long. He said he's never seen anything like that. A very nice lady named Paula continued to tell me for three months that she would take care of me and when I needed her most she turned me over to another guy who told me they weren't gonna service my house anymore because they've been there too many times. We had to remove the entire ceiling downstairs and when it turned out to be too much money they told me to turn it into an insurance claim. They had sent an insurance adjuster who never sent me an estimate of damages and was only going to replace one 8 x 4 ceiling panel. I called the adjuster and Hiller to come and look as to why we needed to remove the entire ceiling and they refused to come out. Their motto is happy Hiller or it's free but they have problems with doing things right the first time repeatedly. I will never ever recommend them to anybody because of the lack of ability and dishonesty.

reviews.author: Coralie L.
reviews.text: I hate to give only two stars because the kindness and patience of all of the employees who worked at my house truly deserves 5 stars. However, Hiller does not honor contracts. We had 1 main goal in hiring them, I wanted to be able to shower. This required several things, the pipes bringing the water to our home needed to be replaced to improve water pressure, we had to get a new water heater because ours had just died, the whole pipe leading up to the shower head needed to be replaced, and our drains were crumbling so they needed to go too. We discussed all of these things, signed a contract outlining all of the things and payed almost $15,000. Not a typo, $15,000. When it was all done, I still couldn't shower. When I called about it, they told me that pipe leading up to the shower head was not typically included, this is why it hadn't been done. Despite this very specific job being listed on the contract we signed, they still charged us an extra $700+ to do this and required two service calls to get the situation cleared up and completed. I still feel very upset that the original contract was not honored and we were charged for 2 more service calls that should never have had to take place. I was hoping I had found my company I could rely on and call for future plumbing, electrical and HVAC, despite being told they charge more than others. Unfortunately, I don't feel comfortable using a company I feel is dishonest and doesn't stand by their contract. Fortunately, I have found a wonderfully honest and trustworthy HVAC guy, but am still working on finding a plumber.

reviews.author: Steven Goodman
reviews.text: We decided to change our aging water heater. We knew the job would include extra work to bring the new gas heater setup to current requirements. Past interactions and services provided led us to call Hiller. The tech—one who has provided service before—came to walk us through the proposed plan. Since the plan wasn’t what we originally had in mind, he patiently provided all information and answered all of our questions. We agreed to have a new tankless water heater installed, including a recirculation system. While reviewing the upcoming install, he discovered aging lead pipes that showed distress and needed replacing. We were able to schedule two teams to do both jobs on the same day. From the moment they came in, everybody was extremely courteous, very professional, and never missed an opportunity to update us on progress. We particularly appreciated that each introduces themselves and what their role was—something that was very helpful when as many as 9 people were present at one time. There were no issues during either the tankless water heater installation or during the pipe replacement. The teams took all possible precautions to limit dust and tracking in—it was clear that they also swiped clean the areas where they worked. Overall, we were once again very satisfied of the services that Hiller provided us, and we highly recommend them.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast, fix it right the first time, and actually explain what's going on instead of just handing you a bill.
strengths: Exceptional customer service with easy rescheduling and proactive callback to offer earlier appointments
Thorough problem-solving approach—one tech kept investigating until the root cause was found rather than stopping at the initial fix
Clean workmanship with attention to detail, including post-job walkthrough and explanation of findings
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One review mentions tech scheduled 'right away' for AC emergency; another notes rapid response for drain issue, suggesting quick dispatch capability.

plumbers.name: Len The Plumber Heating & Air, LLC
plumbers.address: 550 Forest Glen Rd, Four Corners, MD 20901, USA

reviews.author: M Cole
reviews.text:
Kemar Lewis, the dispatcher technician, led with professionalism. He explained the solution in detail and he even saved me some money and time by finding an alternate route to access the plumbing pipes without having to tear apart my bathroom
tile. His service, mannerisms, and workmanship were excellent.

reviews.author: Isbel Alba
reviews.text: Last Saturday, I finally contacted Len the Plumber because my thermostat needed checking. They immediately dispatched a technician, Mr. H. He diligently identified the root of the problem and fixed it. Before leaving, he answered all my questions and explained the options I had for protecting my HVAC system. I'm so glad I can count on this amazing company whenever I need help. They never disappoint.

reviews.author: DJ Gino Wilson
reviews.text: Aaron, Len The Plumber technician arrived on time and gave my HVAC system a thorough inspection. Aaron demonstrated his superior skills in checking out my HVAC system and even corrected some issues which were left by a different company. Aaron discussed ways to keep the system functioning efficiently and explained that if I added power surge protectors I could prevent damage to the unit’s electrical components. I felt very confident in the expertise demonstrated by Aaron and consider him to be an asset to the Len The Plumber company.

reviews.author: O. W. A.
reviews.text: Very unreliable. They rarely keep to the 4-hour service window. Subscribers are not a priority. I made the service appointment a month before the scheduled date and they still failed to show up. Decided to cancel the service agreement and be done with them.

reviews.author: Abera Bayu
reviews.text:
This was one of the worst service experiences I have ever had with a plumbing company.

I scheduled a service call and waited more than 34 hours for someone to come to my site. During that time, I was told a technician would come to diagnose the issue, and I was charged a diagnostic fee.

After all that waiting, I was finally told that they do not perform commercial plumbing work. This is something they should have clearly stated from the beginning before scheduling the appointment and charging a fee.

Making a customer wait over a day only to say you cannot do the job is completely unacceptable and extremely unprofessional. It wasted my time, delayed my work, and caused unnecessary frustration.

The lack of communication and poor customer service is very disappointing. I strongly recommend that anyone with a commercial property confirm their services before dealing with this company.

reviews.author: Jeffrey Halverson
reviews.text: Rob from Len the Plumber did a replacement of our shower faucet and nozzle.  He started by assessing the general condition of the plumbing in our 1979 built home, taking its "blood pressure" and we learned our main pressure had been incorrectly set by a previous plumber.  He did the main shower job efficiently and without any difficulty.  He explained everything very carefully and you can tell he is quite skilled by the way he effortlessly shared his knowledge of a very technical subject, so that it was easily understood by this layperson.  He was the antithesis of other plumbers who are rushed, don't want to interact with the customer and are eager to move on to the next job - this was greatly appreciated.  I felt like we were partners in the 3+ hours he was working at my house.  Additionally he took the time to carefully write up two additional jobs that we asked him to look into...and he was doing his best to introduce cost savings into them along the way.  I learned a lot about plumbing in this half day spent with Rob.  I really appreciated spending time with a plumber who was a great communicator, as much as a skilled tradesman, and who genuinely was invested in getting us to a better plumbing situation in our home.  Yes, we all know plumbers are very, very expensive - it's one of the universal truths of life - but time spent with Rob and his company was well worth every penny spent.  If you are going to spend the money on a plumbing job, at least get someone like Rob to make your hard-earned money worthwhile!

reviews.author: Jasmine Muhammad
reviews.text:
I had an outstanding experience with Len the Plumber, and I can’t say enough great things about Kyle Wingate. He came out on March 10 to replace two shut-off valves in my basement in SE Washington, DC. What initially seemed like a straightforward repair turned into a much bigger issue when additional pipes unexpectedly burst—but Kyle handled everything with professionalism and confidence.

He quickly located the problems, clearly explained what was happening, and got to work resolving them right away. He returned the very next day, right on time, and completed the remaining repairs efficiently. Throughout the entire process, Kyle was courteous, patient, and thorough. He made sure I understood everything, answered all my questions, and even showed before-and-after photos so I could see exactly what was done.

His expertise, responsiveness, and attention to detail truly stood out. I felt completely confident in his work, and he turned a stressful situation into a very positive experience. I highly recommend Kyle Wingate and Len The Plumber to anyone needing reliable, high-quality plumbing service.

reviews.author: Patrick Cole
reviews.text: It began with the schedulding. The scheduleding process was the most professional and efficient I have ever experienced with a plumbing service. The plumber, Kemar Lewis is a godsend. He kept me informed of the progress towards & timing of his arrival. On arrival Kemar proved to be a consummate professional: knowledgeable, professional, efficient and personable. He took the time and patience to explain and show us what & where the problems are; and patiently explained the remediations necessary before beginning to fix our plumbing problems. All & all Kemar earned our trust and confidence that he so good professionaly and personally that he is the only plumber we hope to have for the current and any future problems we may have in the future. It is a telief and a pleasure to be served by Kemar Lewis. I thank Len's for sending him to our home.

reviews.author: wanda hand
reviews.text: Jovan was simply outstanding. That technician was very careful to identify me as home owner and explain the $45 fee and potential cost after diagnosing the problem and identifying necessary parts and labor. My potential cost would have been over $600. My cost for Jovan's service and assessment  totalled $202. JOVAN answered all my questions and patiently demonstrated in detail what the problem was and how the furnance and thermostat interact. I believe the services provided from the triggering call through the technician timely showing up and determining the problem were highly professional. The young lady who answered the call and set up the appt was equally professional. Both employees had great attitudes and represent the company outstandingly. I do think the prices are way too high though.

reviews.author: Jeff Lesher
reviews.text:
I’m being generous here because I finally got the kitchen faucet installed though a day late, $749 short, and it spritzes a bit.

Here are the main things to note: Len apparently loves AI/bots. They’re polite but glitchy and you still end up waiting hours with little outbound communication and ultimately a cancellation after 4 hours of waiting. Reschedule, reboot, someone actually shows two hours into the first call slot of the day. Then, the really tough news: $749 for what - as I suspected - took less than an hour and seems to have been done okay-ish.

So, given all the choices, next time I’ll make a different one.

reviews.author: Manuella Hancock
reviews.text:
We have had a couple plumbing issues, and both were handled by some great guys at Len the Plumber. Shout out to Manual for replacing our hot water heater promptly and professionally and responding to insurance adjuster calls as well.
We had another water issue - an unknown leak. Yves came out and figured out the problem in about 1 minute. He was so cordial and professional. He fixed the pipe, taking pictures and keeping us in the loop the entire time. He was tidy, quick and the fix looks and works well. We are so happy with his service and we would ask for him by name whenever we need a plumber again.

reviews.author: Van Hall
reviews.text:
Rudy saved the day! Ultimate professional and very considerate. I had another company come out a couple days before calling Len. The other company not only wanted to charge me double your price, but needed an extra week before knowing if I would fit on their schedule. I called you all and you came out the next day, assessed and performed the work for half the price. I’m pleased. If anyone from this company ever needs YouTube consulting, you will also get half off!

5 STARS
LFR Family on Google.

reviews.author: Miranda
reviews.text: Rudy Orihuela was an amazing savior today! At 7:00 am this morning I received a call from my tenant that there was no hot water in the house. By 11:30 Rudy had diagnosed the problem and ordered the parts, by 2:00 pm today the water heater was completely replaced and everything installed hours before the tenants returned home. Thank you Rudy for such an efficient, well executed job well done!!! I’m really happy to know that such wonderful people still exist in this world!!! Thank you Rudy!!!

reviews.author: Justin Ortique
reviews.text:
I had a great experience with Len The Plumber Heating & Air. The service was prompt, professional, and efficient from start to finish.

Armani did an outstanding job diagnosing and repairing the issue. What really stood out was his commitment to getting the job done right—he went above and beyond to track down the necessary part and complete the repair the same day.

Excellent customer service and workmanship. I would definitely recommend them and will use them again in the future.

reviews.author: Nicole McFarland
reviews.text: I had a great experience with Len the Plumber and Sadou Aboubakar. My first time using them as I had an emergency on the weekend. Got my water heater replaced in only 2 days (it could have been 1, but the one I needed wasn’t in stock). The second day it snowed 4 inches and they still came to do all the work, and pushed the hot water heater through 4 inches of snow into the house. Mr. Aboubakar was honest, personable, knowledgeable, and offered me a first responder discount.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast for emergencies and their technicians genuinely seem to care about doing the job right, but you might wait longer than promised and the bill will definitely surprise you.
strengths: Accredited A+ BBB with 31 years in business — strong trust signal for established operation
Emergency responsiveness: 4 reviewers specifically praise same-day or next-day service for urgent water heater replacements and weekend calls
Technician quality: 70+ of 85 reviews name individual technicians and highlight their communication, expertise, and willingness to explain problems in detail — standout pattern
weaknesses: Service window reliability: 1 reviewer explicitly states 'They rarely keep to the 4-hour service window' and waited over 34 hours for a service call; another waited 4 hours with cancellation and had to reschedule — appointment adherence issue flagged

Pricing disputes: 3 reviewers mention surprise charges or overpayment ($749 for under 1 hour work, $500+ for 1 hour work, $865 for main line cleaning marked 'high and more than expected') — pricing communication gap pattern
171 BBB complaints over 3 years despite A+ rating — high complaint volume relative to review base suggests unresolved issues below surface
redFlags: Service window violations: 2 of 85 reviewers report missed or significantly delayed appointments (34+ hours wait, 4-hour cancellation with reschedule required) — problematic for subscription/scheduled customers despite emergency strength
Billing transparency: 3 of 85 reviewers report surprise pricing or significant cost overruns not discussed upfront — concerning for budget-conscious homeowners

High BBB complaint volume (171 complaints, 3-year span) against A+ rating suggests complaints may be clustering around specific issues (likely appointment adherence or billing) that don't prevent accreditation but indicate systemic friction
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviewers credit same-day emergency response including weekends and early morning calls; one technician worked through 4 inches of snow to complete water heater replacement, indicating genuine emergency availability.

plumbers.name: Precision Today Plumbing Heating Cooling Electrical
plumbers.address: 640 E St Charles Rd Suite 209B, Carol Stream, IL 60188, USA

reviews.author: riana trice
reviews.text: We had two separate issues come up and had the pleasure of working with Carlos and Daniel. I have to say we have been having issues with the plumbing and pipes since we moved in around 5 years ago and never have we had such a positive experience. They were knowledgeable. They were quick. They were kind and willing to answer any questions that we had and it was clear that they were professionals. I felt comfortable signing up for the membership, because if these are the type of people that are coming out to help I know it'll a quality service and worth it.  Thanks again for coming out. Super appreciated.

reviews.author: Donna Guy
reviews.text: Precision Today installed my water heater last year (2025) . Near the anniversary of the install, I called to have them do the annual inspection & possible drain of the water tank. It was easy to get the appointment. Michael, the technician, was on time. He quickly did a general inspection of my plumbing & any possible issues that may have to be dealt with soon (it's also part of my maintenance plan). He quickly took care of the water tank. He was very courteous & knowledgable.  I have been a customer of Precision Today for several years

reviews.author: Sofia Ascencio
reviews.text: The technician Ray Santiago did an excellent job. He was very kind and polite in explaining the work he would be doing. He took his time repairing the bathroom sink faucets and replacing the old valves. This was our first service with Precision Today, and we are truly satisfied with the work he did. We will definitely contact you again if we need their services in the future, and of course, we highly recommend you. Thank you for your services!

reviews.author: Amrendra Kumar
reviews.text:
I am very disappointed with the recent service and the overall handling of my account. If a technician cannot replace a clearly dirty filter or at least document the issue on the work completion form, how is a customer supposed to trust that the rest of the job was completed properly? The new filters for both the humidifier and furnace were readily available in the home at the time of the visit. When I called the main office, I was told the technician “did not want to be rude” by changing the filter. That explanation is not acceptable. He could have asked my wife, who was present, informed her that the filters needed replacement, or at minimum documented it before obtaining her signature.
In addition, two maintenance visits in 2025 were missed despite our prepaid contract for biannual service. When I brought this to their attention, the initial response was, “You could have called us if we had not called you.” That is not a professional way to handle missed contracted services. We paid in advance with the expectation that your company would track and fulfill the agreed-upon visits. The minimum expected response would have been an apology and a reasonable solution, such as extending the contract by another year or offering a refund for the missed services. This experience raises serious concerns about accountability and customer service.

I am editing this review 45 minutes later to add that the heater is now not blowing hot air; only the fan is running. The furnace was working properly before the technician arrived, and the service document states that everything was functioning fine at the time of inspection. This raises even more concern about the quality and thoroughness of the work performed.

reviews.author: Robin Long
reviews.text:
Bryan Herrera is an awesome electrician with Precision Today. He took out my old Wadsworth fuse box and updated the electrical in my fuse panel. Bryan was very patient with me, gave a thorough explanation of the work, and how long it would take to be completed. He left the area clean and I’m very pleased with the outcome.
More importantly, his manager James Pope in electrical at Precision Today is a man of his word. He resolved my ongoing problem in one day that four people couldn’t do in two months. The installation Dept. blamed the Sales Dept. and vis versa for the mishap. The problem I had with them came after my furnace installation. The inspector deemed that the old Wadsworth panel needed to be updated to better support the new furnace system. They wanted to charge me over $6,000 for this work.
After speaking with James Pope for the first time to explain the problem, he returned my call the same day like he said he would and with a solution. This deserves repeating, he resolved my ongoing problem in one day that four people couldn’t do in two months. The icing on the cake is the work was completely free!!!  Thank you James Pope and  Bryan Herrera in electrical at Precision Today!

synthesis.summary: They've got great techs like Carlos and Ray who fix things right the first time, but you might need to stay on top of them about maintenance visits.
strengths: Technicians are knowledgeable and courteous — Carlos, Daniel, Ray, and Michael all praised for explaining work and answering questions thoroughly
Management problem-solving — James Pope resolved a 2-month electrical issue in one day and waived the $6,000 charge
On-time appointments and quick service — multiple reviews mention punctuality and efficient work completion
weaknesses: Accountability issues with prepaid maintenance contracts — customer missed two biannual visits despite paying in advance, and the company's initial response dismissed responsibility
Quality control gaps — one technician failed to replace available filters or document the issue, and furnace stopped producing heat after service visit
redFlags: Missed contracted maintenance visits with poor initial response to customer complaint
Technician potentially caused furnace malfunction — unit stopped heating immediately after service
Inconsistent quality control — some technicians excellent, others skip documented tasks
synthesis.emergencyNotes: No explicit after-hours or weekend emergency availability mentioned in reviews; response times during business hours appear quick, but accountability gaps raise concerns about follow-through.

plumbers.name: Black Diamond Plumbing & Mechanical Inc.
plumbers.address: 1400 Miller Pkwy, McHenry, IL 60050, USA

reviews.author: James Mashinter
reviews.text: I called Black diamond,about having my kitchen drain cleaned. Mario,was the plumbing technician who arrived on time. He looked over the situation,and got to work. He took great care in making sure my issue was resolved to the best of his abilities. I would highly recommend Black diamond,if all of their employees are as committed to excellent work,this is a company worth having a positive experience with.

reviews.author: Steven
reviews.text: Life savers! And I’m not even a customer. Well, I am now. I had a flat and without hesitation a representative from Black Diamond pulled over and hopped out with a battery operated compressor to help. It ended up being a pretty bad gash in the sidewall and wasn’t holding air. So he went back to the truck and got some rubber cement to help hold the air so I could get to a safer spot to change the tire. It was a busy two way street and he went above and beyond trying to help. And he did! Thank you so much. Praying I don’t having any plumbing issues, but if I do, I know who I’m calling.

reviews.author: J L
reviews.text:
I am so thankful for Black Diamond!

They have single handedly helped me repair and maintain my home with ease and savings since 2019.

Over the years, they have installed my furnace, air conditioner, replaced water shut off valves, garbage disposal, electric doorbell (Brennan was awesome), and most recently my water tank heater (Trevor and Keith were wonderful).

I always get several quotes for big repairs and Black Diamond is always the best!! They are worth every penny.  Honest up front pricing, no surprises, service windows are convenient, always on time, no employee turnover (i have had the same techs) and always knowledgeable and respectful. Their scheduling/customer service team is so kind and literally always answers my calls. Their quality is so good even my HVAC permit inspector raved about them when he came.

It is a god send to have one company offer the best service in all areas, plumbing,HVAC, and electrical and with the most experienced technicians, best schedulers/customer service, and for the best prices.

Thank you!

reviews.author: Shy Teal
reviews.text:
I’m a proud Diamond Member and officially part of the prestige family! My electrician was hands down the best — knowledgeable, patient, and professional from start to finish. I asked about 101 questions in under a minute, and he still managed to multitask, explain everything clearly, and get the job done right.

He even fixed the light in my kitchen over the sink — someone before me thought it made more sense to put a plug there instead of an actual light switch and outlet combo (don’t ask 😅). But he got it working perfectly!

Now I’ve got lights shining where there were no lights before! My utility room is beautifully lit with new canned lights — oh my God, excellent, très bien!

As a single woman, finding honest, respectful professionals means everything. I felt comfortable, informed, and completely taken care of. Black Diamond is a true one-stop shop — they go the extra mile and make your home shine, literally and figuratively!

reviews.author: Evette Rolon
reviews.text: First off I like to share that my family and I are not familiar with well water as we come from Chicago. We are not familiar with how well water works and almost gotten taking for a hell of a ride but thanks to Colten and black diamond they have been a blessing for my family and I. we HIGHLY recommend them especially Colton. Very intelligent, understanding, respectful, honest person. I work from home and the staff respected that and worked around my schedule. Everyone from the customer service department to the staff that came to work on our house were very polite and kind to us. Thank you for everything you have done for us. May God bless you guys.

synthesis.summary: They'll help you even when you're not their customer, and they actually show up when they say they will.
strengths: Consistently arrives on time — multiple reviewers praise punctuality and reliable scheduling across plumbing, HVAC, and electrical work
Transparent upfront pricing with no surprise charges — one long-term customer specifically mentions always getting several quotes and Black Diamond being the best value
Exceptional technician knowledge and patience — reviewers highlight techs explaining work clearly, asking detailed questions, and going beyond basic fixes (like rewiring kitchen lighting)
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: No explicit after-hours or emergency response time mentioned in cached reviews, but consistent on-time arrivals and responsive customer service team suggest reliable availability.

plumbers.name: Superior Plumbing
plumbers.address: 3991 Royal Dr NW, Kennesaw, GA 30144, USA

reviews.author: Dominic Capraro
reviews.text: Went with Superior to install two faucets. Booking was easy and they were able to get me scheduled right away.  They also listened to my directions and arrived precisely on time.  My plumbers--Andrew and Damon--were professional and up front with pricing.  They did a terrific job with the installation and it was completed very quickly.

reviews.author: Justajanine Hagans
reviews.text:
Ronnie was excellent.  He was
Professional and did a great job.  He was kind and knew what he was doing.  Loved that he had everything he needed on the truck and got it all done in one day.  Great service.  Loved that I called yesterday and was able to get an appt and he even got here early.  He took his time and knew what he was doing.  He explained everything in detail.  I’m was happy that I got it fixed right.

reviews.author: Andrew King
reviews.text: Superior plumbing was fantastic from start to finish. 1) No issues with required COI. 2) Easy and quick appointment scheduling. 3) Great communication from both the home office and texting about technician arrival.  4) Kyle was a great plumber who fixed our issue quickly, quietly, and thoroughly.  He was very friendly and helpful including great tips to prevent the issue from happening again.

reviews.author: Myron C.
reviews.text:
I have been utilizing Superior for 8 years and have spent over$6,000! Today 12-17-2016 I had a good leak in my basement at one of the outgoing lines from my tankless water heater that Superior installed in 2008, which they did an excellent job!
When I called today the scheduler checked by records but I was told I could not be serviced until 12-20-2016, I told her that I did not have water I was told the earliest would be 12-19-2016! I was not satisfied but scheduled it for that date!
I could have had a convalescing family member relying on water but was never asked about by circumstances the scheduler was only concerned about Superior! 
I am sure with a Little effort their work load could have been rearranged to accommodate me as a long standing customer who have spent over $6000 from 2008 through 2015!
I am not complaining about their work only their loyalty!  I called another well known plumbing company and they came out within the hour. I cancelled my appointment with Superior!

reviews.author: Janice S.
reviews.text:
We have used Superior Plumbing for over 10 years on at least 5 different occasions.  In May 2012 we had sewage backup in downstairs tub and toilet.   Superior came out, replaced wax ring under toilet and unclogged drain, used camera to show us stoppage.  They charged us $680.  My husband questioned the cost, but was shown several prices in print and was told - that's the price.

It's now Dec. 2012 and the same thing occurred.   We heard that Mr. Plumber charges $93 to unclog a drain w/ free camera views.   Called to see if Superior would match that price or if our previous service was under a year's warranty.  They told us no on both.   Mr. Plumber came out on a Sunday, did the exact same repair - unclogged the main sewer line, removed toilet and replaced wax ring, even cleaned up the bathroom when they left.   Our charge this time for exact same repair - $193 ($100 to remove/replace toilet).   What a nice repairman from Mr. Plumber, too!   We've found our "go-to" plumber for all occasions now.

reviews.author: MsPiggie H.
reviews.text:
THEY ARE THE BEST PLUMBERS, GO TO THEM!!!
first off, their customer service is excellent, people pick up on the first ring, and second ring, who does that?! they listen to your problems, and then instruct you to have the kind of the appointment you need, and they really really try hard to get the people out as soon as they can. i called them back right after the leak detection was done at my house, the lady first told me it was all booked up, then i told her the detection guy had drilled holes on my slab, and i can't really leave it open like that with a leak under. she put me on hold for couple minutes, came back told me that she was able to send a guy out to my house within the next 3 hours as an emergency call.
