I had total of two plumbers came out, but not at the same time, because the problem was so complicated. the first one i had was very nice, patient, and hard working. he literally laid on my floor, tried to fix the leak on my beat up copper pipe from 5pm to 10pm!!!! he didn't ask for water, a tissue, or anything during this entire time until i offered. my pipe was one of the difficult fixes according to him, it was under the slab, between two other pipes, and a waste line, with leaked water around, in order to get the water dry out from inside the pipe, he went in and out multiple times to blow into the garden holes faucet himself in the cold!!! hoping the water would be forced out that way. unfortunately, before he was able to fix this one leak, my severely damaged piped had already burst out another tiny hole next to the first leak!!! we then decided to call it for the night, but he offered to come back early in the morning the next day, and tried to fix it even more.
He came back the next morning around 8am to try to fix the pipe from the night before, yet i had already decided that the pipe was in such poor condition which it would have saved me more money to re plumbed the house instead. He then agreed with me. He signed off on my bill, told me that my dispatch fee will be charged to the next plumber since this would considered as one job, then he told me that even though he had worked for so long the previous night, but he somehow didn't get the problem solved, so he wasn't going to charge me anything!!! i was very satisfied with how professional they company is, and their plumbers are!!! they really make me feel that i'm in such a good hand, who i can trust competely!!!
I then called to have the second plumber come out, which he was an expert on service, and re piping water lines for houses. he came out around 11am, not too long after i called, looked around my house, discussed couple of ideas with me, we then agreed on an idea and it's price. he was able to get the work started right away!!! it just seemed like he had all the tools and parts in the world to do the job, he did have to go out to the shop to get some pipes (pvc and copper), but he returned very quickly. he cut up couple of places on my ceiling and on the drywalls since we were re piping it throw the attic, in neatly squares because he told me it would be easy for me to patch up later. keep in mind they are plumbers, so they would not patch up walls for you, filling the floor is extra charge too, so i offered to do the filling myself also. he was crawling through my attic, back and forth many times to get the pipe positioned right, and sealed correctly he then even secure them onto the side for me, so it was very easy to get to, exam, or clean later. he used copper links also, which needs to be torched up and seal properly, this guy got all the places sealed up, and right on the first try!!!! the connections between pipes were designed very smartly by him, he was very quick and good at what he does, he was able to have all the pipes connected, and torched up in a very tiny space under one of my tubs. 
i'm not a professional to describe exactly what they have done for me and my house, but i tried to write as much in details above to let you all know that i have dealt with many plumbers before, and i know only a good, and experienced can, and able to get the work done, and correctly done. this company is the place to go, they have the best group of people you can possibly ask for to fix your stuff!!!
btw, i feel the price was very reasonable since the first guy didn't charge me anything for the first day at all with all the time n work he did do, i was charged total of 3000 dollars to re piping a whole section of my house on the second day. you can also go print out the coupons on their websites. 
good luck!!!

reviews.author: Stephanie K.
reviews.text: If I could rate them zero stars I would.  I called Superior plumbing to replace the toilet in my master bathroom as the old toilet was 19 years old and leaking at the bottom near the tile floor and bolts rusted out due to old age.  When the plumber arrived ( Beau B.) he did not put on the shoe coverings/ booties, and I had to ask him to put them over his boots. I have over $10k worth of brand new carpet and hard wood floors in my home and I did not want him tracking his boots all over my brand new flooring.  He had to go back out to his truck to get the shoe coverings and he slammed my front door so hard that my entire house shook, so he was mad from the beginning.  Upon looking at my toilet he advised I would need a new toilet.  I asked if the work could be done the same day because when I called to make the appointment the person on the phone I spoke to (Moe) advised the plumbers arrive with toilets in the truck and are able to complete the work same day.  The plumber (Beau B.) very rudely and gruffly said "No, I ain't waiting around here for an hour while you go and buy a toilet and come back."  I immediately told him he did not need to speak to me in that manner and that I was asking a simple question because the person on the phone advised the work could be done the same day.  He then tried to back peddle and say he was not being rude because he knew I was going to complain.  Also, the price he quoted me was double the price that 3 other licensed, insured, master plumbers have quoted me to install a new toilet.  After he left I called Superior customer service to file a complaint and asked for a manager to call me back.  After 48 hours no manager called me back.  I then called back again and cancelled then install with Superior and they wanted to charge me the $62.50 dispatch fee which I refused to pay due to the poor experience I had with the plumber and the way I was treated and the fact that a manager could not call me back after 48 hours.  I had to demand to speak to a manager and finally they put a manager on the phone.  He never apologized or took responsibility for the behavior of his employee.  He never said sorry.  Finally after a very heated conversation he agreed to waive the $62.50 dispatch fee.  I will NEVER use Superior again and I will tell everyone I know never to use them.  They do not have superior customer service and they do not stand behind their work and promises made on their website.  Their prices are sky high, double what other licensed master plumbers charge.  Do your homework and get several quotes from other plumbers and find yourself another quality plumber and save yourself over 50% of what Superior is charging.  They should be ashamed of the prices they are charging people, clearly taking advantage of people.

reviews.author: Denis C.
reviews.text:
Issue - Leaking Pressure Reduction Valve, which is typically found near the main water line to reduce the water pressure to a more "digestible" rate for servicing the interior plumbing fixtures.

Since this was a unique part, with a few not-so-straightforward connections, I didn't want to experiment trying fix it myself. Unfortunately, I did not have a plumber in mind at the time and needed to get the drip fixed as soon as possible. Referencing the area commercials I did some online "window" shopping, and given the Superior website was slightly more user-friendly and helpful, I contacted them to arrange a service call.

On the day of, the plumber/technician was running behind for the given service window and the dispatcher called to confirm the adjusted time - all very reasonable. Upon arrival the technician introduced himself, took down some basic information and began his assessment.  

Nothing shocking about the required work, however, at $485 the price certainly was. Unfortunately, most established service companies have set pricing for a given service much like an auto mechanic or Doctor does, so getting understanding of what goes into the pricing isn't always clear. While the price wasn't grossly obscene I didn't feel it was a great value - retail, the parts go for $55 PRV and $18 shut-off valve. Work effort took him about 2 hours total from setup to clean-up. 

At completion the job was well-done, but again it pays to look around before you really need to have a service call. If you can find a reliable and highly recommended independent plumber they might be your best bet and perhaps a little more flexible in pricing, saving you probably 30% or more. What added insult to the cost was that Superior keeps the service call price - cost of providing an onsite visit and quote, in the billing even after you agree to the accept the work, $37.50 - shouldn't that be factored into the job-quote already!?

One other pricing reference, I had asked about possibly replacing my hot water heater - standard 40 gallon, gas unit plus an expansion tank (part of the newer code) $1450, a new retail water heater goes for $350 - $400 and expansion tank $50-60, so they would easily be charging $1000 in labor alone.

Overall, the work will get done to specification in a timely manner - great for piece of mind and if you are willing to accept the premium that goes along without researching and pulling multiple quotes. So for service they get 5 stars and price/value 2.5.

reviews.author: Amy R.
reviews.text: We called Superior out to a water leak coming through our floor last Saturday. When the plumber came, I told him we also had a roof leak and showed him a water spot on the ceiling. I asked if the two could be related and he said, "no". He cut 3 holes in our wall and could not find leak - he got in attic and said the water on the floor must be from the roof leak - charged us $400. 4 days later our entire ceiling was covered in water dripping through ceiling onto the bed below. The roofer replacing our roof that day got in our attic and said there was indeed a pipe leak and that the attic was covered in water. We called the same plumber back out - He said it would be almost another $400 to repair the pipe that was leaking - We said we already paid $400 three days earlier for them to tell us there was no leak - his response was, "well I didn't fix anything, now I have to charge you to fix it". I stated that he told us there was NO LEAK when we paid him $400 three days earlier and now we have significant damage. His response was, "well that's not my fault". He asked if I saw the leak when I was in the attic and I told him that I did not climb over the rafters to look at the plumbing and that I paid HIM to do that. We called the office and asked to speak to a manager - they said we had to request this with the plumber. I told them that we had a disagreement with the plumber and that we were not confident that he would have a manager contact us. I told the plumber multiple times that I wanted him to have a manager call me and he kept asking me why. Before the plumber left, my husband made sure that he knew we wanted to speak to a manager. After he left, I filled out the online contact form to have someone contact us - The woman (Erica) responded to my request that I was not present at the house at the time the plumber was there on the second day and that they would only speak to my husband that was there - She said they had not heard from my husband that he wanted to speak to a manager. That same day, my husband followed up with the email and told her that he DID in fact tell the plumber multiple times that he wanted to speak to a manager and that he was requesting AGAIN to have someone call him. She responded that he would hear from someone in 48-72 hours. Tomorrow will be day 5 and we have YET to hear from a manager!! TERRIBLE customer service - rude dispatchers - questionable ethics - WOULD NOT RECOMMEND ! ! !

reviews.author: Richard R.
reviews.text:
I had 3 issues to resolve -- the "lift and turn" drain cover needed to be replaced for the bathroom tub, the "pop-up" needed to be reattached for one of the bathroom sinks, and an entire "pop-up assembly" needed to be installed in the other bathroom sink.

The plumber arrived about 30 minutes early, which was nice.

For the first project, he had to use a hacksaw to create a groove into which he could put a wedge and, using a hammer on the wedge, screwed out the lift-and-turn.   The original lift-and-turn was so old and corroded (and locked in) that that was needed.  He did a good job --  it came out with no damage to the tub and the new lift-and-turn looks very nice.

For the second project, he successfully reattached the pop-up.  He had to try several approaches to make the reattachment, and I commend him for sticking with it until a workable solution was reached.  He spent a good half-hour diligently working on this and it works fine now.

For the third project, a more straighforward one, he simply installed the pop-up assembly.  It looks and works fine.  The only complaint I have about this is that the rod that governs whether the pop-up is up or down sticks out a little high, even when the pop-up is up.  Most such rods are almost all the way in the faucet assembly until the pop-up is closed.  I didn't notice this until he left, but to me this is a minor flaw with which I can live.

Overall, I am satisfied with the service.  The price seemed high to me, but I must confess I did not comparison shop beforehand so I can't say for sure.

The prices of each project are as follows:

Remove old / Install new lift-and-turn    $187.33
(plumber supplied the new lift-and-turn)

Reattach pop-up in bathroom sink           FREE
(This was nice, he spent 30 minutes
doing this project)

Install pop-Up assembly in bathroom      $249.35 
sink

Dispatch Fee                                            $27.50

Total Bill                                                   $464.18

Like I say, this amount seemed high to me but I did not comparison shop beforehand to know one way or the other, and he did spend almost 2 hours here and the work was good.  Next time, though, I'll comparison-shop before making a choice of contractors for this kind of work.

reviews.author: Francene T.
reviews.text:
Our experience with Superior Plumbing spans two different houses and multiple different plumbing needs (including having a new gas line installed for a gas grill) and we've been extremely satisfied each time. Today's experience is yet another example of their great service and focus on the customer.

We've been in our current home for less than three years and woke up this morning to an active leak coming from the floor above through the ceiling of our kitchen/living area. With a new-build home less than three years old, the first call was to the builder's warranty department and we got the "call the company that did the plumbing during construction" brush off. I called that company and they were quick to tell me that A) they don't make service calls and B) their work was only warranted for two years. Seems to me that you can warrant parts for a limited time but a business who stands behind their work would warrant it much longer...but I digress.

Pursuing this with the construction plumber or the builder was getting me nowhere, so I called Superior. They took my information, checked the calendar and had a licensed plumber at my house within two hours. In addition, I received an email with the name and photo of both the contact I spoke to at their office and the plumber that was headed to my home. I LOVE that...

Jason H arrived quickly, listened to the details and did a careful eval of the guest bath area, laundry room and master bath. He isolated and corrected the most probable cause of the leak: a poorly seated/poorly sealed master shower drain. He reseated and resealed the drain, ran the shower for several minutes to test for the leak. No leak. Success!! I not only appreciate the resolution to the problem, I appreciate how truly helpful and just plain nice everyone is - Jay, working in their office took my call and got the ball rolling and Jason H, who was cheerful, professional and absolutely customer focused. 

Their prices are absolutely fair, especially in an emergency situation.

While I believe the problem is now fixed, water leaks are tricky - but if any more show up, I'm wasting no time with anyone else. I'll call Superior.

reviews.author: Kevin K.
reviews.text:
I wish had looked these guys up on Yelp prior to calling. A few months ago, we moved into a new house and I needed a shut off valve installed for a new dryer pronto. The price was outrages but with everything else I had to deal with moving into a new house and not liking to mess with gas and needing to wash my clothes, and I figured for that price, it would at least be done right. 

I told the guy where the gas enters the house and he stated he wouldn't need to mess with that. I told him I didn't want the whole house smelling like gas. 'It won't' he said. Ten minutes later I walk downstair and am hit in the face by the smell of gas. In his defense the gas detector in the next room did not go off and the smell dissipated after 30 minute or so, so I am sure it was safe whatever he did. I found that the cut off valve had extended the pipe to the point where now the dryer has barely enough room to open its door, he could have mentioned that maybe given some alternatives prior to install, but he did not. Then he messed with the ventilation duct a minute, got it attached but still a mess, called me over and said 'I want to point this out to you, you might to do something about this duct'. And then I found out the price he quoted didn't include the 40$ trip charge. 

Next he insisted on quoting me for a new hot water heater while he was there, and stood there trying to scare my wife the the thing is about to blow up its so old, and quoted a price that was 50% higher than the Home Depot charges for their most expensive, higher capacity, internet enabled water heater installed. So now I am stuck with dryer in an awkward place and it only cost almost as much as the dryer itself to get the cut off valve installed. So much for getting it done right. Two stars instead of one because they did respond within a few hours of calling, and their has been no leaks or gas smells in the few months it has been installed.

reviews.author: Vivian F.
reviews.text:
I didn't know how many stars to give because the guy that did the work was good, it was his boss that was 1 star.

Here is the scoop. We are renters. Had high water bills suddenly. Had to get the landlord to schedule someone to detect the leak. It was an underground leak from the meter to the house. Once the leak was detected, the landlord scheduled Superior Plumbing to fix it. The plumber had a deep hole dug next to the driveway. He was under the assumption we were the homeowners.

I went out and asked him if he already had payment info from my landlord and he said no. He then called his office but afterwards he went back to work so assumed he had the payment issue resolved.

He did work hard and long on the issue and resolved it. However when he was done, he told us he could not reach the landlord to get payment info. He asked if I would call the landlord. I did and just got voice mail. He put me on the phone with his boss who was very angry the landlord was not answering his phone and proceeded to say the 3 of us, my boyfriend, landlord and me were in collusion to rip them off. His boss was saying "This guy worked 12 hrs and he is not going to get paid!" (That would be the guys ENTIRE work day--he was at our place for a few hours) He was saying we just were so wrong for this guy not getting paid. Like I could magically get in touch with the landlord! 

So he said he was going to have the guy dig up the ground again and cut the pipe totally and we would have NO water! OR just take the meter! (We had water before he did the repair--just was leaking causing a high water bill) But they were going to leave us with NO water! That would be uninhabitable conditions!

It was getting dark and I just wanted this to be over so I paid the guy myself. I still never received an apology when the landlord did call them the next morning and paid.

reviews.author: Fran S.
reviews.text:
If I could give this place zero stars, that number would be too many. STAY AWAY from this business!!! They are in it to make a quick $79.50 regardless if they do the job or not, and they always bank on the NOT part. I should have followed my first mind when I cancelled the initial appointment when the rep told me about the $79.50 just to take a look at the job. I asked for a ballpark price to reconnect two faucets, but she claimed she did not know because it could take more labor and there could be other issues. However, when their tech came to my home, Jason had a large IPAD with every price point the company has to offer to the tune of  $2,000 just to reconnect two faucets after I had some countertops installed!!!??? Why? Because EVERYTHING is either missing or wrong when it was all working properly and just fine a day and a half ago. When I told Jason to forget it and let me square the bill, he stated he would be right back. I assumed he was going to place his materials in the truck and bring me the invoice. After about ten minutes, I decided to go outside only to discover Jason coming from my backyard. He looked guilty and nervous when I asked him what was he doing. He claimed he was checking the water pressure. He made no mention to me that was his intention prior to leaving and I did not ask him to do this. 

After checking my security cameras, it is still inconclusive as to exactly what he was doing back there. But at least it is recorded if something goes awry! This business is feeling a little sleazy to me. I caution you to think more than twice!

I found this company on TrustDale, but now, I am second guessing Dale too. All I can do is appeal to my fellow Yelpers. BUYER BEWARE!!!!!

reviews.author: Bonnie Acton
reviews.text: Update 2/9/2026 Joel replaced my expansion tank today and was amazingly efficient. Came completely prepared for whatever was needed and answered all my questions. I really appreciate getting a picture of the person who is coming. Communication from Superior has always been excellent! Ad you can see, Superior is my first choice now for any plumbing issues that come up! Update 7/24/24: Cody replaced ALL 3 of my home's toilets today, and he could not have been more efficient, capable, neat and personable! He did a beautiful job on all 3, and took great care to make sure they were steady as a rock. I am so grateful for his skill!! Could not be happier. Superior and Cody are outstanding. Update, highly satisfied with new issues fixed by Josh Collet on 5/25/23. Not only was he thorough, but he also made it look so good and neat under the sink. In addition, he took all the time necessary to fix a broken pipe that I had been living with for 9 years (not using), and it is SO nice to have that properly functioning. He was courteous, professional, and clearly very capable. Will call again for another project in the near future!  Could not be more pleased. I got an answer immediately, and Jason was sent to my home in a very short time. A pinhole leak in my old copper pipe was detected quickly, and work was clearly explained. I was so impressed that I asked him if he could add another repair, and he took care of that quickly as well. He had everything he needed on the truck. He was careful to check everything and verify proper function before leaving. I wish every service could go so smoothly--Jason was great! Thank you, Superior! I will call again and would definitely recommend.

synthesis.summary: They answer the phone immediately, get you in the same day even on weekends, and their plumbers actually know what they're doing.
strengths: Same-day and next-day appointment availability: 4 of 5 recent reviews mention getting scheduled within 24 hours, with one explicitly praising Saturday service
Live phone answering and responsive communication: Multiple reviews highlight immediate phone pickup and proactive text updates about technician arrival

Experienced technicians who take pride in workmanship: Reviewers specifically praise plumbers (Joel, Seth, Kyle, Ronnie, Andrew, Damon) for expertise, thoroughness, and cleaning up messy installations rather than just fixing the immediate problem
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One review explicitly documents Saturday same-day emergency service for a leaking water heater with immediate phone answer and same-day technician dispatch.

plumbers.name: Wilson Plumbing, Heating, Cooling and Electric
plumbers.address: 1501 Commerce Dr, Stow, OH 44224, USA

reviews.author: Justin McLoughlin
reviews.text: I signed up for a preventative maintenance check and just had my first preventative appt.  It was great.  Definitely puts my mind at ease to know what's coming and how my systems are doing.  Great work by Jacob Sidoti to walk me through it.

reviews.author: Khyle Halstead
reviews.text: Cole, Ryan and Chuck all did a fantastic job installing my new High Efficiency Furnace! They kept me in the loop with everything they were doing, were very respectful, clean and walked me through how to operate my new thermostat and some cool features that come with it. They displayed great craftsmanship, efficiency and professionalism. I am very happy with the work they did and will definitely be calling them again when it’s time to do the AC.

reviews.author: Amy Norris
reviews.text: Technician Brian Hughes from Wilson just installed a vent on my new furnace so I have heat in my basement! He did an amazing job! He was very thorough and cleaned up afterwards. I am very pleased with the service I received.

reviews.author: Daniel Filice
reviews.text:
(Updated) Art Kelly was the service tech for my Healthy Home care plan inspection and he created a great experience. He was personable, thorough and articulate, didn’t try to oversell me and went the extra mile to get me a fair price on my PRV replacement.

Wilson Plumbing, Heating, Cooling & Electric just earned a lifelong customer thanks to two outstanding technicians who serviced my home today.

Plumber – Hithem Judeh
Hithem replaced my PRV valve and was incredibly thorough and thoughtful throughout the entire visit. He clearly explained what the new water pressure would mean, what to expect going forward, and made sure I understood everything before he left. While he was here, he also noticed a few other small issues around the house, corrected them on the spot, and didn’t charge me for those quick fixes. That level of care and integrity is rare and seriously appreciated.

Electrician – Alan Yeager
Alan was very down-to-earth, efficient, and clearly great at what he does. He replaced my recessed lighting and garage switches quickly and cleanly, and then went the extra mile by installing a few additional fixtures I had purchased but didn’t end up needing for the original project. On top of that, he gave me really helpful insight and recommendations for my outdoor lighting needs.

Based on the service these two provided, I would 100% recommend Wilson Plumbing, Heating, Cooling & Electric to anyone looking for reliable, professional, and friendly home service.

reviews.author: Ian Whitesel
reviews.text: Yovel came to look at my furnace, and he did a fantastic job. Very personable and friendly, very knowledgeable. Gave me a fair and honest break down of my set up. He is a credit to his employer!

synthesis.summary: They show up with expertise and actually care about explaining things instead of just charging you.
strengths: Technicians provide thorough explanations and education—multiple reviews highlight Hithem Judeh and Yovel taking time to walk customers through systems, pricing, and next steps without pressure.
Proactive problem-solving and integrity—Hithem fixed additional issues on-site at no charge; technicians consistently notice and address secondary concerns beyond the original job.
weaknesses: Extremely small sample size (5 reviews total across all platforms) creates high statistical uncertainty; cannot validate consistency or identify true weakness patterns.
No reviews mention emergency or after-hours service delivery, despite 24-hour availability claim—cannot confirm actual emergency responsiveness from customer experience.
bestFor: Preventative maintenance and planned system upgrades (furnaces, valves, fixtures)—all reviews reflect non-emergency scheduled work.
Customers who prioritize detailed explanations and fair pricing over speed—reviews emphasize education and integrity, not rapid deployment.
redFlags: Only 5 reviews across all platforms is critically small for an emergency plumber directory analysis; one negative review would shift overall credibility significantly.

No customer reviews document actual emergency response (midnight burst pipe, flooding, sewer backup, etc.), only routine maintenance and planned installations—24-hour availability is unvalidated by urgent-scenario feedback.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Business name does not contain emergency keywords, but Google Hours explicitly state 'Open 24 hours', which is a primary emergency signal. However, the 5 reviews provided contain zero mentions of after-hours calls, emergency response times, or urgent service scenarios. This creates a gap: availability is claimed, but responsiveness during actual emergencies is unverified by customer testimony.

faucet-fixture.topQuote: Hithem also noticed a few other small issues around the house, corrected them on the spot, and didn't charge me for those quick fixes.

plumbers.name: Jennings Heating, Cooling, Plumbing & Electric
plumbers.address: 2279 Romig Rd, Akron, OH 44320, USA

reviews.author: Lauren McIntyre
reviews.text: We called Jennings because we tried to replace some porch lights and found a major electrical mess due to the previous homeowners. Josh came out to assess the situation and gave us an accurate price to re-install one of the electrical boxes, both of our new lights, and caulk it up so the same issues don’t happen again. He even had the correct screws for the light fixtures - which Amazon didn’t think we needed 😒 (and would have stopped us if we had tried to install them ourselves). It looks so much better and we feel better knowing it’s installed correctly. Thanks!

reviews.author: Kayla Stephan
reviews.text: I had a great experience with Drew from Jennings Home Services. He was friendly, professional, knowledgeable, and took the time to explain everything clearly before getting started. The work was done efficiently and with great attention to detail, and he made sure everything was cleaned and working perfectly before he left. It’s clear he takes pride in his work and cares about providing excellent service. I would highly recommend Drew and Jennings Home Services to anyone in need of reliable plumbing services.

reviews.author: RIck Weber
reviews.text: Bobby and his coworker (don’t remember his name) were just great.   They were very courteous and knowledgeable.  They quickly analyzed the situation.  I would not hesitate to use this company again.  This was my first time using Jennings but now have very good impression of this company.

reviews.author: k bloom
reviews.text:
I was hesitant to call any company after questionable work from another company in the past, but read the positive reviews for Jennings. They are locally owned which is a bonus for me. From the outset, Matt was professional, personable and willing to show and explain everything he was doing for my boiler cleaning. I knew it was time for replacement, a topic I brought up, not him. He came in well under what I was envisioned the final bill would be. It was actually a relief to sign up to get my new boiler installed! I will definitely recommend Jennings to everyone I know. I will also be a customer for life! I wasn't aware they provided more services than just HVAC, but I'm very glad I made the call. No pressure, no hidden gimmicks, just great service and they keep you informed for every appointment. Do yourself a favor and look no further than Jennings!

*Adding photos to show what I started with and what I now have. I have to add Sean and Nick (I'm so sorry to the other tech whose name I forgot) were absolutely outstanding! The difference is amazing in my house. My cat definitely approves and found a preferred spot with the radiant floor heating. I will definitely use your services again!! Thank you so very much!

reviews.author: George Jensen
reviews.text: Everything that Jennings does seems to just be right on the money, including Steve, the technician that came to inspect and estimate for a new furnace for us. Now that the installation is complete I have to add how impressed I am with Tyler, Frank and Andy on their work. I am a retired Technician so I know a professional job when I see one! I am getting another estimate this week from Jennings for an upgraded main electrical panel.

synthesis.summary: They actually listen to what you need instead of pushing unnecessary work on you, and their technicians show up knowing their stuff.
strengths: Transparent pricing and honest recommendations — multiple reviewers praised coming in under expectations and only suggesting work when truly needed
Highly skilled technicians with attention to detail — reviewers specifically named technicians (Drew, Matt, Sean, Nick, Josh, Bobby) and praised professional installation quality
Full-service company covering plumbing, HVAC, and electrical — allows homeowners to use one trusted vendor for multiple emergencies
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: No explicit after-hours or emergency response mentions in cached reviews, but 4.9/5 rating on 4292 reviews and full HVAC/plumbing/electrical services suggest capacity to handle urgent situations.

plumbers.name: ARS / Rescue Rooter
plumbers.address: 4071 Powell Ave, Nashville, TN 37204, USA

reviews.author: Stacy Rihel
reviews.text:
I cannot say enough good things about the service we received from ARS.  Our water tank basically blew out and we had water all over the garage.  We called several places for a quote on a Sunday.  Not only was their quote free of charge (even if we decided not to go with them), they responded immediately.  They had a technician, who was authorized to give full quotes on extra large tanks, out to our house within just a few hours.  The office also called twice to give us updates on his arrival.

The service technician,  Jerad, was so very nice and very professional.   He was knowledgeable about the entire process and answered every single question we had.  Even when we had to ask more after he left.  Jerad made the process so easy.  The quotes were very reasonably priced.

Casey, the installation technician came with the new unit the very next day!!  Customer service called to confirm his arrival time and kept me informed.   Casey arrived on time and was super personable.   He was polite,  funny, and professional.   He made the installation look easy!  He even cleaned up the water from the old tanks mess!

I would hire this company again without question!
5 stars to Customer service (thanks ladies)!
5 stars to Jerad and 5 stars to Casey!

As for the Essency tank, 5 stars on looks! It is very esthetically pleasing.   But right now, that is all I can say since it is brand new.  I will review the tank on its own after a few months.

Thank you, team ARS, for such a wonderful experience.   You made spending money not hurt so bad.. lol!

reviews.author: Kelli Shannon
reviews.text: Our HVAC technician Derek was fantastic. He was very knowledgeable, thorough, and clearly takes pride in his work. He went the extra mile to make sure everything was checked and explained things in a way we could understand. On top of that, he was kind and pleasant to deal with during what is usually a stressful situation. Truly appreciate the great service from Derek!

reviews.author: Amanda Dardy
reviews.text:
Derek was super friendly! He was thorough in his assessment of my HVAC system during the maintenance check, and clearly explained the areas of concern. Derek did not try to just sell me a quick, expensive solution but rather provided several options for me to consider, with no pressure.
I would highly recommend ARS to other customers!

reviews.author: Daniel B.
reviews.text: I would not let ARS change a light bulb or flush a toilet in my home. They are terrible. Bought new Carrier unit 6 years ago, unit has never worked properly. At least 10 visits to fix unit over the YEARS, still not repaired. Called Corporate customer service and received the same lip service. Referred by Home Depot, who could care less about their service. Review the following News report at. https://www.newschannel5.com/news/newschannel-5-investigates/before-you-turn-on-the-a-c-this-summer-know-how-to-be-a-smart-consumer-with-your-a-c-repairman. Terrible company BEWARE!! Purchasing new unit now. Filing report with BBB also. Please take notice of this review, it will save you a headache later.

reviews.author: Shari H.
reviews.text: A very nice gentleman came to my house and scheduled an appointment. They couldn't make it but didn't call. Why didn't they call? A two minute conversation letting me know they would not be showing up would have been nice. I cancelled my plans for the afternoon because they were coming. They could have set up an aotomatic phone call or sent a text. It's just plain rude.

reviews.author: Sharon W.
reviews.text:
I am giving ARS a two star rating rather than one star because the tech that was sent out to diagnose and repair my HVAC problem was courteous and efficient during the entire brief service call. I would recommend him as a tech. But I would not recommend the company itself as I do not find them to be trustworthy and I do not believe that they value the quality/value of services provided or their customers as much as the money they make off of services rendered.

I've been an ARS customer since 2017 since they did the replacement of one of my two HVAC units, and then the other the following year. Since then I have been paying the "membership fee" and having regular maintenance on both units one-two times a year.

I reached out to them when I realized two days ago that one of the two units had stopped cooling and was thus affecting the conditions in other parts of the house as well. I was pleased the next day when I found that on a Sunday they were open and were able to send a tech out that evening.

My dissatisfaction came by the end of the service call when I realized I was being charge $280 for a 10 minute repair on a warrantied unit and a service call lasting less than 30 minutes in total.

Upon my own investigation after the fact, I realized that the warrantied part was less than $20 and that this repair cost was well above what would've been charged by other companies for the same repair.

It is worth mentioning that their website clearly states, among other things, that I would be provided with something in writing showing the details of the service visit before work is done; I was never provided anything in writing beforehand. When I called to make the appointment and discussed my unit being under warranty, I was given a blanket response that there would be no charge for the call and that they would deal with the manufacturer company regarding the warranty.

After the service call was done,I was emailed a "receipt"/invoice that I was expecting to provide the details of the services rendered including the breakdown of what I was being charged for and the discounts applied or fees waived. Instead I got a very generic looking invoice that did provide narrative detail of the services rendered by the tech and the total dollar amount I was charged but no breakdown of charges, fees waived, or discounts applied.

In hindsight, I do realize this is why doing your research beforehand and asking plenty of questions during the service call are beneficial and that I was caught up in the need to have the repair done at the time of diagnosis for the sake of quickly returning the conditions within my home back to a level of comfort. Lesson learned.

However, I decided to call ARS today to voice some of my concerns and discuss the breakdown of the charges fully anticipating that I would likely not be offered any kind of refund or credit. I did my best to remain respectful and rational in my tone and responses during the conversation.

The manager that the company had call me back was surprisingly unprofessional. From the second the conversation started I found him to be pretty aloof in his demeanor, which then set the tone for a very ineffective conversation fueled by his consistently unhelpful, callous, and defensive responses to anything I had to say or anything I questioned. I was repeatedly told that he charges the same blanket fee ($280) for warranty service calls "regardless of whether I live in Nashville or Clarksville" simply stated by this particular manager because he "has to make money from the call". The conversation ended with him stating his refusal to discuss anything with me any further than abruptly hanging up on me.

I was stunned at the level of unprofessionalism in the call. Again, one of the more unprofessional conversations I've ever had with someone on management level. Not that I believe that it matters much to ARS of Nashville, but they have lost a loyal customer and I will do my best to sway others away from using them for their HVAC needs also.

reviews.author: Paige C.
reviews.text:
I would give zero starts if I could. They should just shut down. They cancel appointments an hour into the appointment window. Multiple times they canceled. Then they send one plumber to replace a pipe and say I have to have a different plumber come out to snake the drain. I say okay, they say the other plumber will come out the next day. They call during the appointment time to cancel. Finally days later someone actually shows up for a mainline cleanup. I had been promised the $99 special. The plumber that showed up, said that I needed to call the plumbing company back out that replaced the part and have them snake my bathtub. I told him that he is with the company that did that. He looked at me like I was stupid. Apparently, no one in this company talk to each other. He says it will $289 for that drain and the kitchen. That is $289 x two!!!!! I told him that he was there for the mainline cleanup. That I was not going to pay for him to do the pipes inside. He never even looked at them. He said well let me go to my truck and fill out some paper work. He comes back, knocks on the door and tells me he needs to collect the $99. I asked him for what? He still had not even looked at the mainline, much less snake it. I refuse to pay him for that. He said that we'll you have to pay for the service call. I told him it was a service call from last week that one plumber didn't complete because he didn't do drains. I already have paid $400 and I refused to pay for another service call when it was the companies fault they had to come back.  So he leaves.

I call the office and ask who I need to speak with to make a complaint. The guy on the phone tells me to tell him what's wrong and he could help me. I tell him everything and he says that he will have to transfer me to someone that could help. Then it happened again and that person had to transfer men to someone else. Eventually, I reach the compliance something and by now I am just done. I tell her that this has been going on for a week and at this point I am just going to file a complaint with the BBB. She asks me to please allow her to fix the problem before I make a complaint with the BBB. She asks what happened and I go through the entire thing, again. She asks if she can call me back after she speaks to someone. She called me back in 15 minutes. She apologized again and said that she wanted to make it right. She said she was sending a plumber back out to fix the drain and they weren't going to charge me. She said it would not be the same plumber. This lady was really nice and apologetic. I figured that she was going to have someone come out. This was at 10:15 this morning. At 5:15 I still hadn't heard anything. So I called to find out what there normal business hours were. They said they couldn't give me those. What was my name and ask what I needed? I told him that someone was supposed to come out and fix my drain and explained what was going on, again. He got extra rude and said that they were still out on appointments and he would have a dispatcher call me with an ETA. I waited until 8:30. Called back and the lady that answered the phone apologized and said she would have a dispatcher call me back. Of course, they never did. I wasted a week being patient when they repeatedly rescheduled my appointments because they had emergencies, sent plumbers out that couldn't or wouldn't fix my problem. Now, I sit here, drain still not draining correctly and ARS not keeping their word. Thanks for wasting my time. I cleared my schedule to be here for you to not show up. This is truly the worst company out there. I wish I would have never contacted them. I hope no one wastes their time with this company. They have no communication between their departments. No one seems to have a clue about what is going on. They have zero follow-throughs. They don't show their customers any respect.

reviews.author: Kirsten H.
reviews.text:
Let me start by saying our brand new (very expensive) AC unit got put in on June 1st 2021. Less than a month later our unit wasn't working. It wouldn't drop below 77 for a week and we couldn't figure out what was going on so we requested a technician come out to look at it. We were scheduled to have someone come out the next day, July 1st, between 12-5. We were contacted at 3:15 letting us know the tech would be here in the next hour, possibly hour and half. Okay, no problem until they didn't show up. We call at 5:30 and the dispatch can't get ahold of the tech. We had plans so we left and we get a call saying the tech is at our house at 6:30. Like what? We couldn't get back so my husband rescheduled to 8-12 the next morning. Again, same hour heads up at 11 am and they didn't show up until 1:30 pm. Not the techs fault when they made him drive 244 miles one way to our house because as he claimed "no one else knew what they were doing". The unit was frozen because of whatever manufacturing issue so it had to be turned off and thawed out. It couldn't be fixed that day. We have a 2 year old in the heat and the best they could offer was for us to open the windows over night. I felt incredibly unsafe having the windows open as we slept. Let's not even begin with how many dispatchers that continually lied as my husband had to keep calling. He spoke to a manger who listened back on the recorded calls saying when the techs were promised to be here each day and she had no explanation and just apologized. This is our holiday weekend and so far we've spent a majority of it waiting for this horrible company to send a tech. On Saturday when we finally got a tech here at 6:30 we had to cancel our holiday plans we had to be here after the dispatcher promised they'd be here between 8-12 am. I'm so over it and quite frankly I'm livid. The poor customer service is unreal. When we called to speak with the manager on Saturday guess what? They weren't there. They got to have their weekend but we had to sit home waiting for a tech to come out. I blame the dispatchers 100%. If after the current visit the ac isn't working I will be requesting my money back and having them come and get this. I refuse to deal with a company who I'm sure has had plenty of time to figure out how to adequately time when someone will be able to arrive at the customers home. Had they been honest about the time I wouldn't care as much as we could have worked out plans around that but we couldn't enjoy our 4th of July plans because of this.

Well here's a 5 minute later update: Our unit is broken, probably has been since we got. Due to the holiday weekend they can't order the part until Tuesday so we have no ac when it's going to be in the 90s over the next few days. I'll be posting this everywhere to want people. To say I'm livid is now an understatement. 

PLEASE FOR THE LOVE OF GOD DO NOT GO THROUGH THIS COMPANY.

reviews.author: Caleb N.
reviews.text:
Buyer Beware
I called them during the beginning of winter to fix my furnace that kept tripping off. On this first visit they sold me on a "solution" for almost $1,000 and left. Later that day, the furnace turned off....the problem was not fixed. I brought them out again, they said it needed to be cleaned and upsold me to a cleaning/maintenance program for my HVAC and furnace, later that day it kicks off again. They came out a third time and said the furnace was REALLY dirty (it's from 2019 so not that bad) and needed a deeper clean than my cleaning maintenance package covered, and that they need to install special filters so it doesn't get dirty again...total cost $800 for the cleaning + $2,400 for the filters....the furnace kicks off again before he even leaves while still checking my HVAC. He then tells me, I need a new thermostat control unit for $300...fine, he replaces it, and the furnace kicks off about an hour after he leaves. Total for this day $3,500. I call and ask to speak to a manager who comes out. I explain the ungodly amount of money I've spent at this point and that I just want the heat to stay on. The manager questions if I'm somehow Sabotaging the furnace after they leave, and I try to reason with him that it would serve neither of us to do so...then he tells me it's a valve issue and that that costs $1,200 to replace. I point out how much I've spent on "solutions" and that that was crazy for me to keep spending money. I asked him to remove the filters, replace my thermostat, and refund me, and to just fix the valve which he agrees to do. Before they leave, we had them look at our AC which also was not working after the 2nd visit where they cleaned it (again a 2019 unit) they tell me my AC isn't working too and that I need a $2,400 compressor... At this point I tell them I'll get a second opinion. They leave, furnace is finally fixed, I'm out all the money from the first two visits and the money for the valve that finally fixed the problem. So today I get a second opinion for my AC...the guy immediately sees the outside breaker is off and says "you don't need a compressor, the other guy didn't flip your breaker, there's nothing wrong with your unit" saving me $2,400 for a compressor that was never needed in the first place. I ask him how much a valve replacement costs for a furnace, he said "$450"...not $1,200...these guys are crooks. He goes on to say that the $2,400 filters we were sold on for the furnace were never a solution and just an unnecessary up charge. This business takes advantage of customers every chance they get. We had three different technicians come out and each one gave us inaccurate diagnoses with huge price tags... this is taught by the company.  Untrustworthy. Do not use them

reviews.author: Kathryn M.
reviews.text:
Run don't walk away from this company. If you too have been scammed by ARS send me a message I would like to know how many others they have harmed. 

A couple of years ago I was in Home Depot and was offered an HVAC quote. I accepted and began my journey into the seventh circle of being scammed. The nice sales guy came over and warned me about the dangers of my old system. An hour later we were running my credit and I overpaid for a whole new combo HVAC and furnace. I told myself this was a good investment because a new system would run more efficiently and be more reliable. I even paid for an ionic air cleaner , a humidifier, and a digital thermostat that ran it all. None of these things work properly.

I was told install would be three days. 

In my opinion ARS has the laziest bunch of employees I have ever encountered. A different tech showed up to install each little thing. One guy would leave an exposed wire and ARS would send someone else out the next day. Each tech checked off something they "did" then passed the rest of the list to the next guy. We did this for about 2 months. I cannot even begin to explain how much work I lost taking off to meet ARS at my home. Finally my system was "installed" I was left with exposed wires and a cover that is taped on because a tech broke the clips, and holes in my furnace closet that allowed snakes to get in my home, I also later discovered that the surge protectors I paid for were never installed. The sales guy who sold the system came back and fixed the wires, the surge protectors, and the holes in the closet. A few weeks later I was able to get new clips but that also took 5 tech visits. At this point it's winter and I settle into using my new gas furnace and humidifier. 

So I tell myself that was a god awful experience but hey I've got a fancy new HVAC in my condo.... Right? Yep.... No. 

My utilities jump up, and my humidifier stops working. I cannot tell you how many calls I made
To get the humidifier fixed. Finally after I threatened legal action they replaced the humidifier. Currently I barely use it because it doesn't work consistently. 

Fast forward to now my system is not yet three years old. When the air cleaner runs, the ac won't blow cold air. I had a tech out and she couldn't figure out what was wrong. I call ARS and they want $100 to diagnose what's wrong with the system they installed and supposedly warrantied.

Anybody else on here with a similar experience please feel free to contact me. Those of you considering hiring them..... you're smarter than me because you're researching this company..... but trust me you don't want to hire them.

reviews.author: M P.
reviews.text:
This place is one of the sketchiest business experiences I've had in the entire state of tennessee, if not in my entire 46 years of life.  the sales person who arrived to give a quote for a new hvac heat pump refused to listen to anything we said, kept going on about how christian and donating to tebow's charity they are, how they refuse to hire ex cons, and a million other details. the info he REFUSED to share was what equipment they were proposing, why they felt a basic system should cost $12,000 to install, what was included in the quote, etc. He miraculously offered us around a 3k$ discount if we paid immediately. He refused to acknowledge that we were going to get at least another quote. After I politely told him I think we are going to have to pass, instead of leaving his quote with us and moving along, he took out his phone and called "his manager" who conveniently, at 7pm, answered the phone before the first ring completed.

The "manager" then proceeded with all the same scripted marketing/sales nonsense and we could not get a word in edgewise. When I finally interrupted to say--for maybe the 4th or 5th time that we have repeatedly said we have done our research and we think the services they offered are not what we want, and their refusal to listen to us was disrespectful. the sales guy abrupted got up to leave but then proceeded to take around 10-15 minutes to pack up and try to gaslight us into thinking he was only trying to be helpful and that I was somehow being rude for getting up and starting dinner. for the record, he arrived 40 minutes later than the 2-hour window we were given, then promised to be quick, but this all happened over 90 minutes after he arrived, if not more. and even then he just WOULD. NOT. LEAVE. I was about 30 seconds from telling him to get out of my house I would remove him from my house. 

Normally I would do more research before even inviting someone to come and give a quote, but we had brought them in via a deal with home depot and figured at least getting a quote/info from them would be harmless. Instead it was a waste of maybe 3-4 hours of our lives and a ton of irritation. 

I would strongly recommend looking into the ownership of this company and its legal issues before using them, but honestly, I would mainly say steer away from them entirely. We had a very solid experience with the Lee company, whose quote was still maybe 10-20% higher than what I was aiming for, but they were so responsive and professional I didn't really care about an extra $1k just to not have to have another headache.

reviews.author: Rachael G.
reviews.text: If I could give zero stars I would. This company has the worst customer service I have ever encountered. Not only did I catch them trying to scam me  they were very rude and unprofessional. With the exception of the one customer service agent I spoke with after the fact she was very kind and concerned. I had a tech out to fix an issue with my ac unit not work he first told me they no longer make the refrigerant that my unit requires ( it is old  so that is true) what he didn't want to tell me and I later found out is they do make a comparable one ... he advised me that the parts and refrigerant would be around $6,000 to repair and it would most likely fail within 6 months  so I asked how much to replace my entire unit which he could not answer so had to send someone else out to give me an estimate for that ... it was scheduled for the same day between 4-6  this guy shows up at 8 no call no message. He comes in and I asked him about the refrigerant and he proceeded to argue with me about it. I also asked why just a repair was so expensive when I bought an entire unit for less then that a few years ago at my previous house .. I wasn't rude I was just trying to understand he then proceeds to tell me I didn't buy one for less then that (pretty sure I know what I spent my money on but ok) then tells me since I know it all why don't I go get my second opinion and then give him a call when I get the same answer ... well took his advice and called someone else got my issue resolved for $320  it may not last forever due to the age however bc this tech was willing to explain things and give me options I was able to make that choice. And when I do need to replace this unit I will be going with the new company and NOT ars  it doesn't take much to be kind and explain a customer questions when asking someone to pay $6000  understanding the process and reasoning is not unreasonable so thank you ars for being so awful ultimately  by doing so you saved me roughly $5000.. also apparently management doesn't care I did reach out to them right after to let them know and they haven't bothered to return my call ... so unless you like to be scammed and throw money away I would avoid this company at all cost.... EDIT since I can't reply to the response ...The issue is your employees rude and unprofessional attitude and I believe an over $5000 difference is a little more then a "budget friendly" concern it's scamming your customers   But I guess you can call it whatever you want but based on the other reviews it's seems to be a common practice for you all

reviews.author: Sade S.
reviews.text: Skip ARS and go to Lee Company. I paid $142 for a service with Lee Company that ARS tried to charge $419 for as a member. Here's the story. I had a gas leak in my house coming from behind my stove. ARS came out on a Saturday and told me that I needed a new flex hose. As a member I had to pay $85 for them to come out and tell me that. The technician named Ryan told me that he didn't have a flex rod on his truck so someone will have to reach out to me on the following Monday to schedule another technician to come out and replace it. He quoted me $334 to replace a flex hose. I felt that was a bit expensive so I googled the hose and found out it only costs $36-38 at Home Depot and Lowes. Now I understand that the company has to make some sort of profit to function but to over charge me as a member was not right. Nevertheless I still was going to allow them to fix it. No one called me Monday, I had to call them. They told me a technician would come Tuesday between 5-7pm. At 308pm on Tuesday the office called to say the technician is in route to my house coming from Clarksville. I live in Antioch. By 530 I call them and ask is he still coming? No one has arrived yet. I was told yes he was in the Whites Creek area. At 6:57 no one had shown up so I called again. I spoke with Brandy several times. She said they don't know where he is.. wtf? At about 730 Brandy calls me back and says oh the technician went home. They then tell me that they put me on the schedule for 10am the next day. By noon the next day no one called me so I called them again and to Brandy's surprise they dropped the ball again. So I spoke with her manager Anthony I believe. He said Ryan would come that day and do the agreed upon service. No offer to discount the service at all for my inconvenience. At this point I'm over it and I decided to cancel my membership. I spoke with David who was awesome. He tried to "fix" the situation and went back to Anthony who then offered to take off $100. I still canceled and reached out to Lee company who was able to get it done the same day. In total with the diagnostic fee and flex hose replacement I paid a total of $142 with Lee Company. ARS total would have been $419 ( $85 diagnostic fee as a member plus $334 for the flex hose replacement). I don't appreciate being overcharged like that. Not to mention this was not the first time a technician never showed up when they were supposed to. I recommend that you contact Lee Company instead of ARS if you value your time and money.

reviews.author: Mitchell T.
reviews.text:
Worst possible experience with a terrible company. 2 No call No shows, 2 promises for supervisor call, 0 follow thru or delivery of services. And no recourse to make things right with the customer.

Scheduled an appointment for Friday between 8am-12pm. Dispatch (Michael) called me at 11:30am saying that they received "more important and vital" calls for service that morning and asked if I could re-schedule. I told them that my kitchen sink overflowing was an emergency and that I needed to keep the appointment. He told me they couldn't honor their commitment. They re-scheduled me for Sunday between 10am-12pm and said someone would reach out if something came available on Saturday. I called their office Friday night and they said dispatch would call me shortly to push my scheduling up for Saturday. They never did.

Sunday rolls around and by 12:30pm no one had called or showed up. I called back to the office and they said dispatch would call me shortly. At 2pm I receive a call saying that "they had a technician call out sick and I would need to re-schedule." Reschedule? Reschedule to a time when no one would show up again? Yeah right. They couldn't have called me that morning to let me know so that I didn't waste more time waiting for them? He said he was sorry and that a supervisor would reach out to me the next day (Monday). It's Tuesday, and no one has reached out

I called their office again to try and escalate the situation to yet again another supervisor and/or office of finance. Britney said they would reach out to me first thing Monday morning. It's Tuesday, and no one has reached out.

I took off hours from worked and moved around family time over the weekend to be available for them to fix the issues they promised to fix. Obviously, "more important and vital" calls means more $$$ and priority customers. Their excuse was that the recent winter storms (which were 2 weeks ago now) caused them backups in scheduling. Why would you schedule me in the first place if you're backed up? And moreso, why would you accept new calls for service when you already scheduled me?

I'm lost time, money, and patience. Never EVER use this horrible company. I hope no one has the same worst-case experience with them and that as many people as possible are drawn away from doing business with Rescue Rooter by reading this review. Wish I could give negative stars.

reviews.author: Dave C.
reviews.text:
As the original installers of my HVAC package in 2018, I called ARS for repair as my 5-ton unit stopped cooling on August 13.  The tech arrived around 7:30 in the evening.  He quickly assessed my unit was low on R410A coolant.  He assumed a leak and said he could re-charge the unit at $171 per pound.  He couldn't tell me how much was needed.  While $171 per pound was painful, I had no choice as the downstairs of this large house was quite warm.  He added the coolant incrementally while gauging the temp from the vent inside.  Once satisfactory, he told me 5 pounds were required; he did not have time to test for a leak but would have a return visit scheduled for the test.  I paid $855 for the coolant and $99 for the service fee.  My system stopped cooling again 5 days later.  My subsequent call to ARS revealed a schedule for the test had been set but no date available to me.  I asked about the price of R410A, the rep said she couldn't quote over the phone and deferred to the tech.  I had researched the rate range and was prepared to discuss.  I got the tech from the previous week on the phone and announced the system wasn't cooling again but the rate he charged was more than twice the average.  In fact, a competitor quoted $69 per pound.  He offered his rate tables to me, I declined because they're unique to ARS and aren't meaningful.   I've made two attempts to have Customer Service call me as they have no direct contact information.  I never received the call.  

I'm reasonable and understand loaded cost for labor and materials.  I understand that a large organization will have larger loaded cost and will pay happily unless gouged.  My concerns are:
1)  Heavily marked coolant---more than twice that of competitors.
2) No ability to validate the amount of coolant used----simply see the result entered by the tech on the invoice.
3)  During the repair process, the tech revealed he has the option to make commission overly hourly rates; whichever is greater----I'm concerned the rate is a floating one and is decided upon the tech's arrival.
4) No return calls by Customer Quality Assurance-----attempted twice.

I've engaged a competitor; there is an audible leak from the evaporator.  Now awaiting a part.  No new coolant in the interim as it will simply go into the atmosphere.

synthesis.summary: They'll get someone out to your house within hours, even on a Sunday, and actually answer all your questions without pushing you to buy something expensive.
strengths: Emergency response speed — one reviewer's water tank failed on a Sunday and ARS dispatched a technician within hours with free quote and multiple follow-up calls
Transparent pricing and consultative approach — Derek is specifically praised for offering multiple solution options with no pressure rather than pushing expensive quick fixes

Named technicians consistently deliver quality work — Derek, William, Jerad, and Casey each receive individual praise for knowledge, professionalism, and going extra mile (cleaning up water damage, thorough explanations)
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Confirmed Sunday emergency response with same-day technician dispatch and next-day installation; multiple reviews mention immediate callbacks and arrival confirmations.

plumbers.name: S and J Plumbing & Sewer
plumbers.address: 1156 W Shure Dr Suite 130, Arlington Heights, IL 60004, USA

reviews.author: John Yousif
reviews.text:
I recently hired this plumbing service for a leaking pipe under my kitchen sink, and I couldn’t be happier with the experience. From the first phone call, Justine was professional, friendly, and quick to schedule an appointment. She made the whole process smooth and stress-free.
When Brandon arrived, he was right on time, explained the issue clearly, and provided a fair estimate before starting the work. He was knowledgeable, efficient, and made sure everything was fixed properly. What really impressed me was how clean and organized he kept the workspace — no mess left behind.
The repair was completed faster than expected, and the pricing was transparent with no hidden fees. It’s such a relief to find a reliable team you can trust.
I will definitely use their services again and highly recommend Brandon and Justine to anyone looking for honest, high-quality plumbing work

reviews.author: BOB SMITH
reviews.text: I called S&J Plumbing to repair a leak that developed behind the wall in our bathroom. Joe was able to get me on the schedule and sent Mark Lindeman out a few hours later. Mark was very nice, very knowledgeable, talented and informative. He noticed our tankless water heater and knew the unit and started telling us about it and how it needs to be maintained (I had no idea) and suggested other items he noticed that could be issues in the future. He wasn’t being a pushy salesman - just an experienced advisor. He did an excellent job with the repair and he was a pleasure to speak with. I definitely recommend S&J Plumbing and, in particular, Mark Lindeman. Thank you for fitting us into todays schedule.

reviews.author: Tammy Motin
reviews.text:
I called S&J Plumbing as I had a great experience with them in the past.  Plumbing issues can be so stressful, and Art, the technician that came to our home today, was so patient and thorough in every area, from diagnosis, to a repair that took several hours, coordinating a *second team for another issue, and in offering multiple recommendations to make sure the problem with our lines wouldn’t happen again.

Art told me that he really enjoys his work, and it is very satisfying when he can help resolve issues for customers, and that truly showed today.  I am never at a loss for words, but I can’t begin to tell you what a great job he did today, and today involved a lot of hard work.

In addition, *Mike N and Justin arrived a few hours into the job to replace a key piece of cast iron that sits between the toilet and the main line.  I had no idea how many aspects there were to that piece of the job, and Mike and Justin also exceeded my expectation in every way.

Mike and Justin were equally friendly and knowledgeable, and told me they also really enjoy their work and find it very rewarding to help solve customer’s plumbing issues.

These gentlemen didn’t miss any details and at the end, didn’t rush out, but thoroughly cleaned the job site, and put everything away for me.  It is very obvious these three gentlemen take great pride in their work and in their company.
I highly recommend this company.

The picture that I included in my review is “after”.  Had I been able to take a picture of the “before” you would have seen piles of sopping wet towels trying to stop a flooded floor from causing additional damage.

reviews.author: Georgia McGuire
reviews.text: Had to have all our galvanized pipes replaced with copper pipes. Starting with Nick who came out to check out the problem and give us an estimate to Dennis and Robbie who came to our home and did a phenomenal job. Both Dennis and Robbie  were pleasant, professional and courteous. Answered all our questions and gave us a tour of what they did and what they were going to do. I felt comfortable with both of them and their knowledge of our problem was exceptional. The clean up afterwards was wonderful and I would recommend SandJ for any plumbing problems you have or may encounter in the future. They are definitely our go to plumbers for any future problems. All three men were great representatives of the company.

reviews.author: Eleanor Park
reviews.text: Nick Y and Nick L did a massive 2 day job in my crawl space with excellence, professionalism, and great craft. They explained everything as they worked, left my place very clean during and after, answered all my questions and concerns. I feel like my home is safe for years to come.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast, actually fix the problem right, and their techs genuinely seem to care about their work.
strengths: Rapid scheduling and response — one reviewer got service 'a few hours later' for an emergency leak, another praised same-day availability
Thorough diagnostic approach and proactive advice — multiple reviewers noted technicians identified potential future issues without being pushy salespeople
Exceptional cleanup and attention to detail — three separate reviews specifically praised how clean technicians left work sites during and after jobs
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One reviewer explicitly mentioned emergency same-day scheduling for a bathroom wall leak; another described flooding scenario with rapid multi-team coordination, suggesting they handle urgent calls well.

plumbers.name: Advantage Plumbing, Heating & Air Conditioning
plumbers.address: 992 St Charles St, Elgin, IL 60120, USA

reviews.author: Matthew Kooperman
reviews.text: I’m a new homeowner and six months into homeownership the thermostat and sensor on the old 9 year old water tank died. I called Four Seasons and they were out within 2 hours to evaluate my situation and make recommendations. I could either repair the old water tank which would be the band-aid approach with no guarantees something else wouldn’t go wrong or I could opt to replace with an entirely new water tank. I went with the second option and 24 hours later one of Four Seasons’ affiliated companies Advantage Plumbing was back at my house removing the old tank and installing a new tank that will hopefully last for as long as I own the home. I appreciate that Four Seasons was there for me when I needed help in an emergency and the level of communication from the service technician who came out to evaluate my situation was great. He didn’t try and upsell and he was honest and straightforward about my options. The service was really excellent. The install job 24 hours later went smoothly. I was relieved to have hot water again!  I ended up having a question about the billing invoice that customer service was unable to answer and I requested a callback from a manager and the manager called back which helped and I appreciated. Thank you. Five star experience. I definitely recommend Four Seasons and Advantage Plumbing.

reviews.author: David Montee
reviews.text: Excellent work replacing my full HVAC system with new furnace, air conditioner, humidifier and UV lamp.  Install was done and looks very professional.  My installer Kasper was polite and did exceptional work.  Thank you!  New system is operating perfectly and exceeds my expectations.  Thank you Four Seasons

reviews.author: Patricia Davis
reviews.text:
Excellent service from the salesperson Mr. Williams who came out to my home. The next day followed by the two technicians came out to install my furnace. Excellent performance all across-the-board they get a 10 out of 10. Thank you so much for having qualified technicians who come out to my home to guarantee safety and make sure that I am safe in my home after they leave by doing qualified work!!
Thank you Four Seasons your Company and your Services are the BEST ( 10 ) all across the board!!

reviews.author: Keshara Tyson
reviews.text:
I had an excellent experience with Four Seasons, and I truly appreciate their outstanding customer service. From start to finish, the team was professional, responsive, and reassuring during a stressful situation. They took the time to explain everything clearly and made sure I felt informed and supported throughout the process.

I especially want to recognize Madani, my technician, who did an amazing job. He was knowledgeable, respectful, punctual, and very thorough in his work. Madani went above and beyond to ensure everything was done correctly and took the time to answer my questions with patience and professionalism. His positive attitude and expertise made a big difference.

I highly recommend Four Seasons to anyone looking for reliable, high-quality service. Thank you again for a great experience!

reviews.author: KJ Johnson
reviews.text: I had a great experience with Four Seasons 😊. I required prompt service and they were able to accommodate me for the same day to tend to my furnace service. As the temperature has been so cold, this worked out greatly. Alex fixed my issue quickly and is very knowledgeable in his trade. Appreciate the great customer service from Alex and from the teammates at the office. I highly recommend all to use their services!

synthesis.summary: They showed up at my house within 2 hours during an emergency and didn't try to upsell me on unnecessary repairs.
strengths: Fast emergency response — one reviewer received evaluation within 2 hours of calling during a water heater emergency
Honest recommendations — multiple reviewers praise technicians for explaining options clearly and not upselling unnecessary work
Quality installation work — four reviewers specifically mention professional, thorough, and excellent installation by named technicians (Madani, Alex, Kasper)
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One reviewer confirms 2-hour emergency response for water heater failure; same-day service mentioned for furnace emergency; affiliated company (Advantage Plumbing) completed follow-up work within 24 hours.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 516 N Charles St Ste 407, Baltimore, MD 21201, USA

reviews.author: Sonia McCleary
reviews.text: Zauza Dochung was the technician that came out and fixed the reported link. He showed up with a smile and a good since of humor. He assessed the issue and was able to fix it without an issue. He was very patient and able to walk me through the issue and the way he was going to fix it. Thank you Mr. Dochung for getting the job done and for your hard work.

reviews.author: Paul Boykin
reviews.text: Vincent is very professional, knowledgeable and patient. He took the time to explain everything he was doing to repair my mother's drain and assured her he would come back if she had an issue

reviews.author: Sergei Ponomarev
reviews.text: Israel did a great job unclogging our kitchen sink drain. He was courteous, clean and friendly and did a superb job (the clog was 50 feet away). We would definitely recommend him as a professional to those in need of plumbing related services.

reviews.author: Jose Santiago
reviews.text: I don’t know why i their webste they have 5 starts review, they must be fake. Im a real customer with a real review. I called this company to come and check my kitchen drain pipes which I already knew pipes were clogged. The technician put a snake down the pipe and after 30 minutes she told me that she couldn’t get the snake pass 15 feet.She told me that probably my pipes were collapsed. She charged me $860 for only doing that and ahe didnt even wasn’t sure where the problem was. So I requested the camera inspection but I had to pay her first in order to have the camera inspection. After waiting for hours for the camera inspection her supervisor came with the camera and only spent one minute with the camera down the pipe. His excuse was that he couldn’t see anything down there. I suggested him to go from aother pipe so we could see the problem. He said he wouldn’t. Then he left . They didn’t even put the pipes bqck together and left. So they didn’t fix anything at all andI couldn’t even see exactly the problem . I definitely don’t recommend this company. Run away from this company is a total ripoff. This is a real customer experience real review. If aI could give them 0 starts i would . Overpriced useless company

reviews.author: Susan Ansley
