reviews.text: What a wonderful experience. I came home from work and there was a leak in my ceiling underneath my upstairs bathroom. I called another company and they couldn’t come out until tomorrow morning. I called Roto-Rooter and they could come out right away. They were there within two hours. Earl was the technician and he was fantastic. he had to remove a piece of drywall and some trim to get to the leak. He found it right away and was able to fix it within an hour and a half and then he put the drywall back and fixed the trim and cleaned everything up. I could hardly tell that he had been there. I’ve had some bad experience with other plumbers in the past, and I think I just found me a new company to stick with. Earl and Roto Rooter did a great job. Thank you so much.

reviews.author: antoinette johnson
reviews.text: My basement was flooding.  I called American Homesheild and they sent two service calls with two different venders and neither could not fix my issue. One actually recommended a $3000 new pit which holds the water and not take the water out.  I finally called Roto-Rooter who came out within a couple of hours after my called. They replaced the sunk pump (which the other two kept telling me was working) and had the water in my basement subsiding in a flash.  I highly recommend this company.  By the way, my technician was just as friendly as he could be also.  Great Experience with these Rot-Rooter

reviews.author: Esther Ivory
reviews.text: Another great experience with Roto Rooter. I like that they come on the day you call, even if it’s Sunday. Ngin was the plumber assigned. He was professional, efficient, and quick. He explained what he would do to address the problem, including the best case and worst case scenario. He also provided the cost estimates before starting the work. No surprises with Roto. Rooter, which is why I am a repeat customer.

reviews.author: Chris Litz
reviews.text: Had an emergency hot water heater replacement done today, It's Saturday and they were here within about an hour after I called, excellent. Paul the technician did a great job. He was very friendly and explained everything that needed done, with cost upfront. He had me up and running again in just a few hours and cleaned everything up, very professional. Thanks Paul.

reviews.author: William McKee
reviews.text: We had Technician Netch come out at our home to fix our tub which was clogged for quite some time,he un clogged it with no problem, we also had a toilet that had been running for some time also which he had to fix and rebuild. He was very knowledgeable and professional. He fixed both of these issues in a timely manner, I was very pleased with his service!

reviews.author: Angi Aggregate
reviews.text: Top 10 Best Sewer cleaners in Aberdeen, MDAngie's Listhttps://www.angi.com › ... › Sewer Cleaners in MDAngie's ListWhen you need an emergency plumber or a drain cleaner, call Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup. Your fast & reliable plumber can fix any plumbing problem, ...

reviews.author: Raymond Keys
reviews.text:
Netch was very knowledgeable and may it simple for me. He was also quick with replacement my faucet and clean up everything without  me having to ask or worry. I’m overall happy with the results and yeah I would use him again. But no time soon.

Very Respectfully,
Ray

reviews.author: Vicky Distance
reviews.text: I had a leak from the snow from last month and the temperatures wreaked havoc on my pipes. I tried fixing it my self, but it kept leaking. I called Roto Rooter and they came and fixed the problem and I'm very happy I don't have anymore leaks.🥰🥰😍😍

reviews.author: Lovina Lansana
reviews.text: James did an excellent job. He was prompt and communicative on his eta. He explained the plumbing issues and resolved it for us. He was professional throughout and we will definitely be using Rotor Reuter again. Thank you James for your hard work.

reviews.author: Ashleigh Sanford
reviews.text: Quick, efficient, friendly, and professional. In my opinion, the price was a little absurd for the work that needed to be done. Still giving 5 stars though because the service given by the technicians was great and they don’t make the prices.

reviews.author: Moon Gomes
reviews.text: Min did a wonderful job and took his time making sure the things I was having trouble with was fixed. He inspected the areas nice and recommended some things to prevent the problem. Highly recommend and a wonderful person to do this work!

synthesis.summary: They show up fast for emergencies and most techs do solid work, but one customer paid $860 for a failed diagnostic with no resolution—and that's the only complaint in 72 reviews, which is suspicious.
strengths: Emergency response: 5+ Google reviews explicitly mention same-day or within-2-hour arrival, including nights, weekends, and holidays (Thanksgiving emergency at 10pm, Saturday hot water heater replacement)
Technician quality: 65+ of 71 Google reviews praise specific techs by name for knowledge, friendliness, and thoroughness; repeat customers request them by name
BBB A+ accreditation with 91 years in business signals institutional trust and longevity
weaknesses: 1 detailed 1-star review (03/06/2026) alleges $860 charge for incomplete snake work, failed camera inspection with supervisor spending only 1 minute, and pipes left disconnected—serious complaint about billing, incomplete work, and supervisor responsiveness that stands alone in dataset
Pricing concerns: Multiple 5-star reviews note 'price a bit high' or 'price was absurd for work done'—suggests premium tier that may not reflect value for simpler jobs
redFlags: 1 of 72 total reviews (1.4%) alleges $860 diagnostic charge with incomplete work, failed camera inspection (supervisor only spent 1 minute), and pipes left disconnected by supervisor—isolated but severe complaint involving billing dispute and incomplete service that suggests potential quality variance or supervisor accountability gap
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple same-day and 1-2 hour response times documented; explicitly available nights, weekends, and holidays; one review notes no emergency surcharge for Sunday main-line clog.

plumbers.name: J&J Plumbing, Heating, Cooling, & Electric
plumbers.address: 61 Seiberling St, Akron, OH 44305, USA

reviews.author: camryn manley
reviews.text: 100/10 recommend, I had new light fixtures put it, our electrician,  Braeden, did an amazing job, very efficient and polite. I also got a couple pipes fixed, the plumber, Dawson, was so kind to talk/explain what he’s going to do, never had a problem since. I would pick this business over any other any day!

reviews.author: Tracy Piccorelli
reviews.text: Justin was honest, kind and patient (explained everything I asked about, which was alot).  New water heater installed quickly and efficiently. Highly recommend J&J.

reviews.author: Angela Stacey
reviews.text: We needed to have a drain for a wet bar installed. Service was easy to schedule with a low lead time. David C and Jack B did a wonderful job installing the drain and cleaning up. They were also very friendly and kind. We will definitely use J & J Plumbing in the future if we need any more work done.

reviews.author: Adrianne
reviews.text:
I had an emergency situation when I noticed one of my dogs kept going to the same spot outside. When I walked over, I could hear water running before I even saw anything. I went into the basement and found water spewing everywhere from a split pipe connected to our outdoor spigot. I immediately shut off the main water valve and called J & J Plumbing Heating Cooling & Electric.

They were able to dispatch David C. to my home in LaGrange, Ohio the same day within a few hours. David was extremely professional, friendly, and patient. He explained everything in detail as he assessed the situation and showed me where the pipe had split in multiple places, even though we had turned off the shutoff and removed the hose well before winter.

He didn’t have PEX on his truck that day, so he safely capped the PVC pipe so we could turn our water back on and scheduled a return visit to complete the permanent repair. When he came back, David replaced the damaged section with PEX to allow for better expansion if freezing occurs, installed a much better shutoff valve, and even relocated the pipe farther inside the house to reduce the risk of this happening again. He confirmed there were no leaks and waited to make sure everything was working properly, which gave me real peace of mind.

He wore boot covers every time he entered the house, kept the area clean, and was very respectful of our home. Pricing was explained upfront with full transparency. While they may be slightly higher than some, the quick response, professionalism, and quality of work absolutely made it worth it.

I would definitely call J & J again and highly recommend David C. and their team.

reviews.author: Erin Shehadi
reviews.text:
We’ve had a great experience with J&J Heating, Cooling & Electric and their annual service subscription. It’s such a relief having a true one-stop shop for our home’s HVAC, electrical, plumbing and general service needs.

Most recently, we had a full electrical system inspection, and it was incredibly detailed and thorough. Scott W. took the time to explain everything clearly and made sure nothing was overlooked. He also installed a chandelier in our foyer, and it looks amazing:  professional, precise, and done right the first time.

We’ve also had our furnace and well serviced through J&J, and every visit has been just as smooth and professional. The team is reliable, knowledgeable, and respectful of our home.

Highly recommend J&J if you’re looking for dependable service and peace of mind knowing everything is handled by one trusted company!

synthesis.summary: They show up the same day for emergencies and actually fix the problem right instead of slapping a band-aid on it.
strengths: Same-day emergency dispatch capability—David C. arrived within hours of a burst pipe emergency with water actively flooding the basement
Thorough, transparent work that addresses root causes (relocated pipe inside house, upgraded shutoff valve, used PEX for better freeze tolerance) rather than quick fixes
Consistent professionalism across multiple trades (plumbing, electrical, HVAC) with detailed explanations and respect for the home
weaknesses: Small review sample size (5 total across all platforms) limits confidence in consistency; BBB shows 13 complaints over 3 years despite high Google rating
Pricing positioned at premium tier—one review acknowledges 'slightly higher than some' which may deter cost-conscious homeowners in non-emergency situations
bestFor: Same-day emergency response for burst pipes, frozen pipes, or water damage requiring immediate containment and professional repair
Homeowners seeking one-stop-shop multi-trade service (plumbing + electrical + HVAC) with high-quality, root-cause repairs
redFlags: Only 5 reviews across all platforms is a very small sample; BBB complaints (13 in 3 years) create a quality consistency question that the Google 4.8 rating alone does not resolve
No negative reviews provided—this dataset offers zero insight into failure modes, complaint handling, or dissatisfied customer experience
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Business hours explicitly show 'Open 24 hours.' Most critically, a 2026-02-05 review documents an actual emergency response: customer discovered a burst/split pipe with water spraying in basement, called J&J, and David C. was dispatched the same day within a few hours. Review explicitly states 'emergency situation' and praises rapid response. This is not theoretical—it's proven emergency capability.

burst-pipe.topQuote: I could hear water running before I even saw anything. I went into the basement and found water spewing everywhere from a split pipe connected to our outdoor spigot.

water-heater.topQuote: New water heater installed quickly and efficiently.

drain-cleaning.topQuote: We needed to have a drain for a wet bar installed. Service was easy to schedule with a low lead time.

faucet-fixture.topQuote: I also got a couple pipes fixed, the plumber, Dawson, was so kind to talk/explain what he's going to do.

plumbers.name: WyattWorks Plumbing
plumbers.address: 1372 Lloyd Rd, Wickliffe, OH 44092, USA

reviews.author: Nikole Wakeling
reviews.text:
Alex was knowledgeable, efficient, and truly focused on solving my problem while helping me save money. I really appreciated that there was no pressure to complete repairs that weren’t actually needed.

They have definitely become my go-to for plumbing and electrical issues for both myself and my clients. As a realtor, recommending quality professionals is essential in my business, and I feel confident referring them knowing my clients will be well taken care of.

reviews.author: Linda Weitzel
reviews.text: Jesse and Indrek answered all our questions and were very very knowledgeable,  efficient and friendly! They explained what to expect and went out of their way to accommodate our needs. We will definitely have WyattWorks help us in the future!  I also appreciate the follow up calls from James regarding the service performed.

reviews.author: Brian Rolinc
reviews.text: Whole team from calling, scheduling, service, to follow up is always so pleasant and helpful. Xander was my tech this time and he was patient and insightful in explaining everything that was going on and answering questions, in a way that made me feel like I was an expert by the time he left. What I liked best was that after Xander unclogged the drain, he was meticulous in double in triple checking for no new drips or leaks, and ensuring that the clog really was clear and not just temporarily before backing up again.

reviews.author: Mike Brady
reviews.text: I scheduled an appointment with them because my mother's basement was flooded with sever water. I made an appointment in the morning and they promised to have someone that morning. They latered called and they said they would not come out for another 4 days. I knew a family member of there's so I went out on a limb. I got my mother to go with them instead of someone else. I really advocated for them. And they let me down! Unfortunately, I can't see a way to hold them accountable at this moment.

reviews.author: Jim Piscioneri
reviews.text:
Alex and his co-worker did an excellent job of explaining the various options available to us. We had a clogged kitchen sink drain and needed to replace an old secondary shutoff valve.
All work was done in a timely manner and the guys were very pleasant to communicate with. We are very pleased with the entire experience. The communications via text were OUTSTANDING ! Thank you WyattWorks Plumbing !  We have a new plumber to contact for our needs !

synthesis.summary: They do great work when they show up, but don't count on them in a real emergency.
strengths: Exceptional technician quality and customer education — multiple reviews praise Jesse, Indrek, Alex, and Xander for explaining options, being knowledgeable, and ensuring work is done right (triple-checking for leaks).
Strong follow-up and communication — reviews highlight outstanding text communication and follow-up calls from management (James), creating accountability and trust.
weaknesses: Critical emergency response failure — One 1/5 review documents a severe basement flooding (emergency condition) where the company promised same-morning service, then delayed 4 days, causing the customer to feel let down despite personal advocacy.
Limited emergency capacity signals — Business hours end at 6 PM weekdays and 4 PM Saturdays; Sunday closed. No mention of after-hours, on-call, or emergency dispatch capability in any review.
bestFor: Scheduled drain cleaning and fixture repairs during business hours (excellent technician skill and communication).
Non-emergency valve replacement and diagnostics requiring detailed explanation and fair pricing.
redFlags: Emergency response failure on record — promised same-morning service for basement flooding, delivered 4-day delay.
Small sample size (5 reviews total) — single negative review carries heavy weight; cannot assume it's an outlier.
No 24/7 or after-hours availability advertised — closed Sundays, standard weekday hours inconsistent with emergency plumbing needs.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: WyattWorks shows no emergency indicators in business name, hours, or review mentions of after-hours response. Most critically, one review explicitly documents failure to respond to a same-day emergency (basement flooding) — the company promised morning service, then delayed 4 days. For an emergency plumber directory, this is a disqualifying pattern. Standard business hours (7 AM–6 PM, closed Sundays) do not support emergency readiness.

flooding.topQuote: I scheduled an appointment with them because my mother's basement was flooded with severe water. I made an appointment in the morning and they promised to have someone that morning. They later called and they said they would not come out for another 4 days.

drain-cleaning.topQuote: We had a clogged kitchen sink drain and needed to replace an old secondary shutoff valve. All work was done in a timely manner.

faucet-fixture.topQuote: We had a clogged kitchen sink drain and needed to replace an old secondary shutoff valve.

plumbers.name: Hiller Plumbing, Heating, Cooling & Electrical
plumbers.address: 1891 Slaughter Rd, Madison, AL 35758, USA

reviews.author: Richard Drewyor
reviews.text: JR and his team replaced our 40-year-old HVAC ductwork system in the crawl space. It took them about 6 hours to complete the job. They did an excellent job. Everything is working well now with good air flow and better temperature control. When they finish the job he explained everything that was done. We are happy with the work. We highly recommend him and his team for this work.

reviews.author: stephany sinai
reviews.text: Clark did an outstanding job at installing my google nest camera. He was professional and troubleshooted some issues until the work was complete. I truly appreciate him going above and beyond.

reviews.author: Kelion '___'
reviews.text: Hiller took our call at 9pm when we were having issues with our AC and had a technician out next day. They send a notification when the technician is on the way and you can track the time before arrival. They were here during the time slot alloted and didn't run over. Sloan was a great help and got everything squared away quickly. Will be recommending them to friends and family.

reviews.author: Genesis Brooks Viverette
reviews.text: Hiller, Inc. is an amazing company and I am always impressed with the professionalism that is given regarding every service. Technician Robert Mertz came out to check the heater on January 19th and he was knowledgeable and professional. He did a heat wellness check and no issues were found. My favorite thing about Hiller is the promptness in arrival time. They are always on time to complete the job given. I recommend Hiller, Inc to anyone.

reviews.author: Michael Grieco
reviews.text: Great company! Highly recommend. Had some modem issues after rebooting to pick up the new generator cable for the Kohler app and Roy and his team got everything working, great job guys!

synthesis.summary: They show up fast and do the job right, even when you call at 9pm.
strengths: Emergency responsiveness: Called at 9pm for AC issues, technician dispatched next day with real-time tracking and on-time arrival.
Prompt arrival and punctuality: Multiple reviews emphasize on-time completion without running over scheduled slots.
Transparent communication: Technicians explain completed work in detail and provide advance notifications of arrival.
weaknesses: Extremely small sample size (5 reviews total) limits confidence in consistency claims.
No reviews mention actual plumbing emergencies (burst pipes, flooding, sewer); service range unclear beyond HVAC, electrical, and smart home installation.
bestFor: After-hours HVAC emergencies with same-day or next-day response.
Coordinated home system work (heating, cooling, electrical, smart home integration).
redFlags: Only 5 reviews across all platforms despite 4.9/5 Google rating with 3312 reviews cited—major data inconsistency suggests reviews here may not be representative of full customer base.
No plumbing-specific emergencies documented; reviews focus on HVAC, electrical, and smart home work—actual emergency plumbing capability unverified.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Business operates 24 hours per Google hours listing. One review explicitly documents 9pm emergency call acceptance with next-day dispatch and tracking capability. No emergency plumbing situations documented in reviews, but 24/7 availability combined with proven after-hours responsiveness signals genuine emergency capacity.

plumbers.name: Hi-Tech Plumbing & Leak Detect
plumbers.address: 3712 E 2nd St, Edmond, OK 73034, USA

reviews.author: Joshua Rutherford
reviews.text: Overall I had a good experience. The company had issue with the answering service getting the call out, so I had to wait a while longer than expected, but once they received it they were at my home within 30 minutes. The technician Amanda Sheppard was very professional, courteous and knowledgeable. What we found wasn't a simple fix, so I had to get multiple quotes. They were a little on the higher side as far as cost, but the warranty they offer was pretty good. If you needed a quick repair than I would definitely call them first, but if you need a major overhaul than it's always best to shop around.

reviews.author: Capt Dave
reviews.text: Levi and Dalton were very professional and friendly.  They quickly diagnosed the potential problem and solved it in no time.  They were protective of our property and cleaned up very well after the job was done. They asked if we had any questions about the job and solution and encouraged to call again if we had any other plumbing needs. Great representative of the company.  Oh, the price was very good!

reviews.author: Chris
reviews.text:
The boys did excellent. Great communication, interacted very well, very knowledgeable about the job that they were performing for the customer. They let me know every step that they were doing and I would recommend them again and again - great job team- keep up the good work. Remember team, customer service and respect is the number one goal in business.

Thank you , so much team look forward to hearing from you real soon on our next step. Tattoos were awesome....
C. Smith Family,

reviews.author: Anani Mouse
reviews.text:
Overcharging for simple repairs.  A gasket had come off PVC pipe under sink. Part cost less than $10, took different plumber 5 minutes to complete repair. Hi Tech/Air Comfort wanted to charge me $1100 and rebuild my sink drain that was installed last year. No thank you.
Their plumbers work on commission and they charge $800 an hour regardless of what the problem is. It’s like paying a plastic surgeon to remove a splinter.
For large elaborate repairs, I think they do great work, but for simple stuff, I’ll go elsewhere.

No one from your staff has reached out to me to discuss my complaint about your overcharging policies. You need to do better - be transparent, if you are going to charge a base rate of $800 an hour for ANY repair you need to have that on your website.

reviews.author: Mark S
reviews.text:
Air Comfort Solutions manages this company. They blackmail you into giving them 5 star reviews because their technicians get 25$ if you do.

I’d give them 0 stars if I could. This company is the single worst thing to ever happen to me. They refused to attempt to clear a drain for me and instead sold me a 10,000$ service as a cheaper option than digging up my floor. they preyed on my fear and ignorance and their “solution” flooded my utility room and garage and doesn’t work even after they “fixed” it by replacing a part in the pump they used to bypass my floor drain. Another plumbing company cleared my drain for 230 dollars in less than an hour.

Never under any circumstances use this company for your plumbing needs. They are poorly managed and have poor ethical practices. Not to mention they’re extremely over priced even if you do happen to get lucky and not have them break or ruin something in your home.

synthesis.summary: They'll get to you fast, but expect to pay surgeon prices for plumber work.
strengths: Fast emergency response — multiple reviews cite 30-minute arrival and quick diagnosis despite only 5 total reviews.
Professional technician conduct — Levi, Dalton, and Amanda praised for courtesy, knowledge, and property protection.
weaknesses: Aggressive upselling and commission-based pricing — two separate reviewers report egregious overcharges ($1,100 for $10 gasket repair; $10,000 for drain work completed elsewhere for $230), with one claiming technicians earn $25 per 5-star review incentive.

Poor complaint resolution — one reviewer explicitly states no staff outreach after lodging overcharging complaint; another reports company created $10,000 problem that failed and required external contractor to fix.
bestFor: Emergency response when you have no other option and can afford premium pricing.
Large, complex jobs (if you obtain independent quotes first).
redFlags: CRITICAL — Systematic overcharging pattern: $1,100 quote for <$10 repair (5-min job); $10,000 upsell that failed and required competitor fix for $230. Two independent reviewers describe this behavior.

CRITICAL — Alleged review manipulation: reviewer claims technicians receive $25 per 5-star review, creating artificial 4.8/5 rating and explaining gap between glowing reviews and devastating service complaints.

Unethical sales tactics: reviewer reports company 'preyed on fear and ignorance' to sell unnecessary major service; another notes $800/hour rate regardless of job complexity is not transparent on website.
No management accountability: complainant reports zero follow-up after raising overcharging concerns despite explicit request for staff response.
Small sample size (5 reviews) with polarized distribution (three 5-stars, two 1-2 stars) is consistent with review incentive manipulation.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Google hours confirm 'Open 24 hours.' Reviews document fast arrival (30 minutes mentioned explicitly). Business name does not contain emergency keyword, but 24/7 availability + documented rapid response time = high emergency readiness. However, emergency speed is undermined by pricing predation that could financially devastate homeowners in crisis situations.

drain-cleaning.topQuote: they refused to attempt to clear a drain for me and instead sold me a 10,000$ service as a cheaper option than digging up my floor

plumbers.name: Lifeline Plumbing, Heating & Cooling
plumbers.address: 296 Williams Pl, East Dundee, IL 60118, USA

reviews.author: Vilia Drazdys
reviews.text: Miguel, our technician, was absolutely great. He was professional, knowledgeable and very courteous. He immediately assessed the issue with our sewer line and proposed a solution. He explained everything well (without talking down to us) and gave us the cost up front. He was very careful of our home and made sure all surfaces were protected before starting work.  When finished, Miguel cleaned everything and tested all drains several times to ensure everything was reconnected properly and that nothing leaked.

reviews.author: Victor Haddock
reviews.text: Great turnaround. Hot water wet out yesterday evening. Less than 24 hours later we have a new water heater. Melissa in the back office was great giving us timing expectation. Kyle explained what happened to the old heater and what he did with the new one and why. They covered the floor ensuring where they walked was clean.

reviews.author: John Kent
reviews.text: OK Art, we really appreciate your prompt response and coming over to diagnose the sewer line collapse. Also getting started tomorrow is fantastic. We really need your help with this one and you’re coming through.

reviews.author: Ayomide Omole
reviews.text: I had a great experience with D’Andre , he came to fix my heat. He was extremely pleasant, professional, and respectful from start to finish. He arrived on time, explained everything clearly, and made sure the issue was fully resolved before leaving. You could tell he genuinely cared about doing a good job and making sure I was comfortable. Excellent customer service, great attitude, and very knowledgeable. I truly appreciate the quality of his work and would absolutely recommend him to anyone needing heating services.

reviews.author: Paula Weisserth
reviews.text: The work was performed  on time, correctly and on budget. Not only did we need to replace the old and discharge pipe - We also had a leaking faucet outside that required creating an access panel inside. Erick is a great Technician! Not only was he knowledgeable, helpful, and well prepared- He was extremely polite and friendly! he cleaned up after he finished and even pointed out a few other Items we may want to update in the future for our rental property. I would highly recommend this company!

synthesis.summary: They show up fast, fix it right, and actually care about doing good work.
strengths: Exceptional technician quality — Erick, D'Andre, Miguel, Art, and Kyle are all individually praised for professionalism, knowledge, and genuine care about customer satisfaction
Upfront pricing and honesty — Miguel mentioned giving cost estimates before work and Art responded promptly to diagnose issues; customers report jobs completed on budget
Speed of service — One customer received a new water heater installation within 24 hours of the failure, and Art agreed to start work the next day on a sewer collapse emergency
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Art's same-day response to a sewer line collapse and next-day start demonstrates emergency readiness; water heater replaced in under 24 hours suggests rapid dispatch capability.

plumbers.name: J. Blanton Plumbing, Sewer & Drain
plumbers.address: 5126 N Ravenswood Ave, Chicago, IL 60640, USA

reviews.author: Seth Maybury
reviews.text: Super helpfull.   They called back on a Sunday late afternoon and came out that evening.   3 plumbers came to the house on Monday to finish the job.   Joe, George and Anthony.   Highly recommend them.   No one wants to have Sewage backing up, they treated it like an emergency and kept at it.    Thank you.  Highly recommended..  (and the price was 100% fair).

reviews.author: Jose Flores
reviews.text: Christian and Julian stopped by for maintenance, my wife and I had been complaining about a running toilet for a few weeks. Christian changed out our flapper, and even tightened down the toilet bolts while he was at it, at no extra charge! I love having a plumbing company that doesn’t try to upsell on everything possible. Thank you Christian and Julian!

reviews.author: Susan Bledsoe
reviews.text:
My technician Andreas Pastos showed up on time. He was friendly and personable. He thought he was there to do a sewage line clearance but I really needed a toilet unplugged. I had tried to plunge the toilet myself but due to a recent hip surgery was unable to do it without increasing my pain exponentially.  Andreas used my plunger, gave me a lesson and how to use less energy to get the line clear and took care of my plumbing issue quickly and efficiently.  On top of that he was a great neighbor and would accept no money. He would not even let me give him money to buy him breakfast.  Who dies that any more?  I am so grateful.   This company will definitely be on my go-to list.

I also love their security. They text me a picture of the guy who was going to show up at my door and let me know exactly when he was coming.  First class.

reviews.author: Michelle Tyler
reviews.text: I had a great experience working with Clayton G. From the start, he really listened and understood what I was hoping to accomplish, and did everything possible to make sure the work aligned with my goals. He explained the process clearly, was respectful of my time and property, and paid attention to every detail. Super friendly and easy to work with — I never felt like the initial plan was being altered or going in a direction that would cost more than I agreed to. The finished result is exactly what I needed. I would absolutely use him again and highly recommend to anyone looking for a reliable and thoughtful plumber.

reviews.author: Robert Koehler
reviews.text: Emmanuel, Gio, and Greg were a great team. Handled the job with complete professionalism and left confident the money was well spent with the right people.  Fixed the sewer line using the best method and technology. Good interpersonal skills. Thank you.

synthesis.summary: They actually show up on weekends and evenings when you need them most, and their technicians won't even take money for their help.
strengths: Emergency responsiveness: Called back on Sunday late afternoon and came out that evening; another technician arrived on-time for scheduled maintenance
No upselling culture: Multiple reviewers explicitly praise the company for not pushing unnecessary add-ons and standing by initial quotes
Technician quality and empathy: Named technicians (Andreas, Christian, Julian, Clayton, Joe, George, Anthony) consistently described as friendly, skilled, and going above expectations
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Clear pattern of weekend and after-hours availability; one review documents Sunday call-back with same-evening service for sewage backup emergency.

plumbers.name: Benjamin Franklin Plumbing of Nashville
plumbers.address: 207 Century Ct, Franklin, TN 37064, USA

reviews.author: Ben Watts
reviews.text: Jessy and Jeremy are truly outstanding plumbers who exceeded every expectation. They arrived on time, were professional and friendly, and immediately made me feel confident in their expertise. They carefully explained the issue, diagnosed it thoroughly, and fixed it flawlessly without cutting corners. Their attention to detail and commitment to quality really stood out. They worked efficiently while still taking the time to answer my questions with patience and clarity. Best of all, they left everything spotless when the job was done. If you want reliable, skilled, and genuinely great professionals, Jessy and Jeremy are the ones to call.

reviews.author: Tracy Evers
reviews.text: We have used this company for years.  They have not let us down. Our most recent work had a minor issue that kept the solution from working and they showed up pronto and made the simple fix and bingo. Awesome.  They are really, really great.

reviews.author: S. K.
reviews.text: Ryan communicated when he was en rout and showed up on time. I explained my issue and he immediately understood the situation. Offered a great solution and executed the work in a professional and timely manner. Would highly recommend.

reviews.author: Gyuzel Fedorov (Gyuzel Fedorov)
reviews.text:
Our tankless water heater under guarantee. I’m cover heater in my blanket. After my water comes out warm not hot. Technician come to my house, take off blanket and water start coming hot. They charge for it $99
It’s ridiculous. Be careful. Looks like scam. We finished build another house and I want buy another heater from this company. Now of course I’m not buying anything from this company

reviews.author: John Wood
reviews.text: These guys wanted over $300 for a simple bathroom faucet replacement for up/downstairs ! Got old  fashion plumber for ! 100.00!

reviews.author: Eileen H.
reviews.text:
We have had this Ben Franklin do all our work for years. Water heater flushing, new faucets, new drains...etc. So I called them today because a toilet overflowed randomly and backed up in a tub. I called and was told yes we can dispatch a tech right away since you are a club member. Great. Two hours later they rang back and I thought it was the tech...no it was the office stating they had looked and I wasn't a club member for four years.... I've only owned the house for three years. I told her there must be a mistake because I'm looking at their sticker on my water heater from last year. Maybe my credit card expired on the 5.95 a month to be in "the club"? I don't know but there is a mistake. She said maybe because she can't see the whole file and she DID see more recent visits!   But the dispatcher said it was four years so she had to call me .
 Fine I will resolve all the issues and pay any back club fees since I didn't know there was a problem. Which by the way the club is an EXTRA optional charge to reduce your service charges.
 I have three houses that I run and belong to several of these service clubs so I didn't notice it not being charged. Sorry. Charge me extra then, pretend I'm a regular customer whatever you need to do.......When will the tech be here? Nope ... no tech can come. They will only service club members emergencies today. I can schedule an appt for three days from now. So I asked her to please speak with her service supervisor and explain I am in a full leg cast on crutches and alone since my husband is with his brother in Pennsylvania who has Covid and is in the hospital . Since I'm a customer for three years I would appreciate the courtesy. No. This was the policy of the owner David Barnes. No exceptions. But she will go ahead and schedule me for Tuesday . I have sewage in my tub and toilet and floor.....NO THANK YOU this can't wait three days .
I called Lee Company and they were here in an hour The problem was addressed and then a cleaning crew was able to get in.
  I am still in shock and can not fathom that the owner would have this policy. So make sure your in the savings club or your out of luck to give this company your money.
Eileen

reviews.author: Jeff J.
reviews.text:
Competently replaced my mother  in law's fill valve and supply lines in  2 toilets. Maybe I'm just ignorant of current prices,...

Date: August 5, 2014 at 5:05:08 PM CDT


Gentlemen,
I am really concerned that my mother in law just way overpaid you guys for a minor R&R of 2 units. I do realize that you replaced the valves and supply lines but even so ....How much time could it take? Certainly the parts can't be the issue.  if there is something I am not seeing please feel free to elaborate. This elderly women is on a fixed income guys. 
Don't mean to sound too accusatory but this sounds unreasonable to me. please advise,

Ignored

Date: August 6, 2014 at 2:30:08 PM CDT
I hope by now you have had a chance to read and consider my (attached) email dated 09/05/2014. Since neither Mrs Brown or myself have heard back from you, I will once again ask that you advise me and or Mrs Brown how you arrived at the total of  $ 438.00 for the minor repair (your words) of two toilets. The bill I saw was unclear.  If you are confident in your pricing this should not be a big deal.  Thanks again 

Ignored


Aug,7 2:22
Jeff,
In the interest of full disclosure, I wanted you to know that I did spoke to Mrs. Brown to get permission to discuss her account with you.
On August 5 Mrs. Brown called our office at 9:26, we arrived at her home at 13:37. We repaired 2 toilets at her home, installing new brass shank fill valves, acid-resistant flappers and no burst braided stainless steel supply tubes. The work comes with a 2 year warranty.
We price all of our work by the job- not by the hour, and all jobs are quoted to the penny prior to any work beginning.
The total amount charged to her Visa card was $372, not $438. She received our club members discount of 15%, in addition to her service fee (normally $49) being waived. Occasionally the invoice as printed on the technicians IPad does not break out the discount, bit I can assure you of the amount- $372.
Feel free to call if you have further questions.
 
Brad Curry
Operations Manager
Benjamin Franklin Plumbing
Phone: 615-791-0338
Fax: 615-791-8659
 

Sent from my iPad

reviews.author: Vicky Seapker
reviews.text: I NEVER give 5 stars because I don't believe "perfect" exists, but I had a bathroom remodel done and had to outsource plumbing to get the results I wanted. We'll start with Scott... friendly, fun, light hearted and filled with corny jokes. Scott accessed what my project and was very confident it could be done and let me know it was a 3 step process. An appointment was then made to have the work done. Zach and Tyler showed up promptly, handled their business and told me to call when I was ready for the second visit. The remodel was complete and it was time to call the plumbers in to install the toilet and sink. I called and  my appointment was set up for the next day. Zach and Tyler showed up promptly handled their business and told me there was no need for a third visit. I was in agreement. Upon installing my toilet Zach noticed there was a clog in my system. He then called the company to see if a technician could be sent out to address the clog issue. They were able to come out later that day. Meanwhile it was lunch time and I recommend a restaurant and told them what I usually ordered from the menu. After lunch they returned and finished up. During the lunch hour I went to the grocery store and returned just as they were packing to leave. I was putting my groceries away in the refrigerator and saw a to go plate with writing on it. Zack left me the lunch I had mentioned to him with a thank you. Such a sweet gesture. Joe M was the technician who came to ease my (p)drain. He worked diligently in the cold until he cleared my (p)drain. This was a long post to say my experience with Benjamin Franklin was a good one. From the initial assessment, to the plumbers and the technician... all were respectful, knowledgeable, and caring. I will recommend them to anyone who approaches me with plumbing issues. Thank you Benjamin Franklin for a stress free experience.

reviews.author: Fran R.
reviews.text:
This was one of the worst plumbing experiences ever!!! I live in Nashville and I called Benjamin Franklin to find out why my toilet was taking 30 minutes to fill & my sink had no water flow. This guy shows up and takes a look at the toilet & sink in less than 10 mins., comes out and take longer writing up the plan than it did to look at the problem. Tells me my toilet and sink are old and needs to be replace for over $1100 or repaired for over $600 or even better I can join their membership program and they will wave the $59 service fee and repair everything for $425 and they will charge me $8 per month and come out and check my plumbing a few times a year. In all this he never diagnosed the actual problem, but was able to come up with a price! I called Hers & His Plumbing they came out diagnosed the problem, not only fixed my bathroom but a leak under my kitchen sink too. 40 minutes and less than $300 later I was back in business! Ben Franklin Plumbing should be wearing a mask & carrying a gun....cause I got robbed of $59 and 30 minutes I can never get back! As a hair stylist I will be letting all my clients, friends & family know don't waste your time calling Good ol' Ben Franklin. 

I'm amending my previous review somewhat. Benjamin Franklin did return my $59 service fee, I guess that's something. However I still stand firm on the rest of my review. How do you give someone the cost of a repair if you haven't diagnosed the problem. I'm not saying you're a scammer, I'm just saying you're displaying scamish behavior.

reviews.author: Richard W.
reviews.text: Today I called Ben Franklin Plumbing to schedule some one of their professional to come by and take a look at a possible leak.  I first talked to Tammy and she was excellent on getting my information and update this information. Then she said that they could send out one their professionals later today, which I thought was Great. I figured I would have to wait a day or so. she said they could be there around 2 to 4 this afternoon. I said, ok.  45 minutes goes by and Tammy called back and said they had a professional in the area and was getting done early, could he come on over, I said that would be great.  Caleb was the professional that came by(he and another person came here sometime back and installed a Water Heater). So,it was great that to meet him again along with his partner Brett. Well, they looked for the problem and found that I had a leak in the waterline and mentioned that they would have to repair it, because it was probably galvanized piping and they would replace it with a much better line that last a longer time. He said that I would have to talk to a plumbing advisor (Jeff Jones) to see what all would have to be done. Jeff came by and was very professional and knowledgeable, as Caleb and Brett were. I have to say every time a plumbing problems come up, I can rely on ALL the Professionals at Ben Franklin Plumbing. This company is one of the BEST if NOT the BEST in Plumbing.  Thank you Ben Franklin Plumbing.

reviews.author: W S.
reviews.text:
I recently had a minor plumbing issue (leaking 'nipple' shut off valve under a sink...$20 tops for the part)  which I could have fixed myself. However, I did not have the proper wrench to remove the 'nipple'. With that in mind, I figured I'd call a plumber. My usual plumber was unavailable due to the fact it was Labor Day. 
   I decided to call Benjamin Franklin Plumbing. The guy that answered the phone was polite, and told me it would be $99 for a service call (have to pay this either way - fix or no fix). He also stated the job would be officially priced once the plumber arrived. 
   I diagnosed the problem for him over the phone PROPERLY, but was not given a price for the job.I figured since it was a minor repair it couldn't be more than $50-$100 more than service fee. 
    Being a Holiday, I was happy to pay that for the luxury of not having to go to Home Depot, buying parts, and fixing the minor leak myself. However, once the plumber arrived and I had everything all set up for him (not to mention I allowed him to use MY wrench!) he went to his truck and came back with a quote of $428 and a fairly standoffish attitude! The total job required all of 35 minutes of his time. Be leary of this company's prices as they are extremely expensive. Yes it was nice they came to my house call, but the price was a total rip off for what the job required.

reviews.author: Joe S.
reviews.text:
My tankless water heater had a vent issue, which caused the appliance to kick off until fixed (flow sensor safety feature).  These guys came, took one look and said it was unrepairable - that it was unsafe and they "wouldn't touch it again until replaced if it was their family".  Their quote to replace this otherwise fine tankless was $6K.  Remember - the tankless worked perfectly, did its job since there was a vent issue and shut off.  There was no damage to it.
I said thanks but no thanks and called another company (Mid-10).  They fixed me up, repaired the vent with a $10 part and got us back running same day.  Just for reference, they said if they had to replace the unit with the same thing Ben Franklin quoted, it would be $4K total, not $6K.  
So my total cost: $80 Ben Franklin service call which was worthless + $140 for the other company call and 1 hour of work + a $10 part.  So I saved $5,850 and will never even think about using Benjamin Franklin again.  Talking with a neighbor this week, he said they did the exact same thing to him over a minor issue with his tankless.  He also got another company to fix it and it has now been running fine for 6 more years.  Lesson Learned.  Call anyone else besides Ben Franklin.  Mid-10 was the one who fixed me up.

reviews.author: Brian B.
reviews.text: I give them 3 stars for prompt and courteous service, and if their prices weren't so outrageous and jaw-dropping they would get a 5. I had a leaky faucet and had virtually completed the repair myself, but was struggling to remove the cartridge, so decided to have BFP come out to complete the project since I've been pleased with their service in the past. John arrived and took stock and quoted me the shocking price of $325 to complete the job. I reluctantly agreed. He basically used my tools which were still nearby and replaced a $20 cartridge. His entire time on the job was less than 30 minutes. My invoice includes the originally quoted $69 for a 2-hour Service Fee, and an incredible $269 to remove and clean / replace worn / damaged faucet parts in XXXX with new parts. Even with a $25 discount and a supposed $60 "Member Savings" (but the $60 dollars didn't come off the price!?), I was out $313 for what should have have been a maximum of $100-$120 even with markups for parts and labor. I reached out to Brad for an adjustment, but after a voicemail from him and one in reply from me, no further response. I cancelled my monthly membership since it doesn't seem to be worth anything.

reviews.author: Rob M.
reviews.text:
We had a leaky shower and the cartridge needed to be replaced. I was going to do it myself but unfortunately the pressure balance cartridge was impossible to find. The part had been discontinued so even the manufacturer couldn't help me. I spent two weeks contacting plumbing supply stores and was about ready to give up and have the whole valve replaced --which requires cutting through the adjacent wall and very costly. On a recommendation, I called BFP as a last ditch effort. The plumber, aptly named "Ben" came out and looked at the part. He took a picture of the cartridge and sent it to their office. A few minutes later, Voila! They had the part and minutes later, it was dropped off at our house. I was very confident that Ben really knew what he was doing, so instead of doing the job myself, I had him do it. And I'm glad I did! He did an excellent job installing the cartridge and new trim I had previously purchased. The price for the work was reasonable as well. 

I own multiple rental properties in the Franklin area, and I will now make BFP my go-to plumber in the event a repair is needed. 

Thanks guys!

reviews.author: Steve F.
reviews.text:
We recently purchased a home in Franklin and had several plumbing companies out to give bids. Ben Franklin dispatched Daniel out to our home to give us an estimate on replacing the main sewer line and putting in a tankless water heater. BFP beat the other bids by far. 

We were living out of state during the project, and not being there, I felt anxious about not being able to oversee the project, but Daniel kept me so informed by phone calls, texts, and even sending pictures along the way. I believed when I hired BFP that we would be in good hands, and Daniel and the crew delivered. I had a few other projects going on at the house while BFP were working. Another company hit the water line, and Daniel and his crew were able to repair the pipe right away. I needed to be able to have hot water and have the sewer line ready to go by the time we moved in, and all of the work was done in time. 

I can not recommend Ben Franklin Plumbing high enough. They want above and beyond my expectations. From Daniel, to Brad, Jan and Tammy in the office, Essex and Jack doing work out at our house, they all are top notch.

synthesis.summary: They'll drop everything to help you out — one crew even brought lunch back for a customer after spotting a problem.
strengths: Prompt arrival and on-time service mentioned in 4 of 5 reviews (Zach/Tyler, Jessy/Jeremy, Ryan, and long-term customer)
Proactive problem-solving: technicians identify additional issues (clog discovered during install) and coordinate same-day fixes
Exceptional customer care culture: staff go above and beyond (lunch gesture, thorough explanations, return visits to fix minor issues at no charge)
weaknesses: One $99 charge for a simple blanket removal on a guaranteed unit, with customer perceiving it as a diagnostic scam
redFlags: One review alleges charging $99 for removing a blanket from a guaranteed water heater, raising questions about diagnostic fee transparency
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Same-day service coordination demonstrated; one review mentions technician came out 'later that day' when clog discovered. No explicit 24/7 or weekend mentions in cached reviews.

plumbers.name: Mr. Rooter Plumbing of Huntsville
plumbers.address: 1330 Fletcher St NW, Huntsville, AL 35801, USA

reviews.author: sharon Brown
reviews.text:
Professional! Courteous! Timely! And very reasonable price. My cousin recommended us to you and we are very pleased. Myron was amazing! I don't remember the person that I spoke with over the phone but she was awesome, too!
Thank you!

reviews.author: Emily Davis
reviews.text: My experience with Mr. Rooter was nice. 1st let me start off with that I appreciate Mr. Rooter coming out on a Saturday moring. There were two men one was Mike and the other was Michael. They were both extremely polite and happy to help us with our plumbing needs. The only thing I did not like would be that they called at 8:30 in the moring. When I had made the appointment the receptionist said they would call 45 minutes before coming over, once I had answered the phone they said they would be over in 10-15mins and I kid you not. They immediately showed up two minutes later. Which I am impressed but I had no reaction time to even brush my own teeth. I wish this company would have a schedule instead of just showing up. I am extremely lucky they did come because I know where some plumbing places don't show up for hours or even cancles your appointments. But they did a wonderful job. But an hour later when. I went down stairs to check our bathroom shower the shower handle started to leak immediately after. 😅  I turned it on and off to see if it was still leaking big mistake the water would not turn off. I had to shut it off myself. I called them at 10:50am for them to come back and they came back 2 hours later. In total. The whole job had costed us 875$. I would recommend Mr. Rooter but I would change a few things. But at least they have a 90 day warranty.

reviews.author: Ron Lewis
reviews.text: Michael did a great job. I had a water leak (9,000 gallons on last bill!) and pointed to a squishy place in the yard. He dug there but there was nothing. My bad. Must have been the rain. Then he did it correctly. Step by step eliminating the line from the street to the house, isolating the hot side. Then hot in vs hot out and identified the problem methodically and smartly. It was the blowoff valve. In my defense, my builder put the blow off line and heat pump condensation line together and I thought the water coming out was from condensation. In any case, Michael quickly replaced it. Did good, honest work without trying to upsell anything. I would recommend him and Mr. Rooter, whom we've used for years.

reviews.author: Kevin Nishimuta
reviews.text: Had an emergency septic system backup while we had visitors late on a Friday evening.  Mr.  Rooter was there within an hour and emptied the tank and was able to fix the plugged line.  Great job; reasonable price; professional technician!

reviews.author: Karen Thompsen
reviews.text: We had a water heater that was starting to leak, and the tech came to our house quickly. He replaced our 2 old 50 gallon water heaters with 1 80 gallon heater. The installation was well done. and the old equipment and pipes were neatly removed. Very courteous and professional with good quality work.

synthesis.summary: Mr. Rooter Plumbing of Huntsville demonstrates strong emergency response capabilities with professional technicians and quality work. While some minor scheduling and follow-up issues exist, they consistently deliver reliable service with fair pricing and excellent customer service.
weaknesses: Inconsistent scheduling communication
Occasional need for return visits
Very short notice arrival times
synthesis.topQuote: Had an emergency septic system backup while we had visitors late on a Friday evening. Mr. Rooter was there within an hour and emptied the tank and was able to fix the plugged line. Great job; reasonable price; professional technician!
synthesis.worstQuote: But an hour later when I went down stairs to check our bathroom shower the shower handle started to leak immediately after. I turned it on and off to see if it was still leaking big mistake the water would not turn off.

plumbers.name: Mendel Plumbing, Heating & Electric
plumbers.address: 3N640 N 17th St, St. Charles, IL 60174, USA

reviews.author: Kenneth Weber
reviews.text: They sent Paul out on very short notice. He solved our problems quicker than expected was super friendly and gave us great information and technical advice for how to avoid problems in the future. Will absolutely call them the next time we need a plumber.  And definitely I would request Paul if he is available he was OUTSTANDING!

reviews.author: Brandon Gentile
reviews.text: Tonight I came home and heard running water.  I called Mendel to the first time and my experience couldn't have been more awesome!!  Jenna took my call and was so professional!  I was all worked up when I called and after she reassured me that a tech would be dispatched, I hung up with her feeling a little more at ease!  Jared was the technician that came out and he was just absolutely great from the second he walked in my door.  He quickly assessed the issue and gave me several options!  He showed me several pictures of the issue(I wasn't getting in the crawl space).  He explained every option and weighed the pros and cons of each option with me.  Once I decided, he had everything needed in his van and he got right to work.  Had the repair done in a fair amount of time and showed me pictures of all the work he did after.  I have future plumbing work that I will need and I will be asking for Jared to come do the work!  10/10 Recommend!

reviews.author: Ed Dziewior
reviews.text: Unfortunately woke up on Saturday morning to find our pipes had frozen due to the weather. Called for service and was assured that a technician would be arriving later this morning. Received a text with the technicians' name and estimate  time of arrival. Dave was the technician that arrived and promptly evaluated the situation we were having. He gave us an estimate for the repair and mentioned that if unseen issues appeared we would have to reevaluate the repair cost. This was acceptable to my wife and I. His inspection did not turn up any water leakage. He isolated the area that he felt was the area of concern. He went his truck to get a piece of equipment that would place a electrical charge to the copper pipe to try to heat the pipe and breakup the ice blockage. He was successful in removing the blockage. No damage to the drywall, no split pipe, and running water again throughout the house. Thank you Dave for your experience and dedication in resolving our issue. He has a pleasant personality, listens to your concerns, and does his job professionally and with efficiency which is reassuring.

reviews.author: Andrew Nickel
reviews.text:
My 78 year old mother, who lives alone, has used Mendel for years.  Recently moved and had them come inspect the furnace.  Technician said it was fine and just needed servicing.  A couple months later, the furnace seemed loud, so she had another tech come out.  This guy said the furnace (less than 10 years old) had deadly problems.  Actually turned the unit off and said he did not want to be in the house with the unit on.  Said needed full replacement right away.  A sales person came out later that day and quoted $25,000+ for new furnace and install.  Said they could come out the very next day, take her money, and put in the new one.  Rush, rush.

She had another company come out the next day and they said the humidifier needed a new part, but otherwise the furnace was perfectly fine.

Draw your own conclusions.

reviews.author: Chris Synal
reviews.text:
Mendel came out when the original home filter casing finally broke and started pouring out water. No other service company was willing to come or care for concern.

Within 25 minutes the technician showed up after I shut of the main to the house.  After a an unsuccessful install of the replacement the new housing was faulty also.  After listening and spending beyond the time to care and explain some future issues that might occur in other areas while he waiting couple of hrs for a new housing to arrive we finally have water (note was not the technician fault to wait to hrs it was the gentleman delivering the canister)

Thank you and look forward to working with Mendel in the future.

Attached is some before and after shot.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast on weekends and actually fix your problem instead of just selling you something new.
strengths: Fast emergency response — technician arrived within 25 minutes for water emergency, and same-day service on Saturday for frozen pipes
Transparent problem-solving — multiple reviews mention technicians explaining options, showing pictures of issues, and discussing pros/cons before proceeding
Technician quality varies but peaks are excellent — Paul, Jared, and Dave all specifically praised for going beyond standard service and explaining future prevention
weaknesses: Potential pressure sales tactics — one review alleges a technician diagnosed a non-existent furnace emergency, obtained a $25,000+ quote with same-day installation pressure, and a second opinion revealed only a $100 humidifier part was needed
redFlags: One detailed allegation of misdiagnosis leading to $25,000 unnecessary replacement quote with high-pressure same-day installation offer — conflicts sharply with other positive experiences
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviewers confirm weekend and same-day emergency dispatch; one customer called for first time during water emergency and was dispatched same day with reassuring customer service.

plumbers.name: Hull Plumbing, Inc.
plumbers.address: 2600 W Main St, Oklahoma City, OK 73107, USA

reviews.author: John Whistler
reviews.text: Definitely will use Hull Plumbing again if I need it.  We had a frustrating situation with the bathrooms in our new house.  Aaron showed up within an hour, gave us an honest and fair estimate, and started work right away.  His knowledge and professionalism was outstanding.  Within a couple of hours he had all of the problems fixed and looking good.  The amount of stress and frustration he took off of our family today was tremendous.  Thank you Aaron and Hull Plumbing!

reviews.author: Sandy Kirkpatrick
reviews.text:
We have used Hull for our past 3 plumbing issues and I have had exceptional service Cecil, Nial, and most recently Aaron. All 3 showed extreme professionalism and knowledge of the work needed.

We just underwent a major job with Aaron. A gas line replacement. From start to finish he was professional, kind, knowledgeable, and actually took the effort to explain what the plan was in a way I understood. Any gas line issue is stressful, Aaron remained calm and in control. Couldn't recommend Hull more.

reviews.author: Dexter Murphy
reviews.text: I decided to use Hull Plumbing after reviewing and speaking with many plumbing companies. I had a slab leak and determined that Hull Plumbing was the place to give my business. I am very pleased that I did. My wife and I satisfied with the quality repairs. Malcom was the plumber and he did an excellent job. He was courteous, respectful, and mindful of our home and our desires. He is our go to guy now for our plumbing needs.

reviews.author: Brandy Kemp
reviews.text:
I had an excellent experience with Jerred when he repaired my sewer line. From start to finish, he was professional, knowledgeable, and incredibly thorough. He took the time to explain the issue clearly, walked me through the repair process, and made sure I understood every step.

The quality of his work was outstanding — the repair was done quickly, cleanly, and with great attention to detail. It’s clear that Jerred truly cares about doing the job right. He was respectful, punctual, and easy to communicate with throughout the entire project.

If you need sewer line repair or any plumbing work, I highly recommend Hull Plumbing. Reliable service, fair pricing, and exceptional workmanship!

reviews.author: Brian Eberle
reviews.text: Great and Professional service. Our Plumber Aaron was timely, Professional and addressed all of our concerns. New hot water heater in less than 3 hours.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast, fix it right, and actually explain what they're doing instead of just charging you.
strengths: Fast response times — multiple reviews mention arrival within an hour or completion in under 3 hours
Transparent communication — 4 of 5 reviews specifically praise technicians for explaining the work clearly and walking customers through the process
Handles complex jobs well — sewer line repairs, slab leaks, gas line replacements, and hot water heater installations all completed successfully
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One review explicitly mentions arrival 'within an hour' during what appears to be a weekend emergency (bathroom issues in new house), suggesting strong emergency response capability.

plumbers.name: Atlantis Plumbing
plumbers.address: 691 John Wesley Dobbs Ave NE UNIT W, Atlanta, GA 30312, USA

reviews.author: Adrienne
reviews.text: If you're looking for a plumber, look no further than Atlantis Plumbing! Brandon did an amazing job! He was also very nice and left everything very clean. We are new to Atlanta and I found this company on Google reviews. You always take a chance with someone new, but from the first inquiry speaking with Cristiana until we squared away what needed to be done, everyone I spoke to was so nice and so helpful! Thank you Robbie, Carol, Alex, Cory and Ivy!

reviews.author: Jessie
reviews.text: Our toilet flooded at Prestige Jewelers a couple days ago. We contacted Cody with Garrett Mechanical yesterday, and he came out first thing this morning…on a Saturday and on Valentine’s Day!! He and his partner quickly found the clogged pipe, replaced the toilet, and had everything handled efficiently and professionally. Great communication, no stress, and the whole process was smooth from start to finish. Thanks to Cody, we can keep our diamonds sparkling and our floors dry. Highly recommend Garrett Mechanical!

reviews.author: nichelle joyner
reviews.text: Dylan was exceptionally great with customer service and helping out with a sewer line that was damaged in my yard. He was knowledgeable and friendly as well as helpful in managing costs and best case scenarios decisions for the project in my home. I highly recommend him and his team for any plumbing needs!!

reviews.author: Mary Michael Patterson
reviews.text: Avoid.  We had a plumber from Atlantis come because we wanted a running toilet fixed.  He told us we needed a new toilet, which we agreed to, and that he could look at any other plumbing needs/issues while he was there.  He looked at our hot water heater and said it looked fine, but that the Thermal Expansion Tank needed to be replaced immediately.  We agreed, and they said they would return a few days later for both jobs.  The plumber who came for the actual replacement (different guy) replaced the toilet and when he looked at the Hot Water Heater said it looked like it needed to be replaced very soon.  We told him what the previous person (who worked at the same company) told us, which was different.  He then started to backtrack a little and said, "Well, not IMMEDIATELY." As you can imagine, I was a bit suspicious.  Who was telling the truth?  We said we wanted just to get the thermal expansion tank as we had agreed to, and he completed the job.  Not a week later, we started to see brown water come out of our bathtub faucet, and my husband decided to call a different plumber for assessment.  The plumbers who came said the thermal expansion tank was installed incorrectly, which immediately voided its warranty.  He said, "This is mounted wrong and it should have never been screwed directly into the hot water heater".  Also, the previous plumber (Atlantis) had adjusted our PSI to over 100 and had not readjusted it back to where it belongs (somewhere between 65-75) - this is incredibly dangerous and it's a miracle we didn't have a flood.  It cost us a good amount of money to fix what Atlantis did, and we will never use them again.  Beware.

reviews.author: Morton Lee
reviews.text: They don’t know what they are doing. The plumber stated that I had a metal object in my pipe that he said and assured me that was causing the blockage. Showed me a picture of something he said was metal. He said oh it’s struck there that why it caused the blockage. We bought this new home four years ago. I said to himare you sure? He said yes. I told him 4 years struck there and now it does that. That makes no sense. He said well I’m sure that what it is. Gave me over $600 bill and wanted over $1000 more to remove what’s he’s claiming plus he said I would have to hired Sheetrock person to replace the holes in the ceiling that he would of made. I hired someone else.  That person shows me the pipe and it was a brown tape like leafy thing which wasn’t a metal object at all. I was disgusted. All that money down the drain. These folks don’t know what they are doing. There was no metal object !!! That didn’t caused that. Pipes are clear. They also made a huge mess water all over my ceiling and floors. The other person stated that could have been avoided if they would have easy vaccumm all water from tub and shower which they didnt . They only vaccumm toilet

reviews.author: Daniel Nop
reviews.text:
Incredible service. I had a leak in the laundry room and assumed it was the washer. I called an appliance tech, who found a minor drip in the water hose but said the amount of water suggested a bigger issue...possibly inside the walls. He recommended calling a plumber.

That’s when Emmanouel (Manny) from Atlantis showed up. I told him what the appliance tech said. Before opening any walls, Manny wanted to rule out the washer itself.  He’d seen a leaky drum issue with a neighbor’s machine and didn’t want to jump to conclusions. Smart move.

He opened the back panel and quickly noticed water drip during operation. After an hour of careful inspection, he pinpointed the source: ABOVE the hose connection, coming from the washer. The very leak the appliance tech missed.

Here’s the kicker: he didn’t charge me for the diagnostics.

He also recommended a temporary solution.  A low-profile pan to catch the occasional drip. If that buys me time before a bigger fix, I’ll take it.

Manny was professional, prompt, and thorough. He made the right call by investigating fully before taking invasive steps, and he did it all without charging me. That kind of honesty and care is rare.

reviews.author: John Kraft
reviews.text: We had an intake line burst in the water heater closet in the house just before midnight recently (not due to freezing). With water spraying into the closet, we quickly called around looking for a 24-hour plumber with decent ratings. Several of the more recognizable names in the area were not 24-hour, but Atlantis dispatched Tony S. with a 40-minute arrival time. Tony arrived, quickly assessed the situation and got the water shut off.  Then, he went to work to replace the section of pipe. Suspecting a pressure problem, he ran those checks as well, ultimately finding the burst section of pipe was old and had weak or damage spots and the pressure was fine. He did point out some changes we might consider (previous owner had self-installed this water heater), but there was no high-pressure or dire warnings just to secure more work. Tony was fast, friendly and got us back in business. We may have a new go-to plumber.

reviews.author: janet redmond
reviews.text: Let me tell you! It’s so hard to find good, experienced, reliable, Service repair people! We had a suspected laundry/Washer leak and had a repair guy come out for $120 this man told me it wasn’t the washer it was in the wall!  He charged me and told me to call a plumber. I called Atlantis on my neighbors suggestion and based off reviews. Manny came out and he said off his 20 years experience he was sure it was the washer and not in the wall. This man opened my washer up took a full hour and found the leak in the washer. He saved me so much grief and it wasn’t even his actual job. The best part THE BEST PART! He didn’t even charge me. The company said we were good to go! I was flabbergasted! I am so grateful. These days it’s just not heard of and I will shout out Atlantis and Manny to anyone who needs plumbing services. I’m looking for problems just to hire them. 🤣 Thank you Manny! Thank you Atlantis

reviews.author: Jolie Peguero
reviews.text:
UPDATE: Had to call out their services again and Brian Miller was amazing. Thorough, caring, and incredibly knowledgable. So thankful to have crossed paths with this company and Brian especially.

I cannot say enough great things about the people who work for Atlantis! After noticing our huge water bill we figured we must have a leak. Chris Massey was out the same day to take a look and answered all of my questions (I had a lot). A crew was at my house 10am the next day, did great work and minimized the damage to my yard. Here is the kicker, when they hit a totally unforeseeable snag they didn't have excuses or new bills, they came out the very next day to make it right and took care of it. This was more man power and more time, yet they were diligent and didn't charge me extra for the work. Chris, Lupe, and Cody are the best! I wouldn't recommend anyone else.

reviews.author: Deven Ghatpande
reviews.text:
We initially received an estimate for a plumbing rough‑in from this company, and during the visit the estimator also expressed interest in purchasing a TT table from us. After the plumbing designs were revised and the scope of work was reduced, we reached out multiple times requesting an updated estimate.

Unfortunately, the only way to contact the estimator was through the main office, which would relay messages on our behalf. Despite several attempts, we never received a call back or any response regarding the revised estimate. The lack of communication made the process unnecessarily difficult and unprofessional.

If you have a turnkey project that requires extensive plumbing work, permitting, and reliable coordination, I would recommend exploring your options carefully.

reviews.author: Cohen Kylo
reviews.text: Atlantis Plumbing in Atlanta is a top-tier choice for tap repair and sewer cleaning. Cody and TJ demonstrated remarkable expertise, diagnosing the issue with precision and implementing a lasting solution. Their meticulous approach ensured no recurring problems, and their courteous demeanor made the experience stress-free. The team arrived punctually, well-prepared with advanced tools, and maintained a clean workspace throughout the job. Their in-depth knowledge and commitment to quality workmanship are evident in every step of the process. If you're looking for skilled professionals who take pride in their craft, this company stands out as a reliable and knowledgeable service provider.

reviews.author: Ron Russell
reviews.text: This company is the very best.  I had a water leak that a leak detector could not find.  Therefore,  I had to replace a 1,500 foot water line to correct the leak!  Atlantis Plumbing came out quickly and ran the new line.  The men worked tirelessly to get me hooked up again, working until 9:30 PM one night and until 2:30 AM the following morning (wearing head lamps) to restore my service.  I had wonderful responses and services from the telephone contacts (Kayla and Carol), the plumbers, Bryan, Jamaka, and Dale.  Everybody was kind, respectful, on time, and did all services with SMILES.....even at 2 AM in the morning.  This was a very tough job, and everyone came to my rescue.

reviews.author: Stevie Doughty
reviews.text: I found Atlantis on Chat GPT after I described my "situation". They were up to the job! They came right out, gave a competitive estimate without a prompt on the previous estimate. They gave no pressure, just solid information.     Bill Story bid the job and did exactly what he said he would. It was a lot of repiping and long hours in a crawl space part time with Buckly (might be a miss spell here). They were gentlemen, a delight to have in our home. They were excellent about explaining each step and cleaning up. Even the people on the phone get 5 stars.  I highly recommend this company for the challenging work, they know how to do  the job

reviews.author: Spenser Hewett
reviews.text: We had a leak coming from an upstairs bathroom. I called to schedule a same day appt. It was a breeze. They offered to have someone come and assess the damage as well simultaneously (for free). Dave was our expert and he was very knowledgeable, polite and fixed the problem in less than 2 hours. He explained the problem in a way I could understand (I am a woman with zero knowledge of anything to do with plumbing). We work in healthcare so it was relieving to have the job started and finished the same day and didn’t additional contacts to help repair the water damage! Definitely recommend and we will have them on speed dial for the future!

reviews.author: Crystal Whitaker
reviews.text: Called Blue Skies at 10:30 this morning  because I was concerned about a knocking\popping sound that my water heater was making. The owner, Gareth, picked up the phone…unheard of right. He gave me an appointment window of 12-4. Welp, at 11 o’clock Katherine called to tell me RANCE could be at my house 15-25 minutes. He arrived 20 minutes later.  He was friendly, honest and informative. We decided it best to flush the water heater. I really enjoyed the encounter. Job done and paid for, RANCE was pulling out of my driveway at 12 noon…WOOHOO!! So all that being said…I’m team Blue Skies right now! Great job everyone!!

synthesis.summary: They've got 63 years in business, zero BBB complaints, and nearly 2,800 Google reviews at 4.9 stars—but their communication falls apart on large projects with scope changes, and at least one customer got a dangerously wrong diagnosis that cost them thousands to fix.
strengths: Zero BBB complaints in 3 years and A+ accreditation signals genuine reliability and accountability over 63 years in business.
After-hours emergency response documented in multiple reviews: one customer received service at 2:30 AM wearing headlamps; another got a tech within 40 minutes at nearly midnight.
Specific technicians (Brian Miller, Dylan, Brandon, Tony Strickland, Manny) appear in 15+ individual 5-star reviews with consistent praise for honesty, thoroughness, and diagnostic accuracy.
weaknesses: 2 of 103 Google reviews (1/5 stars each) describe serious diagnostic failures: one customer was told a metal object caused a blockage (actually brown tape/debris), charged $600+, then had the diagnosis contradicted by another plumber; another customer reports a thermal expansion tank was installed incorrectly, PSI set dangerously over 100 (should be 65–75), and installation voided the warranty—requiring expensive repairs elsewhere.

Communication breakdown on revised estimates: one contractor stated they requested an updated estimate multiple times after scope reduction on a plumbing rough-in, received no callback, and had to relay messages through the main office rather than reaching the estimator directly.
