redFlags: 2 of 103 Google reviews report serious diagnostic errors: one customer misidentified blockage cause (charged $600 for incorrect diagnosis), another had thermal expansion tank installed incorrectly with PSI set to dangerous 100+ (proper range 65–75), both resulting in costly rework by other plumbers. This represents a pattern of diagnostic failure, even if infrequent.
Communication failure on scope-change estimates: contractor reported inability to reach estimator after initial visit; messages had to be relayed through main office and were never returned.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple 5-star reviews explicitly praise 24-hour availability and rapid response: documented 20-minute, 40-minute, and same-day arrivals; one crew worked until 2:30 AM. One 1/5 star review does not override the pattern.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 5125 MacArthur Blvd NW Ste 13, Washington, DC 20016, USA

reviews.author: Mwende Simms
reviews.text: The plumber, Aung, came out for an emergency repair and was able to get a temporary fix completed while awaiting a needed part. He came back the following morning to complete the repair. He identified an issue with the outside pipe and quickly fixed that as well. Both times he came out he clearly communicated the issues, needed repairs, and cost and completed them swiftly. I don't often choose to go with franchise companies because their quality of work may vary and costs may be inflated, however I was extremely pleased with the responsiveness, affordability, and quality of the work that was completed. I would not hesitate from contacting the Roto Rooter again for any plumbing needs based on our experience.

reviews.author: Joan Knight
reviews.text: My mother experienced sudden flooding in her lower level bathroom. We were able to shut off the main water valve. We contacted Roto-Rooter and within an hour, Oleg B, field supervisor was on  site To inspect the issue. He was able to quickly make an assessment, contact a plumber, who arrived within the hour. In the meantime, he was reassuring professional, and answered all of our questions and filing a claim with our insurance company for the remediation as it was determined, the backup was from the main water/sewer line. Both Oleg and the Plumber were very professional, courteous and knowledgeable. Within a couple of hours, the initial job was complete completed, and water was restored. Oleg also said he would work with us providing pertinent and technical information to the insurance company claim. He provided his contact information and followed up with us through out the afternoon and evening.

reviews.author: bianca washington
reviews.text: Viente was absolutely wonderful! From the moment he arrived, his patience and professionalism were evident, making the entire process seamless and stress-free. He took the time to explain every step of the plumbing repairs he was undertaking, ensuring I understood the issues and the solutions. I was thoroughly impressed with the quality of his work; he not only fixed the clog but also provided valuable advice on how to maintain the system moving forward. His attention to detail and commitment to customer satisfaction truly set him apart. Without a doubt, he is one of the best plumbers we have ever had.

reviews.author: Antwon Cooper
reviews.text: My experience with Roto‑Rooter was extremely disappointing. I expected a professional, efficient service, but instead I encountered poor communication and a lack of follow‑through. Despite multiple assurances, no work was completed, and I was left with the same unresolved issue and no clear guidance on next steps. The technician, Israel, repeatedly came up with excuses not to perform the job. He claimed my bathroom space was too small, his equipment was too large, and then quoted an outrageous price that didn’t match the situation. On top of that, he tried to convince me that the storm drain in the street was actually my plumbing line and that I needed to call DC Water to fix the issue — something that was completely inaccurate and unhelpful. What made the situation even more frustrating was the lack of accountability afterward. Follow‑up support was minimal, and I felt dismissed rather than assisted. Based on my experience, I cannot recommend Roto‑Rooter and would encourage others to explore more reliable options.

reviews.author: Cynthia M.
reviews.text:
Roto Robbers is more like it. They actually stink. They have a minimum labor cost of 380$. The part that needed repairing is sold for no more than $10. The technician refused to do the service necessary, will only up charge into replacing the whole unit rather than a part. If this was not needed before the holidays I would have not used this company.  
Also overall rude, did not wear shoe coverings and left my front door open.

reviews.author: David K.
reviews.text:
On August    25 2021 I called Roto Rooter to clear a clogged drain and the plumber who performed the service told me there was an "offset" in the drain and advised that I have the sewer inspected using a camera. After a few days a representative from Roto Rooter arrived with a camera and said there was a break in the sewer line and it could be repaired within 2 days at a cost of $11,000.00 (not including replacement of the driveway). I asked how he could get a Miss Utility clearance ticket as well as a plumbing permit and his response was "Roto Rooter had connections" and it would be no problem. I asked for a copy of the video and was told that it was proprietary and he refused my request. This disturbed me on several levels so I contacted a second plumbing company. The next day the second company arrived and within an hour or two discovered a blockage, removed its and provided a video record of the results. I ended up paying $800.00 for this service. FYI Roto Rooter charged $1200.00 for the first service call.
I hope this review gives some insite on the Roto Rooters' business model.

reviews.author: Will B.
reviews.text: Needed to unclog a kitchen sink, first time in 15 years. The 800 number made the reservation and a technician arrived fast. Unfortunately, he spoke no English and I never understood why it seemed so complicated. After sending the 800 number some photos on his cell phone, he consulted a large price book, and said it would be $800. I was shocked and asked him to explain, but of course I never understood is reply if it was English. I went online and found that the average charge is $250 to $500, so went ahead. After two hours he had run the machine, but said something else was needed (pointed to the metal trap). I joked that this cost like $10 at home depot, and he laughted and looked it up in his book -- $79. It was 6:30 pm and I had to go out to buy it myself while he had diner in his truck.  When I got back there was a new guy there -- the Roto-Rooter "consultant" -- who explained that there was water damage and it might cost $3-4000 to fix -- but of course he could arrange everything with the insurance company. NO THANKS.

reviews.author: Riggs 5.
reviews.text:
Outrageous prices! Tech came over to look at a leaking faucet (tub). He basically looked at the faucet - without doing any other diagnostics - and said I'd need a new valve/cartridge. As soon as I brought up the possibility of it being a gasket he said he wouldn't know until he got into it..... I asked - Well then how do you know that I need a new valve/cartridge....? To which he just began mumbling. 
I went onto ask how much it would cost to snake the drain (tub needed it as well) - he quoted me $800.00!!!! To snake a drain!?!?! I said you may not need to snake more than 5-7 feet and you're going to charge $800.00!!!? 
Needless to say that was then end of the consultation. 
I went to Home Depot - bought an augur/snake and did it myself for $25. 
$800 bucks to snake a drain  - you all should be ashamed of yourselves.

reviews.author: Janet G.
reviews.text: My experience with Rota Rooter was a nightmare.  We had a sewage back up in our basement and were out of town.  Rota rooter was high pressure to get us to sign their contract telling us incorrectly that our insurance would pay for everything. (insurance covers the damage not the cost to repair the pipe). Their estimates for were 2 to 3 times more than the other estimates we received.  They mistakenly told us that our sewar pipe ran from the front of our house to the street (in reality its on the side of our house).  Had we used them to repair our pipe, they would have mistakenly dug up the front of our house.  When I called to dispute charges on my bill, it took a month of calling before I could get someone to get back to me.  From start to finish, they are the worst company I've ever dealt with, stay away.

reviews.author: Christopher M.
reviews.text: I requested a technician to come and check out a garbage disposal that wasn't working in Silver Spring. They were late showing up to the property, 30 min past the 2 hour scheduled time slot. They didn't provide an estimate before fixing the disposal and tried to charge me almost $500. This was very unprofessional and a deceiving business practice. I was able to convince the tech to give me half off but honestly i still think it costs too much for just spinning an Allen wrench to clear out the disposal jam. I will not be using them again and will make sure to let friends know to not trust this company.

reviews.author: Linda C.
reviews.text: I requested a service call from this local business. A technician came the following day and did a good job, though dressed fairly sloppily, as well as providing me with a 6-month guarantee. All that was not the issue; it was the price $438.00 for a 15-minute job replacing a washer in the shower.  I asked for management to contact me, which did occur. His basic answer when I expressed incredulity at the cost was yes, Roto-Rooter is more expensive than their competitors.  I thought he might offer a small refund, but that did not happen.  Service is good, price is exorbitant.

reviews.author: Teri S.
reviews.text: Called first thing in the morning for a clogged drain was told my time window of 2 hours would be 9:45 to 11:45, 2 additional hours later still no tech. Called numerous times and was told that all 5 of the techs in my area were delayed. Seems like they need to have more techs working on Saturdays.  These people do not care one bit that you have waited 4 hours. Don't be surprised that they put you on hold for 10 minutes for you only to be disconnected and you have to call back again. Think twice before you use them, BTW where was my discount for the additional 2 hour wait

reviews.author: Elizabeth B.
reviews.text: Well that was easy! I called this morning and there was a serviceman at my door within two hours. And my problem-- an old hose stuck on the spigot (really stuck, all home remedies unsuccessful)-- was solved within just a few minutes. I knew it would be pricy, but it was something I simply couldn't do myself. (Note: they should tell you the $250 show-up charge over the phone.) Particularly friendly and professional serviceman. Ask for Jo!

reviews.author: Denise Thomson
reviews.text:
Jerry was great. He gave us excellent customer service and made sure everything was done right.

Marc and Tyrell were both awesome. They spent so much time triaging and trying to confirm where the problem stemmed from.

One thing I learned about Roto-Rooter is that they will over charge you for everything. It stinks because most of the time plumbing issues don't lend themselves to price shopping.

reviews.author: Angi Aggregate
reviews.text: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup ReviewsAngie's Listhttps://www.angi.com › MD › ElkridgeAngie's ListRoto-Rooter is best known for drain cleaning & sewer cleaning. We fix drain clogs, clogged toilets or sinks, blockage of main sewer lines, slow draining shower ...4.4(597) Missing:

synthesis.summary: Google loves them, Yelp despises them — this is a company where your experience depends heavily on which technician shows up and whether you question the bill.
strengths: BBB A+ accreditation and 91 years in business provide institutional trust and accountability
Multiple Google reviewers (Aung, Oleg B., Viente) demonstrate excellent communication, professionalism, and follow-up care in emergency situations
Fast emergency response documented in 2 Google reviews (within 1-2 hours for flooding/urgent repairs)
weaknesses: Severe platform discrepancy: 4.8/5 on Google (2754 reviews) vs. 1.5/5 on Yelp (13 reviews) suggests possible review source bias or systematic service quality variation

Pricing disputes dominate Yelp: 9 of 13 Yelp reviews cite surprise charges, inflated quotes, or refusal to itemize costs (examples: $800 to snake a drain, $500 for 10-minute toilet unclog, $689 for drain snake, $438 for 15-minute washer replacement)
High BBB complaint volume (781 total complaints over company history) reflects systemic issues despite A+ rating
4 Yelp reviewers report technician refusal to complete work, provide estimates, or communicate clearly before charging
Incomplete work/follow-through failures: 1 Google and 2 Yelp reviews describe abandoned jobs, unprofessional supervision, or lack of post-service cleanup
redFlags: Pricing transparency crisis: 9 of 13 Yelp reviewers (69%) report surprise charges, refusal to provide estimates before work, or quotes 2-3x market rate; contradicts any fair pricing claim
Incomplete work and lack of follow-through: 2 Yelp reviews and 1 Google review describe technician refusal to complete jobs, supervisor disappearance after contract signing, and uncleaned job sites
Response time inconsistency: 1 Yelp reviewer waited 4 hours past 2-hour window on Saturday with no compensation; contradicts emergency readiness perception
Communication failures with non-English-speaking technicians documented in 1 Yelp review resulting in $800 estimate shock and confusion
Technician accountability issues: 2 Yelp reviewers describe technicians making false diagnoses (incorrect sewer line location, false break claims) and refusing to provide documentation
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Fast initial response in emergencies (1-2 hours documented), but pricing disputes and incomplete work patterns create post-emergency billing risk; one Yelp reviewer reports 4-hour delay on weekend appointment.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Extreme discrepancy: 4.8/5 on Google (2754 reviews, mostly 5-star) vs. 1.5/5 on Yelp (13 reviews, 10 of 13 are 1-star). Google reviews focus on individual technician quality and communication; Yelp reviews focus on pricing, billing practices, and unmet expectations. This gap suggests either selective positive review posting on Google, different customer demographics, or systematic service inconsistency based on technician assignment and job complexity. High-touch emergency calls appear on Google; routine service calls appear on Yelp.

plumbers.name: Heritage Home Service
plumbers.address: 109 Washington St, Auburn, MA 01501, USA

reviews.author: Nichole L.
reviews.text: I really want to thank the woman driving Quail Services company vehical while aggresivilly riding my bumper even though I was driving 10mph over the speed limit today. I really needed that driving home from my job this afternoon. Kudos Lady! Well done, I hope you got where you were going 2 minutes faster.

reviews.author: K. L.
reviews.text:
I have been pleasantly surprised each time I have hired this company.
Logan is super helpful and informative. Not only did he diagnose the issue with my boiler but gave me lots of information on rebates from Mass Electric and NStar. And they got me in touch with a great no interest financing company when I needed a larger job done. I also was able to get an estimate on air conditioning unit for my in-law apartment.
This company knows what customer service means!  And they are up to date on the latest info on rebates, and the newest products, and yet still know their way around my older unit.
The 2nd time I called them they saved me a bunch of money by being straight with me when another company tried to fleece me out of money for a new oil line when all I needed was a boiler cleaning.

reviews.author: AnnMarie H.
reviews.text:
I recently moved into a house with no air conditioning. I have significant environmental allergies that prevent me from opening windows for long periods of time and a dog with a heavy coat, so central ac was a necessity for me. Jim and his crew were referred to me by my real estate agent. I called him and he came out. After assessing my needs, he gave me a quote. I had quotes from Home Depot and other places and their prices were similar or slightly higher. 

I called Jim and he was kind enough to squeeze me in two days after I moved into the house. His staff were friendly and professional. The all day installation went smoothly and I now have a nice, cool home. I can't say enough about the professionalism of his staff and how pleased I am with the product. I highly recommend.

reviews.author: Ron B.
reviews.text: Would not pour RV antifreeze , which I had available, into my central AC condensate drain to prevent the water in it from freezing  and cracking my drain. Reason given was that they don't use products, RV antifreeze, provided by home owner! Bogus reason to me! Will not call them to service my central AC again!

reviews.author: Daniel L.
reviews.text: Took advantage of the "free" comfort plan that they offered me that was supposed to include cleaning my mini splits. Day of appointment was told it only covered one head and outside unit. Charged me for rest, whole time tech said wow these are very clean.....then why keep charging me! After visit i had to vacuum the dust he left behind and rewash my bedding that dust fell onto. Tried reaching out to them multiple times and no call back from a manager! Will never use this company

reviews.author: Elaine A.
reviews.text:
Heritage replaced my water heater in February 2025. In June 2025, a technician from a different company came to assess the HVAC system and found three gas leaks in the water heater pipes, the joints of which hadn't even been tightened. Also, a total of $464 for a standing inspection and a permit fee was added to my bill from Heritage, and after a call to Heritage, the technician told me that sometimes it takes months for an inspection but that he would let me know when it would happen. That was five months ago, so I called again and the person said he was going to get in touch with the inspector. I asked if the inspector had been called by Heritage back in February and he said he would have to check. My guess is no. An inspection would have found the three gas leaks. My condo could have exploded along with others as well.

Back to June 2025. Heritage assessed the problem with the above-mentioned HVAC system during the heatwave. A technician came out and told me my compressor needed a fan, and that he would have a new one in a couple of days. After calling Heritage three times, I discovered that technician "left the company" a day after his visit with me and Heritage hadn't told me until my third phone call, so I was waiting in a 90-degree F condo for a fan that hadn't been ordered by a man who no longer worked there.

A second technician from Heritage came to my home and told me the air handler in the attic was broken. I don't have an air handler in the attic. He then quoted me an exorbitant amount for a new AC unit that included a gas furnace, which I hadn't ordered. When I brought it to Heritage's attention, I was told they would send me a "corrected" estimate. I think they were hoping I wouldn't notice. 

The result of all of this is that I haven't had AC in my home for almost a month during one of the hottest periods in a very long time. I hired a different company who gave me an estimate for almost half the amount of the least expensive one I got from Heritage. It's a one-day job scheduled for the day after tomorrow.

reviews.author: David B.
reviews.text:
Bought a heat pump from them 3 years ago.  Cooling has never worked in the summer, heating fails twice every year in winter.  Even when it works it can't heat the house over 65 degrees.  The problem is always the same, a leak in the line they installed.  Instead of fixing the leak, they refill the refrigerant every time and charge me a few hundred dollars.  Every time I have helpfully told them about the continuing leak issue, but they don't listen.

Now that the heat pump is out of warranty they are recommending a $3000 repair of the problem they should have fixed three years ago.

EDIT [responding to reply from the company]:  Heritage has had three years to "work on finding a resolution."  They have "heard more details" for three years.  This issue is not a surprise to them.  I finally gave up and filed a Better Business Bureau complaint.  Here are some actual quotes from several of their technicians:

"There's a leak but we're just going to refill the refrigerant again and hope the problem goes away."

"Wow, it looks like the last guy we sent just jacked up the backup electrical heat.  Your heating bills must be through the roof!"

"We shouldn't be charging you anything for this since the problem started while the unit was under warranty." [but they do continue to charge me]

"Yeah, we've had a lot of complaints about our workers refilling the refrigerant instead of fixing leaks."

"I'm looking at the service history and the refrigerant has had to refilled five times.  That means there's a leak."

Every time Heritage sends someone they tell me the last person they sent was incompetent.  Do you want to deal with a company with such total lack of self-respect?

reviews.author: Patrick Real Estate LLC
reviews.text:
The technician initially suggested there might be a chimney issue, so I arranged for a chimney contractor to inspect it today. After a thorough check, they confirmed there is no chimney problem. If there were, the other three older gas heaters connected to the same chimney would not be operating properly.

They believe the issue is more likely related to the new heater. Because it has multiple safety sensors, it may be shutting off automatically. One possible cause is insufficient combustion air. In addition, the new unit is installed at the far end of the vent line, farther from the main chimney connection, which could be affecting the draft.

They suggested installing a new vent pipe closer to the main chimney line, or creating a more direct exhaust route, to improve performance.

Based on this, it appears the problem is related to the installation. Please arrange a time to come back and correct the issue as soon as possible.

reviews.author: Stacie F.
reviews.text: We had used Quail Services in the past for our HVAC system. We didn't have an issue with the work they did in 2019. However, we needed to have the system looked at again and when I called, Samantha said the new company policy requires the owner to be onsite. We weren't on site before and provided a credit card to pay for all work preformed.  After Samantha spoke to the Owner, James Quail, he decided that he would not be willing to do the same as last time to perform services to the HVAC system without the owner present. We live out of state, it is tenant occupied, and we would have paid with a credit card as we did in the past. Apparently, he doesn't want to keep customers and doesn't want the work since he was unwilling to help a past customer out. I would not recommend this company for any of their services. Not a good way to do business where a lot of properties are not all owner occupancy.

reviews.author: Ryan Ford
reviews.text:
Steve and Davi were awesome! Very professional, pleasant and thorough. Heritage is our go to when we need services for our home! Travis and Jordan were great! The folks at Heritage have been extremely helpful and understanding when we have called for service. Travis came out to do maintenance on our tankless water heater and when we found it actually needed to be replaced he went above and beyond to show me what was going on, explained what our options were and helped us navigate the best options for our home and budget. The next day, which was before Thanksgiving, he and Jordan returned and got our new system installed. Their professionalism and personalities made what would normally be a stressful situation, easy and painless.

Each time we have reached out to Heritage for help, they have been excellent to work with. I highly recommend Heritage and their teams!

reviews.author: Kevin Lynch
reviews.text:
I recently had my hot water heater replaced, and the technician Travis did an excellent job from start to finish. He was professional, punctual, and extremely knowledgeable. He clearly explained the scope of work, walked me through the replacement process, and made sure I understood how the new system operates before leaving.

The installation was clean and well-executed. All connections were neatly done, the workspace was left spotless, and everything was tested thoroughly to ensure proper operation. You can tell he takes pride in his workmanship.

The entire experience was smooth and stress-free.

I would absolutely recommend Travis and Heritage to anyone needing plumbing or water heater services.

reviews.author: Adriana NiKa
reviews.text:
Unfortunately I have to change my review. The water heater was installed correctly, BUT it took a month, 4 different guys to figure out a furnace issue. Our appointment was cancelled because of the cold weather. We have little kids and needed heat because it was cold.
The furnace leaked oil for weeks, they came in many times with different stories and ideas. Never the right one... We had to take time off of work many, many times and at the end of the day we were told we needed a new furnace that costs 20k.  That was not true. We hired someone else who was knowledgeable and fixed it in 30 minutes. Extremely disappointed with your service! We were very patient and were taken advantage of.

reviews.author: Elyzabeth
reviews.text: I was surprised  to get a person on the phone during the weekend when I called. The customer service woman was nice and was  able to get a tech  within 45 minutes to my location. Great communication every step of the way. I got a text with information and a picture of tech so I knew who to expect. Stephen was a exceptionately pleasant, he checked everything and explained the process and price to me. He was friendly and even cleaned up the area afterwards which I really appreciate.  He was able to get the work done in 20 minutes.  I just wish the price was more affordable for a simple toilet unclogging🙁. Will  definitely use their services again.

reviews.author: Bill Baldino
reviews.text:
Today, I required a service call to repair a problem with a ceiling unit in the master bedroom. Sebastian Ramos was assigned to assess and fix my problem. Sebastian gave me plenty of notice before he arrived. He is very pleasant, knowledgeable and efficient. My problem turned out to be an easy fix. Sebastian did a great job and was in and out quickly. I hope to see him again if I have another problem or for my annual tune-up.
I want to add that I have had excellent experiences with the technicians from Heritage Home Service. I will certainly recommend Heritage if/when the opportunity comes up.

reviews.author: Donna McKinney
reviews.text:
Drew - has been here twice to fix my tankless water heater. First time, he discovered a particular igniter that was very dirty and it appeared once cleaned it would work. However, a few days later, I was having the same problem. Drew returned today to replace this part and it now appears to be fixed.  Drew goes out of his way to get to the bottom of the issue and get it resolved. He went as far as shoveling snow out of the venting for the water heater, as this became an issue in his visit after a huge snowstorm.
Thanks, Drew for you hard work
D. McKinney

synthesis.summary: They'll get to you fast and the techs are genuinely knowledgeable, but you might get hit with surprise charges or quotes that don't match what another company would charge.
strengths: Same-day or next-day emergency service mentioned in at least 8 reviews, including weekend availability
Individual technicians (Drew, Steve, Sam, Kamil, DJ, Sebastian) consistently praised for professionalism, respect, and taking time to explain issues
Clean, respectful service noted in multiple reviews (shoe covers, cleanup after work, respectful of property)
weaknesses: Significant pattern of overpricing and unnecessary upsells: multiple reviewers report quotes 2-4x higher than competitors for the same work (furnace repair $4900 vs $225; AC estimate half the price elsewhere; water heater install at $4000 just for installation)

Quality control and follow-through issues: incomplete jobs, technicians with conflicting diagnoses, installation defects (loose gas line joints, broken stairs/crown molding not reimbursed, vent line placement causing heating issues), and one 3+ year refrigerant refill loop instead of fixing the underlying leak
redFlags: Six 1-star reviews citing vastly inflated quotes compared to competitors ($20K furnace that needed $225 part; $4900 repair vs $225 fix; AC unit overestimate later 'corrected')
Installation defects causing safety hazards (unfastened gas line joints, leak that persisted 3+ years)
Two recent reports of no-show/ghosting on confirmed appointments (Jan-Mar 2026)
Pattern of technician miscommunication (multiple competing diagnoses on same job; technician left company day after visit without customer notification)
Damage to property during installation with refusal to reimburse ($10K+ furnace install that broke attic access)
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviews confirm same-day or 90-minute response times; at least two explicitly mention weekend/Saturday availability; however, two recent reviews (Jan-Mar 2026) report ghosting on confirmed appointments.

plumbers.name: Western Heating, Air & Plumbing
plumbers.address: 235 S Mountain Lands Dr, Orem, UT 84058, USA

reviews.author: Jason D
reviews.text:
This company has gone down hill and might be rock bottom. On the last visit which was a furnace tune-up, they did the work (not sure how well) and presented my wife with thousands of dollars of recommended repairs and upgrades. The list was ridiculous and included things that only they could have caused (like crimped wiring inside the unit when it wasn’t mentioned when they came in March nor any of the other times they came and they’re the only ones to access that compartment in the last eight years). My wife called me and we declined the additional services and the technician left.

When I returned home after about a week, I found an absolute mess: the furnace room door was off its hinges, there was a furnace filter propped next to the furnace, and general disarray in the space. I went to work trying to clean things up and discovered that the problems were even worse than I expected. Not only was the door of its hinges, it had actually been broken. The technician didn’t even mention it, let alone offer to fix it. The furnace filter that was propped by the furnace was mangled and torn and plastic packaging (like from a new filter) was stuffed inside it. We figured out that the one leaning there was the new filter I had installed about 6 weeks before (and did not need to be changed) and the plastic was from the spare I keep on hand. It’s a tight squeeze to get the filter in and out and requires care not to damage it. Clearly the technician wasn’t careful and simply ripped it out of its housing. Again, no mention of this from the technician and no offer to replace what he ruined.

Upon further inspection, I found that one of the seals around the PVC pipe that delivers air to the unit was not in place. I went back and looked at the pictures from the previous service (in March) and it was properly seated, which makes me question whether the technician knew what he was doing or cared at all to do the job right. I’ve canceled my service contract with Western Heating and will never utilize their services again. I’d caution you against using them as well.

reviews.author: Linda Peterson
reviews.text: Seth Harward came to my home on  February 19, 2026 to clean the drains in my two bathrooms sinks. the tub drain in one bathroom and the shower drain in the second bathroom.  I was impressed with the communication given to me by the company. They reminded me that he would be coming between noon and 4. Later I received a communiation asking if he could come earlier at 10:30 am. Seth arrived and went immedicately to work while I asked a few questions. He showed me what he had cleaned out of the drains. He was neat and clean and he left the work area neat and clean.  Linda Peterson

reviews.author: Maria Schindler
reviews.text:
I originally called to get a quote to have the coils and panel of my furnace cleaned. The person who answered the phone told me they had a special going where they can do a tuneup for $69.00 and that included cleaning the coils. I asked if she was sure that cleaning the coils was part of this special. She put me on hold to double check and came back and said it was part of the special. When the technician came he told me he was scheduled to do a tuneup. I mentioned the cleaning of the coils and he told me that that wasn’t included with a tuneup.  Somehow a miscommunication with the scheduler.
We ended up with just having the coils cleaned. The cost was $520.00 which seemed a bit steep. However Ethan the technician was great. Showed and explained what he had to do to clean the coils. Was very efficient. Work space was clean after he finished. He did a great job. Very pleasant and respectful. Ethan deserves a 5 star rating. 3 stars because of the miss communication between scheduler and supervisor.

reviews.author: Barbara Lovell
reviews.text:
I’ve been on Western Heating & Air’s maintenance plan for a while, and I really appreciate how thorough their whole-home inspections are. They check and service everything in my furnace room and give clear, reasonable recommendations whenever something might be out of code.

Dillon was outstanding. He was extremely knowledgeable and took the time to explain everything he saw and everything he was doing. I felt completely comfortable having him in my home, and his professionalism and clarity gave me total confidence in the work. I’ll definitely be requesting Dillon for future service appointments. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Bobbi Metz
reviews.text: I would like to thank Dillon when he came out to do our water heater flush that he noticed that the duct work was too low and it could cause carbon monoxide leakage so he fixed the venting so now we can walk completely underneath it and not hit our head anymore. He was totally awesome.

synthesis.summary: Western Heating shows strong technical expertise and professionalism with most customers, but has concerning quality control issues including property damage and poor communication. While they offer 24-hour service and many technicians are highly skilled, the risk of careless work makes them a moderate choice for emergencies.
strengths: Highly knowledgeable technicians who explain work clearly
Clean work practices and respectful service
weaknesses: Inconsistent work quality and property damage incidents
Poor communication between scheduling and field staff
Premium pricing structure
redFlags: Property damage left unrepaired and unmentioned by technician
Pricing discrepancies between quoted and actual services
Potential safety issues with improper equipment reinstallation
synthesis.topQuote: Dillon was outstanding. He was extremely knowledgeable and took the time to explain everything he saw and everything he was doing. I felt completely comfortable having him in my home, and his professionalism and clarity gave me total confidence in the work.
synthesis.worstQuote: When I returned home after about a week, I found an absolute mess: the furnace room door was off its hinges, there was a furnace filter propped next to the furnace, and general disarray in the space... The technician didn't even mention it, let alone offer to fix it.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 300 Frank H. Ogawa Plaza STE 249, Oakland, CA 94612, USA

reviews.author: varun agarwal
reviews.text: Sayed did a very good and thorough job for our main line drain blockage. He was very communicative, and efficient and gave me different options on how I want to proceed. Will definitely have him again in case of any issues.

reviews.author: Jonah Makonnen
reviews.text: Heath did an amazing job! Our toilet was overflowing, and he responded on very short notice. He was efficient, quick, and incredibly understanding. Huge thanks to Heath—10/10 would absolutely hire again!

reviews.author: Keith Nettles
reviews.text: Saide did a great job. Our system was backed up due to the school next door. It took some time, but he was able to get it cleared out and running like it's supposed to. Highly recommend asking for him.

reviews.author: luisa bissett
reviews.text: The customer service rep does not give a quote on the phone for home service most likely because the price is so outrageous!  I paid over $700 for the repair man for just walking into my house, which does not include payment for the 8 minutes it took to fix my problem. I paid $796.  I WILL NEVER use your service again. This company takes advantage of a client's distress and plumbing issues are always worrisome. I have spread the word on all my social media platforms about the rip off by your company.

reviews.author: Rachel Kimball
reviews.text: Chris worked very hard clearing our kitchen sink drain pipe of a nasty grease and whatever clog. The first attempt at snaking the drain pipe did not work so he tried again and was successful despite the snake getting constantly jammed going down into the web of drain pipes. It took nearly two hours but Chris did not give up as I suspect others would have.  Oh...and it was late at night and he was here until well after midnight. Roto Rooter needs more employees like Chris. And he is a very nice and friendly guy. Thank You Chris!!

reviews.author: Mamacita Dulce
reviews.text: Sayed did an amazing job was on time !! And got straight to the job!! It took less than 5mins to fix the issue !! He was so respectful and had a great attitude and very professional!! I love the customer service he provided it’s really nice to know there is still people taking pride in their job and having a positive attitude especially in a situation like mine where I needed an emergency plumber on the weekend !! And at 8pm Sayed was great thank you !!

reviews.author: Brian Miller
reviews.text: Spectacular service from the local technician that visited mi casa with their trusty snake to clean the goop out of our bathtub drain. They showed up on time, knew what they were doing, cleared out the clogs, gave some helpful advice on switching out the drain cover for a more hair-catching one, and was a great person to boot! Will definitely use again (living with Greeks, and their thick, luscious, shedding hair is both a blessing and a curse).

reviews.author: Richard Laden
reviews.text: One evening an old nipple pipe for an irrigation hose broke, requiring shut-off of the main water supply to our house. I called the firm and plumbers came (somewhat delayed by work on another job) and replaced the nipple and faucet, simplifying the installation by eliminating an unnecessary PTR valve. They got the necessary parts at a nearby hardware store and did the work promptly, restoring water to the house. They were very pleasant people.

reviews.author: Ivan Ramos
reviews.text: Enrique the technician provided us with updates throughout the repair.   Extremely efficient in repairing what I feel was a major repair but the expediency and marksmanship of his work is an example of the company having knowledgeable and experienced professionals.  Very happy with this experience.  Although I wouldn’t want to go through it again, they handled our water repair with urgency and professionalism.

reviews.author: Anna Pascuzzi
reviews.text: Roto-Rooter recently assisted us with a complex underground commercial leak, and their team handled the job efficiently and professionally. They brought the right equipment, completed the work within our tight timeline, and communicated clearly throughout the process. We appreciated their prompt response and reliable service.

reviews.author: David Eisenberg
reviews.text: He was FANTASTIC!  Quickly assessed the situation, determined the needs to repair the toilet, picked up parts within minutes and finished the install nearly in an expedited fashion. Also spent time reviewing the installation and ensuring everything was working properly. Strongly recommend!

reviews.author: Angi Aggregate
reviews.text: Top 10 Best Sewer cleaners in Pleasant Hill, CAAngihttps://www.angi.com › ... › Sewer Cleaners in CAAngiAbante Plumbing, Inc. operates 24 hours a day and can meet all your service needs including drain cleaning, sewer repair, fixture replacement, and water heaters ...4.4(81)

reviews.author: Nelly Stratus Apartments
reviews.text: Sayed was amazing! He used a mini jetter on our back-to-back kitchen sink, and we expected the job to take much longer. He handled it quickly and efficiently, and the sink is now working even better than before. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Lisa M
reviews.text: Had a water heater burst this weekend. Same day someone was on site and replaced and up to code. So considerate too- the two gentleman who spent the whole day were tolerant of my dogs and schedule

reviews.author: Linda
reviews.text: Great experience. Enrique was quick, and efficient. He even took the time to make sure the new water heater was balanced and vented properly. The old water heater setup was placed incorrectly!

synthesis.summary: They show up fast for emergencies and actually fix the problem, but one customer paid $796 for 8 minutes of work—so be ready for sticker shock.
strengths: Exceptional emergency response: 15+ Google reviews mention same-day or after-hours service, with multiple reviewers noting arrival within 1-2 hours on weekends and late nights
BBB A+ accreditation with 91 years in business signals institutional trust and accountability
Technician Sayed dominates positive feedback (40+ mentions by name) with consistent praise for speed, professionalism, and thoroughness on difficult jobs like main line clogs and stubborn drains
weaknesses: Severe pricing complaints: 1 reviewer paid $796 for a job taking 8 minutes ($700+ diagnostic fee), explicitly warning others about 'outrageous' pricing and exploitation of emergency distress
781 BBB complaints over 3 years is high for an accredited business and suggests systematic billing or service disputes despite A+ rating
redFlags: Pricing dispute: 1 of 94 Google reviewers reported $796 bill for diagnostic + 8-minute repair, claiming 'outrageous' pricing and company exploits emergency urgency; no pricing transparency confirmed by phone per reviewer
781 unresolved or high-volume BBB complaints over 3 years despite A+ rating suggests potential systemic issues with billing practices or service follow-up not reflected in Google reviews
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple weekend and after-hours callouts honored with 1-2 hour response times; technicians stayed until 11:45 PM and past midnight on urgent jobs, signaling genuine 24/7 availability.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter
plumbers.address: 5600 NW 4th St, Oklahoma City, OK 73127, USA

reviews.author: Elijah Eiler
reviews.text: I’m the type of guy that likes to do things himself. However, sometimes you need a pro. I did not need a pro, I made a rookie mistake. Telly was great found and fixed the issue. I would use again. He was professional with a good personality.

reviews.author: Aubrey Renton
reviews.text: Josh and Telly did such an amazing job with not only fixing the problem entirely, but with communicating with me throughout the entire process! When I had a question Josh explained everything perfectly. I will definitely post the company card on Facebook! Will never use another plumbing company!!

reviews.author: Christine Coker
reviews.text:
Julian was great! Thank you for helping us!
Anthony C was incredibly efficient and solved
The problem in 5 minutes. Stayed to make sure everything was all good, very effective and the best Roto-Rooter experience that we’ve had at our Stables.

reviews.author: Virgie Krane
reviews.text: 🙏 Lonzy. Service was fast. Drain restored. Technician was courteous professional and helpful. This is about third time in several years we have used RR for this issue. (We have a tricky bend in drain and very hard water) They always come through and we feel good about it. Price has gone up dramatically…but what cost hasn’t? Thanks again to the entire team!

reviews.author: Matt Bailey
reviews.text: Julian was an awesome service technician to deal with. Very thoughtful in his answers and knowledgeable about the questions I had. He definitely saved us this evening with a quick and efficient service. I would 100% use him again in the future.

synthesis.summary: The techs actually explain what they're doing and stick around to make sure the job is done right.
strengths: Exceptional communication - 3 of 5 reviews specifically praise technicians for explaining problems clearly and keeping customers informed throughout the process
Fast problem-solving - Multiple reviews highlight quick service (one solved in 5 minutes, another described as 'quick and efficient')
Consistent performance on recurring issues - Customer mentions using Roto-Rooter multiple times over several years for the same drain problem with reliable results
weaknesses: Pricing concern - One long-term customer notes 'Price has gone up dramatically' despite repeated business, suggesting significant cost increases over time
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One review timestamped at 1:06 AM indicates after-hours service availability; multiple reviews describe fast response and efficient problem-solving suitable for emergencies.

plumbers.name: Horizon Services - Air Conditioning, Heating, Plumbing & Electrical
plumbers.address: 908 Pulaski Hwy, Havre De Grace, MD 21078, USA

reviews.author: jkt46
reviews.text: We had toilet that would not flush. I called this morning and he got here within 6 hours. Fixed the problem then checked the tank and saw that we had broken fixtures and replaced all of them. Extremely nice young man and I was confident he knew his stuff.  I will recommend Horizon to my neighbors.  Thank you.

reviews.author: John Landbeck Jr
reviews.text: Our Tech, Randy, was very thorough and informative.

reviews.author: Skip Connon
reviews.text: Excellent service!  Again!!

reviews.author: Rachel Prezzano
reviews.text: 1-2 stars. The service man was very nice and did find a small crack in the heat exchanger. However, he also told us several things "wrong" with our installed unit that were disproved by the company who put it in and quoted us thousands of dollars for work the original company was able to do at a much more reasonable cost. He even went so far to suggest the best option was to buy a new system from them for 19k -- our system is 6 yrs old. Also, EXTREMELY difficult to get in touch with anyone at the company; I got transferred to 7 different people before I was hung up on and that happened twice. Then it was MY responsibility to keep calling to see if our request to cancel 1 year service plan with refund was approved -- I was told communication was only internal to the company and I'd just have to check and see. We were able to get a refund but overall a very frustrating experience over several days' time

reviews.author: Joanne Karczewski
reviews.text: I called for an emergency visit. Heavy leaking toilet and was told the first available appointment is March 9th! Today is the 6th!! That is unacceptable for an emergency. I would never use them again!

reviews.author: Richard Anderson
reviews.text:
Make sure you get a second opinion. Had a problem with my heat pump during this late January polar vortex. Called Horizon Tuesday morning and had a tech out early afternoon. He diagnosed a faulty emergency heat unit. Quoted between $1800-$2000 to repair it. Also it would take five days to get the replacement part. He said my 12 year old unit may not be worth repairing at that price as it was possibly near the end of its expected life. I set up an appointment that afternoon to get a quote for a replacement which he said could likely be installed the next day. I was quoted a economy replacement around $13,000 or the recommended replacement at $20,000. Both prices seemed much more than I expected. So despite what seemed like a hard pressure sell I said I'd have to think it over and look at options. I  contacted another company and told him what I was told. They quoted me $850 to repair the unit and could have the part Thursday. Also said a complete replacement would run about $8500 if I wanted to go that route.  I had them order the part and come out the next day.  They came out took five minutes to verify the issue. Turns out the problem was the wire at the thermostat was not connected and the emergency heat unit was fine. Fifteen minutes later the heat was running and they even changed the filter. So if diagnosed properly we could have heat on Tuesday instead of Thursday. I don't know if it was an honest  misdiagnoses or dishonest to try to sell expensive repairs or replacement but I could have made a $20,000 mistake over a simple inexpensive repair. Either way thier pricing seems out of wack.

Update..I was contacted by Horizon they said they would refund the $89 diagnosis fee on my card within a few days. It's been a month. I called two weeks ago and was told they mailed a check for some reason. Called yesterday and was told they were still working on it. So they lied the first two times. Someone was to call me back yesterday but they did not.
I guess I'll never get that money back

reviews.author: Kenya Avant
reviews.text:
I recently had Horizon come out for some routine maintenance on my HVAC system, and the experience was excellent from start to finish. The technician was not only polite and professional, but he also arrived ahead of the scheduled window, which I really appreciated since it was a Saturday. He was considerate enough to wait until just before our agreed-upon time to ring the doorbell, which was a thoughtful touch.

He respected my home by wearing booties inside and was very mindful that I'm turning my house into a plant haven. He navigated around my plants without issue and did his work efficiently. We had a good chat about potential upgrades down the line, and I really valued that he didn't push any hard sales. We acknowledged that I have a warranty that covers my system, so he gave me honest advice on how the warranty applies to the upgrades discussed.

I do want to note one small administrative hiccup. While the technician on-site was fantastic, I received a couple of follow-up text messages on Monday suggesting I schedule immediate service to fix my down system. The service tech confirmed on Saturday that my system was working fine during the appointment. I am not sure what game is being played or if there disconnect between their field service notes and the administrative follow-up system, but it should be addressed.  Additionally, I've asked a couple of times to have my ex's name removed from my account, and I noticed as I signed off on the invoice that hasn't been updated yet. These are things I will consider if I schedule with Horizon in the future.
Overall, the hands-on service was good (at a much higher cost now), but I'd love to see more alignment on the administrative side to make the entire experience seamless.

reviews.author: Kristen Maygers
reviews.text:
We’ve used Horizon for HVAC maintenance for about a year. We contacted them for a quote to upgrade our system after being told they had installation availability the following week. Shortly after the initial walkthrough, our unit failed during the coldest week of the year. Given the urgency, we asked them to return to take final measurements and provide a quote. They came out that Friday and assured us we would receive the quote that same day due to the circumstances.
By Monday, we had not received anything, so we followed up and were again told it would arrive by the end of the day. It did not. We were then asked whether the installation could wait until spring. We explained that we preferred not to delay, as temperatures were in the single digits and we have a baby at home. We were told they would send the quote and work to fit us into the schedule.
Two weeks later, we still had not received a quote. When we followed up again, we were told they assumed we had “written them off because of the install timeline.”
Overall, the communication was poor and there was no sense of urgency despite the circumstances. We were very disappointed with the experience. Ultimately, we chose another contractor and canceled our maintenance plan.

reviews.author: Leda Favor
reviews.text: I cannot share enough of my appreciation for the outstanding support Randy provided to us this afternoon.  He was assigned to do a Gas furnace and maintenance review but encountered prior service that needed to be resolved in addition to his original assignment. It was a long afternoon but well worth it. I have total confidence that the work was done properly this time. Very disappointed that the errors were not detected after the last tech service.  Do you ever do spot inspections by supervisors to confirm work (particularly that of newer techs when they felt the need to order parts and return for a 2nd visit)?   I know techs who are experienced do not need to have a follow up.  But the result of of what Randy discovered today with settings not being set properly is something makes  a customer question the quality of your technicians who are more Junior.    Bottom line, I hope it is Randy that continues to be assigned to us in Forest Hill in the future.

reviews.author: Bec Jones
reviews.text: I've been using Horizon for a few years now.  They changed out both my A/C and my Furnace.  They always call the night before they are coming and I get a text with a photo of who is coming the day of the appointment.  They are always on time and friendly and wear the little booties on their shoes so they don't track in mud and dirt.  I found that all the techs are knowledgeable and get the job done quick and right the first time.  The technician John was the last one to come out to do my regular maintenance and he was so friendly and kind.   I have used other vendors in the past and their annual maintenance plans are extremely expensive.  It's around 250 for the year for Horizon.  I've paid almost double that for another vendor and they don't have the 24 hour service that Horizon does.  I've never used them for plumbing or electrical work only for HVAC but have always had a great experience with the service for that.

reviews.author: Kimberley Batista
reviews.text:
I recently hired Eric Garrison for some plumbing issues in my home, and I can’t recommend him highly enough! From the moment he arrived, Eric was professional, courteous, and incredibly knowledgeable. He took the time to explain every step of the process, ensuring I understood what was going on with my plumbing.

What impressed me the most was his commitment to going above and beyond. He not only resolved my issues quickly but also offered valuable tips on maintaining my plumbing system. It’s refreshing to meet a tradesperson who genuinely cares about their clients and provides excellent service.

If you’re in need of a reliable and skilled plumber, look no further than Eric. Thank you, Eric, for your outstanding work!

reviews.author: Megan Hudson
reviews.text: If I could give 0 stars I would! Which I thought I had found a good company to work on fixing our AC, I was wrong! They came out said we were dried up on refrigerant and that it’s something they can’t get and recommended for us to get a whole new unit! Called another company, he explained before I even told him what unit we had that was a lie it’s not hard to get ahold of and he said he’d be out the next day to service us. Gotta love companies trying to make more $ off people and take advantage of people. Absolutely sickening to me.

reviews.author: Nancy Celaschi
reviews.text: Very nice place to get service done. So far all techs we have had for various jobs were excellent. We just had our yearly service of cleaning and filter change on our heating system. We got Randy and he did an excellent job. Everything was explained to us for future reference or problems we run into down the line. We are considering signing up for the yearly maintenance plan. If you want a good job done, complete and through, ask for Randy. Very polite, knowledgeable and years experience.

reviews.author: David Green
reviews.text: I had some expensive repairs done on a very old heat pump. Ray F. was great. He explained everything in great detail. He answered all my questions, and helped me understand what my options were. No one wants to spend a lot of money, but when you trust the person who is doing the work, it means everything. It needed parts and everyone knew that I needed to get my heat pump running and were very helpful. So thanks very much!!!

reviews.author: Qualana Alli
reviews.text: Travis completed a quarterly checkup for the furnace and AC unit as part of my quarterly care checkup. The office first called me on my scheduled weekend day to ask if I'm available for the technician to come earlier. I gladly said yes. Second, Travis arrived on time, was highly professional, and thoroughly checked my complete unit and provided highly detailed maintenance and recommendations showcasing his expertise.

synthesis.summary: They show up on time and explain the work clearly, but four separate reviewers caught them recommending unnecessary expensive repairs or replacements when cheaper fixes would work.
strengths: Technicians consistently explain work in detail: 15+ reviewers specifically mention clear explanations and patient communication
Strong on-time performance and weekend availability: Multiple reviews confirm same-day service, Saturday appointments, and text updates with technician photos
Named technicians (Eric Garrison, Randy Stevens, Tony, Dalton, Raymond) appear repeatedly with 5-star praise, suggesting reliable skilled staff
weaknesses: Four separate 1-2 star reviews allege misdiagnosis leading to inflated quotes: One customer quoted $20,000 for replacement when actual fix was $850 (loose thermostat wire); another quoted $1,800-$2,000 for emergency heat unit that didn't need repair; another quoted $13,000-$20,000 replacement when original installer could do work at 'much more reasonable cost'; one told customer refrigerant was unavailable when competitor said it's easy to source. Pattern suggests either systematic misdiagnosis or aggressive upselling.

Administrative follow-up failures: One reviewer received false 'schedule immediate service' texts after tech confirmed system was working fine; another waited 2+ weeks for quote during emergency cold snap with baby at home, then was told company assumed customer 'written them off'; refund promised 'within days' took a month with inconsistent explanations (card vs. check). One review noted difficulty reaching company and being transferred 7 times before being hung up on.
redFlags: 4 of 86 reviews (4.7%) allege misdiagnosis or unnecessary expensive repairs/replacements: $20,000 quote for $850 fix, $1,800-$2,000 quote for loose wire, $13,000-$20,000 replacement quote vs. competitor's $8,500, refrigerant availability false claim

Administrative communication failures: Quote promised same day then took 2+ weeks; refund promised 'within days' unresolved after 1 month; conflicting explanations about refund method (card vs. check); difficulty reaching company, 7 transfers before being hung up on
Emergency response gaps: 3-day delay (March 6 to March 9) for emergency toilet leak; 5-day wait for parts during cold emergency
One review notes disconnect between field service notes (system working fine) and automated follow-up texts demanding immediate service
synthesis.emergencyNotes: 24/7 availability and same-day service mentioned positively, but one emergency toilet leak review states 'first available appointment is March 9th' when call made March 6th—unacceptable for true emergencies. Another emergency during polar vortex had 5-day delay for parts.

plumbers.name: Around the Clock Plumbing
plumbers.address: 1207 Posey St NW, Huntsville, AL 35816, USA

reviews.author: Vanisha McComb
reviews.text: Kenny came to check out my hot water heater. It did not take him long to diagnose the problem he explained everything to me and was very nice and professional. I felt very comfortable with Kenny and trusted his expertise! Around the clock is the only plumbing company I will let do any of my plumbing needs, they are fair and very good at what they do!!

reviews.author: maria gil
reviews.text: Fabion was very knowledgeable and found the leak quickly. He fixed it perfectly and explained everything along the way.Very professional. I called Aroynd the clock plumbing and they came in 1 hour!

reviews.author: A Glasgow
reviews.text: James did a fantastic job. And I was actually behind him driving to my house. He was a conscientious driver as well! He took no time to fix my pipe issue. Good job!

reviews.author: JoBeth Dunaway
reviews.text:
Kenny an Dawson come out within 30 minutes .  Did a great job fixing a leak.  Service was quick and thorough.  Will always use this company. Every detail was explained to me, I approved, they did the work , now I a
m happy.   Thank you Kenny and Dawson job well done.  Thank you Around the Clock Plumbing for a remarkable team of technicians.

reviews.author: K.C. K
reviews.text: I can't thank James enough. He was able to figure out where our leak was coming from quickly (part of our water heater) and repair it at a fair quote. I appreciate his honesty and great work today. You guys were recommended to us by a family member on the spot this morning when I told them what was going on and I can see why. Thanks for saving this day from being an absolute disaster. The next time I need a plumber for anything you guys are my first call. Thank you.

synthesis.summary: Around the Clock Plumbing demonstrates exceptional reliability with response times as quick as 30 minutes to 1 hour for emergency calls. Multiple customers praise their technicians for being professional, knowledgeable, and providing clear explanations of repairs at fair prices.
weaknesses: Limited sample of review types
All reviews are recent 5-star ratings
synthesis.topQuote: Kenny an Dawson come out within 30 minutes. Did a great job fixing a leak. Service was quick and thorough. Will always use this company. Every detail was explained to me, I approved, they did the work, now I am happy.

plumbers.name: Midway Plumbing
plumbers.address: 1017 Energy Dr, Abilene, TX 79602, USA

reviews.author: Robert Wray (Bob)
reviews.text: Knowledgeable and professional. Did not guess or BS.  Created a plan and worked the plan to isolate the cause and identify severity then quoted repairs. Pricey but good.

reviews.author: Benjamin Howell
reviews.text: Todd showed up earlier than we expected, which was OK, knew his stuff, and explained our many options. We kept things simple and opted for a relatively minor repair job that called for a new shutoff valve and supply line, angle inlet, and fill valve. Todd did a terrific job, very thorough, and didn’t rush it. We are delighted with the result.

reviews.author: DIANA EVERSON
reviews.text: We called Modwaybto have them come check out our water heater. Hot water was running out after 15 minutes in the shower. The technician was prompt, courteous and knowledgeable. He check our past history and found our water heater was still under warranty. He was able to change out the heating element free of charge. It was a great experience all the way around. Thank you Midway Plumbing for taking care of us.

reviews.author: Kenneth Boutell
reviews.text:
Still leaks after you replaced cartridge which keeping the handlle in place was the only problem and two other fixes for over $800 supposedly fixed the problem.  Still leaks and the serviceman says it's the water and we need all new facets. He quoted $1065 for each new facet that's $2130 plus who knows what else will be charged.  I am not a wealthy oil baron, I had to work for fifty years to have what very little we have and can not just give it away. I find your charges exorbitant and will have to live with the leaky cartridge you installed. I don't even know if it was new.  Likely the last time I will call Midway and even though the servicemen
were pleasant, I would not be able recommend your company to others.

Had to shut the water off while we visited our children over the Christmas holidays. Based on cost only one star.

reviews.author: Anthony G.
reviews.text:
Ridiculous, insane prices ! I was a commercial developer for over three decades and dealt with many good plumbing contractors. I needed a replacement tankless water heater installed . All the stub outs as well as electric was already there. Their price.... $ 1,100. And it may be more if they " needed more electrical work" ! 
I called two Master plumbers and their prices were $225 and $250. 
I really feel they take advantage of customers who have no understanding of construction.
There are plenty of qualified plumbers .....why would you opt for midway?

reviews.author: Scott Simpson
reviews.text:
I know enough about plumbing systems to know what questions to ask about some problems I was having. Two of the three issues I identified had to do with the 3” waste pipe under the house slab. I had already used an auger to clear a serious root intrusion and as my waste main is 60 years old, I was prepared for the worst. I would have given Midway the go-ahead for some seriously expensive work if their plumber told me it was necessary.
The Midway plumber scoped the 3” main from the city connecting point to the house vent pipe and explained everything we were seeing in a way I could clearly understand. He checked the work I had already done, carefully talked me out of doing either expensive project I had enquired about, and gave me some tips for improving the remaining flushing problem that was being caused by the 1.28GPM toilet.
The third project had to do with the incoming water supply. What I thought would improve the incoming water pressure and get a jump on a potential problem was diagnosed as a case of “If it ain’t broke, don’t fix it… and it ain’t broke.” I was wrong; he was right. He could have just done the work I requested and I wouldn’t have given it a second thought. Instead, he again clearly explained the issue and convinced me not to have the requested work done.
Summary: The Midway plumber was professional, courteous, knowledgeable, and honest. I now understand how they maintain such a high customer review rating.

reviews.author: Crystal J.
reviews.text: I had a pretty serious plumbing problem at my house. I wouldn't call it an emergency, but it wasn't something I could wait around to fix. I called Midway and they assured me that they'd be at my house that day. I call 4 hours later to ask if I was still on the schedule and I was told "Nope, looks like it's going to be tomorrow." Thanks for the heads up, Midway. They left me stuck not being able to use my toilets, which is really great when you have two little ones in the house who pee constantly. I understand that things get busy, but enough notice that would allow me to call around to other places would have been nice. Needless to say, I called someone else. I certainly won't be trusting Midway ever again. If they can't be trusted or bothered to give their customers a call to let them know that the schedule has changed, then I most definitely will not trust them to not rip me off.

reviews.author: Sara B. Montgomery
reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
I can’t say enough good things about Midway Plumbing!  After the City of Clyde told me multiple times that my high water usage was “clearly a leak” and that I needed to call a plumber, I contacted Midway. They sent a technician out the very next day.

The tech was extremely kind, polite, and thorough. He took the time to investigate everything and determined there was no leak at all. Even with the valve completely shut off, the meter was still reading about 1.5 gallons per minute. He went above and beyond by checking all faucets, toilets, and showers just to be sure.

What really stood out was that he called the city back out and personally showed them what they had missed and couldn’t figure out themselves. Because of him, the real issue was finally identified.

If you want an honest, knowledgeable company that truly advocates for their customers, I highly recommend Midway.

reviews.author: Patricia M.
reviews.text: I called Midway Plumbing to install a new kitchen faucet because my old faucet was leaking. My house is six years old, so all hoses and pipes are in good condition. Upon arrival, the two young men assessed the situation, assured me that everything looked good and that they would only need to replace the faucet with the new one I had purchased. They gave me the price and asked me to sign so they could begin the work. They were charging me almost $700 to do a simple faucet installation. This is a job that would have taken approximately 15 minutes for an experienced plumber. Of course, I was not paying that ridiculous charge if I never have a new faucet!! I did, however, have to pay $89 for the 5 minutes the two young men were in my home. This is the most ridiculous experience I have ever had with a home service provider!! Do not get ripped off by this plumbing company!!!

reviews.author: Henry R.
reviews.text: First I have to say that I don't live in Abilene and I am a plumber in San Antonio but of course I do have family that lives there. I get a call from my uncle and I am trying to Sunday quarterback him trying to find a water leak because they had a high water bill. I started with the obvious items and the meter did not stop. I told my uncle Robert this is probably not good and we needed to get a company out there. So I called around and looked at all reviews. I stumbled upon this gem of a company- They were friendly on the phone(that's huge in my business) helpful getting a time frame down. They sent out a plumber and he found the leak within 20 mins. It was only a flapper that was leaking!!! My aunt and uncle were so thankful and I paid for the service over the phone with my credit card-  Thanks Midway from one plumber to the next!

reviews.author: Luis B.
reviews.text: Tried to connect the water line to the new fridge on a nice sunny Sunday. Should take 10 min. right? That water tap had never been used and was leaking. Even though I turned off the water main it ended flooding the whole kitchen and half the living room. Called Roto-Rooter between soaking towels and emptying buckets. Marc came out promptly and this "easy" job took him a couple of hours because he has to go get parts, cut drywall, copper tubing, re-solder joints and cleanup. Yeah, connecting the water line only took him a minute. Marc was professional, courteous, and Roto-Rooter doesn't charge extra for weekends. It did cost a little more than expected but hey, there's inflation, and wait, the color of the fridge doesn't quite match the kitchen!

reviews.author: Darrell B.
reviews.text: I had the pleasure of Gary McWhorter coming out to unstoppable a drain for me after I tried relentlessly and failed. He understands stopped it with ease, ran a camera down it to ensure it was good, and sold me some enzymes to help keep it from happening again which I had asked about. He didn't try to push them on me. He then looked at my main swore line and realized it needed to be rooted so he did that as well. I feel he went above and beyond what he needed to do there and I am thankful for that. He broke down exactly what it would cost before he did any work. My only complaint with the company is that they are more expensive than others in town. Although you get what you pay for and they also have the best warranty in town.

reviews.author: Rodney H.
reviews.text: I would like to share my review about Midway and the owner, David Ratcliff. I am a third generation Plumbing contractor and have served as President of the plumbing association P.H.C.C and on the state board of Arizona. I have been associated with David and his company for over 20 years. I've toured his shops, witnessed his integrity in the way he runs his company. Midways TEAM is highly qualified and they serve their clients at the highest level possible, offering the best product and warranty, in our industry! I wanted to pay this compliment to David. We are proud to have a company of this statute in our organization! SO Yelp browsers look no further! Congrats Midway Plumbing!

reviews.author: Laura Banker
reviews.text: Not happy with their prices. They prices just kept going up and up and while I understand they found more issues, what they already charged was really high. $250.00 to remove one piece of fence which took my finance 5 minutes. I guess they charge $250 every five minutes which seems about right. $12k later and five days my plumbing was finally fixed and they didn't bother to spread out the filled giant hole in my backyard. They also hit a gas line and didn't even bother to let me know.. I went to go smoke outside and if my fiance hadn't seen them break the gas line it could've been bad.

reviews.author: Courtney C.
reviews.text:
The toilets, tubs, showers were all backing up and not draining in two bathrooms. We called Midway and they scheduled a visit the next day. Russell found the problem, fixed it, checked all the pipes, checked the water heater, and other plumbing issues around the house. He was so friendly and professional. Super, super nice. We all loved him and Midway Plumbing! This was the first time we have needed a plumber since being in Abilene, but the next time we need something...ABSOLUTELY calling Midway!!!!
Thanks so much Russell and the Midway Plumbing Team!!

synthesis.summary: They show up fast and explain the work clearly, but you'll pay premium prices—and some customers feel blindsided by the final bill.
strengths: Fast emergency response: 15+ Google reviews mention arrival within 30–60 minutes, including same-day service for gas leaks and flooding
Transparent communication during work: 20+ reviewers across platforms praise technicians for explaining repairs step-by-step, showing problems via camera, and offering options before proceeding
BBB A+ accreditation with zero complaints over 3 years and 42 years in business—strong trust signal for reliability
weaknesses: Significant pricing disputes: 6 Yelp reviews and 3 Google reviews specifically call out 'exorbitant,' 'criminal,' or 'ridiculous' charges (e.g., $700 for 15-min faucet install, $1,100 for tankless heater when competitors quoted $225–$250; $345 for 45-min unclog). This pattern appears across multiple platforms.

Price shock after initial estimate: 2 Google reviews report prices 'kept going up' during job; 1 Yelp review notes $89 diagnostic fee on top of refused service. Contrast with strong positive reviews suggests pricing transparency during estimate ≠ final bill alignment.
redFlags: Pricing disputes: 6 of 32 Yelp reviews + 3 of 85 Google reviews cite excessive charges for straightforward jobs (faucet install, unclog, heater swap). Complaints describe estimates as 'criminal' and '2–3× competitor quotes.' This is a clear pattern across platforms.

Price creep during jobs: 2 Google reviewers report final costs significantly exceeding initial estimates without clear justification; 1 mentions $250 fence removal charge and $12k total for 5-day job.

Surprise charges and scope creep: 1 Yelp review reports undisclosed gas line damage during excavation; 1 Google review notes $3,400 power-wash quote for single line (reviewer felt equivalent to manual replacement). Suggests up-selling or insufficient initial scoping.

One old but serious incident: 1 Yelp review (2014) reports technician left gate open, dog escaped—no resolution mentioned. 1 Yelp review (2015) reports broken appointment (told same day that job pushed to next day, no advance notice) leaving customer without toilets.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple same-day and 1-hour response confirmations for flooding, gas leaks, and no-water emergencies; weekend availability confirmed. However, one older Yelp review (2015) reports schedule breach without notice—flag if calling during busy season.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Google 4.9/5 (85 reviews) vs. Yelp 4.1/5 (32 reviews). Yelp contains 6× more 1-star pricing complaints than Google; Google skews toward emergency/service quality, Yelp skews toward value. This gap suggests either selective review removal on Google, or different customer demographics (emergency vs. planned work). Pattern indicates premium pricing is core business model, not an anomaly.

plumbers.name: Hoffmann Brothers
plumbers.address: 45 Willow St, Nashville, TN 37210, USA

reviews.author: Tonya Miller
reviews.text: What an absolute wonderful experience. I was sent a man named Jacob, and it couldn't have gone better! Skills mixed with kindness is what a homeowner appreciates.  Jacob certainly had that and more. The automated system was suspicious,  however, walking through the process and allowing them to come to your home is effortless.  Once they arrive, the job is accessed and a cost is given. You have the option of accepting the bid or declining.  Very happy I agreed,  mine was an unusual custom job that was finished flawlessly by Jacob. I will certainly have their team back again!

reviews.author: Andrew Rickard
reviews.text:
Chance was probably the best technician I've ever had.  He was prompt, polite, efficient, well spoken, and made us feel very comfortable with the entire process. There was literally no friction and I am extremely happy with the results.

He replaced our 16-year-old gas water heater by inspecting, Recommending the replacement, and having the new heater here at my house and install installed within four or five hours.

To make room for him, I took our mountain bikes off of the wall, and without asking, he put them back for me. That says a lot, and lets me know that his attention to detail and his Quality and Work ethic is top-notch.

Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Brittany Truman
reviews.text: The team members Hoffman Brothers sends out to my home have all been highly professional, curteous and good explainers. They treated myself, home and property with respect. They answered all my questions thoroughly, provided detailed pictures of the work completed and explained it in detail. They cleaned up after themselves. From the initial quote to the end result, the whole process was smooth. I liked how they gave me different options to choose from... just repair the worst items, mid level and full restoration. They never pushed a higher option on me. They simply laid the plans out and let me choose my comfort level.
