3. This one was a bad experience. I think it was the 2021-2022 rain season when there were heavy rains with atmospheric rivers. Backyard flooded and the landscape drain could not keep up. The same pipe that was hydro-jetted a few of years before. Wizard was the first company I contacted in Fall before the next rain season. The person did a camera inspection. He showed me that the pipe was broken on the camera. Pipe camera is not easy to understand for inexperienced eyes like mine. I could see that the pipe was not in a single piece where he showed me. He recommended pipe-bursting to replace the pipe that drains to the street. Quote was $9,000 for replacing the drain pipe, fixing up the sidewalk that they would have to dig up to pull the new pipe from, and hydro-jet all the french drains in the backyard that feed into the drain pipe.

I called Pipe-Thru Trenchless Plumbing for another opinion and estimate. He did a camera inspection of the drain pipe and said that he had not seen a pipe in better condition!!! What about the crack that Wizard showed me on the camera?! Oh, that's a tee where the roof downspout connects to the drain pipe! Further he told me that, if I were to replace the pipe he would recommend against pipe-bursting (which Wizard estimator had recommended), because the drain pipe is too shallow and goes under concrete patio. Pipe-bursting could bust up the patio. He recommended pipe lining inside the existing pipe, if there are cracks. His final estimate was $0, no work needed. Why did the backyard flood then? Large yard, super heavy rains and a single drain couldn't handle it, and there is a small dip in the middle.

If I had let Wizard do the pipe-bursting the downspout would not be connected to the drain anymore!!

A few days later I received a text message from Wizard Customer Care Representative, Andrew F. Jr., reminding me about the $9000 quote and whether I wanted to proceed. I replied back politely with my experience and what the other company told me. There was no response from Wizard.

reviews.author: Tony C.
reviews.text:
No NO NO DO NOT USE THESE GUYS DO not USE they TELL YOU AND MAKE IT SOUND GOOD TO WHAT THEY ARE CHARGING. SELLING A FERRARI BUT WHAT YOU GET IS BODY OF KIT CAR FERRARI AND 1972 SUPER BEETLE WHAT IS CHARGED DOESN'T MATCH WHAT THEY DID THEY CHARGE 80 HOURS AND REALITY KNOWING IT WILL BE 15 HOURS. 

IT'S NOTHING BUT A RIP OFF NEEDED THEM TO FINISH WHAT THERE EMPLOYEE STARTED WAS SNAKE OUT PIPE SO THAT I COULD THINK ABOUT NEXT PHASE WHAT TO DO AS WHAT HE SAW
GIVING ME TIME TO THINK AND GET FUNDS TO REPLACE THE HORIZONTAL SEWER PIPE 40 TO 50 FT. BUT HAVING THAT TIME TO THINK ABOUT IT I DIDN'T GET CHANCE TO THINK ABOUT IT THE SUPERVISOR SHOWS UP TO ASSIST 2 HOURS LATE TO HELP OUT BUT SNAKE GETS STUCK HE STARTS YANKING ON IT ALSO  (BEING THE WRONG SNAKE TO USE) It's noisy Then it is quiet and hear whispers he tells me snake broke through pipe. Decision is now made. Snake is stuck they can't remove it need to come back to get it out . He goes into his truck he is looking like he is in a hurry . He gets in i see he brought his wife girlfriend. Wizard guy and myself wait he goes outside supervisor texts him he comes back supervisor stays in truck. I tall him hey mr supervisor.( coming in hot) that is his nickname you needs to walk me through this estimate. I say you need to justify this cost  and explain work. Screen fades out 3 times while explaining i ask how many days how many guys to do this job he asks for signature it fades out again he taps i sign before it fades. He says 3 guys 3 days 8 hour days. He asks for 2nd sig it fades out again pops up i sign again. I am frustrated thinking ok maybe it does justify cost. I then need to tell him to send me estimate so I can read it better. Doesn't give me business card don't know name. He says he will send it to such and such mail. Wife's email she was at work didn't see it when she started.i am waiting for call like we discussed when they would arrive. I get text from wife 11 and 12. They will arrive. Long story short which it isn't sorry. Two days of work 3 guys one day 2 guys today10 hours  in total.  They are charging 16k there estimate is as if they are plumbing Costco store  But in the end doing the shed size only shed i have called 2 times only get to call center with concerns phone finally got estimator he he starts yelling at me after i l am saying this is nothing like you presented. Saying he gave discount i am looking at the copy my wife printed out for me it was zoomed to the max still difficult to read so small. I look at break down 5k for toilet take off and to put back on. They say they are going to do alot but do not do. 3/4 of it. There is more also this is just screw job to customers IT is brain surgery charge but  getting the greeter who volunteers for the hospital doing surgery...... ...

reviews.author: Scott W.
reviews.text:
$1,830.87 to replace leaking bathroom faucet. Had owner, who is a gravely disabled senior sign contract before starting work. Owner is not able to understand what was being proposed. Relative called about cost. They will not budge. Invoice follows

* Angle Stops: These are the small valves under the sink connected to the water supply. Turn both the hot and cold angle stop valves clockwise to shut off the water. If they don't turn off properly, you may need to shut off the main water supply.
* Open the faucet to relieve any pressure and let the remaining water drain.
Step 2: Disconnect the Water Lines
* Use an adjustable wrench to loosen the nuts connecting the water supply lines to the faucet and the angle stops. * Have a towel or bucket handy to catch any residual water.
Step 3: Remove the Old Faucet
* Underneath the sink, use a basin wrench to loosen and remove the nuts holding the faucet in place. * Once the nuts are removed, lift the old faucet from the sink.
Step 4: Replace the Angle Stops
* Use a wrench to remove the old angle stops by unscrewing them from the water supply pipes.
* Wrap the threads of the new angle stops with plumber's tape to ensure a tight seal, then screw them onto the water supply lines.
Step 5: Install the New Faucet
* Place the new faucet into the sink holes, making sure it is aligned correctly.
* From underneath the sink, screw on the mounting nuts to hold the faucet in place. Tighten them with a basin wrench. * Apply silicone sealant or plumber's putty under the faucet base if required by the manufacturer to prevent water from seeping under the faucet.
Step 6: Connect the Water Supply Lines
* Attach the water supply lines to the new faucet and the new angle stops. Hand-tighten them first, then use a wrench to snug them up.
Step 7: Turn the Water Back On
* Open the angle stops slowly to restore water flow. Check for any leaks around the faucet and the angle stops. * Tighten any connections if necessary.
Step 8: Test the Faucet
* Turn on the faucet and check the water pressure. Ensure that both hot and cold water are functioning correctly.
Once everything is in place and there are no leaks, you're done! Let me know if you need more details on any of these steps.

reviews.author: Taryn R.
reviews.text:
Generally we use wizard plumbing. Never had a bad experience until recently. We had a leaking faucet. I called, and asked for the earliest availability and someone was sent out the next day. When I called, I said our faucet hose is broken and needs to be replaced. I knew the brand of our faucet, and the problem. So that's the backstory. 

They send someone out, he comes out and says it's going to be $350 to diagnose the issue( again, I knew the issue) but if that's their rate I'm not going to fight it. He proceeded to tell me that my faucet is dated and parts are unavailable. Also that our shut off  knobs don't shut all the way and need to be replaced. I said I don't think that an issue as we've never had a leak prior and maybe the water he heard running was the dishwasher that was going. He called a few plumbing stores, said we couldn't get a replacement hose as they don't exist. I do have text message proof- and that we needed to buy a new faucet. Okay cool. I agreed. He said the turn around time was 3 weeks with the possibility to be sooner. He said he'd send a photo of the new faucet so I could approve before purchasing. Mind you our sink is leaking. So 3 weeks isn't a quick solution. Now it's about 5pm, assume he's off for the day, and decide to chat with the manufacturer of the faucet. Right away, I send him a photo- and he says that hose is xyz, it's available and here's a discount code. HONESTLY not even 3 minutes into a chat and the hose is available. Then he proceeds to tell me, guy plumbing has it in stock, and I should call them. I call guy plumbing- they have the part, they schedule an appointment for early the next morning. They show up and within 15 minutes the leaking hose has been replaced. Nothing is leaking, the shut off valves work fine- and ultimately in less time and money to FIX THE PROBLEM than the diagnostic charge from Wizard. 

This hasn't been my previous experiences with wizard but if this the nonsense they're sending out, upcharging clients and lying- I'll happily take my business to honest, quick and far cheaper places like GUY. So after 1000s of gallons of water wasted. The problem is solved and we won't be using wizard again.

reviews.author: Kelly C.
reviews.text:
Save yourself the $$$$ and hassle and just call a different plumber, I cannot recommend Wizard Plumbing AT ALL :(

Called them out on a Sunday evening for an emergency main drain line blockage in my home, after 3 hours onsite with a drain snake they couldn't solve the issue (hard blockage) and said they needed to come back first thing Monday morning to use a camera scope and then excavate the blocked pipe. We were left WITHOUT a functioning toilet or shower in a 1-bath house (and paid $975 for the sunday visit) (the plumber who showed up was nice, the only positive aspect of this experience)

Called them back Monday morning when no one showed up at the designated time and was told my appointment had been moved to the afternoon without anyone notifying me, even though they were aware this was an emergency issue. They had no explanation for why my appointment was moved and no idea why i was told they'd show up in the morning. Horrible communication and customer service! 

I had to scramble to find a different emergency plumber who could show up in the morning, after I found someone else, Wizards plumber called me to say they were at my house ready to work. I told them they were already fired and that I'd found someone else to do the work. So Wizards is incapable of communicating with their customers AND their own employees! 

The best (worst?) part of all of this is that my replacement plumber was able to actually resolve and fix the issue with just a drain snake in 20 minutes, no camera scoping or expensive pipe excavation required!! 

Had I let Wizards continue with the service call they would've been excavating my yard and tearing up the floorboards in my house for NO REASON AT ALL!! 

Do not use this company, just call Roto-Rooter instead!

reviews.author: Rick G.
reviews.text:
This is the second time I've used Wizard. First time was for landscape drainage to prevent (more) flooding in my house. They were excellent then, and this visit today was even better.
Sewer main was backing up and I figured to get ahead before it was completely stopped up. My call was immediately answered by a real person. An appointment was scheduled for within 2 hours, earlier if they could get someone.
About 90 minutes later Joe showed up. I showed him where the clean out was and stood by in case he needed an extra pair of hands. Joe went right to work. The job turned out to be more difficult than expected. We called in Max, the regional supervisor to "camera" the line (at no additional expense!) Unfortunately the blockage was much more severe than originally thought. We needed to hydro-jet the line. When Max showed me the camera video I could see we had temporarily dodged a bullet. We did complete the hydro-jet. The line is now clear and we have decisions to make about our plumbing future in a 70 year old house. I really dislike paying for home maintenance or repairs that I think I can do myself. But the professionalism and the technology used convinced me I was in over my head. Joe, Max, and the two techs whose names I did not get ( they run the hydro-jet machinery) were the best! Clear explanations about what was happening and why. Really clear explanations of what our options are going forward, and excellent communication on the prices charged today and any potential projects in the future. A really great experience for a literally poopy problem. I will ALWAYS recommend Wizard in general, and Joe and Max specifically. Well done guys!

reviews.author: Warren S.
reviews.text:
My experience with Wizard was less satisfactory than other reviews. I knew tree roots kept growing back to the main sewer line but managed to use it by snaking it every a few years without the replacement. 

At last it was clogged again and I decided to replace it. Wizard was hired to unclog the drain and to provide a proposal. The person finished snaking much faster than other plumbers I used before. I was alarmed and asked if he cleaned the sewer line thoroughly. He said he did and would guarantee his workmanship for 3 months. 

Then he was eager to provide a proposal for replacing the sewer. However, he completely misunderstood the layout of the drain pipes and gave an over-priced quote. After I told him he didn't understand where the pipes were correctly, he insisted he was right. I just let him go since a few other plumbers would come for a quote.

One of the plumbers later lowed a video camera to the drain. I was surprised to see that the "workmanship" of that Wizard guy. The roots did fill the pipe section. He did as little as possible and managed to punch a hole with the size of 1/8 of the pipe section through the roots. I let it go sine I was replacing the sewer line anyway. However, I would not recommend Wizard or use them .

reviews.author: Stacy G.
reviews.text:
We hired Wizard to replace our sewer lateral in 2018 and it worked fine for 5 years. Fast forward to 2023 when we started a large home renovation that required we provide the city with a video inspection as a condition for our renovation permit. 

It failed inspection. 

There was a sag in the pipe and standing water visible, the connection to the city main was not replaced as it should have been, nor was a clean out installed as required by code. It would need to be dug up and replaced. 

I called them out to discuss it and they were at first pledging to replace it at no cost. The technician who did the job was known to them to have overseen subpar installations and no longer worked there. They had been "cleaning up" his work for a while, I was told. 

More people came out, a second video inspection was performed and also failed to pass. Since then, no one seems to want to talk to me. I've made many calls over the course of the past 6-8 months. They have great reviews on yelp so I was hopeful they would stand by their work and honor their reputation. Doesn't look to be the case. 
Consider this in your evaluation when hiring a plumber who will do the job correctly and stand by their work.

reviews.author: Haiyang H.
reviews.text:
Later update: please check recent reviews. Apparently, my experience is fairly common recently. Do your diligence and check... something has changed at wizard....

The joy of having children around is having clogged toilets often... here I am again trying to fix a clogged toilet on early Friday morning. Called in the morning at 7:30am and was told someone would be here between noon and 3pm. They also mentioned that they are now having a membership program that the technician could explain to me live. The technician would reach out to me to confirm exact timing. About 2pm. No communication yet. I called again. They mentioned they would follow up. Past 3pm, someone called and apologized. They were so busy that they can't find anyone for a small job. Realistically, they can have someone over on Sunday or Monday morning. To fix a clogged toilet, it takes less than 30 min. I thanked him, hang up and called another company. Someone showed up within an hour, fixed my toilet nice and clean and charge me about the same price. 

I am not sure whether the membership program is now the main driver for the insane delay, or the scale of my job is the problem. Just to share the experience here.

reviews.author: Sandra T.
reviews.text: Our family has a duplex property in Trust and we had to address a leaking water heater that needed to be replaced in January by Jose J., and most recently a property inspection report in addition to a PGE inspection report that resulted in an additional repair to 1 unit and a new water heater replacement to the second unit with repairs done by Kevin G. I also had the pleasure of meeting Jose S., The Field Supervisor who was present and making sure that the PGE inspection report item and the health and safety items in the property inspection report were completed. The quality of work was outstanding. Kevin promptly arrived at the beginning of the 4 hour window, spent the whole day at the property thoroughly and completely addressing the jobs on both units. He was so knowledgeable, polite and respectful. The pricing was current market value plus a 1 year warranty. I did need some revisions with the description of work done at the request of our realtor and Jose S went ABOVE and BEYOND to promptly revise our paperwork. Wizard Plumbing and Drain has provided us with many years of continued high quality plumbing services and excellent customer service. Thank you!

synthesis.summary: Wizard Plumbing demonstrates exceptional emergency responsiveness and technical expertise, with multiple customers praising their same-day service and thorough work quality. While they may not service all areas, their track record for urgent repairs and water heater replacements is outstanding.
weaknesses: May not service all areas
Scheduling communication issues
Limited geographic coverage
synthesis.topQuote: Out of all the plumbers I've done business with over the years, Wizard Plumbing proved to be — by far — the most honest, fair, competent, experienced and thorough.
synthesis.worstQuote: The morning of the appointment, I get a text message saying they do not service my area and have to cancel. Why didn't you tell me that three days ago when I made the appointment and gave you my address?

plumbers.name: Rescue Plumbing
plumbers.address: 1137 W Webster Ave, Chicago, IL 60614, USA

reviews.author: Lauren Ortega Falconer
reviews.text: Dex always comes through for us in an emergency! We have had several plumbing issues in our condo in the last 12 months, and Dex has been great. He's always responsive and persistent, even coming back a few times free of charge to tinker with his solutions to chronic issues we're dealing with. He's been trustworthy and friendly, and I would definitely recommend him to anyone.

reviews.author: Charles Hastings
reviews.text: Javier was fantastic, honest, and efficient! I got several quotes from other companies and this was by far the most cost effective. Not only did they provide a fair and up front quote, but they were friendly, and responsive. I will definitely be calling them again for any future plumbing needs!

reviews.author: Andrea Monllau
reviews.text:
What a great and fast job they did! We have a bursted pipe and called them. In less than 1 hour Javier and Jimmy showed up and were able to quickly repair the pipe.

They kept us informed of pricing and steps throughout the whole process.

Javier was really good at identifying where the burst was and only had to do a small whole in the wall.

10/10!! From call to fixed less than 3h

reviews.author: Rhianna Kern
reviews.text:
Dex and the crew literally saved our night (and our hardwood floors!) We got home from work and the kitchen sink was backing up with gross water and quickly started overflowing onto the floor. Between bailing out drainage water and panicking, Rescue Plumbing answered the phone in 2 rings and said we can help you right now.

Not only did Dex and his team show up AFTER HOURS in the SNOW, they were warm, kind and jumped right into action to save my kitchen. They knew exactly what to do, explained it all to me, told me the costs right up front and stuck to it. Within 30 minutes they fixed our building’s drainage issue, cleaned up the kitchen neat as a pin and were out the door with a quick joke.

I hope I don’t need to see them again but also if I need a plumber, they’re saved in my phone and getting every recommendation. 4 seasons and ABC told me I’d have to wait til tomorrow afternoon at the earliest and I think my floors would have been ruined from overflowing sink water if I didn’t have Rescue Plumbing. Thank you Dex and team!!!

reviews.author: Claire Anderson
reviews.text: This team was outstanding. Within 10 minutes of me calling they had three guys in the house working to fix my pipes. Ended up having 6 burst pipes during the Omar vortex that were really complex to fix and Dex and his team were fast and kept me updated the whole time. I have two small children at home and need water to properly care for them and he promised me no matter what he was getting me water by the end of the night. And they did for a good cost. I cannot say enough for how amazing this company is and the men who work for it are. So great fun to have found them

synthesis.summary: They show up fast, even in the snow at night, and actually fix your problem without nickel-and-diming you.
strengths: Emergency responsiveness: 4 of 5 reviews specifically mention rapid response times (answered in 2 rings, arrived within 1 hour, within 10 minutes of calling)
Transparent pricing: 3 reviews explicitly mention fair quotes, cost-effective pricing, and upfront cost communication with no surprises
Follow-through on complex issues: Multiple reviews describe going beyond initial fixes, including free return visits and working after-hours in bad weather to complete solutions
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviews confirm after-hours availability and rapid dispatch (2 rings, under 1 hour response times), including work during severe weather events like the Omar vortex.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 24W500 Maple Ave # 209, Naperville, IL 60540, USA

reviews.author: Burst Flames Entertainment
reviews.text: Kemal with Roto-Rooter was great! He explained everything that was recommended to be serviced and didn't try to upsale me on a service that wasn't needed. I highly recommend Kemal and I'll definitely be reaching out to him again when I need any plumbing service.

reviews.author: Fresh
reviews.text: Erlin was fantastic!  He actually took the time required to diagnose the issue (where previous plumbers did not) and address the true problem.  He was professional, respectful, and effectively communicated the details surrounding the problem and provided tips for future plumbing health.  I highly recommend Erlin and will definitely be using him again in the future!

reviews.author: Joseph Morisco
reviews.text:
I scheduled a plumber to come out and had a few questions after booking the appointment. I called back simply to understand what the technician planned to do since I had already had the line rodded a few months ago and didn’t want to just repeat the same service without understanding the approach.

Unfortunately, whoever answered the phone was extremely dismissive. She repeatedly cut me off while I was speaking and had a noticeable attitude. I wasn’t trying to be difficult — I was just asking basic questions about the service.

Instead of listening, she kept saying they couldn’t tell me anything over the phone and wouldn’t even let me finish my sentences. Because of that interaction, I immediately canceled my appointment.

Good customer service starts with listening to the customer. Losing business simply because someone won’t take a moment to hear a question is disappointing.

reviews.author: Charia Johnson
reviews.text: Roto rooter is a scam... they dont do any work... Im the president of a condo association... called them for a boiler issue the man didnt do a thing... looked at the hot water heater and told me it was old and probably out of warranty and it would be 15k to replace... I even asked like you didnt even look at it.. you arent telling me whats broken just that it old and broke... well I can see that... he made a call to the company about warranty... wrote his paper for the 75 dollar fee and left... did nothing for 75 bucks Nothing

reviews.author: Andrea Gomez
reviews.text: First service a few months ago left me happy, issue got taken care of right away. Appt was made and tech was there within the estimated time frame. The work was done quick and efficiently. Recently i had some plumbing issues and immediately thought to call them. Scheduled an appointment same day within the hour, the tech was dispatched- i waited 2 1/2 hours before calling for an update and being told there is issues with travel and they cant make it in, thats fine, i reschedule for next day, wait over an hour and a half before calling for an update, was told to hold on a bit more. An hour later they call to reschedule that the tech cannot come, made for next day in the AM this time instead of PM. Got a call this morning at 7am-(3 hours before my appointment time) to ask if my appointment can be moved up, the tech can be there in the next 30 minutes, i agree, wait, over an hour later, nothing- i call for an update, am told the technician is supposed to be on the way still but nothing. I cancelled the appointment. Ive had to rearange my schedule for 3 days now and ive been able to try things at home to help my plumbing issues. Crying in the club- send help

synthesis.summary: You'll get a great tech like Kemal or Erlin who actually diagnoses problems, but scheduling reliability and phone manners are inconsistent.
strengths: Some technicians (Kemal, Erlin) provide thorough diagnostics and avoid unnecessary upsells
When service is delivered, work is described as quick, efficient, and well-explained
Initial scheduling can be same-day within an hour according to first review
weaknesses: Severe scheduling reliability issues—multiple no-shows, cancellations, and multi-hour delays across 3 separate appointments in one review
Customer service phone staff reportedly dismissive and unwilling to answer basic service questions before appointment
redFlags: One reviewer alleges $75 diagnostic fee with no actual troubleshooting performed on boiler issue
Repeated pattern of dispatcher miscommunication and technician no-shows in one detailed account
Phone staff unwilling to discuss service approach before appointment
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Same-day scheduling mentioned, but multiple reviews show significant delays and cancellations even after dispatch confirmation—risky for true emergencies.

plumbers.name: Brian B. Quick Plumbing
plumbers.address: 10739 Tucker St Suite 100-B, Beltsville, MD 20705, USA

reviews.author: Danielle Drory
reviews.text: Mike Smith diagnosed our issue quickly and promptly replaced a water valve, highly recommend!

reviews.author: Laura Topper
reviews.text: Patrick was able to arrive hours before the scheduled time. He was very friendly and he worked efficiently. He even made our dog comfortable with his presence(and Kaz doesn’t usually like service workers) Pricing was fair for the materials used.

reviews.author: dom pentone
reviews.text: Very responsive and thurough

reviews.author: Alex Schwartz
reviews.text: I have attached some photos of the damage Brian B Quick caused at my house, they were out to simply replace the faucet in the bathtub. They cracked the tile in the bathtub AND installed it incorrectly leading to it flooding the closet, kitchen, and basement. Would give a 0 star as I’m still dealing with getting things fixed and the refuse to answer my calls.

reviews.author: Mike Robles
reviews.text:
UPDATE: APRIL 2, 2026
It has now been over four weeks since I posted this review. While the company was quick to reply publicly, no one from management has contacted me to actually address the issue.
This makes it clear they care more about their public image than resolving problems for customers.
At this point I will be escalating the matter further, something I originally hoped to avoid.
I strongly recommend not hiring this company. If you doubt this review, try calling and asking to speak with a supervisor or someone in management. No one will be available, and you won’t be given a direct number or email to reach them

I hired this company to unclog my shower drain, and the technician ended up breaking my pipe during the process. We didn’t discover it immediately because the pipe is outside, but  found significant water leaking caused by the damage.
They did come back and repair the pipe, but they initially tried to charge me $200 for the very pipe they broke. They have also refused to take responsibility for the water damage their technician caused. I’ve tried repeatedly for over a week to speak with a supervisor or manager and have gotten nowhere.
Overpriced, poor workmanship, and no accountability. Homeowners should be cautious.

reviews.author: Lisa Ninosky
reviews.text:
I can’t even begin to describe the peace of mind I have with Brian B Quick. The entire team puts you at ease no matter who comes to your house. We had a major plumbing issue that affected the plumbing in our entire home caused by a renovation contractor. I called and after some back and forth they knew exactly how to remedy the situation. I don’t think there is a plumbing situation they haven’t seen. They even had some recommendations that we’ll ensure are cared for in the rest of our renovation. Or we’ll have them come back out to assist.

And for every homeowner out there please always know where your main shut off is as well as exactly HOW to turn the water back on properly. Especially when you’re on a well. It matters and we found out the hard way.

Thank you again Brian B Quick team for always having our backs. We are so lucky to have a team so qualified in our corner.

reviews.author: Stephanie Sherman
reviews.text: I had the Brian be quick out a few days ago to check out my disposal and a backing up issue into the kitchen sink from the dishwasher  (very impressed with their professionalism, expertise in plumbing issues, excellent customer service and would definitely recommend them for future work). James  gave me a few different estimates. Ultimately, the disposal needs to be replaced and because the dishwasher has been causing a backup he suggests snaking the line as well to ensure oil, food etc is cleaned out properly. James was back out within a few days and was able to get everything up and running smoothly again. He is phenomenal at what he does. Very knowledgeable and beyond polite and helpful. He was able to answer my questions about a few other unrelated issues in the house. I will definitely be giving him a call when I need any assistance for plumbing.

reviews.author: Nadira Kabir
reviews.text:
Pierre was outstanding from start to finish. We needed to connect a drain pipe to our hot water heater to meet DC code for a rental license, and he was the first plumber who didn’t dismiss the job as impossible or try to upsell us on an expensive $9K floor drain that could have caused sewage backup issues.

Instead, Pierre took a thoughtful and creative approach, finding a practical, cost-effective solution that brought us into compliance. He truly went above and beyond in both the quality of his work and his attention to detail.

On top of that, his customer service was excellent—he explained everything clearly and made sure we understood our options. As a bonus, he was also very comfortable working in a home with a dog, which made the whole process even smoother.
Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Alexis Burroughs
reviews.text:
I had Brian B. Quick Plumbing come out to inspect my garage disposal after another company, sent by my condo management company, suggested it needed to be replaced. They had originally been sent to address a drain line issue, but claimed the disposal was also the problem.
James thoroughly inspected the disposal and informed me that it was actually in proper working order. The real issue was with the drain line was still clogged, which was a huge relief. I was concerned about having to pay several hundred dollars to replace the disposal, so I'm grateful for his honesty and professionalism.
Highly recommend Brian B. Quick Plumbing for their thorough and trustworthy service!

reviews.author: Patricia Williams
reviews.text:
My husband called Brian B. Quick plumbing after being disappointed by Handyman Plumbing. Dion was the plumber that arrived at my home, my husband explained to Dion that we were having water back up into our kitchen sink after washing clothes.
Dion checked the pipes in the kitchen he checked the main line and all the bathrooms, he diagnosed our problem and went right in. He was informative, he worked clean , he brought his own equipment and he was polite.
I give Dion and Brian B.Quick 100 stars ⭐️. I have another project that I will be using them for.
Thank You Dion for giving my back my kitchen and my laundry room.Awesome service and I will use them again.
Very pleased!!

reviews.author: Randi Wright
reviews.text: I called them on a Friday morning for a shower head leak which turned out to be a faucet drip and they were able to have someone here the next day. They lady Kaitlyn was very nice when setting up the appointment and worked with me on scheduling. The gentleman that they sent out was named Dion. He was very nice, professional, friendly, here when they said he would be, explained all my options and very respectful. He must have been in customer service for a long while because he explained everything in simple terms. He did a great job and I am very happy with my new faucet and shower head. No More Drip! Thank you Dion and Brian B Quick.

reviews.author: Jason Trent
reviews.text: I had a supply line break on a weekend, which is the worst time you could need repairs. The company responded quickly and dispatched a plumber within 3 hours. The plumber arrived promptly, was shown the issue, and provided me a quote within minutes. After a few signatures, the plumber began their work; my issue was resolved not 10 minutes after that. During the visit the plumber was respectful, professional, and quick. If I could give more stars I would. Great pricing, great response time, and wonderful customer service. I will be a returning customer.

reviews.author: shirani fernando
reviews.text:
Earlier in the day, I had an experienced plumber who was able to troubleshoot and propose a less invasive solution, however he was not able to work on it till next week.  So I hired BBQ as they were ready to work that same day.  They were a bit pricey.  And they sent a junior plumber to repair leaking shower.  The troubleshooting was very invasive and costly.  And turns out, he did not install it correctly, so we are going back to the original plumber.

For what its worth, the plumber BBQ sent was super nice, professional, and timely.

reviews.author: Charlotte
reviews.text: Excellent service. Pierre was not only very knowledgeable but he was also very friendly, took time to explain the problem and possible solutions and expertly finished the job. Further more, the P-trap screw broke while he was re-installing it and he pleasantly went to Home Depot to get a replacement. I would also like to add that my plumbing problem happened on a Sunday but Brian B. Quick was able to get me someone in a few hours. This is my first time using Brian B quick but I will surely use them again. I highly recommend Pierre.

reviews.author: Jodi K
reviews.text: Was hoping to use this plumber for all of our on-going plumbing needs but I felt like they took advantage of time/cost. We requested a change of a faucet. He showed up and didn't have the parts and came back the next day. From what we were told the faucet cost around $80.. Took the plumber no more than 30 minutes to change and leave. $530 later.. the job was done. I have called THREE times and left three messages to get an explanation of the bill and yet to receive a call back. Will not use again.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast and do solid work, but a handful of customers got hit with surprise bills and one had damage they wouldn't take responsibility for.
strengths: Fast emergency response: 12+ Google reviews explicitly mention same-day or within-hours dispatch, including weekend availability (multiple Sunday calls noted)
BBB A+ accreditation with only 1 complaint in 10 years of business signals strong institutional reliability

Technician quality: Named techs like Dion, Pierre, Mike, and Patrick appear repeatedly in 5-star reviews with praise for knowledge, patience, and honesty (e.g., refusing to upsell unnecessary $9K floor drain)
weaknesses: Pricing disputes: 3 Google reviews (04/03 3-star, 04/03 3-star, 03/24 1-star) report surprise bills or overcharging ($530 for 30-min faucet swap, $428 for a $50 drain part) with poor callback response on billing inquiries

Damage liability avoidance: 2 reviews (04/02 1-star, 04/05 1-star) report technician-caused damage (cracked tile + flooding, broken pipe) with company refusing accountability and ignoring management callback requests for 4+ weeks
Inconsistent workmanship: 1 review (03/06 2-star) notes junior technician performed overly invasive repair that failed, requiring return to another plumber
redFlags: Billing disputes and potential overcharging: 3 of 87 Google reviews report surprise charges ranging $200–$530 for routine work; 2 customers unable to reach management for explanation despite multiple callback attempts over days/weeks

Damage and accountability failures: 2 reviews report technician-caused damage (tile cracking, pipe rupture, water damage) with company refusing responsibility and ignoring management contact for 4+ weeks; one customer escalated complaint outside platform
Inconsistent quality on complex jobs: 1 review notes junior tech sent for leak repair performed invasive, failed troubleshooting while more experienced plumber had proposed simpler solution
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Strong weekend/after-hours availability documented in 8+ reviews with sub-3-hour dispatch typical; however, damage liability concerns and billing dispute non-responsiveness create risk if something goes wrong.

plumbers.name: Busy Bee Plumbing, Heating, & Air Conditioning
plumbers.address: 40 Burton Hills Blvd #200, Nashville, TN 37215, USA

reviews.author: Ashley Burger
reviews.text: Went above and beyond beyond to figure out my dryer venting issue

reviews.author: John Freeman
reviews.text: Zach was very professional and got the issue resolved quickly

reviews.author: Nicole Doctor
reviews.text:
Five stars for Braxton at Busy Bee! We called with an AC outage at 9 PM on a Friday night, and Braxton was out to save the day by 9 AM on Saturday.

Braxton was on time, courteous, and knowledgeable. He had us back up and running in under an hour. We would definitely recommend this company to others!

reviews.author: Arbed R.
reviews.text:
Heat was out. It was cold. Called Busy Bee and they came out later that day. Tech said the thermostat needed to be changed. We asked how much and tech said $581. It was late and not enough time to call anyone else so we said go ahead. I looked up how much the new replacement thermostat cost and I could get it for $67 to $100 depending on where it was purchased. We called Busy Bee and waited 9 days for a call back from the manager who told us that's what they charge. We did mention we felt like that was gouging and his response was that he had overhead and had to pay for an office, etc. Called another HVAC company who told us they would charge $175 for thermostat replacement.

We appreciated the service - most of the time - that Busy Bee offered but WOW, are they ever expensive. We're very happy with our new company and service arrangement and are even more appreciative of their pricing.

reviews.author: John D.
reviews.text:
We had a kitchen drain clog and contacted Busy Bee to clear the drain. After many hours, drain was not completely clear. Offered to pay half as the work was not completed in a satisfactory manner. Offer was refused by the manager stating that we pay for the attempt regardless of outcome. Expressed desire to discuss it with the owner, William Matheny. 

Over the course of a 27 minute conversation that devolved into name calling, William equated my offer to pay half as negotiating with terrorists, called me a deadbeat, and stated he would smear my name with all the other plumbers in town. Frankly, I'm a little appalled that the owner of a company would talk to a customer in such a manner and refuse to make any sort of attempt to come to an agreement. 

Needless to say, we will not ever be contacting Busy Bee for service of any kind ever again, and would strongly others to look the other way. This is not a company that cares about its customers and making things right in their eyes despite their claim of being the home of the "more than satisfied" promise.

reviews.author: Carly Cott
reviews.text:
We’ve had such a great experience with Busy Bee and truly can’t recommend them enough.

We were put in a tough position with a flooded AC unit, a failed compressor, and some faulty lines—but the Busy Bee team stepped in immediately and handled everything with urgency, professionalism, and care. From start to finish, they made what could have been a really stressful situation feel manageable and supported.

What stood out most was their integrity and commitment to doing things the right way. They took ownership, communicated clearly throughout the process, and made sure every detail was thoroughly addressed. That level of accountability is rare and incredibly appreciated.

A special thank you to Matt, the supervisor, who was not only extremely knowledgeable but also kind, responsive, and hands-on in ensuring everything was handled properly. And Ben, the senior tech, did fantastic work—thorough, efficient, and clearly takes pride in what he does.

We also have an annual membership with Busy Bee, and this experience only reinforced that we made the right decision. They stand behind their work and truly take care of their customers.

Highly, highly recommend Busy Bee to anyone looking for a customer centric team you can trust.

reviews.author: Rachel L.
reviews.text:
It's always the salesperson to get you on this big home repair jobs and then they are nowhere to be found when things go wrong. They sold us on the most expensive and apparently incredible $6000 water heater with all the bells and whistles. Well, it had problems right away, we had to have people come out and do repairs and part replacements a few times. Finally, I asked for them to just install a regular water heater as the fancy one was more of a headache than anything. They put in a nice one but it only cost $2000 -- they Richard to refund us our money for the expensive one because they "couldn't return it" -- they also stopped selling that one due to complaints, go figure. 

The men who came in and installed the plumbing left all of the old piping lying under my house initially. We had to call and have someone come remove it, that guy wasn't real happy about cleaning up someone else's messes. Then we had to call and have them fix bent pitting leading to the water heater. The entire experience was just hard to navigate and so many dropped the ball over and over. I gave two stars simply because in each instance, there was an attempt to make things right but at a $4000 loss I can't recommend Busy Bee.

reviews.author: Jerry R.
reviews.text: They were friendly on the phone, and were able to schedule a time within 4 days of our call. We wanted to join the club, get the plumbing and heating inspection and also have the sink faucet replaced. Eryk was our tech who came out. He seemed to be experienced and knowledgeable. He was also somewhat personable. However, he spent an extreme amount of time on his cellphone from the time he drove up. Also charged an exorbitant amount to change the faucet. We provided the hardware, just wanted a competent plumber to install to avoid leaks. That cost $275 just for the install. And as I said before, more time on the phone, extending the time for our service way past what it should have been. We tried to like them, and were truly hoping that we had found a great company, but there were issues that will have us looking for another company. Apparently the lead in price is just to be able to severely gouge the customer when they get their foot in the door. Not what we were hoping for.

reviews.author: emily s.
reviews.text: A gas leak was randomly found by a different company who said we had a leak and that we needed to hire a company to come out and fix it. So we hired Busy Bee. BIG MISTAKE. They came out and "checked" and said we had no leak, turned our gas back on, and charged us $450. I thought something wasn't adding up so I hired another company to come check the gas and they found the leak exactly where the first company said it was and turned our gas back off then fixed it. We had video proof that there was a leak and Busy Bee still said there wasn't. This is a huge liability. Busy Bee turned our gas on even when there was a leak! Had I not followed up, we'd still have a leak. This company should be investigated. How many people have the told they didn't have a gas leak but they did?!

reviews.author: Collin Pisarra
reviews.text: Ben did an excellent job at the house today. He came into a world of chaos as we were doing work all over the place and he took plenty of time explaining everything to me and making sure to be patient as well. He was really able to give me a sense of what he was doing, and why he was doing it, which I think is such a rare attribute these days from contractors. This was my first time using busy bee as I had gone with a competitor the last couple years. I’m really appreciative of the time Ben gave me and he did a great job helping me prepare for the summer months.

reviews.author: Mike
reviews.text: I have used a lot of plumbing companies in the past and I would have to say this is one of the Best I have ever used. This is our 3rd time to use them and all 3 times they were excellent! Shane the plumber that came out was not only honest but very professional and knowledgeable. He explained the issue very well and gave me 3 options on how to take care of the problem. He worked way past 5:00 PM until he got the job done. I highly recommend you should try this company next time you need a plumber. You will not regret it. Happy Customer Mike Bilbrey.

reviews.author: Luke F.
reviews.text:
Toilet valve would not shut off. Had to turn off main water supply. Called Busy Bee. They said, "We'll have someone there between 10 and 6 today."

5:50p.m. Phone rings. "Uh, we ran out of time with our other emergencies. You we're a 'try to fit you in' and we're not going to make it today."

"You're just realizing this at 10 minutes until six?  You could not have called a couple hours earlier and mentioned you might not make it by today?"

"Uhhhh."

"Forget it!"

DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY!!

reviews.author: G700 G.
reviews.text: You really can't make this up.  Freezing temp and a "VIP" LOL member.  Called as heat was out.  Told by dispatch all techs out.  Call back from Ops Mgr to tell me "I have to take care of my crew and can't have them out at all ours of the night".  I kid you not!  So what is VIP mean.  Nothing.  99.7 advertises they come out immediately when on VIP.  NOT.  Maybe just for them.  Price was less than half with another vendor three days later.  hmmmm.  Maybe this help someone considering them.

reviews.author: John Barker
reviews.text: Had to have 2 repairmen come out. 1st guy missed something. They sent somebody the same day I called for another repairman. Went far above what they had to in order to fix and repair my air conditioner the second time. Also didn’t really charge me for the second repairman 4 days after the first. They take care of their customers and I highly recommend them. Even a manager called after to check if it was ok. This is a business that cares about its people and they have earned my business.

reviews.author: Brock Dugger
reviews.text: We got our HVAC unit replaced at our rental and was surprised at how many options and price ranges were available. It was for a rental house so we wanted something that more fit that budget. Gary worked up a couple different options at the price range and they (Bryce and his team) were installing literally the day after Christmas (today) and I purchased on Dec 23rd! Truly a 5 star experience with an HVAC Replacement! Perfecting timing, right before the cold spell approaching!

synthesis.summary: They'll get someone out to you fast and fix it the same day, but expect to pay premium prices for the service.
strengths: Emergency response speed: At least 6 reviews specifically mention same-day or next-day service for urgent calls, including weekend emergencies

Technician quality and communication: Reviewers consistently praise individual technicians (Cody, Chris, Ben, Jonathan, Zach) for explaining work, being honest about options, and taking time with customers
Problem-solving approach: Multiple reviews note technicians diagnosing issues quickly and providing cost-saving alternatives rather than pushing expensive solutions
weaknesses: Pricing significantly above market: Three separate reviews document charges 2.5-5x higher than competitors (thermostat $581 vs $175 elsewhere, utility sink $397, faucet install $275), suggesting systematic markup
Service quality inconsistency and follow-up failures: Two reviews report incomplete work (drain not fully cleared, no-show with no notification), and one mentions 9+ day wait for manager callback
redFlags: Owner behavior documented in 10/11/2023 review: escalated dispute into name-calling, equated customer negotiation to 'negotiating with terrorists,' threatened to 'smear name with other plumbers'
Safety concern in 08/04/2025 review: technician failed to detect gas leak that second company immediately found; Busy Bee turned gas back on despite leak present
Warranty service failures: 07/11/2025 review reports 2+ week delay on new HVAC units still under warranty, with customer perception company prioritized sales over support
Pattern of premium pricing without transparency: Multiple customers surprised by final bills being significantly higher than market rates
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviews confirm same-day and next-day emergency response including weekends and after-hours; one reviewer received service by 9 AM on Saturday after calling Friday night at 9 PM.

plumbers.name: Hill & Company Service, Inc.
plumbers.address: 5125 NW 5th Pl, Oklahoma City, OK 73127, USA

reviews.author: Andy Crockett
reviews.text: I had a complete heat and air unit installed. The pricing was reasonable, they were on time and very thorough. everything was explained in great detail and all questions were answered honestly. the whole experience was great, other than writing the check lol!! Jeff Holloway was great to work with! I would recommend them to anyone. They are coming out next week to give an estimate on replacing the hot water heater...

reviews.author: Mark Osborn
reviews.text: As usual, I had a great experience with Hill & Company. I was served by Tyler Fleetwood for my furnace inspection. He was thorough with the inspection and clear about what needed to be repaired/replaced and what the options for this were. Thanks for a great experience!

reviews.author: Diana McClung
reviews.text: "I had an amazing experience with Hill and Company. The service was top-notch: they were punctual, left everything spotless, and took the time to thoroughly explain everything. The sales rep was knowledgeable, friendly, and responsive. Plus, a quality control rep came out afterward to check everything over, ensuring everything was perfect. Highly recommend their service!"

reviews.author: Tina Nelson
reviews.text:
Called them on Thursday and had a new heat and air replacement done by Friday afternoon. Very nice, respectful, and thorough. Very reasonably priced. They took great care of my property, covered the floor before the job, and cleaned very well after the job. Did not try to sell me anything I didn't need, and used the old ducts because they said there wasn't anything wrong with the ones we had. Very honest group of people. I will have them replace my mother's AC unit in the spring and would recommend them to everyone. Great job guys. I'm so glad we chose your company. Thank you

Update
We were supposed to get two years of tune up free with our purchase.  When I tried to make the appointment,  they wanted to charge me for it.

reviews.author: Pat
reviews.text:
Can I just say how *unimpressed* I am with my experience with the Hill & Co. group?!

I had a pair of guys come out & take a half-assed look around, then the older, chubbier journeyman sent the younger, skinnier apprentice under the house to poke around at the plumbing (he forgot his flashlight & had to be handed one).
They tried to convince me that I needed to upsize my whole gas line, "for code."
They even had me convinced & up-sold, until I asked the next 2 plumbers about it...
They were *absolutely* trying to take advantage of someone who didn't know better.

Anyway, they quoted me $9,2k for a simple Tankless Water Heater swap.

I though that was too much, so I called around. An outfit called Yarbrough & Sons sent a technician named Cole Hobson to come out & do an estimate for a Tankless Hot Water Heater swap for me, and not only did he do a *thorough* bathroom, utility closet & crawl-space inspection, he came out of the crawl space (looking like the undead 🧟‍♂️), went looking for a shovel, & then went *back* underneath the house to trench out access to allow him to get where he needed to go, to see what he needed to see! I should add that Cole & I are both bigger guys (like the Hill&Co Journeyman), & I would not have wanted to have to do what he just did. And he did it enthusiastically & professionally, all for just a free estimate! Very impressive!

Unfortunately, a guy from a "2 guys in a truck" outfit came in right behind them & immediately underbid their quote.

But I was so impressed by Cole, that I called Yarbrough & Sons, & I asked if they could match/beat the other offer?
And, guess what, they did!

So, we were pleased to get to book the project with them & are having it installed next Monday (So long as I can get my Electrician to come upgrade the power outlet box over the weekend 😅).

For the record, they beat out Hill & Co.'s (who was Angie.com's 1st recommendation) initial quoted price by >45%, coming in at just <$5k.

I also looked up their Google service reviews, and I'm excited to join Yarbrough's membership & bring them on as our annual HVAC / Tankless HWH service provider, & look forward to getting 20% off of any addition future plumbing or HVAC needs!

Thank you, Yarbrough & Sons!
I can't wait to have endless hot water in our soon to be renovated bathroom!

To those guys at Hill&Co, thanks for not letting the door hit you on the way out! You're *way* over priced! 🤑

I would encourage anyone looking at or using Hill & Co. to get a quote for the same work through Yarbrough & Sons!

Update: Yarbrough & Sons put in my tankless water heater today (3/9/26), and I am very pleased with my choice.

reviews.author: D Murphy
reviews.text: Have used Hill & Co for decades-upon referral from my parents, who have used them for over 40 + years. Hill & Co has always been professional, well informed/knowledgeable, courteous, respectful, and responsive. They call & text, with a pic, each time a technician is on their way, which we appreciate. Their maintenance program of our central ac/heat has been wonderful and gives us peace of mind. We recently had to call over the weekend and were surprised that they now have regular weekend hours, and a technician stopped by within 30 minutes, in between his lunch break and next appointment. We had just had our fall heat maintenance performed a few days previously and had then asked that our air flow be turned to winter-not thinking about our continuing unusually warm weather. We soon discovered our mistake and why our weekend call. The tech couldn't have been nicer and we so appreciated no charge for our mistake. They're soon adding plumbing services for customers, and with a home over 50 years old, we will be calling them now for any plumbing needs we may have. We can't say enough about this family owned company and continue to believe them to be the best in our community.

reviews.author: John Curry
reviews.text:
Hill & Company replaced my 35 year old furnace and air conditioning units today with new Trane units! The salesman and techs who came to my home were very courteous and efficient. The whole process started on Friday morning when I initially called Hill & Company. A sales representative came to my home that afternoon to verify what equipment I need, offer discounts, incentives and financing options. The final purchase price was much lower than I expected it would be. Today, four days later on a Monday morning, the techs showed up at my home at about 9:30 and had the job completed around six hours later, including cleaning up after their selves. Thank you Hill & Company for a job well done from my initial call to you till your techs left my home this afternoon! I will definitely call again!

2/7/26
It’s been 3 months since they installed it and Hill & Company came out today and gave my new furnace the first service check and changed the filter. They said it was running great and didn’t see any signs of needed repair. The service tech was very courteous and efficient. Another job well done by Hill & Company!

reviews.author: Meghan McAnally
reviews.text: I have had Hill & Company install a new HVAC system with heat pump.  They did a great job explaining my options when choosing the system I wanted.  They were amazing through the entire install and very respectful. They came back a few weeks afterwards for my peace of mind after I expressed some concern and again were great at explaining what had happened. I also had them install a EV charger for my home.  They were amazing in communicating my different options and what they would look like.  EVERYONE has been fantastic during all of my interactions with them.  While they were mildly more expensive the some other quotes I received there communication was phenomenal when compared to other companies I have used and gotten quotes from. They took the time to explain what I was getting and why they made those recommendations.  It never felt like a sales pitch from them like some other companies did.

reviews.author: map1106
reviews.text:
I discovered a leaking pipe in my wall at 3:30 am on a Saturday morning.
With it being 6 degrees outside, I knew shutting off my water could cause pipes to freeze before someone could show up to fix this issue, but having water spraying in the wall of my living room wasn’t ideal either.
I did a quick google search for 24 hour plumbers in OKC and started calling down the list.
Hill was the 4th company listed and the only one that had anyone available. Within an hour, help had arrived and the issue was being fixed.
The plumber that came out, Michael (if remember correctly,) was excellent - quick, smart, knew his trade well.
Price was very reasonable, not even taking into account it being 3:30 am, icy roads, and 6 degrees outside.
Hill will be my go-to plumbers from now on, and a big thank you to them for all of the help at the worst time possible.

reviews.author: L Crymes
reviews.text: We have been shopping to replace our HVAC system for six months. By far the most professional quote process came from Jeff Holloway. He took the time to inspect each service and return vents to calculate a balance load (turns out we needed an additional 12"12" return vent). Then two days later technicians Ryan & Kasem arrived to install new system. They were polite, hard working, quite efficient, and very respectful of our property. Everyone at Hill&Co were willing to fully answer and explain any questions. Also. the financing terms were quite attractive. We are more than pleased. Eric, the manager of the installation crew, came and preformed a quality assurance inspection of all facets of the work. Have never had this level of service before and are quite happy, especially for such an expensive purchase.

reviews.author: Patricia Chaney
reviews.text: Naturally, my A/C went out on Friday evening which was also one of the hottest days so far this summer.  I called Hill and Co. on Saturday morning.  Since I was a Smart Service member they came right out.  Less than three hours later the service tech was at my home.  It was determined I had a large leak in the condenser coil which was unrepairable.  A whole new coil would be necessary which would be a very costly to repair.  Since my unit was over 14 years old, I decided put that money towards a replacement unit.  One phone call and the salesman was out that same Saturday afternoon to discuss my options.  By Monday morning before 11:00, I was up and running with a new A/C and coil.  It certainly pays to be a member of the Smart Service Program with Hill and Company.

reviews.author: Chris Hall
reviews.text: Absolutely the best customer service we have ever had with a heating and air company. I think so far we have had four or five different techs come out for routine maintenance work. Each have been very professional and respectful of our home. They explain everything so well and you do not feel pressured about being up-charged for things you do not need, but they do make very good recommendations regarding various aspects of your system. We have elected to have some of those done and do not regret any of them. The post-work documentation they provide is excellent. We recommend you sign up for their maintenance plan. It will easily pay for itself. The office personnel are very professional and competent, as well. A+

reviews.author: Katie Crum
reviews.text:
These guys were quick and easy to schedule with. Transparent with quotes and estimates. Timely. Kind. Quiet. Professional. Educated. And they did a fantastic job. These guys made me feel safe inviting them in and they didn’t ask for anything. I highly recommend them to anyone and everyone!

Edit:

I just recently started working with their electrician! They just added onto their IT service options. I got a coupon in my email for electrical inspection so we went for it. Josh fixed a problem we had right off the bat, taught me what I needed to know about the process and maintained all of the quality we’ve come to  expect from hill & co. It is SO nice to have an electrician we can trust!!

reviews.author: Leigha McCool
reviews.text:
Toan Nguyen came out on Christmas Eve, and a Saturday to fix our heater. What a blessing. So kind, quick, and the person that took our call got us in when I called that morning. Very thankful for this company and their ability to fix our heater so quickly and not charge an arm and a leg. Highly recommend this company and will continue to recommend them to others. Thank you!
1.2.2026 - Matt has came twice now and our family loves him. He is kind, helpful and personal.

Love this company, it doesn’t let me leave additional reviews so I’m editing my original comment. Highly recommend this company. We’ve had other techs as well and they have all been great.

reviews.author: Shell Belle
reviews.text: Thank you Mr Hill, for all your help, with my parents house.  My original review was not so favorable.  Since that time Mr Hill personally reached out to us. He helped my elderly parents with the repair costs, when he had no legal obligation to do so.  I can’t thank him and his company enough for that.  His staff reached out to me several times and kept me informed of all the repairs. His workers did an excellent job putting in the new ducts and my parents were very happy with the quality of their work, when it was all said and done.  Thank you again, for your strong work ethics and for working with us. Respectfully M. Womack

synthesis.summary: They'll get to you fast in emergencies and explain everything clearly, but expect to pay premium prices and watch your invoice carefully.
strengths: Emergency responsiveness: 5+ reviews specifically mention same-day or within-hours service during urgent situations (weekend heat failure, 3:30am leak, emergency AC failure), with one reviewer noting they were the only 24-hour plumber available

Technician quality and communication: 35+ reviews praise technicians for thorough explanations, professional demeanor, and taking time to educate homeowners; many mention photos/documentation of work performed
Honest upselling practices: Multiple reviews note technicians did NOT push unnecessary upgrades (e.g., 'did not try to sell me anything I didn't need', 'no pressure about being up-charged')
weaknesses: Pricing disputes and transparency: 5 reviews specifically flag high costs—one reviewer rejected an $840 labor quote for a warranty part replacement, another rejected $9,200 for tankless water heater (competitor quoted $5k, 45% less), one noted 'huge fee just to come out' not credited to final bill, and one found invoice 'confusing'; this pattern suggests pricing may not be clearly disclosed upfront

Service agreement follow-through: 1 review (1/5 star) documents that free tune-ups promised with HVAC purchase were later charged for when customer tried to book, indicating terms may not be honored consistently
redFlags: Pricing disputes in 5 reviews: $9.2k quote rejected (competitor 45% lower), $840 labor quote for warranty part deemed excessive, service call fee not credited to final bill, confusing invoice, and characterization as 'very costly'—pattern suggests upfront pricing clarity is inconsistent
Service agreement non-compliance: 1 review documents promised free tune-ups were charged for upon redemption, indicating terms may not be consistently honored

One 1-star review (10/31/2025) initially very negative regarding repairs/costs, later updated to 5-stars after owner personally intervened and assisted with costs—suggests company recovery process exists but initial billing practices prompted complaint
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Strong emergency signals: Multiple reviews confirm 24-hour availability, same-day scheduling, weekend service with 30-minute response times, and willingness to send technicians during extreme weather (6-degree night, ice storms); one reviewer was unable to reach other plumbers and Hill was the only option available.

plumbers.name: Integrated Plumbing Solutions
plumbers.address: 3111 Moon Station Rd Ste B, Kennesaw, GA 30144, USA

reviews.author: George Remias
reviews.text: Integrated Plumbing Solutions spent the time needed to understand my plumbing issue and provided me with reasonable options to correct it. They completed the job in two days but never left us without working water/sewer. Their cleanup was so good you can’t tell they were here. Everyone was friendly and professional, keeping us up to date on progress throughout the repair.

reviews.author: Price Hamilton
reviews.text: I made an online request for plumbing service last night. Matt B was at my house by 11am today and resolved a clog and replaced a corroded pea trap. Matt was very helpful and informative by explaining options and how to identify where the clog was and the best path forward.

reviews.author: Vera Koenig
reviews.text: Great job. Definitely recommend. Matt B was the technician.

reviews.author: Alex D.
reviews.text: Tried for 30-45 min to get fireplace lit and was unsuccessful. Literally just wiped parts off.  Then the guy tried to explain our options but was either drunk, or on drugs and couldn't get it straight. He rambled for 30 minutes and made no sense.  He then charged us 109 for trying to fix it. Guess it was for his time?  What a horrible experience and way to rip people off during this tough time.

reviews.author: Anice W.
reviews.text: I only got an estimate for gas line repair from Integrated and thank goodness I got a second appraisal! The technician came, extremely unprofessional talking in circles mostly about his personal affairs with confusion regarding repair of my gas line. Showed me $1651.44 to fix the problem, but when I received email with the estimate, scrolled down saw an additional 2342.50 for work not discussed! Shame on you Integrated!!! All this after I told them I am older and on a fixed income. I saved $2843.94 by using a different certified plumber

reviews.author: Erica Gage
reviews.text:
Update: they came back out and corrected any issues. Appreciate them acknowledging my review and trying to set things right!

Changing my review given the actual experience. My initial review was based on the quoting experience. Jacob was very thorough and answered all my questions during the quoting. Turns out we needed our water heater replaced and we ended up adding the filtration system. However the experience was less than satisfactory. First of all Jacob indicated that the schedule was tight and they really wanted to be at my house a certain day to get the job done and wouldn’t be available again for a long time. I shared that it wasn’t going to work because we wouldn’t be at the house after mid-morning. He said they could get here early and be done by then. He severely underestimated the time it would take to complete the job and created unnecessary inconveniences. My poor mother had to cancel appointments to stick around until the three guys left because while they said they wouldn’t enter the house (because I was gone) they actually did and my mother wasn’t leaving our babysitter in the house by herself with my son. They actually were supposed to be at the house at 7am, didn’t arrive till after 8am and ended up being at my house double the amount of time they said it would take to complete the job, leaving over 3 hours later than they said they would. What I also didn’t appreciate was the fact that I was supposed to be enjoying my day off on a boat with friends and I’m receiving multiple phone calls to pay the remaining invoice but Todd (supervisor) couldn’t take the time to call me back at a time when I would be free and possibly have reception (again, I was on a boat) to hear my frustrations on the delay in completion. Now he’s out for ten days with no resolve. And here is the kicker: Turns out they should have added the sediment filter to the filtration system. The manufacturer states that “pre-filtration with a sediment filter is recommended even before the main system, especially if water has heavy sediment loads.” Pretty sure anyone in Kennesaw knows we have high sediment loads in our water, plumber or not.

reviews.author: Dana B.
reviews.text:
EDIT:
I am updating my review 9 months later because I feel guilty about this review and didn't tell the full truth. So I'm coming clean.

The reason why this company has such high reviews is because you get a discount if you leave one. So don't be a dummy and fall for that age old scam like I did.

Also they ripped me off so bad and I was so embarrassed. They charged me OVER $800 to fix a wax seal on a toilet. For comparison, I had a different master plumber buy and install an entirely new toilet in a different bathroom for a total cost of $400 INCLUDING the cost of the toilet itself. Integrated Plumbing told me it would cost $400 but didn't inform me until the work was done that the $400 was ON TOP of the down payment I'd already made. The down payment was for them to perform a smoke test which they chose not to do, even though it was the only reason i called them back in. I also asked them to bring a camera to do a deep search and they told me it "wasn't charged" and it was "unnecessary."

This bothers me daily because I really wanted this company to be a partner but I think they just saw a mark. I still wrestle with an unidentified smell in my house, I never got the smoke test I requested, and I foolishly lost the $1,000 I'd saved to get this work done.

Don't believe me? I'm attaching proof with my invoice.

Apologies to an anyone I misled with my original review. I'm keeping it here so you can see it. And when I get my bonus this year, I'll call a different company to help me fix this issue. I'll never deal with them again.

_____
I have an unidentified smell in my house that I'm absolutely determined to get to the bottom of. Integrated Plumbing sent John out and he's been a true partner in helping me troubleshoot. We've found issues related to previous "flippers" or service providers who didn't install water heater venting correctly and a toilet that was poorly installed. This team is definitely more of a partner than a service and I really appreciate their help! Treat John right - he's a gem!

reviews.author: Alex B.
reviews.text: Back in May or June of 2014, I bought my first home in Marietta and the previous owners used an electric stove so we swapped it out with a gas stove. However, we did not have a gas line connected from the downstairs garage up to the second floor kitchen so I first reached out to Mr. Plumber for their service. I had to pass on them for being overpriced, extremely poor customer service, and exploiting customers lack of plumbing knowledge (please read my review about them). If I recall, I was given a block time when the plumber would arrive and he came in just within the time frame. Also, if I recall his name was Nick. I explained to him my problem, and he said that instead of using copper all the way upstairs, that they could use a flex connector instead for half the price!  I was dumbfounded that Mr. Plumber failed to even mention this to me and go in depth as to the benefits of it. Furthermore, Nick said he could have my gas connected in just an hour instead of the half day or more that Mr. Plumber offered. Nick was missing a part, but went ahead to his office to retrieve at a small cost. Also, he had another fellow to help him install it since you need somebody else to push and pull the cable. Nick's side kick came in really helpful later when he had to crawl underneath the sink to get around to the back of the stove to connect it all. I looked over their connections and everything was secure and neat running up through the garage ceiling.  I was extremely pleased with their work so mush so that I called them back for a second visit to check my water heater.

reviews.author: Sally R.
reviews.text: We had a leak with a shower fixture and needed it replaced. Basically it was a disaster. In summary Integrated Plumbing Solutions sent out Brandon, who seemed nice, who charged us $1,042 to open our bathroom wall and install the base of our new Kohler fixture. When our tile contractor came to do the repair the  tile he noticed Brandon did not follow the manufacturers guidelines on the depth the fixture should be set at. Brandon set it too far out. We got Brandon back to reset it and he said we needed our tile contractor to come back out and reopen part of the hole so he could reset it. We did that. Then when we called the company to get Brandon back and it turned out he no longer worked for them. We got Integrated Plumbing to send someone else out who said it could not be fixed, and called his supervisor for help. The supervisor was looking at some solutions and never got back to us despite our sending emails and calling. Finally we gave up and found another solution independent from them to fix it. This company has NO INTEGRITY. Basically their plumber made a mistake, charged us a boatload, and they ignored us when we asked for it to be fixed. I encourage you to stay away from this crooked company!!!

reviews.author: Ashley R.
reviews.text: Our rental home in Woodstock was a victim of the polar vortex of 2014. One of our pipes froze and when the temps warmed up, it burst causing water damage to a wall in the garage. I don't live in the area anymore, so I had to do a bit of Googling to find a local plumber who seemed trustworthy. This company popped up on Yelp with a good review. When I went to their website, I was happy to see they run a 24-hour emergency line. Score! I talked to Christina on the emergency line who was super helpful! Business was so busy, so I understood that it may be a long wait, but she told me she was confident that one of the plumbers could make it to my house by the evening (I called around 10/11 a.m.). To my surprise, a plumber was able to show up within a few hours and fix the broken pipe. On a day like that day, when it seemed like everyone's pipes were bursting and you'd be crazy to get service same day, I almost can't believe it was fixed so quickly. It cost us, but it's fixed and it was fixed fast. Ask about getting the website discount - you can get $25 to $250 off for visiting the website.

reviews.author: Alex F.
reviews.text:
I contacted Integrated Plumbing Solutions to first ask if they were familiar working on Bosch Tankless Water Heaters. They put me on hold and came back to say YES they were familiar with the system. I scheduled the appt and a few days later, they were there.   

Although the technician was there on time, it was clear he did not know what he was doing. He was on his tablet for over two hours trying to diagnosis the problem when he finally offered us two options... replace a gas valve, a pilot generator and flush the system for $1,100 or replace the entire water heater to something newer and simpilier for $3,600. Needless the say I wanted a second option. 

I called Bosch (which I should have done in the first place) and asked them to send out an authorized technician. The tech got out there and had our system working within 5 MINUTES. It was a dirty ignitor which he was able to clean it and got it relit. Final cost with the Bosch Technician...$89!

Please beware with Integrated Plumbing Solutions, they might not know what they are doing...

reviews.author: Ronald Dickerson
reviews.text: Jordy Fajardo was our technician from Integrated Plumbing Solutions. He arrived on time, he was very professional and he was also very knowledgeable. Before he began the repairs on our damaged gate valve, he explained to me how and why he would approach the repair. Our damaged gate valve was in a very small and difficult location outside of our complex gate entry. The job required a very skillful technician. So, we got lucky when Sandy at Integrated Plumbing Solutions assigned our job to Jordy. Jordy's work was outstanding. I've been a Property Manager and an REO Broker for over 27 years in metro Atlanta. In that time, I've worked with dozens of plumbers, some were good and others not so good. Jordy, he was the best! I would recommend Integrated Plumbing Solutions to anyone in need of a good plumber. Thank you, Jordy. Job well done.

reviews.author: E E.
