reviews.text: We're in a rental so when we saw a leak on our side of the water meter we called the landlady. The following day one of these guys (Eddie) called and said he was on the way. He pulled up in a company van and didn't bother knocking but got the landlady on the phone. I stepped outside since he was on the porch and he turned away from me and went to the street. I heard him say he would have to replace the underground pipe from the road to the house and it would be about $1300. WHAT??? He left (never said boo to me), later the "Call before you dig" people came and identified the underground utilities. We were livid so we pulled the pipe off the meter, found a busted seal, picked one up for a couple dollars and fixed it ourselves. The landlady cancelled with Integrated Plumbing Solutions. WHAT A RIP OFF.

reviews.author: Lisa K.
reviews.text:
I want to start by saying how incredibly impressed I am with this company. Every time that I've requested service from them, I've been promptly scheduled for a technician to come out the very next day! It seems that that's almost unheard of these days, so that also speaks to how terrific their customer service is.

So, the last service technician to come to my house was Jordy Fajardo, who I cannot say enough great things about. He was very kind, well-mannered, engaging, knowledgeable, informative, professional, and just a pleasure to be around! He's a true gentleman, with a very apparent eagerness to help others, and an obvious passion for both his job and his profession. He resolved all of the issues that I needed help with ... and cheerfully, to boot!

Thanks again, Jordy!

reviews.author: Li O.
reviews.text:
Metro Atlanta,

Please do yourself a favor and NEVER use this company.  I live in a high-rise building in Sandy Springs.  These people turn a 2 hours hot water low boy replacement into a 8 hour insurance nightmare for me and my neighbors.  Even after chaos they caused I tried to speak with their management about insurance repairs.  Jenny Irons, refused to listen a hung up on me and told her staff to do the same!  I told him this is improper and unprofessional especially with everyone using social media.  Then he hung up on me.  Jenny Irons when sent an email which said in parts  "Firstly I apologize for hanging up on you, I should have not done that but there is truly nothing i can do."  Right, Atlanta don't let these people do nothing for you.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast for emergencies and most techs are friendly and knowledgeable, but Yelp reviews reveal a pattern of surprise charges and billing disputes that Google reviews don't mention.
strengths: BBB A+ accreditation with zero complaints over 13 years and 103 total reviews — strong institutional trust signal
Multiple Google reviews (at least 8) specifically mention same-day or next-day emergency response, including weekend availability
Named technicians (Douglas, Corbett, Thomas, Matt B, Ben) consistently praised across 20+ reviews for professionalism, thoroughness, and clear explanation of work
weaknesses: Yelp shows 5 one-star reviews (out of 18 total) citing surprise charges, incomplete quotes, and overbilling — e.g., one reviewer charged $800 for wax seal repair (vs. $400 elsewhere), another saw hidden $2,342.50 added after estimate, another charged $1,651.44 for gas line with undisclosed $2,342.50 additional fee

One Google reviewer (2/5/2026) reported severe time underestimation (7am arrival became 11am+, job took 3+ hours longer than quoted), lack of callback from supervisor (Todd), and missing recommended sediment filter on water filtration system
redFlags: Yelp shows pattern of billing disputes: 5 of 18 Yelp reviews (28%) cite surprise charges, hidden fees, or massive overestimates not disclosed upfront — 1 customer charged $800 vs. $400 elsewhere for same work, 1 customer saw $2,342.50 added without discussion, 1 customer told $1,651.44 with hidden $2,342.50 fee, 1 customer quoted $400 but charged additional amount on top of deposit

Google review (2/5/2026) documents significant time underestimation and unresponsive management (supervisor Todd did not return calls); same reviewer notes company did not recommend sediment filter despite acknowledged high sediment in local water
Yelp review (2/9/2026 edited) alleges discount-for-review incentive scheme ('you get a discount if you leave one'), undermining reliability of Google's 4.9 rating if true

Two Yelp reviews report unprofessional technician behavior: one (1/5 from 2018) describes technician on tablet for 2+ hours unable to diagnose tankless water heater (Bosch tech fixed in 5 min for $89), another (1/5 from 2020) describes technician appearing intoxicated or impaired, rambling for 30 min, charging $109 for failed service
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple 24/7 emergency line mentions and same-day/next-day service for urgent leaks, burst pipes, and water loss confirmed across both platforms.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Google 4.9/5 (84 reviews) vs. Yelp 3.5/5 (18 reviews) — 1.4-point gap. Yelp concentrates complaints about billing practices, hidden fees, and overestimation not mentioned in Google reviews. This gap suggests either review manipulation on Google, platform bias toward service quality on Google vs. pricing concerns on Yelp, or genuine improvement over time. Homeowners should request detailed written estimates and confirm all fees before work begins.

plumbers.name: Mr. Rooter Plumbing of The Oakland-Berkeley Area
plumbers.address: 50 Hegenberger Loop, Oakland, CA 94621, USA

reviews.author: Harrison Alter
reviews.text: Got the job done without too aggressive an upsale effort.

reviews.author: Anna Blumberg
reviews.text:
Alfredo Macias took care of everything promptly yesterday .  He was courteous and efficient.    At another time, Alex Beceras installed a hot water heater for us.  He was also courteous and efficient ( 5 stars).
We feel confident when we call your company as we have received excellent service on many occasions.

reviews.author: Alexander Winslow
reviews.text: Good man. Very professional. Dedicated to getting the job done right.

reviews.author: Anthony ..
reviews.text:
I called this company on 12/23/24; they changed my appointment two times. Finally, when the plumber, Josias Estrada, came without looking for a clean-out,  He stated all the main lines were clogged and that he needed to remove both the toilets and snake from there, and that estimate was $827.When I asked your website was showing $88, he said that if I unclogg from main cleanout. This house does not have a cleanout line. It was hard to believe; I told him there was a clean-out line in front of the house, but the plumber insisted it was not the clean-out line. I disagreed with his estimate and called a different company, Cal Home Construction. The following day, they found the cleanout, which I pointed out, unclogged our toilets in less than 15 minutes and charged us less than $100.

I called Rooter Hero customer service and provided the cleanout line photo, and the representative told me someone would call me back, but It never happened. I would not trust this company for any of your plumbing work. BEWARE OF THEIR ADVERTISING PRICE. 

Job Id
817773450182

reviews.author: Gabriel M.
reviews.text:
We called them to get a drained cleared for $77 because it's a great price, or is it? 

For frame of reference I am a journeyman plumber so I know what I'm talking about. Had a clog I couldn't get out so I called them in. To be fair they were here for about two hours and put on a good show, looked like they were really trying, but were they? 

The head they were using wasn't the correct head for the job, it was a flimsy spring head used for looser clogs instead of the needed cutting head or at least a larger spring head. 

After two hours of show they told me they'd need to cut my ceiling open to inspect a pipe and that'll be $300, and they are pushy about it. The proposed job was going to be about $3k-$4k and I said thank you, paid them the $77 dollars and declined them cutting open my ceiling. 

Called another plumber the next day who came out with the right equipment and blew out the exact same clog in about 20 minutes. You do get what you pay for apparently.

reviews.author: Kimma M.
reviews.text:
I don't like to be harsh but I do like to be detailed & honest and I think this company will try to SCAM you.

Before you hire them, give them your hard earned money, and trust them to work on your home, please continue to read this and previous reviews. Also, check them out on the Better Business Bureau site. There you will see they are not accredited by the BBB and received a D- score from them. They cite failure to respond to 9 complaints for the reasoning. Also, there are 64 filed complaints (65 after I file mine) in the last 3 years, 36 closed in the last year, and the neg reviews give them *1.21*out of 5 stars. 

Now, my personal experience. My parents are seniors. They rent out a property which had plumbing problems two years ago. Rooter Hero told them they needed all this work done and charged $20,000. It took two weeks and was a huge job. They also left a huge mess as everything/all furniture was covered in dust, the walls throughout were also coated with a thin layer of dust. They didn't bother to cover anything with plastic or give any warning to move things out of the way before hand. What a way to inconvenience a customer who spent so much money. 

Fast forward to the present. In the same house, a tub and shower are clogged upstairs- even after all the work they did previously. They can't snake it and say it's a separate line that was previously worked on for the $20,000. Which, ok, it was. They come back the next day to assess the downstairs. I'm there to let the guy in. He's in, glances at two parts of the ceiling, merely 1-2 mins to make his assessment, then he's out.  Doesn't ask me any questions about the plumbing downstairs but comes to the conclusion that this needs to be fixed right away. He calls my dad, telling him what needs to be done and asking if I can sign for him. My dad wants to sign on his own, so at the drop of a hat, the guy is willing to drive 50 miles to my dad is for his signature. 

About an hour and a half later, I call my dad for an update. The guy is standing right in front of him for his signature. He says they're starting today and they will be sending guys over NOW.  I was confused at the rush & suspicious at why he would drive so far for a signature.  It was around 3pm, they couldn't wait for the next day?  I complain things need to be covered / moved beforehand and there's no time to do that.

I quickly hop on their yelp reviews and their profile on the BBB site. I read the many reviews of them quoting jobs
overpriced by thousands & thousands of dollars & and possibly preying on vulnerable seniors. I call my dad again. The Rooter Hero guy is gone by this time and my dad had signed. I ask what their quote was. He says $21,000.  They also told him they would finish in two days. What $21,000 job takes two days? And my parents had already replaced so much for $20,000 so what possibly could cost that much? I warn my dad about what I read online. He puts Roto Hero on pause, and I find another company for a second opinion.

Guy came out within an hour of my call. I could tell this person did his absolute best to fix the problem as he spent 2.5 hours trying to snake the pipes, looked for the crawl space, went on the roof, and with permission - cut open the wall a little to view the pipes downstairs. And yes! he cleaned up after himself. He put in so much effort in contrast to what was done by Roter Hero on their assessment. After he did all he could, he gave an estimate for the job- $5200. It ended up being $6800 after they found another issue. But $14,200 LESS than Roter Hero. We went with them of course, and the job was complete in two days.

I think Roto Hero was willing to drive 50 miles unplanned for a signature and wanted to start asap to secure the dishonest estimate for $21,000. A SCAM and they know it. My experience matches the multiple reviews online.  If you are reading this and still considering hiring them, do yourself a favor and consider another company that is honest and won't try to rob you of your hard earned money. (And leave a mess).

reviews.author: Giovanni A.
reviews.text:
This company is a complete SCAM. Look at the previous comments. I had them come out and was hesitant about their services, my tub and shower were clogged. A guy comes out and tries to snake, said he couldn't make it past a few feet (WTF?!) He then said his supervisor needs to come out with a camera, working in Inventory in plumbing I know ALL technicians have cameras if they're dealing with a plumbing issue so that was an immediate red flag. 

They then stated they had to do a hydro power clean out which would cost $1,900 from my roof to flush the debris away. The average cost to hydro power clean a sewer line is $700, the lowest is $300, the highest is $1,100 if you're LUCKY. Running this by my friends at Pace Supply & Ferguson stated this company is a SCAM & that they will literally bait and switch customers, give them one quote then redact it and claim that you need everything replaced at a ridiculously marked up value. 

What this company is doing isn't only unethical, but illegal. They're going to get sued sooner than later, I will fight them tooth and nail in court because the problem I was supposed to pay them to fix wasn't fixed, thus why I refused to pay from the get go and demanded to be invoiced. Their shady practice is nothing short of vile and predatory. I can't wait until a class action lawsuit bankrupts this company and those involved are charged with financial conspiracy, especially around the elderly and vulnerable. 

They recommended I get a $20,000 CLEANOUT installed. A cleanout literally on average costs $650-$2000. Anything above $3,500 is strictly commercial for larger buildings, NOT single family homes. $20,000 for a cleanout is absolutely criminal, I also noticed it's a trend on so many comments under this page that they try to upswell any update for $8,500+ or $20,000 It's interesting how these people working there think that not everyone has their expertise in plumbing. I've done inventory all over California for various plumbing warehouses & know what's right and what's complete fraudulent and unethical. I know what the prices are for every plumbing product and what the average costs is for manual labor. 

After "finishing" the half complete job, they unprofessionally hung around my house for 2 hours, chatting and talking so they could burn time on the clock. This lead my neighbors to contact me saying they felt uncomfortable with their lingering presence. The manager was literally there during all of this, WTF?! They blocked my from exiting my driveway as their car was parked barely a few feet in front of the stop sign *illegally I might add,* and refused to move as I was backing out. They literally had lunch right in front of my house after the fact too, absolutely unprofessional in every capacity. Here's a few pictures of the lackluster job they "completed." 

If you refuse to pay them because of their shady services that are botched they will threaten a LIEN on your property, I'm not even joking. 

Avoid this place like the bubonic plague, I can't wait until they're sued into bankruptcy. This company is EVIL, PREDATORY & UNETHICAL.

reviews.author: Mayank S.
reviews.text:
Rooter Hero Plumbing and Air of San Jose - Yelp Review 

Awesome    Service by Mr Francisco technician, the HVAC tech specialist from Rooter Hero Plumbing and Air of San Jose 

Francisco educated and made me aware of the super beneficial Annual Hero Club Rooter Hero Club combo membership (Plumbing + HVAC)

Paid 270 for a year and got all the fabulous benefits membership comes with. There's nothing like it. Note I am the family member of the Rooter Hero with Priority calling,  Priority scheduling and Priority Appointment service,  all top of the line!!

Francisco worked very hard in detail to clean the choking Condenser outside unit Vents / Coils/ Capacitor / mounted Condenser and Wallah. The outside unit now shines   

Francisco then went under the crawl space, and he installed the brand new insulation cover on all the A/C Condensation and Copper line sets followed by metal tapping and strapping to current codes. He even checked the R22 freon gas refrigerant and ensured pressure was adequate, and volume in pounds filing in the A/C line was UP to the mark, and no major recharge required for R22 refilling. That was super cool off Francisco. 

Francisco then performed the deep HVAC Cleaning by vacuuming the entire Gas propelled Furnace,  clean up all the burners,  check for the CO2 Gas leak, ensuring the Electronic cartridge filters baskets  were not clogged at all and furnace was all in the great condition. 

Francisco also climbed into the house Attic space, check the Air Ducts as well and ensure there are no possible outliers / anomalies w.r.t ducts being dirty and free of smoke,  debris,  Air pollutants, rodents debris etc No major AIR DUCT CLEANING IS REQUIRED,  that was a very good signature. 

I was overall impressed with Francisco HVAc tech deep knowledge about the entire Heating and Chilling unit operation,  functionality,  logistics, and appliance mode of operation. 

Francisco is the JEM OF JEWEL, and I was blessed to have him on board to take care of my home    HVAC SYSTEM.  

Mr Francisco is my now go-to HVAC guy, and I am not even looking for any other tech as long as Francisco is with Rooter Hero. 

ROOTER Hero Plumbing and Air of South Bay is the way to go and I can not say much enough about them other than they are strongly interested recommended and available 24/7/365, day / night doesn't matter. 

For any of the household plumbing and HVAC needs,  the Rooter Hero of the way to go!!

reviews.author: Barbara T.
reviews.text: I had Rooter Hero come out to unplug slow draining kitchen sink because they had 77 special. The guy came in looked at my sink and said I do not want to do further damage by snaking it. I need to look at all of your plumbing which he did. When he came back he said I needed new main drain or my sink would never drain properly and that would be $12,500. also said my pipes were leaking and I needed  a full redrain with all new pipes that would entail opening the walls. That was $37,00 . He said they had financing available and called someone else to come over to explain it all to me but he was 45 min away so he was going to go get a cold drink while he was waiting. I took that opportunity to call another company for a second opinion. When the guy from rooter hero showed up he explained the issues he said they found and my sink would continue to back up because it needed a new main drain. Also said my pipes could burst at anytime without the repiping done to the tune of $37,000 . I said I could only do the main drain so I signed up for the financing and scheduled an appointment to start the work the next day. 2nd  opinion showed up 2 hrs later and inspected my pipes without me giving them any information from rooter hero. They tested bathrooms stating they were draining fine, I did not have any leaks from pipes under the house and all my pipes looked fine. I then told them what I was told the problem was. they said I did not need any of that work done the only issue I had was my kitchen sink which they unplugged for me. I called the company and cancelled my appointment for the next day. Then I cancelled the financing they signed me up for with no problem. Rooter hero showed up the next morning to do the work. When I told them I cancelled that they said they were not informed. Seems they have a problem there. They asked why and I said I had a second opinion that did not recommend the work. The tech then said he stood by his assessment .  If I really needed the work it seems that second opinion would ggive me a lower quote to get the job. Do not trust this company! I almost got taken

reviews.author: Jerry S.
reviews.text:
Outstanding Service!! T A Roberts Plumbing (Find my 5-Star review) recommended "Rooter Hero Plumbing" and, as luck would have it, RHP are running a $77.00 special through the month of July 2023. For $77.00 They unclogged and Video cam'd a nasty sewage line back up that had been vexing an 83 year old Man in an Alameda, one story townhouse, for months. 
Very fast response - onsite within 2 hours. Very professional. Now they are repairing a badly damaged underground (under slab), cast iron sewage pipe, cleaning up the flooded-mess inside the house (two bathrooms, hallway, bedroom), replacing a toilet (or two), and turning frowns into smiles... 

Being a Licensed General Contractor, I get called by friends and friends of friends to recommend "specialists" and oversee these type of repair operations quite regularly. I concurred with RHP's approach, diagnosis, recommended repairs and overall approach & philosophy; not to mention NO-price-gouging! 

Are they expensive? Well, in a word, yes. This business model is likely a national chain therefore needs to kick-$-upstairs a couple of times however, this does not mean they "scam" as other disgruntled reviewers would suggest. They cover their arse (and yours) with right/correct/modern procedures/recommendations. All plumbers working at this level and managing these type of problems are, relatively speaking, expen$ive. 
Blame Liability Insurance, Workers Comp, Licensing, State Regulations, Equipment expenses, warehousing, to name only a few... However, they do provide financing if needed (who does that??), and they perform with a superior level of urgency and professionalism. And believe me, when a homeowner has a mess like this, Rooter Hero is precisely the team you want to show up and make repairs!  

There are so many amazing "knuckleheads" in the service trades; and it's difficult to find good help/service anywhere, at any price, these days. Take a note folks, "Rooter Hero Plumbing" are the right guys to call... Thank you fellas for making ME look like a Hero! LOL

reviews.author: Avi J.
reviews.text:
This company is disorganized, pushy, overpriced, and has questionable policies around providing estimates.

I opened a project here on Yelp for toilet installation. I did not ask for this specific company, but Yelp sent out the request to multiple service providers, as it routinely does. I did not ask for same-day service or for someone to be sent out immediately. I attached a detailed video to my project request, showing what needed to be done: replacing an old toilet with a new toilet. Just watch the video and send me a quote.

Pandemonium ensued. Rooter Hero sent me simultaneous conflicting messages from two different numbers - one asking me when would be a good day and time, and the other telling that they were already on the way. The more I explained that I did not want someone coming out today, the more messages I got stating that the plumber was now on the way to my house. When I called up to make sure he would NOT be sent out today, the phone attendant promised that they would cancel the order and not send him out without coordinating it with me. A few minutes later, the Rooter Hero truck pulled up to my house. I had to literally get someone out of the shower so I could show him the bathroom, even though I had never asked for him to come in the first place and was promised that he would not.

The plumber barely spoke English, so I switched to Spanish. HE SAID HE HAD NEVER HEARD OF YELP. I showed him the toilet and explained that we'd like to switch it out for a newer one. He orally quoted a price that was more than double the cost that was given by other plumbers in response to this project request. I asked him to have his company send me a quote in writing, and he responded that ROOTER HERO DOES NOT EVER GIVE WRITTEN ESTIMATES.

I thanked him and he left.

I called Rooter Hero back a third time and relayed the entire story. I was told that there is an escalations manager who can speak with me, but he doesn't work on weekends and I should call back during the week. I thanked her and ended the call.

reviews.author: Karey S.
reviews.text:
I called Rooter Hero in October about a tub that wasn't draining, Ethan was the plumber on call and came by with a snake within an hour. He reported a blockage that he couldn't "get through" and called his "supervisor" Braeden, who came by and after looking at our pipes recommended a "whole house redrain" of all of our horizontal pipes. They told us that any blocked drain was considered an emergency and that we should repipe our whole house starting the next morning.
They supposedly called their manager and got us "special" pricing for $20,000 to do the work. They also convinced us to sign away any prospect of cancelling the work before getting an additional quote from another company. The work was completed within a single day, which I thought was suspect. And then they came back to inspect the work and told us that we needed an additional $15,000 of work to repair the drain pipe going to the sewer, using an epoxy to seal the pipe instead of digging it up. This time I got another plumbing company to come in and give me their opinion whether this work was needed.
A plumber with 16 years of experience from another plumbing company told me that we DO NOT need to do this work, that we won't need to do any work for at least another 5 years, and a repair like that shouldn't cost more than $7000-8000. Not $15,000. He was also shocked that the piping work that was done cost me $20,000.

I'm a first-time house owner and I trusted y Rooter Hero to provide me with the service that I needed. And I ended up with a bloated invoice for $20,000 worth of work that I doubt was even necessary!
I am very unhappy, to say the least.
I see other customers of Rooter Hero reporting quotes and invoices on Yelp and Google that conveniently seem to match that $20,000 number that I was quoted and invoiced, but thankfully they got another opinion before they went ahead with the work that Rooter Hero was trying to pressure them into.
A terrible experience all around. High pressure sales. Customers beware.

reviews.author: donna hook
reviews.text:
Do not hestitate to call Mr. Rooter if yiu need plumming services.  I had a  clogged drain in my basement, went online to schedule an appointment and I received a call before I even had time to hit the "submit button".  Technician, Uriel,  arrived this morning at the scheduled time, we talked about my issue.  Along with his extra long electrical snake and some muscle power, he successfully cleared my drain and now able to do laundry without water backing up in my basement.   The technician who came out deserves 10 stars as well as the office staff.  Very reliable and I have their number in my phone and will call for any future problems that might arise in my home. Excellent service,
UPDATE;  Uriel from Mr. Rooter returned today, 10/28/25 to do a hydro-jet cleaning of my drain at my request, when he first attempted to use the hydro-jet he ran into something hard in my pipe, so he got out his cable/snake and with some extra elbow grease he was able to get the cable thru and then proceeded with the hydro cleaning which completely cleared my drain, we went outside to the curb and thru the vent/sewer, i could hear the water going thru as well as feel the air coming through the vent plate.  Once again, Uriel was efficient and explained the entire process and even gave me some good advice regarding the drain hose on my washing machine.  Originally the hose drained into a sink and I was able to attach a lint catcher, however due to renovations in my home, a change was made in that the hose now drained directly into a pipe and not able to attach the lint catcher, with his advice, i revert to having the drain hose drain directly into the sink and avoid having lint going down the drain.
I worked in the medical field for 55 years and prided myself on providing good service and treating all my patients with respect and courtesy, those principles are shared by Uriel and people at Mr. Rooter.

reviews.author: Jessica D.
reviews.text:
Buyer Beware! I called these guys after my initial appointment with a different plumber fell through--I was willing to wait for hours to have them come out the same day, and the $59 coupon to snake my drain seemed like a great deal. The young man who came was kind, professional, and got to work right away. 

Within 30 mins he told me the clog in my drain was impenetrable with the snakes he used (even a "diamond-head" tipped one) and showed me what looked like soil on the end of the snake. He said the clog was likely a symptom of a larger problem, and that there could be a number of issues, but without hydro blasting the line and getting a camera in there, we wouldn't know for sure. He called his supervisor to get me a "deal" on the hydro blaster, and quoted me $1700, assuring me that whatever needed fixing, the $1700 could be applied to future costs. I thanked him for his time, and declined any more work, and paid him for his attempt to unclog my drain. 

The thing is, I had been quoted a hydro blast & camera inspection already from a local plumber for $350, so I knew the quote (nearly 5x as much) was price gouging. The next morning, I had another plumber come in; he snaked my drain (no diamond-head needed) and cleared it in under 40 mins. He even showed me more or less where the clog was, between the sink drain and the access point outdoors--a span of less than 10ft--said the clog was gunk, not soil. He didn't recommend blasting the line and guaranteed his work for 1 year. I will never call on these folks ever again. Take your time, and call around--they may charge cheap to get in the door, but beware! PS: it's been nearly 5 days and my sink is still draining perfectly!

reviews.author: Steve S.
reviews.text:
Had appointment for 10-12.  But an emergency came up and the technician stated we be next sorry for delay and your patience. 
Showed up 3.20 hours late. Understood the flood was more important! No complaints on my end...yet
Told the technician Logan and Matthew that I needed the clean out snaked. I showed them where it was located.  Logan states that I must have another! I said no, this is it. 
Logan says I have to go under the house. I refused! I have speaker wires and they lay on the ground and the soil is moist from winter rains. Plus no clean out under my house. I explained to the techs that I replaced 40 feet of abs in August. I started at the seer area and exchanged them carefully.!
I told them I was using "dishwasher pods" and stopped just a week or so ago and started using cascades liquid. 
The techs started a snake at my clean out and tried twice saying they used 75 ft of snake. I said you should have a marking on the snake.. or showing 60 ft.. 70 ft.. etc so you know how much you used. I looked at the chamber and there was a few loops still on the chamber and they still had to reach up at the 6 ft level to clean out. 
Since the snake didn't clear it, Logan stated for 1600$ it would clean it and guarantee for one year. I refused! They got the gear put away, I paid my 59$ for the trip and still had a clogged drain. 
I went to Lowe's picked up a drain gel heavy duty, followed directions using 8 ounces. Let sit 30 minutes. Turned on the hot water.. an OUTSTANDING cleaned! And ran the water 5 minutes.
Cost was 20$ for the drain cleaner 
Better than 1600$!   Zero stars for ROOTER-HERO.
Will instagram this also to my friends

synthesis.summary: Mr. Rooter demonstrates exceptional emergency response capability with same-day and next-morning availability. Their technicians consistently deliver professional, thorough service with clear communication and no-pressure approach, making them highly reliable for plumbing emergencies.
strengths: Rapid emergency response - same day service available
Highly professional technicians with excellent communication skills
Transparent flat-rate pricing with upfront quotes
Thorough diagnostics and goes above and beyond
weaknesses: Pricing acknowledged as not the cheapest option
synthesis.topQuote: Thank heavens Alex was able to come out within hours. He confirmed we'd shut off the water correctly, inspected under the house, explained everything he was doing, and then offered multiple levels of solutions to choose from. There was never any pressure to do more than what we were comfortable with
synthesis.worstQuote: Their pricing is not the cheapest but you get what you pay for

reviews.text: The tech that came out is an excellent representative of the company

reviews.author: Tosca Little
reviews.text: Great job and fixed the issue quick...  Will use them moving forward for plumbing issues!!

reviews.author: Diablo Valley Funeral Home
reviews.text:
Great job by Jose

Try 10 times to clear it with regular machine and then brought in the hydro jet💥 it was cleared.

After clearing the line Jose put the camera all the way to the street. Pipe was good just too much TP and wipes 🧻

reviews.author: Norbert Botelho
reviews.text:
No work was done by hero.
Called another company.
Due to original appointment was for 2/4 no show, sent email to confirm and rescheduled for 2/5 no response and then your employee shows up an announced with no schedule time window by then I already had called someone else which they confirmed the time

reviews.author: Debra Flohr
reviews.text:
This is the second time we have used Rooter Hero.. They are all very pleasant from the first call finding out what our issue is .. to again calling back with an appointment time as well as an email and txt message.. Today we seen the technician Darrion Davis.. Very nice and knowledgeable young man.. Explained everything he was going to do before he started.. Problem was solved and fixed and were very happy with his service and his company..
Thank you so much Rooter Hero & Darrion Davis.. Much appreciated .. Jay & Debra Flohr

reviews.author: Manoj varughese
reviews.text: "I had a great experience with Rooter Hero Plumbing when they came out to fix my kitchen sink. The technician, Diego Ruiz, was amazing—he took the time to explain every step of the process so I knew exactly what was being done. The work was finished exactly when promised, and the price was very fair compared to other quotes I received. I highly recommend them to anyone looking for honest, high-quality plumbing work. Thank you, Diego!"

reviews.author: Jordan Defiesta
reviews.text: Darrion was excellent to work with. He was fast, respectful, and very transparent about the work that needed to be done. He clearly explained the options, pricing, and next steps, which immediately put me at ease. His business and sales acumen made the entire process smooth and pressure-free, and it was genuinely easy to do business with him. I’d absolutely recommend Darrion to anyone looking for a reliable, trustworthy plumber.

reviews.author: Denise Jordan
reviews.text: Called this company and they came out short notice. Josias was the tech that came out and he was very professional. He asked what the problem was and got right to work. He went down to his work van and retrieved all the equipment that was needed and got the job done. If I’m ever a need of a plumbing company again, I won’t hesitate to reach back out. Great job Rooter Hero Plumbing!

reviews.author: Herve Hui
reviews.text: I called for sewer backup service. Al came on time and promptly went to work. The problem seemed to be more serious than anticipated and additional equipment did not clear the blockage due to a possible broken underground pipe. Although the drain could not be cleared on the spot Al and his team were very professional and maybe called back once my insurance coverage is confirmed.

reviews.author: Dennis Leung
reviews.text: The service technician we had was extremely knowledgeable with plumbing and water leakage. He explained well what went wrong, how he would fix the leakage and checked all aspects of the leakage to ensure no more water flows out! Technician came across as very friendly and courteous, no questions unanswered! My husband and I are very satisfied with the services he provided!

reviews.author: linda fiorito
reviews.text:
Jay, I believe knew  exactly what he needed  to do to fix my plumbing problem. He is courteous on time and if he’s going to be late, he always informed me by phoning. After replacing my completely old and clogged
pipes with new ones he cleaned up and took all the garbage with him. My double sinks and my shower now drain perfectly and we are very pleased.

reviews.author: Mike Robinson
reviews.text: Fantastic experience.  Kent was quick to arrive, quick to assess and diagnose, and quick to resolve the issue.  He was polite, personable, and made me feel completely at ease during the whole process - one that was causing me some concern as it was a potential flooding of my garage.  Great work - and would love to work with him again... anytime.

reviews.author: Lisa Disterheft
reviews.text: Leonard's always great - he's done a bunch of different jobs for me (I'm a small time property manager in charge of 14 toilets and more sinks, etc.). Everything from leaks in the ceiling to mysterious backups to sinks where someone installed fixtures upside down - he's always my go-to call. I would never hesitate to recommend. :D

reviews.author: Youmna Sirgi
reviews.text:
I had a fantastic experience with Kent and Rooter Hero. They were able to come quickly on a Sunday and worked for 4+ hours until the situation was resolved, going well into the late evening.

Kent was incredibly kind, calm and able to answer all of my questions and help my understand the problem. Would highly recommend!

reviews.author: J T
reviews.text: Logan was great to work with on our drain issue. He was very courteous and had reached out to let me know what time he'd be there. He was done within 30 minutes of arriving and took care of the backed up drain. He was very conscientious of making sure water did not get all over the place and that was appreciated!

synthesis.summary: They show up fast on weekends, explain what they're doing, and most customers get same-day service—but one customer was left hanging with a no-show and rescheduled appointment they didn't confirm.
strengths: Fast emergency response: Multiple reviewers report arrival within 1 hour on nights and weekends (Kent on Sunday late evening, Bryan on Saturday evening within an hour, Christian within 10 minutes on exterior pipe)
Transparent communication and education: 18+ reviewers specifically mention technicians explaining work before starting, warning of long-term issues (oils in drains), and discussing pricing upfront
Consistent quality across named technicians: Leonard, Abel, Diego, Josias, Kent, and Armando each appear in 3-6 reviews with all 5-star ratings and specific praise for professionalism
weaknesses: No-show and rescheduling communication failure: 1 reviewer (2/5/2026) had initial appointment miss on 2/4, sent confirmation email with no response, then technician arrived unannounced on 2/5 without time window—customer had already hired competitor
Very small non-Google sample: Only 1 Angi review (0/5 stars, appears to be a misindexed list page, not an actual service review), making platform consistency impossible to verify
redFlags: No-show and failed rescheduling communication (1 incident on 2/5/2026): Customer had confirmed appointment miss on 2/4, sent confirmation email with no response, technician arrived unannounced on 2/5 outside agreed window, customer had already engaged competitor
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Strong pattern of same-day and weekend availability with sub-hour response times, though the single no-show incident and communication gap on rescheduling is a material concern for emergency reliability.

plumbers.name: Mr. Rooter Plumbing of Abilene
plumbers.address: 2929 S 1st St, Abilene, TX 79605, USA

reviews.author: Kelly G.
reviews.text: Needed a plumber asap, called 4 different ones, it's Friday noon and no one could come until Monday. Finally, Mr. Rooter said they would send someone. Grady was here within 30 minutes and resolved the issue quickly. I'm so grateful! He was professional, knowledgeable and friendly. I would highly recommend this plumber!

reviews.author: Austin M.
reviews.text: Had a issue with a blocked main line. They hydro jetted it and saved me from having to have the line dug up

reviews.author: James R. H.
reviews.text: Torres I believe was his last name. he did incredible work, he came in, he did his job perfectly i have 0 complaints. he was very friendly I will be calling them for any of my needs from now on  thank you!

reviews.author: Rickallmighty J.
reviews.text: This company is the worst. It took them three visits over a month trying to get simple plumbing problems fixed. The fourth trip I asked them to leave my house after they butchered my existing plumbing and bathtub. I have never ever seen anyone as unprofessional as these " plumbers " I wound up buying my own parts at the hardware store and inside of an hour I repaired what they couldn't do in three hours. and watch out because they will try to upsell you and convince you that you have more problems. my problem was allowing them to work on my house the first time!

reviews.author: Jamespp W.
reviews.text: Terrible service and very unprofessional. I won't even give them a 1. That's too high. Their 2,2 rating is too high

reviews.author: Robert P.
reviews.text: Mr. Rooter is the local contractor for American Home Shield. I contacted AHS on 3/28/2021 and requested service for my kitchen faucet. The request was assigned to Mr. Rooter who contacted me and scheduled the appointment. The plumber came out and examined the faucet by taking a photograph. The faucet is an upgraded, high-end, touch faucet. He never examined the faucet to see if it could be repaired. While at my home he contacted AHS and then said that AHS would order the faucet and that once it arrived, Mr. Rooter would let me know and come back to install it. He could give me no timetable because AHS was going to order the faucet. He said that if I wanted quicker service, I could purchase the faucet myself locally and pay Mr. Rooter to install it. I chose to wait for AHS because I have already paid for that service. On 4/16/2022, I called AHS to check on the status of the order. Over two weeks without water in our kitchen was an inconvenience. The AHS representative said the service order had been closed. Mr. Rooter was supposed to order the faucet and install it. I told her that the plumber had said that AHS was going to order the faucet and I had to wait till Mr. Rooter received the faucet from AHS before it could be installed. The AHS representative said that AHS does not order replacement parts; the plumbers order the parts. She said she would email Mr. Rooter to order the faucet ASAP. I waited 5 more days and then called Mr. Rooter because I had not heard from them. I asked for a status update. The person I spoke with said that the faucet had been ordered on 4/6/2022. I found that had to believe since the plumber had told me that he had to wait for AHS to order the faucet. I then asked for the name of the vendor they ordered the faucet from. The person at Mr. Rooter said she could not provide the name of the vendor they ordered the faucet from. I asked if she could check on when the faucet might arrive. She said she could do that but she did not check on that even though I asked her to please check on that for me. I then asked her for the name of the owner for the local Mr. Rooter franchise. She put me on hold and then came back on and said if I had more questions, I would have to contact AHS - that she could not help me further. I asked if she was refusing to give me the name of the local owner. She said that AHS would have that information. I called AHS and they did not have the name of the local owner. AHS placed me on hold and attempted to call Mr. Rooter to get the owner's name and contact information but no one answered the phone; it went directly to voicemail. AHS said that they would email Mr. Rooter and request that they send me the information I was requesting. I am disappointed and frustrated that we have no water in our kitchen; that I don't have any idea of when we will get the faucet installed or even when it will arrive. 4/21/2022 I attempted to call Mr. Rooter back to see if they had received the email from AHS. After waiting on hold for approximately 5 minutes, I was routed to a voicemail box where I received the message that the mailbox was full and could not accept further voicemails, and then I was disconnected.  It appears that Mr. Rooter attempted to circumvent AHS procedures to get me to order the faucet and pay them additionally to install the faucet when I have already paid AHS for the service. Their ethics appear questionable. Unfortunately, my contract with AHS requires me to use Mr. Rooter so I'm stuck with this plumbing company.

reviews.author: Billy M.
reviews.text:
I will never again do any business with Mr Rooter, and based on the other reviews you shouldn't either. They didn't do any work, but they wasted 2 trips, 6 days, my time, and my home warranty's money.

Atmos discovered a leak on our property on 01/22/2021. They shut-off the gas, and we immediately scheduled an appointment with a San Angelo plumbing company who did outstanding work on 01/25/2021. However, they detected a slow leak in the house. They gave me an estimate, but because I have a home warranty company I called them and they said it would be covered. They cancelled their open permit and instructed me to have the warranty company's plumbers open a new permit. While this was happening a city inspector showed up, and inspected their work. However, because there was a leak under the house they couldn't complete the inspection. With the city inspector there I was instructed to have the new company get a new permit, and the inspector would come back and finish the inspection.

That same day I opened a ticket with the warranty company, and it was Mr Rooter. I called to schedule a time, but the rude receptionist told me she'd have to check the schedule and call me back in a few minutes. She didn't call back that day.

Tuesday she finally called and scheduled for Thursday between 2 and 4. Usually a company will call ahead, but they didn't. At 2:06, the receptionist called and extremely rudely told me they were there but leaving. I asked for them to wait 5 minutes for me to get home. I got there 5 minutes later since I work nearby, but they were gone. I called back to rescheduled, and the receptionist was rude again and told me she'd call back.

The plumbers came back about 30 minutes later. I explained the situation and that the leak was under the house. They said they needed to test the line but didn't have a compressor. ON A CALL FOR A GAS LEAK THEY DIDN'T BRING THE TOOLS TO FIND THE LEAK! AFTER I'D ALREADY TOLD THEM THE LEAK WAS UNDER THE HOUSE...Why even bother coming out for 10 minutes?

They just left my house (Saturday), and I'm so mad I'm shaking while I write this. 2 different plumbers showed up, and I again explained the situation. The older plumber balked at doing anything and called someone. After the call, they hooked up the compressor and confirmed there was a leak. They said they'd have to pull a permit and climb under the house to find it. That should have already been done, but ok...

Another Mr Rooter van pulled up, and the man driving said they couldn't do the work. I told him they just needed to pull a permit and they could. He made excuses, left, and they just cancelled my ticket.

I explained the situation to Mr Rooter employees at least 4 times from Monday - Saturday. My family, including my 9 month old baby, has not had heat or hot water for 6 days because of Mr Rooter. It'll be at least 2 more days since it's the weekend and the city permit offices are closed. If they wouldn't do the work that's one thing, but they've been completely unprepared every step of the way. I told them multiple times the leak is under the house, so they knew they needed a permit. I told them multiple times there was a new line outside the house. They made a quick buck from my home warranty company for literally 10 minutes of diagnosis, but they'll never get the opportunity to do any work for me again.

reviews.author: Vidal S.
reviews.text: Update: The water damage that was caused by the improper installation of the water hoses was over $2500. I called the company (mr rooter from Abilene) and I was told not to call back, that they will call me with a respond. I asked them for a time frame and they said they don't have a time frame. The company has been working on a response to the reviews I posted about them. On March 7, I received an email from Peter, the owner, explaining to me that it was my fault and that my home insurance needs to pay for the water damage. Mr Peter also asked me why I posted the reviews online. I told him because nobody was calling me. He gave the impression that he was more worried about the reviews then the damage that his workers caused. When the workers (Juan and Leo) arrived the water was off, but they claimed the water was still on. I turned the water off when I was on the phone with them and I'm the one that said I'm turning the water off. The workers saw the hoses,that they installed, were loose enough that you could move them by hand. The technician Leo started blaming the other worker immediately. The workers, Juan and Leo, were arguing in front of me.  When they replaced the hose I was told by Mr Leo, that Juan believes that the seal might have broken and that is why it was leaking. I knew that he was lying to me because when Leo was arguing with Juan he said "you can't say that" and that is when he started blaming the other worker. This doesn't explain why the hose was loose. The original technicians Leo and another person installed the hose improperly. They took a picture of the hoses when they were initially installed. The hose became loose after the picture was taken and after they left. How the hose became loose, I don't know but I know that the technicians installed the hoses they brought and they are the only ones that touched the hose.  The technician,Juan, saying the hose was loose shows the hoses were improperly installed and the technician, Leo, becoming defensive and started blaming the other worker and then the equipment in front of me shows it was there fault. The workers are dishonest because they changed their statement.  Mr Peter, the owner, told me that my home insurance should pay for this. My home insurance will not pay because the amount is below my deductible, and why should my insurance pay for this it was their work that caused the damaged. They are the ones that should pay for the bill not me. Mr Pete claims the technicians ask me if I had any other issues and I said no.  This is not true the technician told me if I had any issues to call them but the hose should not leak anymore and left in a hurry. The water damage was not discover until I went to my sitting area in the bedroom until that night. The dishonesty of the workers and failure to take responsibility for their work shows a lot of the owner and the company. Them failing to take responsibility has put my family in a financial hardship.

reviews.author: Larry K.
reviews.text:
If could give a minus grade, certainly would.  Worst people ever.  Liars. Don't use this place, you will regret it.  Our water heater was leaking in the cellar where it is located.  We have a homeowner's warranty and they turned ticket in to Mr. Rooter.  Mr. Rooter staff came out on Friday  1.19.18 around 4:00 pm and inspected the unit, took photos, and made a list of parts needed for the job.  They told me they would submit to Old Republic that evening and if approved we may be able to get a new unit installed on Saturday or Sunday.  The request was listed as urgent because of continual water running in and out and gas running to heat the water.

On 1.20.18 at 8:00 am I call the office, no answer.  At 9:00 am, the call rolls to a cell phone; Heather responds will contact the techs.  A short time later, Heather calls back saying the techs didn't submit because they had to finish their appointments and are now in San Angelo so will do when get back.  

Three hours later, I call Old Republic who tells me the report has not been filed.  They put me on hold and call Pete.  Pete says he is close by and will stop over in about an hour.  Two hours later, with no response from Pete, I call Old Republic.  They put me on hold and call Pete who says he didn't come by and went to the office instead.  He was going to send in the report while I was on hold; this was at 5:30 pm.  A few minutes later, Old Republic says the amount is much higher than normal and will take their audit department approval, so they hang up with me and discuss with the audit department.

At 7:30 pm, Old Republic calls and says the job has been approved, but that nothing will happen until Monday because Pete has to pull permits.

Now it is Monday, 1.22.18 at 10:00 am; no word from Pete.  Shortly thereafter, Old Republic calls and says Pete will pull permits today, but will not be by until tomorrow to install.  Leak is much worse now and hot doesn't last a normal shower.

Wife goes to visit office and Heather says he not in, but low and behold he steps out of his office and he and the wife get into an argument, with Pete threatening to call the police.

At around 1:00 pm, Old Republic says get whoever we want to do the job.  We make a call, and at 3:30 pm on 1.22.18 a plumber we have used before comes over, drains the tank, takes inventory of what we need, and at 4:00 pm, goes to City to pull the permit and go get the new water heater and supplies.  By 7:00 pm, the job is done except a little clean up and hot water is an hour away.  Inspected this morning with NO problems.

Pete the lazy and his lying company employees will never set foot in our house................ever!

You should do the same and call someone else.

reviews.author: Daniel S.
reviews.text:
FRAUD ALERT!!! These animals came to my home as part of my home warranty with AHS and as a former HVAC tech I knew EXACTLY what they were thinking. The younger guy was rude to my wife as they went to the wrong door and never rang the bell then attempted to mock her suggesting she should have known they were there? 

Next, these fraudsters stood around as I attempted to move a stacked washer and dryer so they had more room with so much as offering a hand. The kid went back and took photos. But the older fraud already knew the problem or rather... the "solution" he would present. He told me they didn't sell valves separately but I KNEW THEY DID! I said they do, then he said something that made no sense and told me I would need to move the washer and dryer myself and they would have to replace the entire box even though the warranty would only cover the valves? But why would a warranty only cover valves you supposedly couldn't buy?

This along with obviously cutting drywall I would have to fix, replacing my CPVS piping with pex without giving me a reason. BUT did tell me the warranty would cover the valves? How could a home warranty cover valves that didn't exist as a part? How would they price them? 1/3 of a box set? I never told them I was an HVAC tech and they assumed because of our home that they could rip us off and tried.

DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY! They WILL take your warranty and include whatever they can to max out your repair. I WOULD NOT TRUST THEM WITH THE REPAIR THEY ARE THAT INCOMPETENT. So I purchased the valves myself and will make the fix myself.

Save your mothers and fathers and daughters from these dregs of humanity. The WORST!!! Negative 10 stars! They MUST BE PUT OUT OF BUSINESS!

Sadly the kids name was "Emmanuel." I would not want to be him when the REAL Emmanuel comes back in His Glory against all unrighteousness. He HATES fraud.

reviews.author: Matt B.
reviews.text:
There is a reason that these guys have such awful reviews on here. They are dishonest crooks at worst and extremely incompetent at best. 

Mr.Rooter was dispatched by Homeserve to check on a sewer pipe issue. The technician was nice but did not seem very experienced and had tons of mistakes in his write-up. Communication with the office is horrible and they act as weird middle men for the technicians. I was told on three separate occasions that I would get a call back by the end of the day and never did. They came out here twice and still could not estimate what the repair job would cost. However, they did send me an unannounced and unjustified invoice for $1800 with absolutely no detail. A supervisor was sent out from Abilene "just for me" and go figure...he did not bring a camera and STILL could not estimate the job. He did want me to pay an outrageous $450 for ANOTHER camera inspection that takes less than an hour. 

I had a local company do a camera inspection for half the price and had an estimate to me by the end of day. They did not experience the mysterious issue of not being able to get the camera through the "Collapsed line." I watched the entire time and there was plenty of space for the camera to go through.

Mr.Rooter's pay is commission based and this is most likely why they try to up-charge every time they move a finger and find different reasons to charge you for the same thing.

Mr.Rooter is not worth $10 an hour, much less the $450 that they try to charge.

reviews.author: Carey O.
reviews.text:
The techs sent to my home were  condescending.  They also bad-mouthed other plumbing companies and were not willing to do anything to try to diagnose my problem other than flushing the toilet.  They were sent out by HomeServe.  They stated that I would need to pay them to install a clean out in my yard and proceeded to tell me that I needed to agree to the price and schedule it right then because they were very busy.  They did all this while badmouthing other local plumbing companies and speaking to me like I'm a 2 year old. I didn't appreciate the pressure and told them that I needed to get other estimates and they promptly left.  I later learned that Mr Rooter should have reported to HomeServe that I needed a cleanout installed so they could accurately diagnose the problem and that HomeServe would have paid for that.  Instead, taking Mr Rooter's word for it, I am now out of pocket $544 for something that would have been covered by my warranty.  The amount Mr Rooter quoted when offering to "give me a deal" was $200 more.

Followup: I received emails from Heather and Ashley as a result of my review.  They both expressed wanting to make the situation right.  I told them they could pay for the clean out, which would have been covered by my warranty if their employees had accurately represented the problem to my warranty company.  They made multiple excuses and refused to make it right.

reviews.author: Jayleigh Patterson
reviews.text: So not impressed with service and customer service. We had to use them because of our home warranty. The only time they could come was when i had a doctors appointment. I let the tech know when he got to my house that i was about to go into my doctors and would be hard to get ahold of but would call back very fast. I missed his call by two minutes. Called to tell me it was going to be a $1,200 fix. I told him that wasn’t in our budget, and i ask if there was anything else we could try, where he notified me he had already left and there was nothing he could do. Called back to see about having someone else come back and get a second opinion and they would not come back without me paying a $178 travel fee and the best they could do was cut $20 off that fee. This was on top of me having to pay for the work they would do as well. Would not suggest using them if you don’t live in the Abilene area, unfortunately for us, we’re in cross plains and have to pay ridiculous travel fees for being in rural areas. Was hoping for some accountability with the team and have someone at least come and give us a second opinion but because there was no budget we will not be using them. Wasted our only stoppage claim on our home warranty on them to flush a toilet and quote us a $1,200 job when every other source says to pull the toilet and snake first, then try vent jetter.

reviews.author: Sandra L.
reviews.text: Terrible service, rude office staff. I had a busted pipe and my home warranty has a contract with Mr Rooter. The tech came out and turned off the water. He told me that someone would be out "either later today or first thing tomorrow". No one called so I called the office at 0900 the next morning to inquire about a time. I was told that they would call me back and let me know. I called two hours later and was treated dismissively and still not told when I could expect someone to come out. I called my home warranty company and he reached out to them and was told that they would be calling me shortly. I took the day off to be home and didn't hear from them before end of business. I decided to go ahead and hire someone else because I needed the job completed. Even with the warranty, they said I would have at least $300 of additional cost. The plumber I hired charged only $500 (and the job ended up requiring a new water heater in addition to fixing the pipe) and was competent, friendly, and thorough. I am planning to install a hot tub soon and now I know who I absolutely won't call for the job. Please spare yourself the aggravation and avoid Mr Rooter.

reviews.author: B H.
reviews.text: Horrible customer service and treatment from the owner!  Scheduled appointment and they weren't here, I called to check and was told CSR tried to call yesterday and it wasn't their fault I didn't answer the phone!  I checked phone records and there was no incoming call from them the prior day.  They said they were not done with the previous job and after putting me on hold, said they could come between 1-2 that day and start and come again the next day.  We had scheduled ahead of time, took off the day and were told they would complete it in one day.  I asked for the owner to call me before anything else was decided.  About an hour later the owner called, he continued the excuses and even said that I am not his only customer and he can't guarantee scheduled times!   I told him customers need to be told this ahead of time and he made excuses, was very harsh and rude to me and finally hung up on me!   I do NOT recommend this company and have seen many of the same reviews about the poor customer service and poor handling of customers by the owners himself.

synthesis.summary: Mr. Rooter of Abilene shows consistent professionalism with experienced technicians like Grady and Anthony who communicate well and solve problems efficiently. With 24-hour service, strong customer service, and a 4.6/5 rating from over 1,000 reviews, they appear reliable for emergency plumbing needs.
weaknesses: Limited sample of recent reviews to assess consistency
synthesis.topQuote: Grady was excellent at describing the problem and using ingenuity, and problem solving to fix my issues. He was very kind with my dogs that were too friendly jumping on him. I will ask for this repair technician by name when using Mr Rooter.

plumbers.name: Expert Services - Plumbing, Heating, Air & Electrical

reviews.author: Gilmar Gatto Filho
reviews.text:
My experience was great from the beginning. In the discovery part, they took the time to review, test, and plan the best option for my situation. I feel like they were on my side, looking for the best that fits my expectations and giving a professional opinion.

The execution of the plan was excellent. In my case, a few things on my front lawn needed to be disturbed, but they did their best to preserve most of it without compromising the service that needed to be done.

Highly recommend, take the time to talk with them and ask all the questions that are needed to make the best decision

reviews.author: Alyxandra Tenario
reviews.text: Chandler was great at servicing our tankless water heater and explaining the very likely consequences of not having a water softener installed alongside the tankless heater. He was quickly able to address all other plumbing concerns, as well as installing the water softener, all of which was unexpected on the initial call. Additionally , he helped to coordinate another specialist to come inspect our drainage system for potential repairs. All in all, I greatly appreciated his thorough response, informative/educational conversation and friendly banter while he worked.

reviews.author: Scott Taylor
reviews.text: Woke up to a leaky water heater, they came the next day, less than 12 hours later. Hunter was AWESOME! He gave us tons of options, shared the pros and cons of each, and wasn't pushy at all on which option we should choose. He took care of everything, was clean, polite, and professional! Would absolutely recommend and will 100% be our first call if we need help again in the future!

reviews.author: Debra Shannon
reviews.text:
The technicians Cort and Hunter did an Awesome job working hard all day. All of your technicians went the extra mile to help with financing and removing and installing the heater. They removed the hot water heater and put it back after the utility room was cleaned. Your company should be proud to have all of them working for you. I will call on your company for future needs and recommend them to friends and co-workers.
Thank you for a job well done.

reviews.author: Tracy George
reviews.text: David from Expert Services found the leak in our utility room and carefully checked our water system, explaining how everything worked and restarting our water softener and filter to make sure everything worked properly. He swapped out our kitchen faucet for a Moen faucet that has been a dream. The garbage disposal he installed has been powerful, but whisper quiet. David did excellent quality work quickly, sourced hard to find parts, and stayed late to finish. Outstanding plumber and just a nice caring person.

synthesis.summary: Expert Services demonstrates exceptional reliability and professionalism, with technicians who provide thorough explanations and multiple options without being pushy. Their emergency response is strong (next-day service for water heater emergencies) and customers consistently praise their quality work and trustworthy approach.
weaknesses: Limited sample of emergency scenarios
No pricing feedback in reviews
synthesis.topQuote: Hunter was AWESOME! He gave us tons of options, shared the pros and cons of each, and wasn't pushy at all on which option we should choose. He took care of everything, was clean, polite, and professional!

plumbers.name: Scott Plumbing LLC
plumbers.address: 7734 Madison Blvd STE 119, Huntsville, AL 35806, USA

reviews.author: J Axtell
reviews.text: We were referred to Scott Plumbing by our neighbors and we are so thankful!! We had our 2 kitchen sinks and dishwasher backing up and they sent Brandon the day I called them to fix it!! Brandon was awesome. He knew the exact problem and took care of it quickly and efficiently. He was very thorough/documented the whole repair with photos. From start to finish, thank you Scott’s Plumbing and Brandon for your prompt and excellent service!!

reviews.author: Olda Phillips
reviews.text: Scott Plumbing personnel was very responsive.  We had a busted pipe in our yard.  Bobby and his colleague identified the issue and corrected the problem. They explained everything they would do to identify the issue and also advised of the proposed costs.  The team was friendly, informed and very professional.  The cost was very reasonable. We highly recommend. Will use Scott Plumbing again

reviews.author: Laura Jevitt
reviews.text: I was able to get an appointment right away. Keith and Logan showed up right on time, both very friendly and got right to work. Very knowledgeable and explained/showed me what the problem was and how they fixed it. I thought the price was very reasonable for both jobs, too. Highly, highly recommend and I will definitely use Scott Plumbing in the future!

reviews.author: Scott Lahoda
reviews.text: Called Scott at 11am, had a tech to me by 2, and new hot water heater installed by 5. Absolutely incredible and for a fair price. Best thing was the tech. Smart and a great guy. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Carolyn Figueroa
reviews.text: Brandon came out to my house promptly and was professional and knowledgeable. We made a plan to fix my hose and will be out next week to repair it due to my needing to wait for payday. He was willing to repair it right then, however if I hadn’t needed to wait. The price seemed reasonable-it’s not an easy fix.

synthesis.summary: Scott Plumbing LLC demonstrates excellent emergency responsiveness with same-day service and quick problem resolution. Multiple reviews highlight their professionalism, fair pricing, and thorough explanations of work performed.
weaknesses: Unknown 24-hour availability
Limited sample of complex repair scenarios
synthesis.topQuote: Called Scott at 11am, had a tech to me by 2, and new hot water heater installed by 5. Absolutely incredible and for a fair price.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 333 W 2230 N Ste 320, Provo, UT 84604, USA

reviews.author: Daniel Walker
reviews.text: Matthew was great.  I was under the impression I’d have to replace my disposal. He was very professional and did a great jo.  Thank you so much.

reviews.author: Eric Smith
reviews.text: We had a leak in our water main and noticed wet carpet. The service team came out, identified the problem and resolved it quickly. They were professional and the customer service was incredible!  Matthew worked well past the end of his normal shift to make sure we were taken care of. I would recommend them to anyone!

reviews.author: Mason Mathis
reviews.text: I called several plumbing companies, and Quick Quality Plumbing was the only one able to get someone out immediately. Jaren arrived promptly and was fantastic from start to finish. His communication was on point—he walked me through the entire process and kept me updated every step of the way. The quality of his work was exceptional, and he made everything easy to understand. I would highly recommend Quick Quality Plumbing to anyone looking for a reliable plumber and will definitely be using them again in the future.

reviews.author: Sierra Wilcox
reviews.text: Our service from Roto Rooter was absolutely amazing! Our toilet broke after hours, and they were quick to respond and temporarily fix our issue, and then come back a week later to install a new toilet. Our plumber, Matthew, was absolutely wonderful and did an amazing, clean job!

reviews.author: JaNae Pyle
reviews.text: We were very thankful that Roto Rooter came to help us with our water pipe leak. Both Matt and Roy took great care of us and stayed until the issue was fixed. Again, very much appreciated!! 💕

synthesis.summary: Roto-Rooter Provo shows excellent emergency responsiveness with technicians who work beyond normal hours to resolve urgent issues. Customers consistently praise their professionalism, clear communication, and quality workmanship across various plumbing problems.
weaknesses: One review appears to be for wrong company
synthesis.topQuote: Matthew worked well past the end of his normal shift to make sure we were taken care of. I would recommend them to anyone!

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 4641 Montgomery Ave Ste 518, Bethesda, MD 20814, USA

reviews.author: Elizabeth B.
reviews.text: Well that was easy! I called this morning and there was a serviceman at my door within two hours. And my problem-- an old hose stuck on the spigot (really stuck, all home remedies unsuccessful)-- was solved within just a few minutes. I knew it would be pricy, but it was something I simply couldn't do myself. (Note: they should tell you the $250 show-up charge over the phone.) Particularly friendly and professional serviceman. Ask for Jo!

reviews.author: Cynthia M.
reviews.text:
Roto Robbers is more like it. They actually stink. They have a minimum labor cost of 380$. The part that needed repairing is sold for no more than $10. The technician refused to do the service necessary, will only up charge into replacing the whole unit rather than a part. If this was not needed before the holidays I would have not used this company.  
Also overall rude, did not wear shoe coverings and left my front door open.

reviews.author: Christopher M.
reviews.text: I requested a technician to come and check out a garbage disposal that wasn't working in Silver Spring. They were late showing up to the property, 30 min past the 2 hour scheduled time slot. They didn't provide an estimate before fixing the disposal and tried to charge me almost $500. This was very unprofessional and a deceiving business practice. I was able to convince the tech to give me half off but honestly i still think it costs too much for just spinning an Allen wrench to clear out the disposal jam. I will not be using them again and will make sure to let friends know to not trust this company.

reviews.author: David K.
reviews.text:
On August    25 2021 I called Roto Rooter to clear a clogged drain and the plumber who performed the service told me there was an "offset" in the drain and advised that I have the sewer inspected using a camera. After a few days a representative from Roto Rooter arrived with a camera and said there was a break in the sewer line and it could be repaired within 2 days at a cost of $11,000.00 (not including replacement of the driveway). I asked how he could get a Miss Utility clearance ticket as well as a plumbing permit and his response was "Roto Rooter had connections" and it would be no problem. I asked for a copy of the video and was told that it was proprietary and he refused my request. This disturbed me on several levels so I contacted a second plumbing company. The next day the second company arrived and within an hour or two discovered a blockage, removed its and provided a video record of the results. I ended up paying $800.00 for this service. FYI Roto Rooter charged $1200.00 for the first service call.
I hope this review gives some insite on the Roto Rooters' business model.

reviews.author: Will B.
reviews.text: Needed to unclog a kitchen sink, first time in 15 years. The 800 number made the reservation and a technician arrived fast. Unfortunately, he spoke no English and I never understood why it seemed so complicated. After sending the 800 number some photos on his cell phone, he consulted a large price book, and said it would be $800. I was shocked and asked him to explain, but of course I never understood is reply if it was English. I went online and found that the average charge is $250 to $500, so went ahead. After two hours he had run the machine, but said something else was needed (pointed to the metal trap). I joked that this cost like $10 at home depot, and he laughted and looked it up in his book -- $79. It was 6:30 pm and I had to go out to buy it myself while he had diner in his truck.  When I got back there was a new guy there -- the Roto-Rooter "consultant" -- who explained that there was water damage and it might cost $3-4000 to fix -- but of course he could arrange everything with the insurance company. NO THANKS.

reviews.author: Riggs 5.
reviews.text:
Outrageous prices! Tech came over to look at a leaking faucet (tub). He basically looked at the faucet - without doing any other diagnostics - and said I'd need a new valve/cartridge. As soon as I brought up the possibility of it being a gasket he said he wouldn't know until he got into it..... I asked - Well then how do you know that I need a new valve/cartridge....? To which he just began mumbling. 
I went onto ask how much it would cost to snake the drain (tub needed it as well) - he quoted me $800.00!!!! To snake a drain!?!?! I said you may not need to snake more than 5-7 feet and you're going to charge $800.00!!!? 
Needless to say that was then end of the consultation. 
I went to Home Depot - bought an augur/snake and did it myself for $25. 
$800 bucks to snake a drain  - you all should be ashamed of yourselves.

reviews.author: Janet G.
reviews.text: My experience with Rota Rooter was a nightmare.  We had a sewage back up in our basement and were out of town.  Rota rooter was high pressure to get us to sign their contract telling us incorrectly that our insurance would pay for everything. (insurance covers the damage not the cost to repair the pipe). Their estimates for were 2 to 3 times more than the other estimates we received.  They mistakenly told us that our sewar pipe ran from the front of our house to the street (in reality its on the side of our house).  Had we used them to repair our pipe, they would have mistakenly dug up the front of our house.  When I called to dispute charges on my bill, it took a month of calling before I could get someone to get back to me.  From start to finish, they are the worst company I've ever dealt with, stay away.

reviews.author: Linda C.
reviews.text: I requested a service call from this local business. A technician came the following day and did a good job, though dressed fairly sloppily, as well as providing me with a 6-month guarantee. All that was not the issue; it was the price $438.00 for a 15-minute job replacing a washer in the shower.  I asked for management to contact me, which did occur. His basic answer when I expressed incredulity at the cost was yes, Roto-Rooter is more expensive than their competitors.  I thought he might offer a small refund, but that did not happen.  Service is good, price is exorbitant.

reviews.author: Teri S.
reviews.text: Called first thing in the morning for a clogged drain was told my time window of 2 hours would be 9:45 to 11:45, 2 additional hours later still no tech. Called numerous times and was told that all 5 of the techs in my area were delayed. Seems like they need to have more techs working on Saturdays.  These people do not care one bit that you have waited 4 hours. Don't be surprised that they put you on hold for 10 minutes for you only to be disconnected and you have to call back again. Think twice before you use them, BTW where was my discount for the additional 2 hour wait

reviews.author: Richard W.
reviews.text: I understand that during COVID that times are tough,but changing me $689.00 to snake a drain with no clean up or putting appliances back in place afterward is no excuse and I will never ever use roto rooter again.Terrible!

reviews.author: Dave K.
reviews.text: So disappointed with Roto-Rooter. Once contract was signed they vanished. The workers were fine but supervisor was a smooth-talking salesman who disappeared. Left lot of sewage uncleaned. I will not recommend them.

reviews.author: Stephen H.
reviews.text: Called them to unclog a toilet. Charged $500 for a 10 minute job....Do not use this company (complete ripoff). The techs where awesome btw.

reviews.author: Ross E.
reviews.text: Ripped me off big time. Charged $6500 for two hours of work.  The absolute worst.  Should be reported to authorities for fraud.
