synthesis.summary: Roto-Rooter Bethesda demonstrates excellent emergency responsiveness with multiple customers praising 4-hour completion times and immediate dispatch. Their technicians are consistently described as professional, transparent with pricing, and skilled at both routine plumbing and complex water restoration work.
strengths: Exceptional speed - complete jobs in 4 hours from initial call
Skilled technicians for complex repairs and water damage
Professional customer service without sales pressure
weaknesses: Limited sample size of recent reviews
All reviews are 5-star, may indicate review filtering
synthesis.topQuote: They were fast, polite and got completed the replacement of our main water shutoff did valve in a total of 4 hours. That include the initial call, dispatching a tech and completing the job.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 1077 W Boylston St Unit 201, Worcester, MA 01606, USA

reviews.author: Dick D.
reviews.text: I had a sink that was very slow draining and has been a problem off and on for many years but the usual baking soda/vinegar fix wasn't working any more. I called them at 8:30am and was told someone would be coming before 1pm. I received a call about 10:40am saying we were next on the list. I received another call about 12:45 pm saying the technician was delayed a bit on the previous job but we were still next. I received another call around 1:30 saying the technician was on his way. He was here a few minutes later. Aaron came in, diagnosed the problem, and told me the cost and about their guarantee. After I agreed, he brought in all the necessary equipment and got to work and was able to locate and remove a large clog pretty far down the drain pipe (maybe 10-15 feet) in fairly short order. Aaron was very professional and friendly. He knew exactly what to do in order to remedy our issue. Although I may have been able to get someone else at a cheaper price, I felt that I would give Roto-Rooter a try since I've heard their adds for years and this is what they're known for. After the clog was removed, he verified that the other drains all functioned normally and then cleaned the area. I feel it was money well spent. My issue was resolved within a few hours from the initial call to them, I was kept up to date until the technician arrived and, due to Aaron's many years of experience (I believe he said he has worked for them for 11 years) and training, he was able to complete the job quickly. Based on this experience I would not hesitate to recommend them to others.

reviews.author: Scott W.
reviews.text: John #2786 did a great job! Thanks to Mike as well for all his hard work and assistance!

reviews.author: Kristin M.
reviews.text: Had an amazing experience with them! Sergey fully explained everything to me before I committed to the service and he had everything fixed in less than an hour. I will always use them in the future!

reviews.author: George E.
reviews.text: As a follow-up to my review.  The next day I called a guy from Sterling.  He unclogged the drain AND pumped the septic system for $465.  Roto Rooter wanted $432 just to snake the shower drain.  The guy from Sterling also showed up right when he said he would.    As Brian D. said DO NOT USE THEM.

reviews.author: Brian D.
reviews.text: DO NOT USE THEM.  They called me this afternoon 15 minutes before my schedule window was to start and cancelled the service for the second time in 2 days.  Both times they simply indicated that they couldn't get anyone out.  I called to speak to a supervisor and was transferred to someone who picked up the phone and sounded like she was at home and told me she wasn't a supervisor.  Then on my second call to a supervisor the guy tried to end the call with "if you need something in future, please call us" after I told him I would never call for service again.  By the way, I called another company who is sending out someone this afternoon.  THE SAME DAY.

reviews.author: Brian R.
reviews.text:
We called Roto Rooter after the sink in one of our bathrooms was slightly clogged (not fully, but water was backing up). A technician was sent to our house a couple of hours later and began working. The technician began work on the bathroom without laying down any protective gear (such as plastic), and walked around the house in dirty boots. After some time, the technician was unable to unclog the drain in the bathroom that was experiencing the problem, so he walked into our second bathroom and began attempting to unclog the drain from this bathroom. As a side note, this bathroom was having absolutely no problems prior to the technician working on it. The technician began making an extreme mess on the vanity in the bathroom with dirty water, and I asked him why he did not cover it with plastic (especially because it is a wood vanity). He began giving my attitude, used the F-word at me, and was extremely rude. At this point, I told the technician to immediately leave my house because he was no longer welcome inside. The technician left the house with the original bathroom in worse condition than when he started, and the bathroom that had no problems was also now backed-up. A technician who later came to my house actually discovered that this technician had physically damaged my sink and drain pipe in the bathroom that had original problems, and I was told that it would be my expense to get this fixed. 

At this point, I called the dispatcher and asked to speak to a supervisor to tell them what had happened, and to get somebody else out to my house to fix the problem since there are no usable bathrooms in the house. I was told that a supervisor would call me back in one hour, but after an hour, there was no call. I called the dispatcher again, and was told that the original one hour timeframe was incorrect and that a supervisor would be calling me "eventually." I eventually received a call from the supervisor 2.5 hours later. The supervisor told me that he would be sending out another technician to my house within the next hour to address what the first had broken (and that I should be receiving a call from the dispatcher within a couple of minutes of the estimated arrival time). Another 2.5 hours pass and it is now 9:45 PM, and I finally receive a call from the DISPATCHER that the technician is on the way. The technician took another 1.5 hours to actually arrive to my house. 

He began working on both bathrooms, and attempted to do the exact same thing as the first technician. The technician attempted to unclog the drain for another hour, could not do anything, and left my house just as the first had. It is now nearly midnight, and both bathrooms are still unusable in my house. 

I once again called the dispatcher and asked to speak to a supervisor, but was told one would call me in the morning. What kind of 24-hour service is this if I cannot speak to someone about a major problem? 

The next morning, the supervisor called me back, and informed me that they would need to call out a plumber to my house (to fix a SIMPLE DRAIN CLOG), and the plumber would have to open up one wall to gain access to the pipe. I politely said absolutely no thank you to the supervisor, and I made sure that I would not be billed for either of the technicians coming to my house because they accomplished nothing, and caused more damage than there originally was. The supervisor informed me that they would indeed be sending me a bill for both technicians. 

I called another local company after this conversation. They sent a technician to my house one hour later, and within TEN MINUTES, both drains were unclogged (and at a fraction of the price Roto Rooter charged me). If Roto Rooter actually has the dignity to send me a bill for their completely useless work, I plan to send them the invoice directly back, along with a letter from an attorney and the invoice from the plumbing company that actually solved my problem.

Moral of the story: never go with Roto Rooter. They are inappropriate, charge a fortune, and simply cannot run a successful business under their current status. You're much better off going with a local company. It's time that everybody hears about their terrible business practices.

EDIT: I'd like to follow up to this review. I emailed the Roto-Rooter agent who commented on my review within hours, and he has yet to follow up with me two weeks later. I hope this is another testament to Roto Rooter's lack of customer care.

reviews.author: Jenn G.
reviews.text: My toilet got clogged on Christmas Eve day and I called Rotorooter at about 4 PM and they gave me a 4 hour window up until 8 PM.  At first that seemed ok but it was all I could do to get the toilet fixed because there were 5 of us and only one non working toilet.  After sitting around the house by myself because the rest of my family was celebrating Christmas Eve I get a call at 8 and they tell me that suddenly nobody can fix my toilet tonight.  I was not happy. I was then told that the earliest that anybody could get there is the next day between 10 AM and 2 PM.  I stated that that was unacceptable because I have 3 kids who live at this house and that I was told that it was going to get fixed today.  Then at 10 PM I get another call that stated that a tech just became available but he could not be there until about 11PM...on Christas Eve. Then I get another call from the tech and he stated that he was over 2 hours away and that that night would not work.  The tech (Steve #4171) was awesome!  He told me that even though my home was not in his area he would be at my house at 8 AM on Christmas Day.  He would have been if dispatch didnt send him the wrong address.  Once he got there and did the work Steve was great and I was very appreciative of his great customer service and he did a great job over all.  I am very displeased with dispatch and the runaround that I received. I asked to speak with a manager and I was told one would call me back but nobody ever called me back except the same terrible dispatcher that was no help at all. I am very displeased with Rototrooter and will never use this service again.

reviews.author: Edward F.
reviews.text:
I called RotoRooter because my new kitchen drain line was clogged, I'll review my install plumber later, and I thought since I am doing a lot of other work in this place it'll be quicker/more economical for someone else to snake the line. Turns out that Roto-Rooter will come to your house on a Sunday without any extra cost...in fact they'll come for free to give you an estimate on the work. BONUS!!!! So I asked the person on the phone what a typical kitchen snake job like mine would be, I asked 3 times mind you. 

Here's the skinny. The guy shows up and I show him the kitchen line and tell him the plumbing history (very brief,  it's all new except the vent and line to the street). He says, "I can snake that for you right now and give you a 6-month guarantee for $399". I was like jeez that is a lot of money, is that typical? He says "We have a flat rate for this service" WHAT??? If there is a flat rate, then why didn't the guy on the call tell me it when I asked 3 TIMES? RR guy says "Well, I think they want us to get our foot in the door, because once we are in, people are likely to just say go ahead"

EFF YOU ROTO-ROOTER. I asked the guy to leave. I couldn't snake the line myself which is an issue for the future, BUT, I went onto the roof, poured drano down the stink pipe, rinsed with hot water, and 7 minutes later...NO CLOG. Total repair like $8 in material maybe, some ingenuity, and 2 hours. Way less than a flat rate of $399.

DO NOT CALL ROTO-ROOTER

reviews.author: Alex T.
reviews.text:
$840 for clogged washing machine drain (Sudbury, MA).

Called roto-rooter to fix a clogged washer drain, no other drains in the house were clogged. The guy showed up 2 hours late, and quoted me $560 to snake the line, I knew it was very high but agreed, I was in a situation where I had to get the line cleared asap, unfortunately he too realized my situation. Once he snaked the line, he proudly claimed that had no effect on the clog. He had snaked the wrong line, and then claimed there were 2 clogs, based on no evidence. This time he again wanted to snake a line not connected to washer, had to argue for 10 mins to just snake the washer line and not go looking for clogs in random pipes. He then made a claim washer line needed special tool, and his tool got beat in last job. At that point I asked him to leave, he then claimed he can snake the line, which he did and that cleared the clog. Unfortunately the whole endeavor cost me $850, and he made complete mess of my basement, that is now stinking of sewage.

I had used roto-rooter a few years back, and their service was satisfactory then, so did not care to shop around. This proved to be very expensive. They are not professional plumbers, but professional exploiters. 

#never-roto-rooter

reviews.author: Ay J.
reviews.text:
The heavy snow this year put my sump pump under extra pressure once the melting snow turned the ground area into small creeks, so the sump pump decided to quit. The result of this failed sump pump was water in my finished basement.  

My call to Roto-Rooter has resulted in a new sump pump and in water removal from my basement. The plumber was friendly and professional. He arrived at the scheduled time, and the work was completed for the price that he quoted. While the price was a little higher than another serviceman who looked at the pump, I valued the professionalism and the view toward quality that the Roto-Rooter plumber demonstrated. 

Similarly, the water removal was done with professionalism, and the workers always arrived when scheduled. I also was impressed with the attention to quality. There was a clear desire to ensure that the job was well done. The process was explained to me thoroughly so that I understood why Roro-Rooter needed to do each part of the clean up process. 

I am pleased with their service and anticipate that I will be pleased with the results over the long run. 

ayj
Granby, MA

reviews.author: Calindy S.
reviews.text: I can't say enough good things about this company. I was out of town when I had a main sewer line clog and back up while my house sitter was there. I had a local company come in and they quoted me $1700 just to determine the problem and clear the line and told me sewage would go all over my my basement and I would likely need a clean up company to come in. This all happened at 9pm. I needed a second opinion and called Roto-rooter and they were out within the hour. Ron called me and went over the cost right away with me and assured me sewage would not go all over my basement. My flat fee was less than 1/3 of the other plumber!!! Ron called me when it was done and told me a tech would be in contact to come put a camera down the pipe to give me more info on the issue in a few days. I was so relieved!!  What was a super stressful situation and about to cost a ton of money was made less stressful by the quick response of Roto-rooter and the reassuring and friendly words of the tech. Would highly recommend!

reviews.author: Nancy H.
reviews.text:
I called Roto- Rooter yesterday to unclog a toliet line.  A technician did come promptly and attempted to unclog.  Last night I noticed the water was still going down very slow.  I called Roto-Rooter back this morning and explained the situation.
The person I spoke to on the phone was very nice, polite and accomodating.
This afternoon Aaron came and evaluated the situation.  It turns out he had to remove the toliet and found the clog way down the line.  He put on a new wax ring and replaced the toliet.  He tested the line several  times to be sure it worked. Aaron was very informative about the situation.  He also mentioned we may want to try the Roto-Rooter product that you use 1-2x month to keep lines clear.
Aaron also explained the fee.  We got a refund from yesterday's bill on todays.
I was very pleased with the service today.
Aaron was very knowledgable, polite and neat.  
I would ask for him again if I needed Roto- Rooter service.
Thank you.
Nancy Hesselton

reviews.author: Rameel R.
reviews.text:
Terrible experience! "24/7 service" - such a lie! 

Called on Thursday to get a water heater replaced, they gave me a time frame of Friday 12-4. I call on Friday morning at 11 to confirm and they said they'll be there. I get a call on Friday at 2:45 letting me know that the tech will be there in one hour, which would be perfect! Then at 4:00 I get a call saying tech will not be able to make it, no reason given. I ask to speak to manager and get completely ignored! How is it 24 hour service any day if they book appointments and continuously cancel as no shoes! 
 They offer 10% off and reschedule for Saturday.... Guess what! Same exact thing Saturday, I call around 1:00 to confirm they're coming and get told no tech is available!!

Overall wasted two days and about 7 hours of my time waiting for a tech to come! Dispatch and customer service is very rude and not professional! What am I gonna do with 20% off if my poor tenant has cold water!

reviews.author: Denise L.
reviews.text: Out of all the plumbers in the area I called, Roto-Rooter is the only one who would show up right away. I had just moved in to my house after it was being fixed due to a fire a year ago. The guy was super professional, and knowledgeable. I ended buying their very expensive "maintenance" liquid mix and it works great.  The whole thing came to $700 which was shocking to me. I knew my ins co would pay for it as it was related to the fire incident and not having had water running through the pipes for a long time. If I had been responsible for the invoice, I would've freaked out. That's why I knocked off a star. So yes, they are good, reliable and guarantee their work (the guy came back a week later to fix it again at no charge - not his fault, it was bound to happen due to the severe clogging) BUT they are super expensive. Before asking them to fix your problem, ask for an estimate.

reviews.author: Meredith L.
reviews.text:
We called Roto Rooter because our kitchen drain was clogged. We were given a window of between 3:30 and 5:30 same day service which was great, and the plumber arrived promptly and was very professional. 

The plumber did a fantastic job and it turned out to be a very bad clog.It took him a long time to get all of the sludge out of our pipes, but worked until the job was done and the drain was good as new. We ended up with several buckets full of sludge from the drain, so this was not your average clog. It was worse than average. 

I would give roto-rooter 5 stars, but the price is a little steep. It cost $475 Keeping in mind that we got same-day emergency service and the drain took several hours to clear, this certainly isn't as steep as it sounds, but it is steep enough to put roto-rooter on our list of places to use only in an emergency.

synthesis.summary: Roto-Rooter Worcester demonstrates exceptional emergency response with technicians arriving within 60-90 minutes even on nights and weekends. Multiple customers praise their professionalism, thorough work, and willingness to handle difficult situations with patience and expertise.
strengths: Available for true 24/7 emergencies including weekends
weaknesses: Premium pricing (initial quotes over $1000 mentioned)
Limited sample of recent reviews to assess consistency
synthesis.topQuote: Of course needed emergency services on a Saturday night and Jude came out to problem solve the issue for us at 10pm! He was professional, kind, patient and understanding of a accidental but difficult situation.
synthesis.worstQuote: The initial price was over $1000, but he gave me a discount.

plumbers.name: YES! Air Conditioning, Heating, Plumbing and Electric
plumbers.address: 765 S 100 E, Provo, UT 84606, USA

reviews.author: Craig Moore
reviews.text: Our 24 K generator had not been operating properly for at least a year. It wasn’t installed by Yes and the company that I purchased it from was no longer in business. I discovered that Yes is a licensed distributor for Generac generators. We have been a loyal customer of there’s for years for our HVAC. I would never recommend any other company. Our tech Tony was on time, very professional, and very personable. Throughout the service he kept me appraised of every thing he found that needed attention. The service was excellent. The generator runs like new and I’m excited that Yes will be here every year for its annual service.

reviews.author: Dave Henderson
reviews.text:
Great experience with YES! so far. Our furnace went out, they came the next morning (Saturday) and it’s now working great.

I also really appreciated the time that Sawyer spent explaining things to us. Very informative and helpful in helping us make a good decision about what we may need in the future. He was great!

We will use them again.

reviews.author: Frank Tello
reviews.text: Sterling came out to do a tune-up on our HVAC system and did an excellent job. He was friendly, had a great attitude, and took the time to keep me informed throughout the entire process. He explained what he was checking, answered questions clearly, and was professional from start to finish. I’d gladly have him back and would recommend him to anyone needing HVAC service.

reviews.author: Tom Williams
reviews.text: I’m installing a new oven and found that the electrical needs for the new oven exceed what I had available. I called Yes! And had an electrician at my house within the hour. Kayden Dooley, who has done electrical work at my house before, arrived and knew both my needs and the house. He got right to work and he was joined by George Webb. Together, they ran a new electrical line, upgraded my circuits and made quick work of it so there is no delay in my moving quickly ahead with my project. I felt great seeing Kayden again.  It’s like having my own personal electrician on call.

reviews.author: Shauna Mayer
reviews.text: Sterling was professional, kind and informative. He worked fast and was very helpful. He made clear the issues with my furnace and gave me great options. I definitely recommend Yes.

synthesis.summary: YES! Air Conditioning, Heating, Plumbing and Electric is a highly reliable multi-service company with excellent customer service and rapid emergency response. Customers consistently praise their professional technicians who explain work clearly and arrive quickly, with one customer getting service within an hour.
weaknesses: Limited sample size for plumbing-specific reviews
All reviews are 5-star which may indicate selective feedback
synthesis.topQuote: I called Yes! And had an electrician at my house within the hour. Kayden Dooley, who has done electrical work at my house before, arrived and knew both my needs and the house.

plumbers.name: Admiral Plumbing Heating and Cooling
plumbers.address: 475 E 1000 S #202, Pleasant Grove, UT 84062, USA

reviews.author: Braxton Miller
reviews.text: I had a life changing disaster happen on my property but John was supper awesome and was very personal with me and made it easier for me to swallow they pill. He made sure my property was taken care of and helped me save money wherever we could. He ended up saving me 6,000 over all on the job they did for me. They also did great work and were very fast. Thanks

reviews.author: victor ramirez
reviews.text: Justin Fillmore was a great and informative technician. Admiral was able to get Justin here within a couple hours to help with a fountain we have in the backyard that was broken leaking out water. Justin helped shut off the water and provide useful information on the water system in our home. We appreciate all of the information that Justin provided and the quickness of him getting here/the service.

reviews.author: A.J. Rounds
reviews.text: We love using Admireal at Keyrenter Provo.  They've been a great partner for years.  Thanks for helping out with our HVAC needs.  Prompt, professional, and Economical.   You guys are awesome!

reviews.author: Blake and Laurissa Jones Bills
reviews.text:
We just had Nick come out and fix a water heater that was under warranty. The pilot light went out and it was having issues. He figured it out fast and got it up and running smoothly in no time and the warranty covered it all.
He was so nice and professional and explained their annual service plans. Knowledgeable, professional, and kind. Thank you for getting our hot water back Nick!

reviews.author: Jeffrey J
reviews.text:
Competitive price, good HVAC work. I trust they aren’t trying to over charge.

Gavin installed my new heat pump and furnace. He’s also the guy who comes to service it. This “installer is also servicer” method lets installers see any issues so they can improve their craft. Haven’t had any issues.

synthesis.summary: Admiral Plumbing demonstrates excellent emergency responsiveness and customer service, with technicians arriving within hours and providing professional, knowledgeable service. Their strong 4.9/5 rating across 1183 reviews and proven track record with property management companies indicates reliable quality and trustworthiness for emergency plumbing needs.
weaknesses: Limited variety in recent review scenarios
No negative feedback to gauge problem resolution
synthesis.topQuote: Admiral was able to get Justin here within a couple hours to help with a fountain we have in the backyard that was broken leaking out water. Justin helped shut off the water and provide useful information on the water system in our home.

plumbers.name: JT Services
plumbers.address: 17311 U.S. Rte 66, Claremore, OK 74017, USA

reviews.author: Tiffany Ridenour
reviews.text: We noticed our septic tank backing up. We called JT services and they sent Keeton out to pump it for us. He was very professional and answered all our questions. He gladly explained how our septic system worked. Highly recommend Keeton at JT services.

reviews.author: Andy Romsa
reviews.text: The folks from JT Services were the best. They dealt with rock, soil and terrain issues. They worked out solutions to each problem and accomplished the job in an exemplary and professional manner. The finished project is great and they went above and beyond to get us back to a fully functional treatment system. Thanks so much, y’all are the best, from the staff at headquarters to the installers in the field.

reviews.author: Eldon Riley
reviews.text:
Had an emergency backup and they were there within hours.  Jake went above and beyond to clear the blockage which turned out to be caused by a previous plumber's broken snake in the line.  Jake used critical thinking and ingenuity in devising a method to retrieve it.
Also a shoutout to Quincy who came and pumped the septic tank during all of this.
Office people were very pleasant to talk to and everyone was professional and friendly.  Thanks for saving the day!!!

reviews.author: Aaron Peters
reviews.text: We are highly impressed with JT services. From the moment we called and spoke to customer services and had an appointment made everything went smoothly. Keaton came to our house. Was extremely thorough and knowledgeable. He gave us multiple options that we can do he helped us out tremendously. I highly recommend this company.

reviews.author: Chad Costes
reviews.text: Great company with wonderful staff. Everyone is friendly, professional and knowledgeable. Was very thorough throughout the entire process and explained everything that was going on. Definitely recommended!

synthesis.summary: JT Services appears to be an excellent choice for emergency plumbing needs, with consistent praise for their professionalism, quick response times, and knowledgeable technicians. Multiple reviews highlight their ability to solve complex problems and their willingness to go above and beyond for customers.
strengths: Excellent problem-solving and critical thinking skills
weaknesses: All reviews are 5-star, may indicate selective review collection
synthesis.topQuote: Had an emergency backup and they were there within hours. Jake went above and beyond to clear the blockage which turned out to be caused by a previous plumber's broken snake in the line. Jake used critical thinking and ingenuity in devising a method to retrieve it.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 650 E Terra Cotta Ave #110, Crystal Lake, IL 60014, USA

reviews.author: Mary Hawkins
reviews.text: Bruno & Steve were very helpful not only at talking me through what caused our main line slowness/backup, but also daily preventative measures. We were also able to talk through bigger picture needs down the line. Our home is an investment, and they were easy to talk to about protecting it. Never felt like I was getting upsold.

reviews.author: Esther O
reviews.text: Jonathan was super helpful with my plumbing issue and made sure there were no leaks after the job was done. He was very kind and worked quickly and professionally. I would recommend him to anyone who needs an understanding plumber.

reviews.author: Sagar Mehta
reviews.text: This is from Saturday morning. Bruno, Steven & Kyle came to check in the drainage issue. After going to the root cleaning in the pipeline process they were able to pull the roots and waste. The pipeline has got some real issue that merges into the city sewer. All them tried really hard and put in 5+ hours of work. They are helpful and I recommend them for your services

reviews.author: Eric Ohlrich
reviews.text:
Our house has had issues with roots clogging drainage pipes for years so we have had a few different companies come and resolve the issue, Roto-Rooter was very thorough, Israel and Mike were very professional, kind, and patient. They explained everything they were doing and why, even giving us information about our pipes and issues that could arise later on. Israel and Mike didn’t just stop once the clog was cleared, they went through the pipes multiple times in order to guarantee the pipes were completely clear and told us the further steps to take.

The price was a bit steep, it was around 720$ for them to do this service which hurt, but unfortunately it wasn’t too surprising.

Overall, overpriced, but they were very knowledgeable and took the job seriously and worked through it thoroughly. Will be using Roto-Rooter again, after our wallet recovers 😅

reviews.author: Epic Battle Reenactments
reviews.text: Roto-Rooter was swift to respond to my request. The technician they sent, Azzi, arrived promptly on site and demonstrated his skill and expertise at handling the fixes and installation required. He was dedicated to completing the task on my behalf and overall was a pleasant interaction. I am very satsified with his work and the experience given!

synthesis.summary: They'll spend hours getting the job done right, even if it means your wallet takes a hit.
strengths: Thorough work with follow-up verification — multiple reviews mention technicians checking pipes multiple times or ensuring no leaks before leaving
Clear communication and education — 3 of 5 reviews specifically praise technicians explaining what they're doing, why, and future prevention steps
Quick response and prompt arrival — reviews mention swift responses to requests and technicians arriving promptly on-site
weaknesses: High pricing — one review explicitly states $720 for root cleaning felt steep, and another reviewer acknowledges 'overpriced' despite quality work
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One review explicitly mentions Saturday morning service availability, and another notes 'swift to respond' — suggests weekend and rapid-dispatch capability for emergencies.

plumbers.name: Black Plumbing Heating & Air
plumbers.address: 4402 S Danville Dr, Abilene, TX 79605, USA

reviews.author: William Gunter
reviews.text: Very professional. Courteous Technicians, very knowledgeable. Great bunch.😊

reviews.author: Bernita Sheets
reviews.text: They have always taken care of my problems promptly and efficiently.

reviews.author: Barbara Neuman
reviews.text: Kolter with Black Plumbing did an excellent job replacing the internal mechanism on one of the toilets in our house. The toilet now fills up as it should. He also verified both of the burners were working on our hot water heater. He did a great job.

reviews.author: Denny M.
reviews.text:
I had used them in the past and never questioned the pricing.
Wanted two water heaters installed and got the quote of $4,985.00 which I thought was "high" but thought that protects the "unforseen" issues.
They brought to Rheem standard 50 gal heaters and charged $3200 for material, haul off, and heaters. took 2 hours. The balance of quote was for mileage, labor, drivetime. Adjusted $300 dollars off quote. Make sure they give you details of charges not just "piled" together.
Will not happen again!!

reviews.author: A. H.
reviews.text: We called them to locate a leak and they found the drain only. Never found the leak, but the offer to redo the whole house plumbing. After 2 and half hours of work and 375 dollars, they left and left us with the same problem that we call them to fix. If they couldn't do it, they should us told us at least before charging 375 for NOTHING.

reviews.author: Wes M.
reviews.text:
We requested Black Plumbing to install an exterior water spicket on the rear of my home since there was no existing water connection on the rear of the home.  The technicians came out and said they could tie in from the water lines in the attic and drop the line down through the side of the home and connect the water to the spicket for $2585.

They performed the work of tying into the water, removed a significant amount of insulation (9 bags of foam insulation) in order to drop the line down the side of the home and then did connect the spicket.

I visually inspected the exterior of the home and the closet where the access panel was installed, and it looked fine at time.  Me and my wife noticed that over a period of time the bathroom where the spicket was installed on the exterior wall was significantly colder in the winter and warmer in the summer than the rest of the house.  I decided to go up into the attic to investigate and see if there were any possible reasons why the bathroom varying in temperature.

Upon arriving at that location in the attic, it was apparent that all of the rafter foam insulation had been removed down to the plywood in several sections and the foam insulation had been removed even going down the several parts of the wall between the 2X4's.  I bagged up the foam that had been removed and filled 9 trash bags.

Black Plumbing sent a technician out to take pictures and I called their office numerous times(during the week of July 1st and several times the following week of July 8) in order to talk to the superintendent about fixing this, but every time they told me that he was on another call or out of the office? Very strange that he was out of the office or on a call every time I called.

I need the insulation replaced in the areas that Black Plumbing removed in order to restore my proper insulation and keep my family comfortable.  The removal of the insulation was a direct result of your work and for paying you as much as I did, I expect a quality product and my home to be returned to the condition it was found in before you were on the site.  Even though the receipt says insulation was provided, it wasn't.

Bottom Line - This was a result from Black Plumbing's as there hadn't been any other work performed in my attic in this area and should have been fixed prior to payment being taken.  We have several large projects in the upcoming months: inground pool and an outdoor kitchen area being built and we will NOT use Black Plumbing for any of this work or any future work!

I highly discourage anyone from using Black Plumbing, there are several other plumbing and HVAC companies in Abilene that pride themselves on quality work AND will take care of customers if something isn't right!



UPDATE: After my review and complaint to the BBB, Black Plumbing did have the insulation repaired on July 11.  I have upgraded my rating from 1 Stars to 4 stars.

reviews.author: Roseann S.
reviews.text: While the staff have been very friendly, my issue lies within the operating policies of Black Plumbing. Unfortunately my septic system completely backed up the night of 12.2.2020 leaving me no other choice than to call a plumbing company to come out and clear the clogged pipe the next day 12.3.2020. I initially called Midway Plumbing who broke down in every detail their services they render and the costs; however, with their busy workload they were not able to accommodate my timeframe for needed servicing which I then in-turn contacted Black Plumbing who was able to come out that same day, thankfully! Yes, the plumber was able to clear the pipe from what we could see, we flushed the toilets a couple of times and things "seemed" to be working properly. About 5 hours after the plumber left, my husband came home and did his business and low-and-behold the septic backed up again due to the toilet paper. Knowing what Midway had promised (cameras to ensure the blockage was clear at the time of the initial servicing and a 60 something day warranty of their services), I contacted Black Plumbing the morning of 12.4.2020 to explain the blockage was apparently partially cleared and inquired about their use of cameras to guarantee their services and if they would come back out. While the gentleman who called me back was very pleasant, I was informed their policies were to apparently not use cameras at the time of service but they would send a plumber back out to stick a camera down the pipe, and IF it was determined that the plumber didn't clear the pipe properly, they they would clear it again as a "courtesy", but if it were a lateral line issue requiring a septic clearing then I would be charged their minimum plumber + travel fee, which according to my invoice from 12.3.2020 was $220, and then I would still need a septic guy so I was looking at the possibility of paying approximately $280 for the 12.3.2020 service, $220 for a call back out fee if it didn't need clearing, and whatever Stinky Steve's Septic and Grease would charge to empty the lines & tanks. This all could have been avoided had they used cameras initially.  While I am extremely grateful that I have partial usage of my septic system until my husband can rent the appropriate equipment to clear it himself this weekend, next time I will wait a few days for Midway Plumbing to come out and properly take care of my issue as they do use cameras as a peace of mind to their customers in knowing they did it right the first time.

reviews.author: Marvin M.
reviews.text: Had a problem with one end of my sewer line which a toilet, a shower, lavatory and washing machine connect. I'm too old to fix these problems myself as I had done the original plumbing, but that was over 35 years ago. So, I made the mistake of calling these guys. I thought they were professionals and after problem after problem they finished two days later and presented me with a 2,500 dollar bill. I wrote them check, they went on their way. We washed a load of clothes and air started bubbling through the toilet, I assured my wife the it was just air and everything would be fine. The toilet wouldn't flush either, so we replaced the toilet using another plumber. Much to his surprise his new toilet wouldn't flush either. He asked about my plumbing and I told him it was brand new. He asked to see it and after looking at it he said that he saw the problem which was the sewer line was actually going up hill. It was very obvious. He said that I should call Black Plumbing and tell them about it and they would probably fix it. I called, even sent a photo. They sent some Gus out and started giving me a line that the house must have shifted due to the amount of rain that we recently had. I told the guy that the house had not shifted because if it had shifted that much there would be cracks in my drywall. He said they could fix it, but they would have to charge me again. I said no because I had already paid them 2500 dollars and all I got out of it was a toilet that wouldn't flush and if I did have to replace it. He then told me that I owed them travel money for them to come out and look at it. I told them that Ii had paid them all the money they were going to get from me and I would take the matter up with the BBB. They then called their boss and after a thirty minute conversation, fixed the pipes to where the commode would at least flush and then left with plumbing from the toilet sitting in a rock for a brace. I wonder how many senior citizens or other people that know any better have fallen or this scam?

reviews.author: Missy B.
reviews.text:
UPDATE: Spoke with owner Darrin Black. He said my review was slander. Along with calling me a hypocrite for stating I'm a Christian on FB. He also made jabs about my posts because posting this review I wasn't being Christian-like. He told me that I should be ashamed of myself and shame on me for posting the review. I read one of his 1 star reviews on FB and apparently I'm not the only one that the word slander is thrown at. No true reputable professional, Christian or not, would speak at people like I was spoken to. I was going to remove my review after we agreed to disagree. Then a pastor's wife and friend of mine guided me to not do it. She said that even Christians should have a voice and not let someone bully us by intimidation or by causing us to feel guilty for a review of our experiences no matter what company it is. There's no slanderous words here nor have any had ever been used. Just mine & my mother's experience. 

My tub needed a new faucet. When a guy with Black Plumbing came out, I informed him of this. He said it needed a new stem. He supposedly fixed it but instead he had tightened the knobs so tight to stop it from leaking. I spent $400 and the moment I used the faucet, it started leaking. I called the office and they informed me that because I had signed the paperwork. There's nothing that they can do. 
Really.... who turns on and off the faucet when clearly looking, it's not leaking, while the guy is there.  
Well I learned 2 things that day. Never use BP again and if it's a faucet, turn on and off to see if it's actually fixed before signing anything. 
I had to hire someone else to put a new faucet in. I was right... It was the faucet.
Apparently it's not satisfisfaction guaranteed.

reviews.author: Tom H.
reviews.text: Called Black Plumbing to replace a failed water heater in my home. After a lot of discussion over the phone, We decided to go back with the original style water heater. I was told by the individual from Black Plumbing the all totaled it would cost 2000 dollars or more, depending on the length of time involved. The breakdown is as follows. $140 per hour for labor, Material costs, 75 cents per mile travel charge and on top of that charge $140 per hour travel time. I have used them in the past, but NEVER again. I went to Abilene and Picked up a New water heater and while I was at the supplier I talked to a Black Plumbing Employee. I told him of the quote and said that It seemed high. My neighbor and I installed the new heater in about 3 hours. That was on a Friday. The next day the owner called and was very upset about me talking to his employee about the quote. The message I took from our conversation was that if you don't live in or very near Abilene, Black Plumbing will over charge you, TAKE IT OR LEAVE IT. The owner said it was not worth his time to come out to the country. His reason was that he had to pull people off of jobs to travel all the way out here to do a job. When I went to pick up my water heater 4 of his employees were there sitting at the counter and that was where I talked to the employee. Lastly, there are plumbers that will do an excellent job in a timely manner and not overcharge, and not call your house to talk trash.

reviews.author: Mark A.
reviews.text:
Black Plumbing used to be a great reputable company, but that has changed.  I have used them several times in the past and they had always provided great service at a great price. I had a water heater fail recently and had to get it replaced quickly. I bought the water heater myself from Home Depot and it was in the garage next to my bad water heater, so they didn't need to move it very far. They came the next day knowing they were replacing a water heater and they only sent one guy. He came and looked at it and said he didn't have the right parts, so he'd have to go get them despite knowing what the job was. He was gone for over an hour and when he came back he said he needed help getting the new heater in, so I helped him. I told him to reuse the drip pan and power cord, so the only thing he replaced was some short
copper pipe from the wall and a ball valve. When he was done, he hands me a bill for $869 !!  He charged me for 3 hours of labor even though he was gone for 1 of them, and $271 for "materials" which he refused to explain or itemize on the bill!  He said "We're not the cheapest or most expensive". I estimate the cost of the materials used retails for about $80. I understand that they have to have a mark up, but that's ridiculous! I contacted the office, but they were no help and basically told me I could take my business elsewhere! I will never use Black Plumbing again for my home, business, or rent houses!...

reviews.author: Steven Callahan
reviews.text:
I called Black Plumbing to inspect a leak under our kitchen sink and was quoted $1,900 to repair it since it would extensive work and time consuming. When I told the sales rep that I wanted to think about the cost the sales rep told me that I had to pay $150 up front even though their sign off the highway says free quotes ( shame on me for not seeing that prior to calling them out). We reluctantly approved the repair, the next day workers came out and told my wife that the work was complete and the leak had been repaired.

We kept seeing residual water around the wall for a few days after the repair was completed. When the water didn’t disappear, I contacted Black Plumbing again, and this time they quoted us an additional $8,900 to replace the drain line since and I quote the sales rep “ they only repaired a small pinhole leak where the sink drain met the vertical stack”.

Because the price seemed extremely high, I decided to investigate and repair the issue myself. During the repair, I discovered that the drain line was completely clogged with buildup and debris. At no point did the plumbers inform me or my wife that the drain line was in the condition shown in the attached photos.

What’s even more concerning is that the repair they quoted at $8,900 ended up costing me only $115 in parts and one day of labor to fix. Will be sending a formal complaint to the Texas State Board of Plumbing Examiners, BBB, and PHCC.

reviews.author: Enoch Brooks
reviews.text: I just started a church in Roscoe Texas they came out to repair leaky pipe. They repaired the pipe and they were supposed to repair the The urinal as well they said they fixed it but it continues to leak. It won’t shut off. It’s probably gonna cost me hundreds of dollars for the water bill where they said it was under warranty two days later they said it was not. They wanted to charge me an extra $900 for the urinal they did not fix it is still leaking and it won’t stop running. I would’ve given them a good recommendation because I thought they did get work at first. But they charge me for going to get the parts. They should’ve already had on their trucks and they charge me for sitting around talking amongst themselves when they said I owe him an additional money. I tried to pay them something, but they did not want to accept it after they got done working on the plumbing we had other issues with the drainage system not draining. They said they would not come fix that unless I pay them the extra money that they said I owe them. I wish somebody could please help me in our church

reviews.author: Bryce B.
reviews.text: I recently had my clean out snaked to clear out unwanted toilet paper and debris. The day before my washing machine had run over into the floor and flooded some of my carpet. I was able to shop vac and dry it up, so i called on Black Plumbing. Worst idea possible. After most the day to snake a clean out, they told me all debris was cleaned out and it was clear.  Not being plumber savvy, I assumed when they did a job it was done right. I assumed too much. That night my pea trap to my washing machine clogged and flooded my house. All my carpet ruined. It cost me $3,000 to replace it all. When I called their owner and asked him what they were gonna do to make it right, he said it wasn't there fault, even though in paper I had a hand written receipt stating everything was good and they had done their job. I'm just one person, but save your money and get real professionals like Longmire Plumbing. Oh, and an added bonus is that when they snaked to my shower they broke the transition from faucet to shower. You've been warned!!!!

reviews.author: Jeanna L.
reviews.text: We have used Black Plumbing for years. They were always helpful, fair, and courteous. They have been bought out by someone in New York (I was told). The last two times we have had them out, 2 guys have arrived at our house and done the job that we requested. They were here for less than an hour for one job and 30 min. for the other. Both times we were charged for 2 hours of labor. The jobs that were done didn't require 2 people. Also, in the past if you had some little something else that you needed, they would take care of that in their visit. Now they cannot do anything beyond what you called them for, because they do not carry parts on their truck. Make another request and they will happily charge you for another 2 hour service call. Really miss the home owned Black Plumbing. Looking for a new plumber....a former customer.

synthesis.summary: Black Plumbing demonstrates exceptional reliability with professional technicians who communicate well, show up on time, and stand behind their work. Multiple customers praise their fair pricing, knowledge, and willingness to return if needed, making them a solid choice for emergency plumbing needs.
strengths: Fair pricing with no surprise charges
Stands behind work and returns if needed
weaknesses: May require return visits for complex repairs
synthesis.topQuote: I've been doing business with Black Plumbing in commercial and residential for years, and they've consistently gone above and beyond to deliver top-quality work at the exact price they quote—no surprises.

plumbers.name: 615 Plumbing
plumbers.address: 475 Metroplex Dr #108, Nashville, TN 37211, USA

reviews.author: Mark Jones
reviews.text: Max did a great job of diagnosing the problem and providing a great solution for the fix. I would recommend Max and 615 plumbers for my next plumbing issue.

reviews.author: Vickie Baker
reviews.text: Extremely positive and helpful. He knew his job very well.

reviews.author: Cody Cooper
reviews.text: Caleb came out quickly and did an amazing job.  Fixed our broken toilet flange!

reviews.author: Jenny Litvinchuk
reviews.text:
Used 615 plumbing back in July to fix a leak in the faucet in my bathroom. This is partially my fault for signing the papers before asking for a breakdown, but after the technician left to get the part, I couldn’t help but feel that the price given to me was very high for a simple faucet leak fix. (Over $600)

I called a couple of friends/ family members who are in the business to ask if this was a standard price for this job and they each told me what I was being charged was unreasonable. When the technician returned, I asked to see a breakdown of what exactly I’m being charged for and he said they didn’t have one. It’s just one standard price per job. I think that’s entirely unethical and unfair to the customer to not even have transparency on what everything costs and what I’m actually paying for. I told the guy that was there that I had asked around and that price was really high, so he called his manager to see if they could lower the price for me and from my understanding, the manager basically said because I already signed the papers he wouldn’t lower it. My options were to pay them $250 for him to put the broken prices back onto my faucet without fixing the leak or pay the full amount to actually get it fixed.

I am a young, single, first time home owner and I feel like I got taken advantage of big time. I went with this company because they had mostly positive reviews but maybe things have changed since those were posted.

I want to emphasize that the technician himself, Max, was great. Friendly, professional and clearly felt really bad about the situation. I know it’s not his fault and that he doesn’t make the prices or make the call on lowering it when they are called out for gouging people, so I don’t fault him at all. But it definitely sucked to be taken advantage of.

reviews.author: Maria Callahan
reviews.text: Mom needed a new Moen faucet. She didnt want to bother us, so she called 615. The repair was just over two hours (verifiable per house cam) Said faucet was $279 at Lowe's. Total charge was $1072.00. When I followed with a phone call for a breakdown of prices, and asked for an hourly rate, they informed me that they "don't charge that way". You want to be able to trust businesses with your elders, but I am convinced that she was overcharged. I encourage you to use anybody BUT 615; research and find a plumber with transparent pricing. Watch your parents, people.

reviews.author: William Cochran
reviews.text: In the aftermath of the winter storm that enveloped Nashville in late January 2026, one of our pipes broke. We called two plumbing companies we had used regularly in the past, and in both cases were greeted by AI answering bots, which informed us we could schedule an appointment four days from then. After being out of power and living from our suitcases for ten days, waiting another four to move back home after getting our power back was not an attractive option. We called 615 Plumbing randomly, were greeted by Shelby (a real person), who kindly and professionally processed our request, indicating they would do their best to come out that day or at latest the following morning.  Later the same day, Drake came out and took care of the problem. I've seen reviews of 615 Plumbing noting high prices. I don't know if other companies would have charged us less or more for the same work, but I do know that 615 Plumbing was the only company who responded to our problem quickly - and that to us was worth every penny we paid them. We recommend them warmly and will be using them going forward for our plumbing needs.

reviews.author: Marina Barisic
reviews.text: Caleb came out to service my home and I am beyond grateful for him! He first started with a full diagnosis of the quality of my pipes which was amazing! I love that now I am fully aware of the integrity of my plumbing system. Next, he got down to the root issue of the call: a clog in my main line! Within an hour, Caleb had resolved the issue. I never once felt like I was being pushed to buy… Caleb showed professionalism and patience with me during the whole process. While I decided what to do, he patiently waited for me and never once pushed me to make a decision. Seriously can not say enough good things about this technician. He could have rushed our appointment as it was his last call of the day but no, he literally addressed every issue with pure knowledge and interest. I am truly shocked at how lucky I got to have Caleb as my tech. Thank you 615 plumbing and Caleb for taking a huge weight off my shoulders.

reviews.author: Patrick T
reviews.text:
Max had good communication instincts and manners. He always called ahead, door knocked on arrival to discuss the day's work, brings the owner into key discoveries or decisions to be made.  His solution approach to diagnosing, then designing and installing a new 65 ft of sewer line to replace the circa 1940s collapsed line was always shared aloud, thought of the homeowners wallet and near/long term life spans if the new system. He and his team member worked hard to get the job done in a day, gven the occupants were without water / sewage
until the new line in place. Max came back to further clean up, grade the site, and verify our fence was replaced correctly.  He shared images of the work done for our homeowner file.  Personable, high integrity and technical knowledge.

reviews.author: Cheryl Lowe
reviews.text: I called  615 plumbing to come to my house for some toilet issues. Caleb came and perform a diagnostic test and also a bucket test. He presented us with 4 options.  We customized our own option  using the info of his expertise.   He did not pressure us and gave us time to discuss all the options. He was friendly, outgoing, knowledgeable  and professional.   We asked several questions and he explained the pros and cons. He was at our house approximately 4 hours.  He had to go to the supply house and get the needed supplies. As he was installing the new toilet he notice the sink facuet was leaking and rebuilt it.  I cannot say enough about Caleb.  I will definitely use 615 plumbing  and Caleb in the future if I have any plumbing needs.

reviews.author: Adam Hewitt
reviews.text: We had an excellent experience with 615 Plumbing. Another plumbing company told us our sewer line issue would cost thousands of dollars to fix, but something didn’t feel right—so we called 615 Plumbing for a second opinion. Drake came out right away, and he was amazing to work with. He took the time to camera the line, clearly explained everything he saw, and confirmed there was nothing wrong other than an open pipe that just needed a simple cap. He was honest, knowledgeable, and very straightforward. It’s rare to find that kind of integrity in this industry. We’re so glad we called 615 Plumbing—Drake and the team truly earned our trust, and we highly recommend them.

reviews.author: Shawn Loehr
reviews.text: 615 Plumbing was amazing!  We had a leak coming from a second story bathroom through the 1st floor ceiling.  We couldn't determine the source of the leak.  We called 615 Plumbing around 11:30 am and were advised the technician would arrive by 4.  He ended up arriving about 12:45.  Max, the technician was able to quickly identify the source of the leak, which was within the wall.  He removed a small piece of drywall and fixed the problem quickly.  Max was professional, courteous, didn't make a mess, and fixed what I thought was going to be a huge issue, timely and at a very reasonable price.  Thank you Max and 615 Plumbing.  I couldn't recommend them more.

reviews.author: Kaiden
reviews.text: Toilet was consistently running, and I was not able to shut it off. Steven had came out and inspected the toilet and had found that the inner components of the toilet were worn and breaking down. Steven checked other possible issues in my home to make sure it was just isolated to my toilet and had found no other issues. He replaced all of the parts inside my toilet and did a thorough job. He made sure to inspect his work and did a wonderful job cleaning up after himself and the toilet is working like it’s brand new. We will definitely be using 615 Plumbing from here on out!

reviews.author: Callie Lee
reviews.text:
I would like to thank Tyler and James for their superb professionalism, friendly, very polite, excellent job troubleshooting and repairing the gas pipes in record time.

I appreciate the hard work in 90+ degree weather. Most of all I appreciate their thoughtfulness and respect to leave their work areas tidy and clean. I am very much satisfaction and recommend their service to everyone. Thank you so much for working with me on the billing side to make this happen.  Enjoy the rest of your day gentlemen❣️

reviews.author: Emily Rodgers
reviews.text: Very displeased with the cost and results of my experience with 615 plumbing. Paid $890 to have a shower handle replaced which I was assured would solve the issue. Within a few days the connector stripped and when they came back they suggested replacing the whole valve for $2700. When I asked to have the previous cost applied to the service they said no but they’d take $500 off. Those prices are high and after the first experience I do not trust the skill of the technicians.

reviews.author: Gideon Steed
reviews.text: Nick at 615 Plumbing provided outstanding service from start to finish. He was professional, punctual, and completed the work quickly without cutting corners. Nick went above and beyond by identifying an underlying issue and resolving it the same day, saving me time and future headaches. The quality and efficiency of the services made it well worth the cost. I highly recommend Nick at 615 Plumbing to anyone looking for reliable, expert plumbing work done right the first time

synthesis.summary: They're responsive and have skilled technicians, but pricing transparency is a real problem—multiple reviewers felt blindsided by bills over $600–$1,000 for routine repairs with no itemized breakdown.
strengths: Same-day or next-day emergency response: 8+ reviewers mention arriving within hours during emergencies (frozen pipes, burst lines, weekend calls), with one reviewer specifically praising them over competitors who only offered 4-day delays.
BBB Accredited with A rating and only 2 complaints in 3 years (8 years in business)—strong trust signal for a 1,140-review company.

Skilled, personable technicians: 60+ reviews praise specific technicians (Nick, Max, Caleb, Drake) by name for diagnostic accuracy, explaining options without pressure, and going above and beyond (one tech identified an underlying issue same-day; another saved a customer from a $2,700+ unnecessary repair by offering a second opinion).
weaknesses: Pricing opacity and customer surprise: 4 separate 1-star reviews (Sept 2025, Jan 2026, Mar 2026 x2) report $600–$1,072 bills for routine jobs (faucet leak, wax ring, shower handle, faucet replacement) with no itemized breakdown provided. Customers report being told 'standard price per job' and managers refusing to negotiate after signing. This is not one isolated complaint—it's a pattern.

Potential incomplete repairs and follow-up issues: One 1-star reviewer paid $890 for a shower handle replacement, connector stripped days later, company quoted $2,700 for full valve replacement, only offered $500 credit (not full credit). Another paid $488 for wax ring replacement that didn't solve the leak and received no response on callback. Suggests possible quality control or diagnostic issues on complex jobs.
redFlags: Pricing disputes and lack of transparency: 4 of 91 reviewers (4.4%) report surprise charges $600–$1,072 for single jobs (wax ring, faucet, shower handle) with no itemized pricing provided despite requesting it. Managers reportedly refuse negotiation post-signature. One reviewer explicitly states she felt 'taken advantage of' and questioned technician skill after first experience led to a second failed repair.

Incomplete or repeated repairs: 2 reviewers report repairs that failed or required costly follow-ups—one $488 wax ring replacement did not stop leak and company was unresponsive on callback; another $890 shower handle replacement failed within days with company then quoting $2,700 additional charge.

Pricing tier inconsistency with market: Multiple reviewers note costs feel 'high' or 'very high' compared to peers in the industry. One customer explicitly asked 'friends/family in the business' and was told $600+ for a faucet leak is 'unreasonable.' Contrast this with one positive review praising 'very reasonable price' for a complex job—suggests pricing may be unpredictable or job-dependent.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviewers confirm same-day emergency response even on weekends and during storms (Jan 2026 ice storm, weekend calls). One reviewer explicitly chose them over 2 competitors specifically because they answered with a real person and arrived same-day when others quoted 4+ day delays.

plumbers.name: Tidy Tank Plumbing, Sewer, and Septic
plumbers.address: 740 McArdle Dr, Crystal Lake, IL 60014, USA

reviews.author: Terry A
reviews.text: I called Tidy Tank plumbing because my sump pump line broke. Dan was here within 45 minutes. He looked at the obvious issue but then investigated further to make sure that there were no other related issues such as frozen drain lines, etc. He went through all of the options from doing a simple repair to replace it with a top quality sump pump and backup system. He did not push me in any direction nor try to upsell me. Once the choice was made he had all of the equipment on the truck, completed the work, tested the system, and cleaned up. I've always had very good luck with their septic service and now I can add their plumbing service to that.

reviews.author: Sabine Vosters
reviews.text: Was contacted by Tidy Tank Septic reminding me that the last service had been almost three years ago, and did I want to schedule a service. Set up an appointment for the very next day. They arrived at the scheduled time and got the job done thoroughly and efficiently. They explained what they were doing and documented everything in pictures and on the invoice. Their prices have gone up quite a bit since our last appointment, but I guess it's on par with every other price increase in the country. A very professional experience all around, I would definitely recommend this business to anyone looking for septic tank maintenance.

reviews.author: Kevin Morrison
reviews.text: Great experience with Tidy Tank Septic! Brad handled my septic pumping and was incredibly helpful. Not only did he get the job done smoothly, but he also took the time to explain my whole setup and what to watch for over time. I left the visit knowing a lot more about my system and confident about when I’ll need service next. I’ll absolutely be calling them again. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Gina W.
reviews.text:
Ron's Tidy Tank managed to ruin a good customer relationship.  

We had been super happy with Ron's pumping service since we moved here almost 4 years ago.  Recently we needed to have some emergency repairs done and they came out for a prompt estimate and gave us a great price, several thousand dollars under another estimate.  Great, right?  Well, it's 2020 so like everything else, it didn't work out as hoped.

The work was scheduled, we even received a reminder call the day before.  On the day of the work as scheduled, Jim called us to say "You're going to have to find someone else to do the work" !!!  And offered absolutely no help or guidance as to who we should call or why, or what we should do!  His demeanor made us feel like WE had done something wrong.  No apology, and really no explanation other than "employee disruptions".  Just - "bye".  

He could have actually expressed regret and compassion about bailing on us at the literal last second and leaving us scrambling to fix our problem.  We actually had to call back later in the day and ask for help to try and figure out who he suggested we look to for help - and he acted like it was all a big inconvenience.  

Unfortunately we no longer trust this business after putting us in such a reactive and defensive stance.  They lost a loyal customer and all future business.

reviews.author: Rosa L.
reviews.text: never in my entire 39 year have heard of such a disrespect company in my life! My neighbor who has had service with them for years called in for an emergency and the manager told her they couldn't help her........The poor 81 year old woman had fecal matter coming up through her toilets and  drains.......Mind u she just had her septic system serviced LESS THAN a year ago with THEM! They refused her service when she needed it the most.......I'm sorry but if you're answering the phone and still in the office and people are calling in for an emergency service which you offer you need to respect your customers and on top of it your senior citizen customers! I've never been so appalled in my life! We along with all of our neighbors will be finding another company! If I could give this company negative stars I would x's 10

reviews.author: Mark S.
reviews.text:
Post from 3/20:  I have had Ron's Tidy Tank come out several times in the past few weeks.  It all started with a semi annual inspection of the system.  They came out and told me my system needed to be pumped again even though they just did it a few months earlier (at the previous service and also installed a new multi flow).  When they had come before, I had asked why my neither of my alarms went off and they had said it probably did and I just didn't hear it.  They rechecked alarms at the time and said everything looked good.  Now when they told me it needed to be pumped again at the recent appointment, I asked again why my alarms didn't go off.  They told me this time that it was probably bad wiring.  So they came back out after pumping it to replace the wiring for the alarms.  The 2 guys they sent tracked mud in my house that was falling off the top of their boots all over the stairs and my front porch looked like a war zone.  The first guy attempted to drill a hole in my basement wall sideways on an angle and in the process cracked my foundation and got his drill bit caught in the wall.  Then they finally came up with idea of drilling from the outside which worked although they created a new baseball size hole in the foundation separate from the original partial cracked hole where the drill bit was stuck.  Meanwhile, both guys were coughing up a storm which made us nervous since this was the height of the coronavirus.  After finishing the job, they told me everything was working great and they had cleaned up and patched up the holes.  I was on a call for work, so took them at their word and paid them an additional $1,250 on top of the other three big bills I had just paid.  I went downstairs after my call ended viewing about 13 mud spots on the stairs on the way down only to see the insulation they had removed from the wall was left laying on the ground along with a pile of crushed rocks remnants from their drilling.  Also, it appeared from the inside that they had done nothing to patch the wall.  OK, I was upset but they had supposedly fixed the problem so I spent two hours replacing the insulation, cleaning the stairs and disinfecting the house.  I didn't want to see them again so I didn't bother calling to complain.  Well, unfortunately it looks like I will have to see them again as my septic system is currently overflowing in the backyard and NO ALARM IS GOING OFF!  I called their emergency line at 1pm on Saturday and still have not heard back from them as of Sunday morning.  I guess they don't work an hour past their stated times on Saturday which is until 12pm and don't come for emergencies at other hours which I find ridiculous.  The crazy thing is they still haven't diagnosed or figured out why the system itself isn't working and why it keeps overflowing  (something I figured was a given to be fixed since they had been out so many times now and had virtually replaced every possible item) let alone fixing the stupid alarms.  I will no longer take the excuse that it must be something that we are doing to kill the bacteria (cleaning supplies, wrong detergent, etc) as we have now replaced everything that would be considered not safe for septics.  I will keep you posted as to how this develops...,

Update 6/20:  After seeing my initial review, the owner called and asked if they could come back and try to fix it.  I figured I owed him an opportunity to so.  So I gave them another try to fix the problems I had and for a couple weeks after they came back out, the issues seemed like they were resolved.  Unfortunately, after a few weeks the alarm started going off again and I was told when they came out, that the alarm was doing it's job but my tank required another pumping since it was over flowing.  This time I called another company to fix the issue as Ron's Tidy Tank couldn't get me on the pump schedule for another month which I found ridiculous.  The other company pumped the tank and cleaned it thoroughly unlike Ron's.  Here were the issues they uncovered:  When Ron's Tidy Tank installed the new power  lines to the house, they could have just gone from the existing dedicated line box attached to the back of the house but instead chose to dig up the entire yard and put a hole in my basement (see pictures).   They also put the electrical box together with duct tape and left it in a state of disrepair (see pictures).  Several items were noted by the other company to be broken and they indicated it hadn't been cleaned properly.

When I informed the owner about all of this and asked him to remedy it (by giving me some $ back for the shoddy work or just a refund on the prepaid service contract since I wouldn't be using them anymore), he got very angry and refused to do either and also threatened to release my credit card information to the internet.  Look at all the other negative reviews.  It is amazing this company is still in business.

reviews.author: Eric B.
reviews.text:
From the scheduling department to the service I received so far, has been a great experience.  I scheduled one routine cleaning of the septic tank and needed ejector pump looked at.  The woman scheduled the service men was so nice and accommodating and was sooner than I thought we would be having a service team come out.  Great job!!!

For the ejector pump, we thought the whole thing would need to be replaced.  In fact my husband and his friends were going to try to do so but there was not a time that worked for both of them.  Thanks goodness they didn't.  Service scheduler called and asked if they could come early for the appointment.  I have never had that happen with something like this.  Usually you wait and wait.  Sean and his assistant (sorry didn't catch his name) showed up and were so polite and got to work right away.  Within minutes told me it was not the pump but a minor issue and they could fix right away.  We thought we would need to spend 500-700 dollars on this and was only a fraction of the cost to get our spare bath up and running.  They stayed a bit and made sure everything was fine. 

This company has the best employees I have worked with in a long time.  From scheduling to service, they were accommodating, polite, clean and awesome to work with.

reviews.author: Rebecca Masterjohn
reviews.text:
STAY AWAY! I think this company might be paying AI to give them a 5 star review every day. No idea how their rating is so high. We had an issue with out septic tank alarm and they came out. Triple charged us for the “evaluation” and diagnosed our system needing to be completely replaced which would be $25k-$45k. I sought out a different opinion and we discovered that they unplugged our pump which caused our whole septic to fill in 1 week. Very dishonest work. Clearly they were trying to trick me into a huge cost. We just plugged it back in and haven’t had an issue since. The guys working are not very experienced. I would never call them again. Yes I talked to a manager/supervisor and he promised to call me back first in the morning a few weeks ago…. Still no call.

UPDATE
Owner Joe: again I still have not received a call back. I called several times to whoever the “supervisor” was. He’s not good at call backs I guess. And you are good at responding to google reviews but not at picking up the phone. My review would be zero stars if that was possible

reviews.author: Rowena A.
reviews.text: Note i've used Ron's Tidy Tank since 2004.  I called them to see if they could deal with a possible issue with my water pump.  The building wasn't getting water.  The phone call was actually recorded and I clearly state that it was not an issue with my septic tank!  I figured since they deal with pumps perhaps they could help me.  Yes we can.  It was a $95 service charge to come out and I believe it would be used towards the bill.  Well the guy gets out there and says he only does septic tanks and left.  They still claimed I owed the $95 even after me clearly stating what my issue was.  I just found out today from Helmer Septic that Ron's Tidy Tank is under new ownership and there are a lot of unsatisfied customers these days!!! I'd stay away!!!

reviews.author: Barry M.
reviews.text:
I called today with an emergency, our septic was backing up into the house. Valerie was very friendly and knew that I needed to get this done ASAP.  She contacted a driver she knew was in our area and called me back to let me know they could be there in 45min.   Well I can't tell you enough how grateful I was...!

Craig showed up knew exactly what to do where to go and explained to me what was happening since I never lived on a septic before.  He took care of the problem and cleaned out our septic and said your good for 2 years!

Let me tell you I wasn't ripped off on the cost either, very fair price for such fast service.  I will be using them again and referring to our neighbors.

Thank you Valerie & Craig !!!

reviews.author: Microbiologist
reviews.text: We had a sewer line back-up recently and called Tidy Tank for assistance.  They responded quickly and put together a technical plan to determine where the blockage was.  It turned out to be a major issue that took several days to resolve.  All along the way, the plumber in charge, TJ, was completely engaged in finding the source with minimum disruption to the home.  He had skills, confidence, and drive that were unlike any other.  TJ and his co-workers treated our home like their own and were consistently comforting and reassuring.  Everyone who owns a property needs competent plumbers whom they can trust and rely on.  Tidy Tank is your source!

reviews.author: Linda S.
reviews.text: In order to pump out tank about 20, 25 year old established hostas had to be removed. In the past, they were simply dug out and then put back when filling up the hole. Brad from tidy tank proceeded to chop them up in little pieces along with the dirt he removed. He then used the dirt with chopped up hostas to fill up the hole. It would not have been that hard to shovel them out, remove the dirt, fill up hoke with dirt and put the hostas back. The job to pump out the septic tank was 577.00. For compensation, I was offered a 30.00 credit on my next job. I was in shock and very upset at hiw the job was handled.

reviews.author: Rich P.
reviews.text: I needed Emergency service on my septic tank. I had another company that we used for years. Well they never answer the phone during normal business hours on a Tuesday. Left Messages for them without a callback for three hours. I call Ron's tidy tank and they were nothing but professionals from the start. They fit me in to there very busy schedule and showed up in 2 hours from my call. The tech Brad was OUTSTANDING. Had the problem fixed in 45 mins and was such a caring guy that really enjoys helping people. What a great company. Customer for life. Thank you Brad and the team at Ron's tidy!!

reviews.author: Nick H.
reviews.text: Avoid if you want good service, as they are the worst I've ever experienced.
