Their service team couldn't clear a main line, dug an unnecessary hole in my yard, and ultimately charged over $200 for getting nothing completed. Jim, the supposed owner, even hung up on me during a follow up call. I wasn't cursing or being irate, simply trying to find a solution or get a refund. To contrast that, T & T Weidners' also had trouble clearing and tried from inside and out (without the digging). The difference: they didn't charge me, and walked me through the proper solution, albeit more expensive.

reviews.author: John M.
reviews.text:
Raiders of the Lost Fart. 
I called Ron's Tidy Tank the other day with some concerns about my septic system.  I'm new to this all so they walked me through my issues. They came out very quickly to service my septic which was beneath my patio. 
The previous owner of the house left no details of exactly where BELOW THE PATIO it was. They found it quite quickly and took care of things in a flash. I am happy that I found a company that I can trust to do a good job and bill me a fair price. Call these folks when the shit hits the fan!

synthesis.summary: They're solid for septic work and show up fast, but plumbing pricing is a wildcard—some people pay fair rates while others get shocked by $500+ bills for simple jobs.
strengths: Fast emergency response: 15+ Google reviews mention arrival within 45 minutes to 2 hours for urgent calls; one reviewer notes they 'fit me in to their very busy schedule and showed up in 2 hours'
Septic expertise and proactive service: Multiple reviews praise knowledgeable technicians who explain systems in detail; they proactively contact customers for maintenance scheduling
Thorough diagnostics: Several reviewers note technicians investigate beyond the obvious issue (e.g., checking for frozen lines when a sump pump fails)
weaknesses: Plumbing labor rates appear inconsistent and potentially high: One Google reviewer paid $521 for reconnecting a kitchen faucet (calling it 'outrageous'); another paid $600+ for replacing a single hose spigot; a third paid $129 just for a service call that yielded no diagnosis. This pattern does not appear in septic reviews.

Vague upfront pricing on diagnostic calls: Two Google reviewers report being quoted only a $129 dispatch fee with no set pricing until technician arrival; one Yelp reviewer from 2019 describes being quoted $75 for inspection but handed a $200 bill
redFlags: Plumbing pricing disputes: 3 of 85 Google reviews explicitly call out surprise or excessive plumbing labor charges ($521, $600+, $129 diagnostic fee with no work). This concentration on plumbing-only services suggests a pricing transparency issue specific to that division.

Platform rating discrepancy: Google 4.9/5 (85 reviews) vs. Yelp 3.9/5 (43 reviews)—a 1.0-point gap. Yelp reviews include more historical complaints (2013–2023) about billing disputes, incomplete work, and refused emergency service; recent Google reviews (2025) are overwhelmingly positive, suggesting either improved operations or potential review filtering.

Refusal of emergency service in older Yelp reviews: Two separate 2019 Yelp reviewers report being denied emergency help on evenings/weekends with minimal explanation or referral assistance, though this may reflect outdated policies.

Property damage incidents: One Yelp reviewer (2020) reports technicians cracked foundation wall during wiring installation and left insulation/debris; another (2023) reports damaged landscaping (hostas chopped up) with only $30 credit offered on a $577 job.

Inconsistent follow-up on complaints: One Google reviewer reports leaving a voicemail about alleged diagnostic fraud (unplugged pump, suggesting unnecessary $25–45k replacement) and states manager promised callback 'first thing in the morning' weeks ago with no response.
synthesis.priceSignal: mid-range for septic; premium/unpredictable for plumbing
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple Google reviews confirm same-day or next-morning callbacks and 45-minute arrivals for emergencies; however, two older Yelp reviews (2019) mention they close at 12pm Saturday and don't handle after-hours emergencies, suggesting limited weekend/evening availability despite some after-hours success stories.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Google 4.9 vs. Yelp 3.9 (1.0-point gap). Yelp contains older complaints (2013–2023) about refused emergency service, billing disputes, and property damage rarely mentioned in recent Google reviews. Google's recent reviews (2025) heavily skew positive, while Yelp's older reviews reveal more service refusals and upselling concerns. This suggests either operational improvement or differential review curation.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Drain Service
plumbers.address: 4207 Mike Padgett Hwy Unit A, Augusta, GA 30906, USA

reviews.author: Cuthbert George
reviews.text: Truly appreciate Trevor for his excellent customer and prompt service. He was very skilled and appreciate the late evening response.

reviews.author: Tony Graham
reviews.text: Mike Vance from Roto-Rooter did a great job repairing leaking sink. Great response time and quality service. Mike also cleaned up the area after the repair. Thank you!

reviews.author: Alicia Hudson
reviews.text: Very helpful was able to come out for an emergency visit in less than 30 minutes. Thanks Trevor

reviews.author: Elizabeth B.
reviews.text: I paid high dollar service and got low dollar work. I won't use them again. I paid them to install new fixtures and it isn't flush or even sturdy. It looks worse than what I had in before. They are scammers who don't know what they are doing. I will be submitting a better business complaint again them.

reviews.author: Jay M.
reviews.text:
Service call was a joke.  "Plumber" knew almost nothing except "replace". No grasp of repair or mitigation. 

Entry level service and company.

reviews.author: Richard P.
reviews.text: I hired them to install new plumbing for a Duplex. Some work had been done incorrectly by another plumber. I spoke w/Cullen 3+ times about the job. I walked it w/him &explained it so there was no confusion. The job was; install 2 water lines/2 sewer lines (inside&outside), remove cast iron vents, install pvc, remove all waste, install vents for kitchen sink/washer,& 2 hose lines. I called 3times to confirm the job&asked that it be in the contract. Each time it was not done as I asked. I called again&emailed them to have it in writing. I hired them for $10,200. The work was to be done in 2+ days. They rushed, didn't check their work/clean up. They ONLY installed water/sewer for 1 unit. The piping that was installed was sloped incorrectly. They used multiple couplings 4 the sewer line instead of using a longer pipe, installed water pipes in the Block walls w/o insulation (not code), didn't strap the pipes, & left the wrong couplings on parts of the drain(not up to code). They incorrectly; bent pex, used the wrong fittings 4 the tub. I told Cullen about him not installing the 2nd sewer/water line&he lied to my face & said that it wasnt in the quote! After I spoke to him 3+times& got it in writing! They didn't cap any of the lines/test the system for leaks. They charged my card for 95% of the balance b4 ANY work was done. We agreed on 50% up front & the rest upon completion. That is fraud. I asked them not to charge the card again & they stated that they wouldn't but they lied & still did. That is fraud. Their workers do not know the code & are operating w/o supervision. I spoke with Mike(mgmt) & explained the issues. He met with me at the site& saw the issues.  He said that they would "try" to get a more experienced worker on the job sometime next week. I had an inspection scheduled for Monday! I asked for a refund b/c I do not trust them to do the work correctly. Mike had an attitude b/c of this & said that I wasted HIS time for having him come out to see. 0/5stars!

reviews.author: Vicky
reviews.text:
I had an issue with the water continuing to run after the toilet was flushed, which could not be controlled by turning off water to the bowl so it ran for more than 24 hours. The 2nd bathroom had a similar issue but the water could be cut off.
The plumber arrived to give an estimate, which took nearly an hour and a half. After an hour he asked if there was another toilet in the house in which I showed the 3rd toilet. This took another 30 minutes
before he came with 3 estimates ranging from $654-$2,999. This covered replacing 2 toilets for $2,999, or replacing 3 fill valves and a angle stop in one for $982 or repair the angle stop and adjust the fill valves for $654.

It was not until I agreed on one of the options that the water was turned off. I saw the plumber with a shovel and tools in the middle of the yard, I went outside and asked what he was about to do and he said turn the water off once he located the water turn off.  I advised the water meter was on the curb by the power pole, he stopped and went to the meter and turned the water off. He was there for about 3 1/2 hours doing the work. (9-12:45)

The last issue was after leaving, the hot water was at a trickle so I notified the company around 1:05. The plumber called at 4:15 to advise he was 30 minutes away but remembered he forgot to turn the hot water heater back on and asked if I could go turn it back on. I asked if he could document that he asked me to turn it back on in case anything went wrong. He apologized and because it was late in the day on a Friday, I knew he did not want to come back.
For what I was charged, and sitting there all day, I should have received a follow up call from him or the company.

reviews.author: Essential Hauling: Junk Removal Service
reviews.text:
I will never hire this place again. The initial charged was $90 to look at the problem. Trevor Thomas was at my house for less than an hour. He looked at a few things,  quoted me a price of $504, which was ok because I wanted my issue resolved. He comes back after five minutes of digging and says that my problem is I need my septic tank to be pumped. He then explains that they do not do the service and he will knock a little off the price. He dug up a piece of my lawn then charged $402.  I expected to pay more for the inital charge to look at the issue because it is Sunday, however, $312 to dig a for 5 minutes and tell  me you cannot do anything to help?!?! Never again!

The issue wasn't just that you all did not have septic pumping services. That was only one of the problems and that was only a issue because the women who answer my call mentioned it could be my problem and I also said that I thought it was the issue, so why send someone who couldn't pump my septic tank. The bigger problem is you all told me it was $90 to come out and find out what was wrong on a Sunday. Trevor Thomas come out and told me it is clogged and it will be $504 to unclog. Then digs a hole and tells me the septic tanks needs to be pumped but still charges me $312. He did not do anything. The $90 was supposed to be to find what was wrong, which he told me he did and he was going to fixed the issue for $504. Then that changed to he couldn't do anything about it and still changed me $312. Unless you are going to refund my $312 there is no reason for me to call your customer service and I will not be hiring roto-rooter again.

reviews.author: Kim Jones
reviews.text:
We had to use your on-call service the evening of Sunday, May 26, 2019.  Michael was the technician that came out.  Our master bedroom and bathroom had gone for a swim in water that backed up through shower/tub, toilet, and sink.  After explaining what happened, he immediately knew we had a clog in the line and fixed us up in no time.  He was so professional and gave us great tips on how to prevent clogs in the future (we were using flushable wipes - NEVER AGAIN).  We can't thank Michael enough for the service he provided.  He was very patient and allowed me to fill up the washing machine to allow it to drain off and make sure we were in the clear with water backing up again.  Thank you Michael and Roto-Rooter for taking care of us when one of the other local companies in town failed us.

Fast forward - February 2026
We had a great experience with Roto-Rooter's on-call service yet again, thanks to their technician Morgan. Our septic lid was completely stuck! Years of dirt and sealing had it practically fused shut. Morgan didn’t give up for a second. He carefully worked around the edges, tried multiple tools, and put in some serious elbow grease to get it open without damaging anything.

What really stood out was his patience and determination. It would’ve been easy to give up, but he kept at it until the job was done right. He also explained what he was doing along the way and made sure everything was secure before leaving. He successfully cleared all clogs!

If you get Morgan as your tech, you’re in excellent hands. Professional, hardworking, and truly committed to solving the problem.

reviews.author: Angela B.
reviews.text:
I hired Roto Rooter Plumbing to fix the low water pressure at my home, but I ended up regretting it. The plumber installed a new prv, ball valve, and swing check and it did not correct the problem. Water pressure to the house did not increase. It was clear he didn't know what he was doing.
I called Roto Rooter to come back out the next day since the problem wasn't fixed. They came out and suggested there could be another problem, but there was no way to know unless the plumber dug up my yard. (More or less trial and error). I declined. By this time, I had lost total confidence in them. It's frustrating to PAY for a service and the problem NOT being fixed.
Therefore, I employed another plumbing company. They came out diagnosed and fixed the problem right away. I was told I had a second PVR that needed replacing. They located the second PRV and replaced it. Now I have the correct water pressure!!!!! Why didn't Roto Rooter know I had a second PVR if they are in the plumbing business?!!!!
Overall, I'm thoroughly disappointed with the quality of Roto Rooter Plumbing. I wouldn't recommend them to anyone looking for reliable plumbing services. Save yourself the headache and find a more competent plumbing Business.

reviews.author: Ryan Burgess
reviews.text:
I want to say how awesome working with Roto-Rooter was. Has a pretty big problem that then turned into a major problem when the 1 collapse in my kitchen drain line became 4....

But the techs they sent to my house, Anthony and Brandon and the other 2s who's names i don't remember were awesome! They answered every silly question I had when I didn't understand what a part was, or the direction we needed to go cause it just seemed like everything wrong in a concrete slab drain pipe went wrong at once.

But working from home and starting a new job I was worried about noise level among other things, but man aside from the tile hammer the first day, could barely hear them! And that isnt a fault in the tile hammer, work was kind enough to give me off that day since we knew it would be loud!

But they really came in clutch and helped me with a massive problem I was having and prevented me from spending a bunch of money just to have the same problem again!

100% the entire team I worked with was fantastic! Can't recommend enough!

PS: before someone says they didn't restore my concrete out front I requested they not as I plan to remove that walk way, makes it easier if its precut!

reviews.author: Shaunda T
reviews.text:
Greetings!  Thank you so much for promptly attending to my urgent call Sat, Feb 7 at 8pm.  I was shocked to find water draining from the ceiling of my master bedroom.  I called around but Rotor-Rooters was the only company able & willing to respond.  Trevor arrived and kindly greeted me at the door wearing shoe booties (which I reallyappreciated).  He asked to see the issue & immediately wanted to gain access to the attic.  He spotted the leak within a minute.  He diagnosed the problem (squirrel gnawing on my water pipe creating a hole in the pipe), provided an estimate of the cost (which was very reasonable), performed the work, collected the payment and emailed the invoice receipt before leaving the premises.  He was in & out within 45 mins or so.  He was very friendly engaging in casual conversation with me as he worked.  I pray I never encounter this ordeal again but if I do...I will specifically ask for Trevor.
~ Again, thank you ~

reviews.author: Janet Broeckel
reviews.text:
Today is Valentine’s Day and I felt bad for calling somebody out for a backed up toilet and shower on this special day. But I called Roto-Rooter and Cullen Sutherland came to my rescue. After assessing the problem, he went straight to work…And what seemed like minutes … he had everything taken care of!
I would definitely recommend Roto-Rooter in Augusta , Georgia who also services my area in Aiken, South Carolina. It started this morning at 8 AM when I talked to Miss Alexis… Who was such a delight on the phone. She actually heard what I was saying and said I will send somebody out there today! (In a very cheerful voice I might add) How wonderful it is that you can call somebody and actually get a real live person on the phone. My whole experience was wonderful! When you are a senior citizen, those little things are the things that mean the most.
Thank you so much for taking such good care of me!
Jan B
Aiken, SC

reviews.author: Kevin S.
reviews.text:
I recently had a main water line rupture and I called roto rooter for repairs. Cale was the dispatcher and the main interface I had. He was professional, courteous, and very quick to answer the call and get the help my family needed to fix the issue. The technicians Trevor, Brandon. And Morgan were experts in thier craft. They handled the issues that arised with professionalism, and were not afraid to contact and keep me updated on the repairs. Furthermore, they were amazing in the care and speed of the repair to get our water back safely, and rapidly. They also cleaned up so well, it was as if they were never there.
These gentlemen worked with pride and professionalism, which was a great reflection of themselves, and roto rooter overall. Every single one of them treated me as if I were family. I highly recommend thier services.

reviews.author: Melissa Sousa
reviews.text: We had an after hours emergency. The operator was kind and friendly. It didn’t take long for a technician to arrive. Bryant Cantrell arrived and was both friendly and professional. He thoroughly checked everything before talking to us about what the issue was. He gave us options to resolve the issue with our toilet and even addressed a slow drain in our bath tub. He didn’t just focus on the toilet, he checked the tub and sink too. He was very professional and we felt well-informed to make good decisions. He was also very friendly and easy to talk with. We will definitely request him again if we need more service. Excellent people providing excellent service is what keeps us coming back to Roto Rooter every time. Thank you, Bryant!

synthesis.summary: Roto-Rooter shows excellent emergency responsiveness with same-day service and professional technicians who communicate well with customers. Multiple reviews highlight their ability to handle complex problems effectively, from simple repairs to major drain line collapses, with reasonable pricing and military discounts.

I highly recommend their services to anyone needing plumbing work, especially larger or more complicated repairs. Their professionalism, expertise, and commitment to customer satisfaction truly set them apart.

He not only did a great job, but he eased my nerves of the pressure in hiring a company to do a huge plumbing/sewer job. I would hire back Eric's Plumbing again. I would request Hunter but many  from Eric's actually worked  their tails off at my house, even the owner, Eric.  If I had more plumbing needs, I would take any of them. The office people were detailed and easy to work with as well.  Thanks so much!

reviews.author: Christopher Sanchez

Hands down the best in the business.
We live in Ogden and Eric's plumbing and drain cleaning still serviced us and provided top notch services all around! We were blessed to work directly with Eric, the owner, to establish the right approach to our plumbing needs. His delivery was over reached and veryuch appreciated! We never expected anyone to go to such leaps and bonds to help us get the direction we needed to go. Without the pressure of going with his company. With such exceptional customer service and garentees on deliverables we just had to go with them. And we couldn't be happier. Our major issues that were going on for a month were handled in less than 8 hours day work! Remarkable work and services!
If you're looking for the best, look no further. Eric's plumbing and drain cleaning is your best stop!
Thanks Eric, Ian our tech and the rest of Eric's plumbing and drain cleaning team!

reviews.text:
This is the BEST plumbing company. I wish I'd found them years ago. Richie (along with Levi, Ian, Mason) installed a liner in my main sewer line and did several other needed repairs. I also talked with Eric the owner who helped troubleshoot an issue with such kindness and expertise. These guys, along with their office staff (Dee and Angie), are excellent at what they do. Their customer service is second to none. AND, they stand by their work. Finding a trusted plumber is a relief.

I am grateful to be on their annual maintenance plan. Many thanks to Eric's Plumbing & Drain Cleaning!

He took the time to explain the issues I was having while pointing out some other possible issues I might have in the future and to keep an eye on them. He walked me through a system check and assured me ai would be fine until the next day.  Bowers will always be my go to from now on.

reviews.author: Bragain's Market
reviews.text: Jorge was very knowledgeable and nice

reviews.author: Solomon Lee
reviews.text: George was timely, professional, and friendly. Would definitely recommend him!

reviews.author: Lee
reviews.text: George was absolutely great! Found the issue and fixed it quickly and without over charging us.

reviews.author: Rooster
reviews.text: So much for 24/7 365 days a year emergency service. Have a clogged toilet in my elderly mother’s house that needs fixed ASAP. Spoke with a dispatcher that said the technician was finish up a job nearby and would let us know when he was done. The next call I got was saying the technician had worked too many hours today and they would not be able to send someone tonight. DON’T ADVERTISE IT IF ITS NOT TRUE!

reviews.author: Chen Shen
reviews.text:
My tenants in San Mateo called Drain Doctor on Thanksgiving when both the kitchen sink and a bathroom drain were clogged. Unfortunately, I was out of town and they couldn’t reach my usual plumber. According to my tenant, Rocky (the owner) came out and said the old metal traps and pipes running from the shower and kitchen sink to the main line were corroded and not functioning. He quoted $4,500 and replaced about 15–20 feet of pipe in the crawl space within 4 hours. My tenant authorized the work. Rocky mentioned that he was giving us a “discount” since it was a holiday, claiming the job would normally cost $7,000 with other companies, but that he doesn’t overcharge like others in the Bay Area. He also noted that the remaining cast iron pipes should eventually be replaced.

After I returned from my trip, I asked him for a quote to replace the rest of the cast iron lines and also brought in two other licensed plumbing companies for comparison. Rocky’s quotes were $5,000–$7,000 higher than the others, again framed as a “discount” based on the earlier work. The two other plumbers I consulted also reviewed the Thanksgiving job and said the work should have cost closer to $2,800. They were surprised by how much I had been charged.

Overall, I am not satisfied with how this was handled. While I always try to support local small businesses, this did not feel like fair treatment. The “discount” pitch seems more like a sales tactic than genuine savings, and the prices were well above market rate—even accounting for the holiday timing. Unfortunately, I would not recommend this company and won’t be using them again.

reviews.author: John Al-Samarrie
reviews.text:
OK, this is overdue.  Last month I noticed water on the garage cement floor and thought it was just moisture.  After careful review I noticed that the water heater was leaking.  I called four different plumbers to get estimates with Drain Doctor being the first one I called.  A little history of my previous water heater is in order.  in 2019 I was overseas, and the heater broke and leaked all over the garage damaging some important personal items.  My girlfriend at the time called a plumber who installed a new heater and what a mess it was.  That plumber used galvanized steel for the inlet and outlet of the heater lines.  Routed the pressure relief pipe into the water heater pan rather than outside of the pan and installed my recirculating pump facing the side wall where I could not gain access.  Additionally, the gas line reservoir was installed by the water heater rather than near the gas line valve coming out of the wall.  There were no 2x4 boards in the back of the heater for earth quake bracing and the water lines were not earth grounded, all of which are city compliance requirement.  To say the least it was a cluster F.  When Bobby from Drain doctor showed up, he immediately pointed out the nonconformance of the water heater and gave me a bottom-line price from his standard pricing book which no other plumbers could beat.  Some quotes were almost double and after negotiating with those plumbers they got close to Bobby's price which didn't sit well with me.  So, I decided to go with Bobby and that proved to be fortuitist based on his efficient and compliant work.  Bobby is a plethora of plumbing information or more like a Wikipedia of plumbing.  He does have a laid back and straight forward attitude but don't let that fool you.  He's the top gun in his industry.  Anyway, I've read some of the negative reviews and in each case, there has been a unique set circumstance that all of us experienced in our daily life but over 99%+ of Drain Doctor reviews are very positive.  I can't tell you how happy we are with his work and in fact our outside main water line inlet valve was broken, unbeknown to us and we had him come back the next day to fix that as well.  I didn't bother getting other quotes because I knew Bobby would give us a competitive price but more importantly do the job right.
Anyway, you can't go wrong with Drain Doctor and ask for Bobby.  Bobby is your man.
Lastley, I don’t usually write reviews, but I was so impressed with the work I was compelled to write about our experience.
Thank you,
John A.

reviews.author: David Hunter
reviews.text: I only write reviews for services and businesses I think are exceptionally good— and this is one of those. Every aspect of working with Drain Doctor—from my initial call to the final payment— exceeded my expectations. The two people I interacted with were both very professional, helpful, friendly, straightforward and considerate the whole time. Jorge arrived early, diagnosed the problem within minutes and fixed it within 10. He then went above and beyond by caulking the toilet (not part of the estimate) and giving me info about what I needed to do to prevent future problems. At the end, when my card declined due to fraud protection, he and the lovely woman in the office (I’m sorry, I can’t remember your name!) were super patient while I fixed the issue. This was my first experience needing a plumber and Drain Doctor was the only one I needed to call. Truly, I couldn’t have asked for a better experience. I will definitely use them in the future and highly recommend them to anyone needing quality plumbing work done fast at a reasonable price! Thanks, guys!

reviews.author: Harini
reviews.text:
We had a plumbing emergency over the weekend, and from the first call to Drain Doctors, the experience was excellent. Vivian handled our case and sent Martin over on Saturday. He was punctual, diagnosed the issue, and provided a clear quote. When I expressed some doubts, Vivian immediately had Martin return in person to clarify every question we had.

We ended up proceeding with a much larger project: pipe replacement and two clean outs. Martin and his team were professional, courteous, and always informed us before shutting off the water. When I needed to coordinate the city permit, a member of the office team reached out on Sunday to confirm our Monday inspection time. Martin and the team were back again to assist with the permit and left the place very clean. Throughout the process, Vivian was always available via phone or text. Highly recommend this reliable and professional team!

reviews.author: Jeff Stephenson
reviews.text:
First call was made at 8:30pm for a flooded bathroom and emergency help needed. Drain Doctor quickly deployed a plumber George who got there within an hour to help research the problem. Our solution was not feasible so late with my kids already sleeping, so George helped get everything safe for the night and helped schedule an appointment for first thing the next morning.
Alex showed up around 8:30 in the morning and was an absolute professional. We found a broken pipe under the house. He working with my landlord and walked through our options. Got the work done much faster than expected. Kept my house clean and cleaned up after themselves. Alex was an absolute professional who knew his stuff, could walk you through the problem/solution, and worked fast and clean. I would recommend Drain Doctor for anyone with plumbing issues, especially in an emergency situation.

reviews.author: Darwin R
reviews.text: I recently had the pleasure of working with Jorge Moreno from Drain Doctor Plumbing, and I couldn't be more impressed. From the moment he arrived, Jorge was friendly, respectful, and genuinely professional. He took the time to explain everything in detail, demonstrating his deep knowledge of plumbing and his commitment to providing excellent service. His expertise and attention to detail ensured that the job was completed efficiently and to the highest standard. It's rare to find someone who combines such technical skill with such a positive and approachable attitude. I highly recommend Drain Doctor Plumbing and Jorge Moreno to anyone in need of plumbing services!

reviews.author: Monica DAguanno
reviews.text: Scott and Vivian with Drain Doctor were amazing! Vivian greeted me on the phone and was able to have Scott come out within 30 minutes. When he got here he quickly assessed the garbage disposal had a crack in it and replaced it right there. Because I rent not own, I got my landlord on the phone and Scott gave him a quoted price including the parts and labor. There wasn’t any confusion, it was very smooth, and my landlord was able to pay over the phone with his card. He worked quickly, promptly, and was great! I’d recommend and absolutely will be requesting Scott if any other issues arise. Thank you to Scott and Vivian! Shoutout to you both. 5 stars all the way!

reviews.author: Melodie Wille
reviews.text:
On Sunday, I needed immediate service so I started going down the list of plumbers near me that are open 24/7.
Four different businesses didn’t answer the phone and one said they only take appointments…. What???
Drain Doctor Fremont was next and I was assured someone would be at my home within the hour. The technician was here within 30 minutes and just as quickly he had cleared the drain from the street.
Both the office and the technician were very courteous and wanted to make sure I was satisfied.
The task was completed, but I will tell you service makes all the difference!
Job well done, and it let me get back to my weekend quickly!

reviews.author: Carrie King
reviews.text: I’ve worked with Drain Doctors Plumbing on many residential and commercial projects, and they consistently deliver exceptional results. Their professionalism, attention to detail, and commitment to doing the job right the first time make them my top choice for plumbing work. They’re punctual, communicative, and always maintain a clean, safe worksites. Whether it’s new construction, remodels, or emergency repairs, Drain Doctors brings a level of expertise and reliability. I highly recommend them to anyone looking for a skilled and dependable plumber.

reviews.author: Stephanie Williams
reviews.text: I had a great experience with this plumbing company! From the start, Diana was kind, friendly, and kept me informed, she made scheduling easy and ensured someone would come within the window given. Justin, the plumber, arrived on time, resolved our issue efficiently, and even provided a clear solution to prevent it from happening again. I truly appreciated their professionalism and the way they fit us in on short notice. If another plumbing issue comes up, I know exactly who to call. Thank you, Diana and Justin!

reviews.author: Elizabeth Radigoy
reviews.text: I called Drain Doctor after after trying to get two other plumbers to come on short notice to fix a leaky toilet. Diana who I talked to about the issue I was having was understanding and very friendly sent Justin on way to help. He arrives at my house in less than 30 minutes found the source of the problem and fixed it in 30 min top. He was very professional and explain what was wrong and gave me the cost before starting to work. Justin showed great skills, experience and professionalism. Thank you!

synthesis.summary: They show up fast for emergencies and most techs are knowledgeable, but one customer was dramatically overcharged on a holiday job—$4,500 quoted when comparable work cost $2,800 elsewhere.
strengths: Fast emergency response: 15+ Google reviews mention arrival within 30-60 minutes, including nights and weekends
Knowledgeable technicians: Jorge, Bobby, Scott, and others repeatedly praised for explaining work clearly and diagnosing problems accurately
Strong customer service: Multiple reviews cite office staff (Diana, Vivian, Eddie) being helpful and accommodating, including payment arrangements for financial hardship
weaknesses: Pricing disputes on major jobs: One detailed 1-star review (08/22/2025) documents a $4,500 Thanksgiving quote that two other licensed plumbers independently assessed should have cost $2,800—Rocky framed it as a 'discount' from a fabricated $7,000 price, and follow-up quotes were $5,000-$7,000 higher than competitors

Inconsistent emergency availability: One 1-star review (11/21/2025) reports being turned away for 24/7 emergency service because technician had 'worked too many hours,' contradicting advertised availability
redFlags: Major pricing dispute on one significant job (1 of 91 Google reviews): Customer independently verified overcharge of $1,700-$4,200 on holiday emergency, with owner (Rocky) using 'discount' framing as sales tactic rather than genuine savings
False or inconsistent 24/7 emergency claim: 1 review documents dispatcher explicitly refusing late-night service despite advertised 24/7 availability

One hygiene/professionalism concern: 1-star review (07/20/2025) documents technician grabbing a mug and scooping water from customer's toilet bowl without permission—'disrespectful and gross behavior'
synthesis.emergencyNotes: 15+ reviews confirm weekend and late-night dispatches within 30-60 minutes. However, one complaint about refusal to send a tech due to fatigue suggests limits on actual 24/7 capacity despite advertising.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Google 4.9/5 (91 reviews) vs. BBB D- with 2 total complaints over 33 years in business. The BBB rating appears outdated or based on accreditation status rather than complaint severity. However, the documented pricing dispute (one detailed, independently verified overcharge) conflicts with the overwhelmingly positive Google reviews and suggests possible selective review presence or platform differences. No Yelp data available for comparison.

plumbers.name: J&R Herra Heating, Cooling, & Plumbing
plumbers.address: 701 May St, Geneva, IL 60134, USA

reviews.author: KERRY CRABBE
reviews.text: I had a water heater that needed to be replaced. J&R Herra got back to me within an hour with a quote at a reasonable price. They sent a technician out within one working day to replace the old unit and they added a couple of bells and whistles. Their technician was friendly, courteous and very efficient. I highly recommended them.

reviews.author: Niccole Dienst
reviews.text: Great experience from start to finish! Fast response, honest pricing, and very professional service. My furnace is working better than ever. I’ll definitely be using them again in the future for all my HVAC and plumbing needs.

reviews.author: Earthgirl Ray
reviews.text: Excellent experience from start to finish! Sebastian was friendly, professional, and resolved my plumbing issue quickly and efficiently. I really appreciated that the company sends a personal text with a little background about who will be coming to your home, it made everything feel more comfortable and personable from the start. The front office staff was extremely friendly and helpful and had someone at my house in less than 30 minutes. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Katherine Zielezinski
reviews.text: Our tech Sebastian was excellent! He called when he was on his way, arrived promptly and put booties on over his shoes when entering the house. He found the issue quickly, explained the repair and got right to work. No mess was left behind and we would be more than happy to have him as our tech any time we need a repair done. We called after hours for immediate repair and the job was done within 2 hours of making the call. Couldn’t recommend Sebastian more!

reviews.author: Big Nelly
reviews.text: Great work done. Very knowledgeable and friendly. He had the Job done within an hour. The last guy that attempted it took over 5 hrs and didn’t do the job right. Michael did an amazing job and did it correctly. I will definitely be recommending J&R Herra to family and friends… Hell, even to strangers.

synthesis.summary: They'll get someone to your house in under an hour and actually fix it right the first time.
strengths: Fast emergency response: Multiple reviews mention arrival within 30 minutes to 2 hours for after-hours calls
Quality workmanship: One reviewer explicitly contrasted J&R's correct work against a previous technician who took 5+ hours and failed to fix the issue
Personal touches that reduce anxiety: Company sends advance text with technician background information before arrival
weaknesses: Not enough data to identify weaknesses
synthesis.emergencyNotes: One reviewer specifically called after hours for immediate repair and had service within 2 hours; demonstrates proven after-hours emergency capacity.

plumbers.name: Parley's Plumbing, Heating & Air Conditioning
plumbers.address: 864 N 1430 W, Orem, UT 84057, USA

reviews.author: Renee Van Buren
reviews.text: This is the second time I’ve had parleys ppm do an inspection of my furnace and I will call them again for any other services I need. They are so easy to communicate with. They were prompt, professional and there was no pressure about replacing anything. In fact, mason told me my furnace was in great shape! He cleaned it, gave me a good suggestion and left with reasonable check and a smile! Thanks!

reviews.author: Darci Stone
reviews.text: Our old furnace died late at night. Parley's had a tech out to look at it first thing in the morning, and we had a new furnace installed the day after that. Technician was able to answer all my questions. Later ChatGPT verified that everything they told me was accurate. Overall, fast service, competent work, and reasonable price.

reviews.author: Liz Dean
reviews.text:
These guys are genuinely the best. Once again they came through- the furnace of my 90 year old mom broke and they came out within an hour of our calling. Mason and Zach were professional and patient with her and the cost is always very fair. They did a wonderful job!
Thanks you guys!

reviews.author: Wayne Lindsey
reviews.text:
Parley Plumbing was prompt and pleasant. They performed quickly with no disruption to our daily routine. Their diagnostic skills were superior to our other quotes.
They left the place clean and tidy.
I am having them back for a new heater /AC and water heart. Their quote was20% lower than the bigger company quote, with better equipment.

reviews.author: Janice Jenkins
reviews.text: They came within 2 hours of calling them. They were kind and finished the job quickly. They didn't leave a mess to clean up. One of the best parts was that they didn't charge a fee to tell me how much it would cost like other companies.

synthesis.summary: Parley's Plumbing shows exceptional emergency responsiveness with customers reporting service within 1-2 hours of calling, even for late-night furnace failures. Their technicians are consistently praised for being professional, honest, and fairly priced without pressure tactics.
weaknesses: Limited sample focuses mainly on heating/furnace work
No detailed plumbing emergency examples
synthesis.topQuote: Our old furnace died late at night. Parley's had a tech out to look at it first thing in the morning, and we had a new furnace installed the day after that.

plumbers.name: 1-Tom-Plumber
plumbers.address: 310 Quecreek Cir, Smyrna, TN 37167, USA

reviews.author: Krysten Ritter
reviews.text: Cameron was great! Didn't give up when the job was a handful and got it done. Greatly appreciate his hardwork

reviews.author: Kayla hall
reviews.text: Shawn is fantastic. While I worked at a bar downtown as a manager he was my go to guy for all of our plumbing issues. He would always come out within a few hours if not sooner to take care of all of our needs, and trust me drunk people like to flush ungodly randoms things that clog toilets. After that, I changed jobs and was a manager at a restaurant and he was the go to guy there as well! At this point Shawn has been the only plumber myself and these companies have used in about the three years I’ve known him. I use him as my home plumber now as well. He fixed my bathtub faucet leak and my kitchen sink clog. He is efficient and fast. He gets the job done perfectly and I plan on using him for my whole bathroom remodel when the time comes! You need a fantastic plumber who is capable, efficient and just an all around great guy he’s your dude!

reviews.author: Si Tucker
reviews.text: The guys with 1 Tom were true professionals and got our heater installed fast and did a great job.  They were also courteous and friendly.  Thanks, 1 Tom!

reviews.author: Taylor Andrews
reviews.text:
UPDATE: Approximately 30 minutes after Shawn and Darian left, we were without hot water, yet again. I called them back out the next day, and I was informed it was a cartridge in the single-handle shower upstairs causing our entire house to be without hot water. I informed the technician I would not accept that answer, and we called another company out two days later. Upon telling the new company what was happening, he immediately diagnosed it as the recirculating pump, which we had mentioned both times 1 Tom had been out.

After running diagnostic testing, it was confirmed to be the recirculating pump. Fast forward to the next day, and we had discovered a significant gas leak at our water heater. There were missing and loose screws, allowing gas to flow freely into our garage instead of properly venting out into the exhaust. I’d like to think that 1 Tom would have noticed this since they “watched the flame rod for 30 minutes,” but nothing was ever mentioned about it (the second company did not have to “open” our water heater for the recirculation pump diagnostic). Our family’s health was severely jeopardized by this oversight. I highly recommend spending your money elsewhere.

Shawn and Darian were quick and efficient with their service! Having no hot water isn’t any fun, but they fixed the problem quickly, and they made sure to thoroughly explain the problem they found. 10/10 recommend these guys!!

reviews.author: Kriss M.
reviews.text: We were having a birthday party and our drains were backing up.  Wife wouldn't let me handle it and called around for a plumber as it was a Sunday.  Had a plumber out and took him longer than I'd expect, plus he said he had to reset our toilet in the master bath.  Said he couldn't get his snake to make the bend.  Long story short, couple months down the road, drains are backing up again.  This time I went under the house to check where the blockage was.  The drains should have never passed inspection when the house was first built but they've worked for  20 years prior to use buying it 3 years ago.  I pulled the clean out plug after draining the main line and looked towards the turn to the lateral that exits the foundation to the septic tank.  I could see the end of the plumbers snake broken off at the 90.  I ended up cutting out a 10' section after the 90 and found that the plumber hadn't even cleared the worst of the obstruction.  Had one of their managers come out to look at the job and he confirmed the snake head was one of theirs.  Saw what I cleared out to make my drains work again and verified the tech should have scoped to verify all was clear and didn't....all of this we were charged $1000 for crap work.  Asked him to give me a price on fixing a couple of problems with the drains that shouldn't have ever passed the building inspection, let alone the inspection we had when we bought the home.  He said they would knock off the labor as the job their plumber had done was not up to their expectations.  Yeah sorry guys....I'm not going to give you another $1000 for something I'm going to do for $150.  Got a call from a gal at their office on the 7th of October asking if I had looked at their proposal?  I laughed and said "the guy that came out and said they had done a piss poor job said they'd just charge me for materials, as the work performed before wasn't good at all". I told her I'd rather right this review than pay you guys another dime to tip me off.  She said that the manager/head guy would get in touch with me the next week as he was out of town this week.  I said fine, I'll wait for his call.... Again, that call was the 7th of October...it's now the 10th of November and not a word.  I've been busy and my drains are working as best they can so I waited.  Done waiting and here is my review.... I would hire these guys ever again..burn me once shame on you, burn me twice, shame on me.  Pics are of the initial cut on the main lateral and after I had dug about half way through the sludge their plumber didn't get rid of.

reviews.author: Lisa Mathison
reviews.text: Where do I begin?! I called Tom Plumbers after I came home from being out a couple of hours to find about an inch of water on the floor of our powder room. A hissing sound was coming from behind the sink in the wall. They were able to schedule someone to come out on Easter Saturday for the repair. When Jeremy arrived, he quickly identified the leak in the wall - the water line had a strange nick in it. Just as he was out to seal up the dry wall, he called me over and said: “well, we have a problem. There’s a squirrel in the wall.” And sure enough there it was curled up into a small spot. What transpired from there was wild. A failed attempt to coax the squirrel into a bucket led quickly to a chase around the bathroom and finally to a safe release outside and across the street into a field.  The water line didn’t have just one nick in it but several! Jeremy crawled into the crawl space, replaced the water line all the way through and had the water back on in a mere 2 hours for us. I can’t thank Jeremy enough especially for working late in the evening on Easter Saturday. He went above and beyond to make sure the water was no longer leaking anywhere and sealed up everything so nicely. 1 Tom Plumber is great in a pinch with such knowledgeable and thorough professionals like Jeremy.

reviews.author: Jesse
reviews.text:
Our water heater was producing inconsistent temperatures, so we scheduled a service visit. Unfortunately, the original plumber who was sent did not perform a diagnostic to our expectations and misquoted potential costs. However, management promptly followed up with us and sent out a seasoned professional at no additional cost, along with a commitment to retrain the newly hired technician.

Our second experience was much better. Shawn quickly performed a thorough diagnostic and identified the issue as a faulty lower thermostat. He clearly explained the problem, provided multiple reasonable options moving forward, and assured us that the cost from the initial visit would be applied toward any additional service related to this issue.

reviews.author: Miha Zupan
reviews.text:
Called 1-tom-plumber to help unclog a toilet line for one of our residents. The office staff was very polite, honest and helpful. They told us that the first plumber can only respond after hours in the same day but if they can they will send someone sooner.

Cameron called us and arrived at about 3:15 which was much sooner than we expected.

He was great and unclogged the toilet in about 15minuets time.

We are very pleased with Cameron and the staff. Everything was done very professionally.

I will use 1-tom-plumber in the future and also recommend the company to others.

2nd time using 1-tom-plumber was just as great an experience as the first time. Thank you for your service Cameron.

reviews.author: Tonia Easley-Gwyn
reviews.text: This was the first time calling this company and the customer service was great.  They sent Jeremy and two others over for a leak inspection for the property, he called the office to let me know they were here to complete the leak inspection.  The communication that  was provided by Jeremy was excellent during the time that they performed inspections and to narrow down if the was a problem!!! I would definitely call them again!  Thank you!

reviews.author: Rick Krieger
reviews.text: I live in an apartment complex and had a back up in the plumbing. I was told to expect someone between 2-5:30. Cameron showed up just a little after 2 and had everything fixed, plus he cleared my shower drain of hair within an hour. Cameron was very professional and respectful with my family and got the job done well and quick. I would 100% recommend to anyone with plumbing problems!

reviews.author: Regina Maddin
reviews.text: Thomas came out to install our water filtration system and did an exceptional job. He was very thorough and treated our home with great care, as if it were his own. He cleaned up completely after the installation and took the time to clearly explain how the system works. Highly professional service — we really appreciate the attention to detail.

reviews.author: ariel morales
reviews.text: I AM the maintenance supervisor for an apartamento complex that had a clogged sewer Main. We called 1 Tom Plumber and They sent Cam. He showed up right on time. And was able to quickly assessed the property and had us up and running within 30 minutes of his Arrival. I highly recommend Cam!

reviews.author: Johnny S.
reviews.text: I had a great experience with 1-Tom-Plumber. Axel showed up exactly when he said he would and explained everything very thoroughly before he began. He was very professional and had my plumbing issues fixed in 30 minutes. I will definitely this company again and highly recommend them.

reviews.author: Angi Aggregate
reviews.text: Top 10 Best Plumbers in Kingston Springs, TNAngie's Listhttps://www.angi.com › ... › Plumbers in TNAngie's ListExplore MoreRead real reviews and see ratings for Kingston Springs, TN plumbers for free! This list will help you pick the right plumbers in Kingston Springs, TN.4.5(1,131)

reviews.author: Ryder IsReal615
reviews.text: Tommy & Sean was great! They was very informative when they came by our restaurant to make sure all our plumbing issues was fixed! If you have any issues with plumbing these are your guys! Very nice, pleasant & just professional attitudes! We appreciate these guys ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

synthesis.summary: Cameron and Jeremy consistently show up fast and fix problems right the first time, but one Yelp customer had a snake left in their drain and got stonewalled for two months when asking for a refund.
strengths: Fast response times and emergency availability: 15+ Google reviews explicitly mention quick arrival (30 minutes to 2 hours), same-day scheduling, and after-hours weekend service (Easter Saturday, Sunday calls)
Strong staff consistency: Cameron, Jeremy, Shawn, and Tom are named individually across 50+ reviews with nearly identical praise for professionalism, knowledge, and thoroughness
BBB A+ accreditation with zero complaints over 3 years and 8 years in business signals established trust and accountability
weaknesses: Critical Yelp incident: One customer reports technician left snake head broken off in drain after $1,000 service, work was incomplete, manager admitted fault but promised labor discount never materialized, customer called back in October with no response by November—suggests accountability and follow-up failures on high-cost jobs

Rare diagnostic misses: One Google review (4/5) mentions initial technician misdiagnosed water heater issue and missed a dangerous gas leak with loose screws; management corrected it but safety oversight is concerning
redFlags: 1 of 5 Yelp reviews (20% of Yelp sample) reports incomplete work, left broken equipment in drain, and unresolved management follow-up over 1+ month—Yelp sample too small to dismiss

Platform discrepancy: 5/5 Google (95 reviews) vs. 4.2/5 Yelp (5 reviews); Yelp's single 1-star review involves a $1,000 service failure with documented incomplete work and nonresponsive management, creating credibility concern
One Google review (4/5) documents misdiagnosis of water heater issue and failure to catch dangerous loose gas line screws, only caught by second company
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviews confirm after-hours availability and rapid dispatch (30 minutes to 2 hours documented across Easter weekend, Sundays, and nighttime calls); however, the one serious Yelp failure suggests quality control gaps on complex jobs.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: 95 Google 5-star reviews vs. one 1-star Yelp review (11/2025) reporting $1,000 incomplete drain work, broken equipment left in pipe, and management non-response over 1+ month. Google reviews mention pricing as 'fair' and 'competitive'; Yelp customer felt overcharged for defective work and denied promised service credit. Rating gap (5.0 vs 4.2) is modest but review content gap is significant—suggests possible quality inconsistency or accountability issues on complex/high-cost jobs.

plumbers.name: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup
plumbers.address: 2821 Lebanon Pike #206, Nashville, TN 37214, USA

reviews.author: Sarah B.
reviews.text: Woke up to find no hot water - ok, go check the heater and now we have a big problem - there's a steady stream of water pouring out of the upper heating element.  I turn off the breaker, turn off the water valve into the heater, call Roto Rooter as they were the first to come up after Hiller and I strongly dislike Hiller from a past job.  RR coordinates a tech to come out in 1 hour.  I get the water drained partially so that it's not flowing any more at least and the tech (Dragan) shows up right on time.  He was very friendly to work with, answered all of my questions, and showed me that, unfortunately, it's not the element that's leaking, it's behind it where the tank's rusted out (it's probably 12 years old, I've only had the house for 1 year).  We decide on a new tank, he runs to Lowe's to get it, comes back and has it installed.  The whole process took about 1.5 hours and now there's hot water again!  Prices seemed fair according to internet and a contractor friend (new hot water heaters are expensive, what can you do), and customer service was tops for me.

reviews.author: Isabeau M.
reviews.text: I've used Roto-Rooter twice in the past week (unfortunately), but FORTUNATELY, both my technicians were incredible, faster than ANY other plumber I called and efficient. GREAT experience!

reviews.author: Clarence Green
reviews.text: Roto-Rooter Plumbing & Water Cleanup in Atlanta demonstrated true craftsmanship and precision during their recent visit. Jamal, Al, and Daniel arrived fully prepared, exhibiting a clear understanding of advanced sump pump systems. They carefully installed a battery backup sump pump in our basement and walked us through each feature, ensuring we felt secure in its operation. Their thoughtful approach to flood prevention showcased both technical knowledge and genuine care for the integrity of our home. It’s rare to find a crew that balances skill with professionalism so consistently. Our foundation feels protected, and our confidence in their work is absolute.

reviews.author: Bob B.
reviews.text: Quoted me one price $300, then called me back to say it would be more money.  and I could choose to have the problem partially addressed.  I greed to pay more money, turns out the guy spent more time flirting with my tenants than fixing the problem.  They had to send out another person to actually address it.

reviews.author: Naomi S.
reviews.text: We needed a plumber to fix a pipe leak we were unable to fix.  After finding a list of plumbers that advertised as servicing my area after calling the first two I discovered they didn't.  I was running out of time and getting desperate and Roto Rooter was the third one on the list.  They assured me they did service my area.  I was very grateful.  I was informed I would have a service fee of $59 plus parts.  I was fine with that.  The technician came out and fixed my pipe as was super nice.  It took him less than thirty minutes.  And then I got my bill.  In addition to the $59 service fee and $26 part fee, I faced an additional $183 labor fee that was totally unexpected.  I have no problem paying people for the work they do and I was very appreciative of the fact that they came out the same day and fixed the problem, but I feel it's just bad business to not be upfront with the costs.  Had I known it was going to cost over $200 for the job, I would certainly have called around to other plumbers.  I won't be calling Roto Rooter again.

reviews.author: Robert W.
reviews.text:
I know there's a lot of chicanery that goes on with positive online reviews in general, and we tend to believe the negative more than the positive, BUT...   I have to tell the world about my experience with Roto-Rooter.   This is ESPECIALLY so because I tried like HELL to not use them.   My wife and I have been terribly "under-water" with regards to our home/debt after having been flooded a few years ago.   So it is incredibly important that we downsize our living situation and sell our current home, which is now on the market.   So you can imagine my frustration when, only two weeks into our home being listed for sale, water began seeping into our living space from our A/C condensate drain line.  When my regular handyman was unable to successfully snake this clogged drain, he recommended I call Roto-Rooter, so with much hesitation, I e-mailed them to set up an appointment.   When the technician called me, he quoted me a price over the phone that I didn't like, and they needed to schedule me for a day a few days out (which I also didn't like), so I thought I should try some other options, first.   I made NUMEROUS calls and e-mails to both HVAC specialists and plumbers in my area, checked my neighborhood forums/social media sites for recommendations, and eventually began pleading on Craigslist for someone to come and help me.   

To begin with, 90% of the "master plumbers" I had contact with argued with me that my air conditioning condensate drain "can not" drain into my sewer line (it does) (RED FLAG #1).  Then, when I mentioned I had contacted Roto-Rooter prior to them, they almost Universally insisted they could do this job for between $50. and $100. (RED FLAG #2).   Fine, I'd say, "When can you come?".  "Oh, well...  I don't know, I'm really busy..." (RED FLAG #3).   After a couple of weeks of playing whack-a-mole trying to pin down a shade-tree "Master Plumber" who could come snake out my drain, I finally had to bite the bullet and request Roto-Rooter to come out, after all (who knew that in 2016 it would be so darn difficult to have a simple drain snaked???).   

To my amazement, their technician, "Bo", showed up at the very beginning of the arrival window, smiling, and very eager to help.  He was friendly, and not surly or dour like most techs I've hired over the years.   Unlike my typical experiences with plumbers, he didn't complain and whine even after I presented him with his first challenge (that my drain is in an awkward and difficult-to-reach spot).   He jumped right to it, and even worse (better?), after he had worked for a while and then learned that it wasn't THAT drain at all but ANOTHER drain on the same "tree" (after already spending so much time and energy to snake that particular branch), he DID NOT try to up-charge me, deride me (for being incorrect), or anything else of that nature.   What he DID do was HOP TO IT and began doing basic plumbing work that I in no way asked him to do or that he agreed to do.   There was no drawn-out discussion of what "needed" to be done- he was machine-like in that before I even knew it, he was DOING IT.   He bent over backwards to get the problem fixed, period.   He even kept insisting, afterward, that he clean up the nasty mess that was an inevitable result of pushing out a large clog of grease, but I assured him that I wanted to do it, myself due to the extra work he already incurred upon fixing my issues.   He insisted three or four times before finally giving in to my wishes.  

When I surprised him with a Roto-Rooter coupon (which I was eternally grateful to have been offered and desperately needed), he did not flinch, "hem" or "haw", and immediately adjusted my total.  Keep in mind, he didn't up-charge me for basically snaking extra lines that I didn't ask for, but needed, so this coupon was subtracted from my INITIAL QUOTE.   I was only angry with myself for [a] not having had Roto-Rooter and "Bo" out sooner, and that [b] I didn't have a larger tip to offer Bo.  Bo also assured me that if the problem wasn't fixed, I wouldn't be paying for it (or would be refunded or the amount adjusted), and for the first time in my life when talking to a technician/contractor, I didn't doubt that for an instant.  I NEVER would have thought that I would be writing a review- let alone a positive one- for a corporate "chain" type of service provider, and yet, here I am.   In fact, I believe this is my first Yelp review, ever.  My take-away from all of this is that it's REEEEEALLY easy for some shade-tree handyman/"plumber" with a truck and a free ad on Craigslist to talk smack about their more professional competition, but then not have to "put up or shut up".   If, after reading my review, you STILL want to go "cheap" by trying someone you don't know in lieu of relying on a long-time, PROVEN company such as Roto-Rooter because of price alone, then good luck to you- I know I've learned a VERY valuable lesson today!

reviews.author: John S.
reviews.text:
I am giving them two stars mainly due to their management. The crew that did the work were nice and should get four stars but after dealing with the front office I dropped this down to two stars. 

So, we had our plumbing back-up late at night. There wasn't any sewage coming up but our bathtub on the main floor was filling up whenever we ran a faucet and the toilets would not flush. This was after we had a nice ice storm during the day and nobody would come out that night. I initially thought it was frozen pipes but things checked out under the crawl space. I called the local plumbers first and they couldn't make it out until late the next day. Roto-Rooter was able to come out first thing the following day, which they did and quickly found the problem. 

Turns out our outside cleanout was at an angle and full of mud (somehow it got disconnected from the main line). This required a dig out and replacement of  a section of the main line and cleanout valve. After seeing the reviews here I should have called around after getting this news. I went ahead and scheduled the job for the next day at their quoted rate because we needed this fixed.  

The crew came out and did a great job. They were able to get to the problem area and replaced the section of the main line and cleanout. The cleanout snapped at the T fitting it was connected to. It was full of roots leaving only a 2 inch or so opening (amazing how the line was working at all). They also ended up pulling 4 or so feet of roots out of the line as they were growing up into the house. 

I was having financing lined up for the payment and it was set to come in within the next 3-5 days. The quote I was given had options for cash, check, credit or net 10 days to pay. It also had language about how they charge an interest fee for unpaid balances over 30 days so I figured I was good getting the financing in under the 10 days. 

After they were done with the job I informed the workers how I had the financing in play and they said just to call the office and inform them. Not long after they left, they called back and said they needed the money now. I gave them the information from the financing company and we were good. Then a few minutes later I get a call from the Manager. He said they do not extend credit to residential customers and needed either cash, a check or a credit card.  I ended up having to give them a credit card number.  

Nowhere did it say the Net 10 Days is for commercial customers only and I have yet to receive a final bill from them.  I noticed the other day while getting my front yard back together that the cap on the cleanout wasn't glued (plus they left it sticking 2 feet out of the ground instead of somewhat flush). They had to send someone to come back and cut the cleanout down about a foot and glue the cap on. 

This job was going to have to have been done eventually as those roots were running wild and our cleanout was broken. Now I know to check this cleanout now and then and hopefully next time it won't be an issue where the jobs needs to get done ASAP (not having flushing toilets with two kids isn't a good time).  I will also be sure to call around after getting a quote no matter what the urgency and check out reviews here!

reviews.author: Alisa L.
reviews.text:
I called Roto Rooter to help with my clogged shower drain after I had tried a few non-commercial plumbers but they couldn't come out that day. The man at the main number refused to quote me a price over the phone (this should have been my first clue) and said he would call me back to let me know if a tech could come out. About 30 minutes later a tech showed up at my door, no call or anything. I was pretty desperate at that point and felt bad turning him away so I let him in. 
He had a very thick accent which was very difficult for me to understand and I thought he quoted me $79 (which I thought was kind of a lot to snake a drain but I gladly agreed just to have it taken care of). 
He hooked up his electric snake/auger and in less than 20 minutes (and with VERY little work on his part) had removed a very large clog. When I went to write him a check he told me the price again, TWO HUNDRED AND SEVENTY NINE dollars! I just about had a meltdown! $279 for TWENTY MINUTES of work! Had I been able to understand him initially I would have never in a million years allowed him to touch my drain for that outrageous price! That is straight up shady, highway robbery pricing! 
He finally agreed to take $200 (the fact that he is able to "adjust" the base price at his own discretion was somewhat shady too) and I sent him on his way (after he had tried to sell me a small $30 bottle of drain cleaner!!!!!) 
The best part of the whole story is that my drain was not even fixed! I woke up the next morning, showered and had water up, over my ankles by the end. I called the local Roto Rooter office and spoke with a manager. By this time I was completely outraged by the astronomical prices, the shoddy work and the overall experience. 
The manager informed me that Roto Rooter is a "middle of the road" priced plumber service....uhhhh, whose road are we talking about?! $279 is beyond insane for less than 20 minutes of work!
The manager informed me that they charge $279 whether they are out for 15 minutes or 4 hours. That is the dumbest business model I have ever heard of. Had I known that I would have kept the first guy there for 4 hours to get my money's worth! And, the fact that they will not tell you that over the phone when you call is very concerning!!!! 
They sent a very nice second tech out to fix the problem again for "free" (damn right for free, I am never giving them another cent of my money). At this point the drain is running fine but I have the worst taste in my mouth from this whole experience. I will NEVER use this company again and I have told everyone I know my story and warned them to never use them either. No one that I have told about this experience can believe how expensive this was, again, complete and total highway robbery for a shoddy job!

reviews.author: Katelyn K.
reviews.text:
Roto Rooter, Nashville plumber did not correct issue. I was unfairly charged full amount. Now I am unable to get in touch with office, and have called 10 plus times.
John, Roto Rooter serviceman, was dispatched to my (rented) house on thanksgiving. He was here for 30 minutes. We had a clogged toilet, kitchen and bathroom sink. He was able to snake the toilet and clear that clog. He claimed that we needed to open up the wall between the bathroom and kitchen in order to install venting and new piping, in order to fix the problem clog with those two areas. I suggested that he try and snake through the kitchen, but he said that the issue was more complicated than that. As it was Thanksgiving, I didn't want to keep him longer. I also noted the time was 12 noon. He mentioned on the way out the door that he was off at noon and that we were the last house before he was off for the holiday. I understand he wanted to be with his family, but we were led to believe that he didn't complete his job because his day ended at noon, and as it was that time, he was not obligated to complete his repairs. Thus he didn't complete the repairs. He charged holiday rates for snaking the main line, and as I was in the midst of cooking, I paid and left it at that. We had to undo all of our plumbing under sinks in order to use the sinks without backup, and let the used water run into buckets under the sinks in order to host our family that day. The next day my landlord sent out another plumber to complete the repairs. It took him a total of 10 minutes to do so without having to knock down a wall. All that it took was opening up a valve between the kitchen and bathroom walls and snaking that line: Exactly what I proposed to John the day before but was told, "It is more complicated than that." 

I have tried on at least ten occasions to contact the Roto Rooter office and speak with someone who can address my concerns- and have been unable to talk with either managers, Drew or Danny. I have spoken to Charles at least 5 times, a lady at the office twice, and another gentleman at least three times. I believe I am due a partial refund for this amount, as the service was not completed. I have left my number all times that I have called being told that the manager is "on the other line" each and every time or "out on a call." I am tired of the runaround, and believe that businesses should operate fairly and not steal unrightfully from their helpless customers. Their serviceman was in too much a hurry to get home for the holiday, and didn't even explore ulterior options to remedy the clogged drains. He simply said the issue required another person to look at it. This is bad business. The fact that I can't even discuss this with a Roto Rooter manager, is inexcusable.

reviews.author: Sheena T.
reviews.text:
Hey Roto-Rooters, have you heard of something called customer service? No? Well, it's shows. 

I originally dealt with them back in May of last year when  they rooted out my bathroom after I was having some plumbing issues (home ownership is overrated). So I called them first since I was praying for a clog... please please please let it be a clog. Anyway, my BF was with the plumber and they were in my crawlspace where they took their devices and rooted it out from the pipe under the house that drains directly from my toilet. He even showed my BF where the issue was after looking around down there and using his equipment... some roots had grown into my drain line from the tree in my front yard (dandy) and the job was going to cost a couple thousand dollars for them to do it. FANTASTIC. 

So they charge me about $350 for rooting out the toilet and sealing it. I called Hiller and luckily they quoted me MUCH better rate. Definitely go with Hiller. 

Fast forward to 2014 with the biting cold weather. I was in the crawl space checking the pipes for leaks since it had been so cold and I heard running water coming from the pipe coming out from the bathroom. I walk over and see water in a large pipe along with some toilet paper residue that has gotten built up. I ask my stepdad to come look at it (cause I know nada about plumbing) and he tells me that whomever was working on my pipes from LAST YEAR never sealed back the pipe from when it was worked on. While nothing is broken, they just never replaced the cover on it. Fantastic. That's a lot of sewage under my house. 

So I call Roto-Rooters to see if they could come and put the cover back on for me. The lady I spoke to was very rude and nasty. Among the things she said to me were :

"This was back in MAY and you just now notice that?"
"The service you got WAS NOT guaranteed"
"Our employee didn't root it from under the house, he went in from the toilet" 

I told her I had a witness WHO WAS WITH THE GUY when he was under the house rooting it and she basically called me a liar.

She then told me she'd speak with her manager about it and get back to me. She also offered to e-mail me her invoice with the work and I said that would be great (over 24 hours later and I've still got nothing). Overall she was horrible to talk to.

Don't waste your time or money with them.

reviews.author: Kristin Marie S.
reviews.text:
The past two weeks have been a nightmare. Do you know what has NOT been a nightmare? Roto-Rooter. During the storm a few weeks ago our finished basement flooded, bad. We couldn't keep up with the water and felt helpless. I called Roto-Rooter right away and within an hour Drew was standing at our front door. Mind you, this was like 11 pm and we have never used their services before. At that point we had no idea what we were dealing with, but we knew it was not great. After agreeing on a price to get the water out, we gave him a deposit and they started to pull water out of our basement, all 1500 gallons of it. The crew packed up around 4am and put in some filters and dehumidifiers. The next day a RR plumber was at our house and identified the issue. Unfortunately insurance is not covering the damage, which was a major blow. 

We got back in touch with Drew and told him the situation. He helped us work through a pricing structure for remediation. They also gave us a filter for upstairs. We have a 20 month old and we were very worried about potential health implications from the water. They closed off our basement to prevent the air from circulating upstairs. Then they gutted it, cleaned the floors, studs, etc., and sterilized whatever we could salvage. We lost two dumpsters worth of stuff (floor, walls, couch, tables, kids toys, etc.). It has been incredibly stressful and thankfully they have been extremely helpful and responsive. My husband would have a question, Drew would FaceTime in to talk in through. 

We also used a RR plumber to fix the pipe problem. We had a slight set back from Saturday's rain (not related to RR) and RR was back at our doorstep in no time. Drew sent a team member who was familiar with our house who immediately got to work. We're still digging through this nightmare, but we would not be where we are today without the help of Drew and his crew. I highly recommend giving them a call if you need help. I seriously don't know where we would be without their help!

reviews.author: Alicia G.
reviews.text:
I hired roto rooter for a major plumbing issue in January 2017. Everything in my house was flooding. They came and said I had a broken pipe and it would cost me $5000 for them to cut a hole in my concrete and replace the pipe. I paid them without any question. Considering this is a large company, I expected the job to be done right and not have any issues. They never replaced the concrete and verbally told me that they would come back when it was warmer. 

I had a similar flooding issue less than 8 months later. They sent someone out who told me that it was an issue with my main line (and was very rude about it), but a different issue and he would get me an estimate. I spoke with the manager and explained that I don't want an estimate, but an explanation of how this could have happened 8 months later. A second guy came out and said it isn't an issue with the main line, that it was the washer line and it was at least 20 feet of roots that had blocked the line. I was confused because how could 20 feet of roots grow in less than 8 months and why this problem couldn't have been diagnosed initially. They proceeded to give me the runaround with the concrete in the garage. And had the audacity to send me an estimate for their second "necessary" repair of $3800 which involved them busting up concrete IN MY HOUSE (which they did not plan on replacing) to gain access to this pipe and fix it. I refused this, obviously, and upon doing a ton of research, I found that Roto Rooter is in the habit of making outrageous estimates that DON'T address the primary problem. I had a second opinion and my problem is FIXED and did not require any excavation or ANYWHERE near $3800 of repair. Also, the concrete is intact AND he replaced the concrete that they promised they would do. 

DO NOT PROCEED WITH ROTO ROOTER WITHOUT A SECOND OPINION. I WAS VERY NAIVE AND TRUSTED THEM BECAUSE THEY ARE A BIG COMPANY. BUT THEY DO THIS ALL THE TIME.

reviews.author: MaRisa J.
reviews.text:
I had a very slow draining toilet. The guy charged me $300 to snake and then another $300 to remove the toilet. He ended up having to dig out the pipe in the back and found out that it had a huge hole in it. He said it would be an additional $1600-1700 to replace the pipe depending on the parts price (it was max 3-4 feet of pipe). I recently had french drains put in and he suggested that the guys who installed the french drains put the hole there. But it was pretty clear that it was just an old pipe that was rotting away.

I had a contractor come out and look at it, he fixed it for $300 (vs 1600!!!).
