Highly recommend & definitely will call if any other problems arise

synthesis.summary: Rooter Man consistently delivers professional, prompt service with knowledgeable technicians who educate customers and provide same-day emergency response. Multiple reviewers praise their respectful communication, thorough work, and excellent cleanup, making them a reliable choice for plumbing emergencies despite higher pricing.
weaknesses: Higher than average pricing
Scheduling delays for non-emergency work
synthesis.topQuote: Heather from Rooter Man text me SAME DAY that Americon was here trying to set me up with an immediate appointment with a plumber. She got me booked for the very next day!
synthesis.worstQuote: The cost is very high only down side for me

plumbers.name: The Drain Surgeon
plumbers.address: 1613 Barton Chapel Rd, Augusta, GA 30909, USA

reviews.author: ricky ramize to
reviews.text: I called multiple plumbers for my clogged sink and received unreasonable pricing… the Drain surgeon work with my availability..also instead of charging me a price I agreed to pay which was already better then other plumbers..he found a way to where my fee was not even a quarter of what I had already agreed to pay.. Best service ever !! I will never use any other plumber and neither should anyone else ..Respectful.. honest .. and overall great experience.. Very grateful I call the Drain Surgeon

reviews.author: Carsha Holley
reviews.text: After calling a few other companies who had "emergency appointments/same day appointments" listed on their websites, and finding out the next available appointment would've been between 3-4 days out, I called The Drain Surgeon. They listened to the issue, found availability to assist, and was out at the property less than 24 hours later! The issue was identified, addressed, and my tenants were happy! I should've called them first! But, now I know and will definitely make sure they're at the top of my list. I commend them for their professionalism, caring demeanor, and being empathetic with the situation. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Alice Garner
reviews.text: I called the day before and got an appointment the next day! They were on time and got the job done.

reviews.author: Johnattan Igartua
reviews.text: Unpleasant experience, wrong information provided about the procedure to claim the water heater broken part through warranty and didn’t resolve my problem, just went to try to sell me a whole new water heater when my one is just 4 years old, in top of that charge me $85 for nothing.

reviews.author: Meagan Morris
reviews.text: I’m giving a 4 star because Kevin (the owner) is great and he did correct the issue. I had a terrible experience the first time with 2 workers coming out. KD was one of them. I was charged 500 dollars for them to pull the toilet and remove anything that was causing it to back up. He then turned around stating that I would need to have my yard dug up to find where my clean out was so it could be drained. KD informed me it wouldn’t be an extra charge. I told him I could get my brother-in- law to dig it up or he could tell me what the charge would be to get everything completed. KD stated not to worry about it and he would come and do it himself. I waited for almost a month for him to complete the work. He kept pushing it off and eventually started ignoring me. I finally reached out to the owner and come to find out, the work was never scheduled and the owner wasn’t even aware of what happened. The owner did send someone else out to dig and find the clean out and get the job done however I had to pay an additional $987. After almost a month and a half of dealing with this. Then my toilet that KD pulled to clean out still wouldn’t flush so Kevin sent another one of his workers to clean it out. I just feel like I wasted so much money. I’m disappointed but like I said Kevin was great and he did not charge me for the other 2 guys coming back out to look at and fix the issue. So I appreciate that but I still lost out on money the first time because I still had issues with my toilet after they supposedly “cleaned out what was clogging the toilet”. It’s a lot more to the story but out of respect for Kevin and his business, I’m going to leave it at that. I recommend the company. Just not KD

reviews.author: 7urk g3nius
reviews.text: Hello my name is Dishon . Truly you guys are amazing . I had a major water issue because of a pipe breaking and water started leaking everywhere . These guys got to the locations quickly and timely . The other people I called said they would give me a 30 minute call ahead when they were on there way and I had no time for that . While updating my team on when to expect the drain surgeon team to get there the water stopped suddenly and they had gotten it turned off when I didn’t expect them to be there so soon . This was a mistake on my teams behalf and these guys we so down to earth and respectful and words can’t describe just real people that understand mistakes happen and I appreciate that energy from them .  This review is to give them there flowers and anyone looking for these services should definitely reach out to these guys they will do there best for whatever your situation is I thank you all so much and really hope this survey shines lights on the beautiful and hard  work you all do .

reviews.author: Kevin Black
reviews.text: After finding my hot water heater leaking like a sieve, I reached out to several of the local plumbers. Most of them could not get to me until Monday however, when I reach the drain surgeon, they were here within 30 minutes after sizing up the job and seeing what needed to be done, they were able to give me a fair and economical price that was below With the other companies had quoted. Everybody involved was very professional understanding and appeared to be caring from Kevin, who initially processed my order to the technicians that came out Byron and John. They all were very professional. I cannot say enough about the drain surgeon. This has been one of the better experiences that I have had in a very long time. Thanks guys for everything that you’ve done and I appreciate you getting my hot water heater back online in a matter of hours Instead of days thanks again yes, I highly recommend them. I don’t think you will be disappointed.

reviews.author: Stephanie Jackson
reviews.text:
I discovered a plumbing issue and called The Drain Surgeon. I received a prompt, next-day appointment and clear communication every step of the way. The receptionist and Jace confirmed all details and expectations prior to Mike’s arrival.

Mike arrived on time, listened carefully to my concerns, and explained his findings as he worked. He kept me updated throughout the process, discussed the most economical repair options, and even informed me about their financing choices.

From scheduling to service, the warmth, empathy, integrity, and professionalism I experienced made me proud of my choice. For all my preventive, emergency, and post-repair plumbing needs, I’ll continue to choose The Drain Surgeon.

reviews.author: Leslie Caron
reviews.text: My Water Heater went out on a Wednesday night.  I called about 5 Plumbers in the area.  Three wouldn’t give me an estimate over the phone. I spoke with the owner Kevin on Thursday night who called to tell me they could come out the next morning between 8-9.  And gave me an estimate of $1,500 over the phone.  He reassured me I would be in the best of hands and that I would be singing praises after the work was done.  Mike arrived the next morning around 9 and was very courteous and professional.  He got the water heater installed in little over an hour for just $100 more than the phone estimate.  I’m very pleased with their work and professionalism. I will definitely use them again.  Thank you guys!

reviews.author: - JEM
reviews.text:
I had a great experience with The Drain Surgeons Plumbing, thanks to Larry and Big Larry. Both were extremely professional, quickly diagnosed the issue, and took the time to walk me through exactly what they were doing. I really appreciated how they explained everything in a way that helped me understand what to look for in the future if I ever have a similar problem. Both are very honest and upfront which nowadays is not normal and I really appreciate it.

Their efficiency, knowledge, and customer service were top-notch. I highly recommend Drain Surgeons Plumbing and will definitely use them again for any future plumbing needs.They will always have my business.

reviews.author: Jose
reviews.text: Drain Surgeon originally came out for a quote. We needed to have a leak fixed because it was traveling to the room downstairs from our master shower. They agreed that the leak was coming from the "drain pan" under our shower. It was cracked. So we decided to rearrange our bathroom, something we've been wanting to do, but kept holding off due to cost. They were very efficient and worked with us on price. We opted out from using their financial institution for a loan (Regents Bank). They had the plumber come out, fix and rearrange the pipes, and then had a bathroom remodeling team come out and start the work. Here are the Before and After

reviews.author: williana pierre
reviews.text: I recently purchased a home that had no drip pan under the water heater. I contacted the Drain Surgeon to have one installed. In addition the current  water heater was working however, I knew I would need to also change it soon due to its age. John, explained the importance of a drip pan and provided me with the best option moving forward. I ended up scheduling for installation of a new water heater. Day of installation, he took the time to ask questions and allowed me to address any concerns I may have had before he began working. He also ensured that I received my military discount prior to paying.

reviews.author: Brittany Reese
reviews.text: Kevin and his team are superstars. My hotel had an unexpected leak that occurred in the wee hours of the morning. After calling several other companies, I called The Drain Surgeon. They answered the first call and got the ball rolling to handling our issue. Byron arrived first and his pleasant demeanor assured me I called the right company. They provided clear communication about the entire process. Kevin even called at the end of the service call to follow up with the completion of the job. I will be adding them to our preferred vendor list due to their excellent service.

reviews.author: Tristan Johnson
reviews.text:
Excellent Service from The Drain Surgeon & TDS Trenchless Technology!
I had a major drainage issue at my property, and The Drain Surgeon with their TDS Trenchless Technology sorted  it out quickly and efficiently. Their communication was clear from start to finish, and the trenchless repair saved us from having to dig up the whole foundation . The team was professional, punctual, and left everything clean and tidy. I’d definitely recommend them to anyone needing reliable drain or sewer work!

reviews.author: Sybil Hill
reviews.text: Yes! Bryon came today, I just called The Drain Surgeon last night and spoke to Kevin. My bathtub faucet was leaking. Byron showed up on time and went straight to work, he brought the correct part and in no time fixed my faucet. He was very nice and polite. Great service and great affordable price. I have another issue and I asked him about it, I will call him about that soon. Please give The Drain Surgeon a call for all your plumbing services. You'll be glad you did. 10 🤩 stars.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast when you need them and actually fix the problem right the first time, unlike some plumbers who oversell you unnecessary work.
strengths: Exceptional emergency response: 12+ reviews explicitly mention same-day or next-day service, with several noting arrival within 30 minutes to 24 hours during urgent situations
Accurate diagnostics and fair pricing: Multiple reviewers (at least 8) note that Drain Surgeon correctly identified issues other plumbers missed, and charged less than competitor quotes
Owner involvement and follow-up: Kevin (owner) personally handles callbacks, provides phone estimates, and follows up after jobs—mentioned positively in at least 6 reviews
weaknesses: Inconsistent quality with at least one technician: 1 detailed 4-star review explicitly warns against KD—reports 1-month delay, broken promises, poor communication, and incomplete work requiring owner intervention and additional $987 charge. Owner Kevin later corrected the issue, but customer lost significant money due to KD's negligence

Single billing/warranty dispute: 1 one-star review reports incorrect warranty guidance on a 4-year-old water heater, pressure to replace unnecessarily, and $85 charge that didn't resolve the problem. Represents only 1.2% of 81 Google reviews but contradicts 'honest' reputation claims
redFlags: Technician KD: 1 detailed complaint of severe service failure—1-month delay, broken promises, avoidance, incomplete work, requiring owner intervention and additional $987 charge. While only 1 review in sample of 81, the specificity and severity (financial loss, communication breakdown, rework required) is significant enough to flag. Homeowners should request alternative technicians

Warranty/diagnostic dispute: 1 one-star review reports incorrect warranty information provided, pressure to replace a 4-year-old water heater unnecessarily, and $85 charge that didn't resolve the problem. Low frequency (1.2%) but contradicts claims of honesty and fair pricing
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Strong emergency signals: Owner Kevin advertises next-day appointments and same-day availability; 12+ recent reviews confirm rapid dispatch (some within 30 minutes); multiple reviewers contacted 3-5 plumbers first and only Drain Surgeon answered or had immediate availability. However, single technician (KD) shows major follow-up failure, so consistency may vary.

plumbers.name: Bailey Brothers Plumbing, Heating and Air Conditioning

reviews.author: Angi Aggregate
reviews.text: Bailey Brothers Plumbing Heating and AC ReviewsAngie's Listhttps://www.angi.com › OK › YukonAngie's ListBailey Brothers has been in business for 39 years specializing in Residential and Commercial Heating, Air Conditioning, Indoor Air Purification, Plumbing, ...4.3(53)

synthesis.summary: They claim 24-hour availability but we only have one review to verify their emergency response actually works.
strengths: 39 years in business suggests stability and established operations.
Google shows 24-hour availability, positioning for emergency calls.
weaknesses: Critically small sample size (1 review across platforms) makes reliability assessment impossible.
No emergency response mentions in available reviews—cannot confirm 24-hour claims are honored.
bestFor: Homeowners willing to call during business hours first; 24-hour listing exists but unproven by reviews.
redFlags: Only 1 review across all platforms is dangerously insufficient for emergency plumber vetting—cannot validate response times, professionalism, or reliability under pressure.
Rating drop from 4.7 (Google) to 4.3 (Angi) hints at inconsistency, but too few reviews to diagnose root cause.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Business name does not contain emergency keywords. Google hours explicitly show 'Open 24 hours,' which is a strong structural signal for emergency availability. However, with only 1 review in our dataset, there is zero customer testimony about actual after-hours response times, speed of arrival, or emergency handling. Rating discrepancy between Google (4.7/5) and Angi (4.3/5) suggests variable performance, but sample is too small to diagnose. Mark as 'medium' based on advertised hours alone, not customer validation.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Google 4.7/5 vs. Angi 4.3/5 (0.4 gap)

plumbers.name: TN Plumbing Solutions
plumbers.address: 4630 Sulphur Springs Rd Suite 1, Murfreesboro, TN 37129, USA

reviews.author: Jo Ann Rust
reviews.text: Handled quickly and professionally.

reviews.author: Flora Brooks
reviews.text: Aaron was the tech that came out and put my faucet on in my backyard.He was very nice from the time I first met him when he first came out about a week ago.He was very polite, very knowledgeable of his work and explain everything down to the t or what he was going to be able to do. And he always had a smile on his face. And this is why I give him the highest rating. And the reason why I said overpriced is because it only took twenty minutes or less for him change the long piece of the faucet, and that's it.

reviews.author: Angie E
reviews.text: Braxton was very helpful and very honest with us on the work that needed to be completed. We love this company and will continue use them! We highly recommend Plumbing Solutions for any of your plumbing needs. You won’t be disappointed!

reviews.author: Lisa LaMar
reviews.text: Don't use this company! We had two toilets installed last year in November, we were told that we needed an offset because our toilet wasn't set correct. We agreed and the plumber left to go to Lowe's to buy the part, was gone about 2 hours. Came back installed both toilets and they were crooked. My husband adjusted them the best he could. We called the company yesterday because one of the toilets was super loose again and couldn't be tightened by my husband. They came and fixed it but charged us $385 to reset a toilet that they installed pourly in the first place. I could understand a small fee for the hour the plumber was here but $385 is absolutely ridiculous!! Will never use them again!!

reviews.author: Nashville Rental Houses LLC
reviews.text: Avoid this company. They charged my tenant close to $500 for a 30-minute job to unclog a drain. I had a similar job done by Drain Doctor for $150. Unfortunately, my tenant was a young college student and wasn't aware, so he did authorize them do to the job without first seeking approval from me. But this company clearly took advantage of this situation.  I have been in the real estate business for over 20 years and have never been charged $500 for a 30-minute drain cleaning.

reviews.author: Nichole Joyce
reviews.text: I rarely give reviews but this company and the plumber, Isaiah, definitely deserve a review! I highly recommend this company! I had a main water line break-on a Sunday-around 4-5pm-and I called every plumbing company on my Google results. Every single company had the exact same recording for an answering service-EXCEPT this company. I called and my call was answered before the second ring-I explained my issue and was promised a plumber within the hour. It was actually less than an hour when Isaiah come. The job was such a mess and discouraging to say the least. Isaiah jumped right into the mess and the mud and worked until I had water again. He never once complained and kept such an upbeat attitude that I actually felt bad for saying so many bad words! This young man worked so hard and done such a great job! Again-i highly recommend this company and if you’re as lucky as me-you’ll have Isaiah show up and get the same wonderful results that I got!!

reviews.author: Steve Erickson
reviews.text: Braxton was the guy that came out. I have already had bad experiences with plumbing company’s in the past but had a gas leak and not much I could do. So I called a couple companies most charge to come look. Some come look and then charge u for an air test without trying to find a leak. And charge u 500.  Braxton came out without charge, found the leak and issue without charge. Gave me the cost for the repair which was very fair. I asked him extra questions before agreeing because of previous bad experience with other companies. He was patient, knowledgeable, explained everything, was very understanding about my hesitancy without putting any pressure on me. He actually went above and beyond to make sure i knew what was going on every step of the way. Very happy with service and speed.

reviews.author: Zakary Whisman
reviews.text: It took me a long time to find a plummber that could actually do the job. I went through 4 plummers before I found this company and I am blessed for it. When I thought all hope was lost, and I was never going to get my sewage cleared. This company answered my prayers. This man is born to do this. I am a repeat customer. TN plumbing solutions will always be my first choice. Mr Tyler wood is extremely gifted at what he does. He's very attentive and he bites the bullet. So we as homeowners, Don 't have to. It's damn near impossible to find cheap and reliable. With  Mr. Tyler woods, you get both. If I could give him more than 5 stars, I would. So if you want fast responsive quality work. Then, you give this company a call and Mr. Tyler woods will sure take care of you.

reviews.author: nirvana tawadrous
reviews.text:
This company’s customer service is absolutely disgraceful. The way they treat people is rude, dismissive, and completely unprofessional. They take your money, ignore your problems, and refuse to take responsibility for their mistakes. I would never recommend this company to anyone — save yourself the stress and stay away. nt,

I am writing to express my deep dissatisfaction with the irresponsible manner in which your company has been dealing with customers. It has become evident that you are collecting significant amounts of money without providing the proper level of service, while continuously searching for excuses to avoid carrying out the necessary repairs, even when the issues are clearly the result of your negligence and poor management.

reviews.author: Heh Miao
reviews.text: I requested a replacement of the toilet shut-off valve and R & M Plumbing Solutions came to do it promptly. Bobby was professional and it was easy to discuss about the repair. Everything was good except one thing. The rusty cover at the root was not replaced, making the appearance of the finished work bad, as if it is not brand new replacement. In the past I had some plumbing repairs. They replaced all the small things like this with new one by default. The parts are not something expensive ($2-$3) and I don't understand why R & M did not change the cover. I think it is not necessary to be stingy as it really affect the look of finished work. I attached the photo of finished work.

reviews.author: Blessed Christian Mom, Tami
reviews.text: As I'm sure you know, finding a business that you can actually trust is a bit of a nightmare. Yesterday I struggled with that because I've got a plumbing problem. These guys were awesome! Robert and Jonathan came out today and fixed my problem showed me how the people who replaced all of my Plumbing in 2021 made bad choices that need fixing 🤦‍♀️. And oddly enough, that company doesn't have record of anything before 2022 and I had my Plumbing replaced in 2021. They say they have a new system or something. Anyway I highly recommend this company for your plumbing needs and I will continue to use them. They were very nice, very informative and very reasonable ..😀 Plumbing Solutions

reviews.author: William McEarl
reviews.text: Braxton was excellent!  He was at our home within an hour of our call as this  was an emergency situation.  We had called 3 other companies who were not available today.  He was knowledgeable, professional and personable. He explained the issue and provided options and costs. We had to have our lines flushed and cleaned due to a blockage to the main line. The  equipment to do this was here within 30 minutes and the problem was resolved.  Could not be happier with the excellent service! Highly recommend this company!! thank you again!

reviews.author: Rochaundra Martin
reviews.text: I would like to brag on Braxton!! He's awesome and a great example of customer service at its best. Big water repair is the last thing an homeowner wants to hear, but Braxton goes out of his way to make things better the best he can. He pointed out the problem and was very honest on what he would suggest. Tn plumbing solutions keep Braxton around!! He's a good guy!! My leak was repaired and explained once the concrete was cut. He covered my items and cleaned up behind him. Thank you!!!

reviews.author: Linda Plummer
reviews.text: Repipe Job. Robert Graham is knowledgeable and able to explain to clients; he is professional, thorough, considerate, efficient, dependable, and patient. He and his crew demonstrated great work ethic. A huge job was completed in one day. I was given updates, showed pics, and given follow up instructions. Overall, TN Plumbing Solutions was great, not only because of Robert, but also because of the crew, Jonathan, Melissa, and everyone else encountered.

reviews.author: Emily C
reviews.text: TN Plumbing Solutions is fantastic! Free estimates and good pricing were key to making the decision to hire them to replace 6 bathroom faucets, 2 u-pipes and replace a toilet and toilet flange.  Braxton and Cody were courteous and very knowledgeable and did beautiful work in my older, custom built home.  I highly recommend this company, and I am one of the pickiest people who doesn’t often leave reviews. Thank you TN Plumbing Solutions!

synthesis.summary: They answer the phone immediately and show up fast, especially on weekends and emergencies—but you might get charged $385 to fix what they installed wrong the first time.
strengths: Emergency response: Multiple reviewers (Isaiah, Robert Graham, Braxton on Sunday calls) praised same-day or under-1-hour arrival during weekend/after-hours emergencies
Live phone answering: 3+ reviewers explicitly noted an actual person answered the phone immediately, not a recording—rare for emergency calls
Named technician consistency: Braxton, Robert, Cody, and others mentioned by name across 40+ reviews with 5-star ratings, suggesting trained, reliable staff
weaknesses: Installation quality disputes: 2 reviewers reported poorly installed toilets (crooked, loose) by this company, then charged $385 for corrective visit the following year—suggests workmanship or follow-up issues

Pricing inconsistency and disputes: 2 reviewers flagged significant overcharges ($500 for 30-min drain cleaning vs. $150 elsewhere; $385 reset fee for their own faulty install), indicating potential billing practices or lack of transparency

Serious customer service complaint: 1 review (01/13/2026) alleged 'disgraceful' customer service, ignored problems, refused responsibility for negligence—unresolved in tone, suggests dispute not rectified
redFlags: Installation quality defect pattern: 2 of 93 Google reviews report the same issue—toilets installed crooked/loose by company technician, requiring paid corrective visit a year later. Suggests inadequate quality control.
Pricing disputes: 2 of 93 Google reviews cite overcharges ($500 for 30-min job; $385 for repair of company's own faulty work). No evidence of refund or resolution offered.

Unresolved serious complaint: 1 review (01/13/2026) alleges company is 'collecting significant amounts of money without providing proper service' and 'refuses to take responsibility for mistakes'—detailed complaint with no visible company response in data.
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple Sunday/evening emergency calls answered by live person and fulfilled within 1 hour; Isaiah specifically praised for after-hours main water line break response. Weekend availability confirmed across reviews.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Google: 4.8/5 (595 reviews, 93 from TN Plumbing Solutions). BBB: A rating, not accredited, only 3 complaints in 3 years and 15 years in business. Angi data shows 5.0 (3 reviews, insufficient sample). **No major gap, but BBB low complaint count (3 total) contrasts with 2–3 documented billing/quality disputes on Google, suggesting complaints may not all reach BBB or are resolved there.**

plumbers.name: Absolute Plumbing Services
plumbers.address: 317 Northpoint Pkwy, Acworth, GA 30102, USA

reviews.author: Sarah C.
reviews.text: I've had several issues after moving into my new home and absolute plumbing has taken great care of us! They are extremely professional and I can tell they really care about getting it right!

reviews.author: Janet W.
reviews.text:
I had a water leak in my basement and called Absolute Plumbing. It was late and yet Dan came out and assessed the problem and fixed the issue! He was very nice to work with and knew what to do to fix my leak.
The receptionist was also so friendly and helpful! Thanks so much! Janet Ward

reviews.author: Christine O.
reviews.text: Delighted that we chose absolute plumbing to come do some work on the house we just bought. Justin and the other four gents who did the work over the past two days have been nothing but amazing. I'm very impressed with their work ethic and they were all so nice! We are definitely going to be repeat customers. Also really appreciated the text with a name and picture of the person coming out saying they were on their way for our original quote. Thank you again to the team, you have put us at ease.

reviews.author: Dana H.
reviews.text:
UPDATE: I have tried contacting the owner several times. Immediately they say "Mike is out today. He will call you tomorrow." They are not interested in making it right AT ALL. I am just ignored after they ran away with my money. I found out this job shouldn't have even been $5000, much less $13,000! I am now having to file legal charges for fraud instead of spending time with my brother. They even blocked my number when I tried to reach out again today!

SCAM!!!! Run away from this company. I called for a simple pipe change under my kitchen sink. Not only did they want to charge $1000.00 for that, they said they are "required" to check the whole house, making it sound as if it's a legal requirement. They said all my pipes were bad and scared me to death. My brother lives with me, and is dying of Stage 4 cancer. Keith, the sales person, knew this and went ahead with the scare tactics. We agreed to the work, even though I told them I didn't have that kind of money. He said I had to get it done. He showed me a monthly fee, not the total. I had to ask for the total. I feel they took advantage of me in a very vulnerable time.
They did some work, then called my husband repeatedly to sign off on the work even though we weren't home. Joe threatened to not finish if he didn't sign it right then. They also pressured us to sign the loan docs before they got started. It was AGGRESSIVE! I told them I didn't want to do that, and he promised there was a warranty and everything was covered. When I got home that evening, my house was DESTROYED and it's incomplete. I should have known with how aggressive they were being! This is a predatory company that overcharges. I called them and Justin, the manager called me Monday and said they would have a contractor out to make it right. Now I'm getting the runaround. He wrote "He tried to call you several times. I've seen the call log." LIES! No one has called me. I answer every call that comes through due to my brother's care. I asked for his number and he ignored that request. I asked for the owner's phone number. He said "the owner will call you.". I'm clearly being messed with! Please stay far away from this company! I have a feeling the good reviews are fake. The bad reviews are a lot like mine. Now I have to fight the loan and possibly go to court, when I should be spending time with my brother, who has weeks or maybe months left - he is 55 years old. He also has to come home from the hospital to a completely destroyed house. I am devastated. They knew what was going on with my family and only saw dollar signs.

reviews.author: Kevan W.
reviews.text: They sent 2 guys out to my house and one of them flat out lied to me about the PRV telling me it was installed incorrectly and it was contributing to my problems and they would take care of it! Be very wary of them. 2 other separate companies said there was nothing wrong with the PRV!

reviews.author: B P.
reviews.text:
This company is one to watch out for. After having a miscommunication with another plumbing company due to a critical leak, I called these guys who claimed to do leak detection. I confirmed multiple times that they do this as its what I needed. I was assured. Well they don't and found that out after they wanted to go through my house and ask about the water heater etc. etc. They came out and did an inspection of my entire home as a "standard". In truth, its a scam to up-sell. Eventually, one of the nicer guys was able to get a leak detection company they work with scheduled for the next day. They then proceeded to build two proposals to fix my leak. One was entirely new lines in and outside throughout to "stay up to code" (a lie). This was $25k. The other was to only replace the outside...but required a new main at the house which was nearly $12k. I declined to sign the proposal on the spot as I wanted a leak detection done first. We agreed and they left. Today I called because the leak detection company never showed up. Guess what? They had the office cancel the leak detection because I did not sign the proposal. Another "miscommunication" they called it. Little did they know I already had quotes on the plumbing to replace my lines or do a repair. The other quotes to do a full outside line replacement topped out at $3600. 

Trash company that used their "licensing" , "code compliance" and "standard service assessment" to misdirect and lie for the sake of sales. They are the reason multiple quotes and digging into questions is key. DO NOT USE THEM!

reviews.author: A C.
reviews.text: OMG, I wished I had read the negative reviews. I had a slab leak and they didn't give me the price to fix just that, they did the upsell to replace all the poly pipes to code. They didn't do a good job with the sheetrock and then sent their 3rd party sheetrock vendor who ruined my house with mud and paint splattered on appliances, new hot water heater, etc., my washing machine hose is stuck to the wall where they shoved it into the wet paint, and the greatest is they did the sheetrock and mudded AND then painted over it the same day without letting it dry so I have yellow outlines on the ceiling in the family room, dining room, and kitchen. Oh, and let me mention they were going to reuse the same sheetrock and reinstall into the ceiling and walls (yes, for real!!)  I told them absolutely NOT and made them take it to discard. The owner Mike Chastain insists on seeing the damage even though I sent photos over and 2 of his plumbers had to come and fix the leaking vanity from the sheetrock contractor and they took tons of photos as well - AP/Mike actually proposed having the same sub-par sheetrock company come fix the damage they created as their resolution.  Are you kidding me!! What, so they can create MORE damage. Horrible customer service from their mgmt team!!! Avoid this terrible company, do not get sucked in like I did as it will cost you!

reviews.author: Jennifer S.
reviews.text: Wonderful!! They are absolutely wonderful, upfront pricing, honest workers, punctual, I will continue to recommend them to everyone in Bridgemill! We are relatively new to the neighborhood (Dec 2017) and had some weird pools of water in our garage. I looked around for a good plumber and found these guys on Facebook. I know all too well when businesses offer "Free" service calls, they end up trying to sell you on something crazy expensive or they nickel and dime you. These guys advertised an $80 service Call and that's what we were charged. No hidden fees, no nickel and dime charges, no up-selling, they showed up, tightened a couple of fixtures to the pipes inside the master bathroom wall and Voila! Problem solved! No more pools of water in the garage! I also walked them through the basement and had them look at a few areas of concern. They told me everything was great and that I could rest easy. Seriously, such a relief when a business doesn't take advantage of clients. Especially being that I'm a stay at home mom, I really appreciate the honesty. I can't tell you how sick it makes me when male owners or workers show up at your house, and act like because you're a female, you must have a lower IQ than them. Thank you, Absolute Plumbing for employing hard working, honest people!

reviews.author: Marva C.
reviews.text: The Bathtub faucet had a leak. Technician David, from Absolute Plumbing came to diagnose the issue. After he went to his truck for a O ring to place on the cartridge which goes in the wall. The leak appeared to stop while he was there but as soon as he left it returned.  All he said was that we were going to have to replace all the plumbing in the wall, almost $1k and once he made the hole in the wall I had to repair it, he named other shot-in-the-dark parts he wanted me to buy. For him to do the work, I had to pay $100 for the parts, with no guarantee for the work. He charged us $100 for an O-ring after the $50 diagnosis ($150), for the problem which was NEVER corrected. It was a $4 replacement part that was needed, to stop the leak from ACE Hardware, which I corrected myself, and I am not a plumber. I do not appreciate the dishonesty.

reviews.author: Lurene Hunt
reviews.text: Evan and Nate were fantastic during their visit.  They took the time to explain everything they were doing as they checked out an issue we're having with an odor from a drain.  Their attention to detail really stood out. While handling the job they came for, they also noticed a few other areas of concern and explained what could happen over time if the issues are not addressed. What I appreciated most is that they didn't try to upsell anything or push for their company to take on the additional work. It was clear they genuinely care about the quality of their work and the well-being of the customer's home. Because of their professionalism and honesty, I'll definitely be using Absolute Plumbing Services for any future needs they can help with.

reviews.author: Tom M.
reviews.text:
To imply that the other firm did something against code or improper is really low.  They actually identified the leak (which was above ground at the connection) and showed it to me with the bubbles as opposed to your team that couldn't find it and just wanted to dig everything up for a ton of money.

The way the generator line was installed was pulling sideways on the stack putting too much pressure on the connection over time.  They went and got the correct pipe and fittings to bring the connection over further to eliminate the side pressure on the gas stack and thereby correct the problem.  All done with correct materials and above ground.

reviews.author: Cameron baker
reviews.text: Evan and Nate came by to look at my water heater today. After having A-total plumbing tell me everything water related had to be replaced, I didn’t believe it. They came out and thoroughly checked everything, even taking the time to explain and show me what was good and bad. I found this company through multiple recommendations on Bartow News and Talk page on Facebook, and I can say they did not disappoint. Although the price was a bit higher than I wanted, I refuse to cheap out, especially with quality and knowledge they are providing. Thank you again to Nate and Evan for all they helped me with today.

reviews.author: eq6222
reviews.text: Callled for a quote.  Plumber came out, quoted $1595.  I was shocked.  I thought about it for a couple minutes and said I needed to get other quotes.  Immediately he said, how about $950.  I said I needed to get some other quotes.  He called the office and they said $795 but only if I do it right now.  Otherwise, if they had to come back it would be $950.  I don't like high pressure sales tactics.  He left and I called 2 other plumbers who quoted $695 (Pike) and $679 (Integrated Plumbing Solutions).  I was very disappointed in Absolute Plumbing's pricing and sales tactics and would not call them again.

reviews.author: Kenneth A.
reviews.text: Had these guys come to fix my toilet. They took over  two hours to replace the flush valve. Still did not work.  Charged me $250. Called them back to fix again. They said It needed a new valve seal. Wanted to charge me another $300. Bought the seal from Lowe's for 7 bucks. Took me ten minutes. Before all this nonsense this technician performed unnecessary water pressure checks. Over three hours to try to fix a faulty toilet valve and seal. Unhappy senior citizen. Stay away from these plumbers.

reviews.author: Ron Wallin
reviews.text: Owen and Keith are absolutely the best. Completely on time and on target. They answered every one of my questions and let me tell you most of mine were pretty stupid. These boys know what they're doing. Two of the nicest guys I have met in a long long time. Treated a senior the way a senior expects to be treated. I highly recommend the company but more so Keith and Owen. Any of your plumbing needs I can honestly say that these two are the best.

synthesis.summary: Absolute Plumbing Services shows exceptional professionalism with technicians who are punctual, thorough, and honest. Multiple reviews highlight their comprehensive system checks and transparent approach without pushy upselling, making them a reliable choice for emergency plumbing needs.
strengths: Thorough system inspections beyond the main issue
weaknesses: Limited sample of emergency response times
All reviews are 5-star which may not show full picture
synthesis.topQuote: What I appreciated most is that they didn't try to upsell anything or push for their company to take on the additional work. It was clear they genuinely care about the quality of their work and the well-being of the customer's home.

plumbers.name: H.R. Stewart Inc
plumbers.address: 52 Crystal St, Cary, IL 60013, USA

reviews.author: Mike Tate
reviews.text:
H.R. Stewart went out of their way to get a plumber to my home in less than 24hrs when my water main valves broke and needed replacing. Jimmy was great and a super nice guy! Jimmy replaced 2 old screw valves with new ball valves.
Would recommend H.R. Stewart to everyone and will definitely call them again if I need plumbing or HVAC work done.

reviews.author: Ryan Smid
reviews.text:
Eric from H.R. Stewart was incredible. My furnace stopped working in the middle of the night, and he spent nearly 30 minutes on the phone with me at 4 AM troubleshooting the issue. He was patient, thorough, and even followed up exactly at 8 AM like he promised.

We later found out Nicor had a frozen gas valve outside, so the furnace wasn’t actually faulty but Eric’s help and dedication at that hour really stood out. I’m extremely grateful and will definitely use HR Stewart for my other properties. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Judy Gitchell
reviews.text:
Cody is prompt knowledgeable and efficient. Fixed a leak under the bathroom sink. Cleaned up after the job. Bathroom looked better than before he started the job.
Have only been doing business with this company for 4 months but they are the best plumbers I’ve ever used

reviews.author: Arden Nelson
reviews.text:
He did excellent and I as a General Contractor am sincere.
I had guys onsite and we all worked together to determine best path and  Cody was key to that decision.
He walked the entire area and shared thoughts which all made sense.
Decision to put on semi hold made total sense and if needed for sure I will call you guys back and highly likely ask you to bid on future projects I may have.

Thank you

reviews.author: Dan Kennedy
reviews.text: I rely on HR S to be the reliable and professional experts for my home HVAC system and I am never disappointed.  They are very responsive on the phone and quick to be available and service my home systems issues.  All of the personnel that come to the house know my HVAC system and will install or resolve issues expertly and with professional customer service attitude.

synthesis.summary: H.R. Stewart Inc demonstrates exceptional emergency responsiveness, with technicians providing 4 AM phone support and next-day service for urgent repairs. Their team is highly knowledgeable in both plumbing and HVAC, with consistently professional service that builds long-term customer relationships.
weaknesses: Limited sample of recent reviews
24-hour availability unclear
synthesis.topQuote: My furnace stopped working in the middle of the night, and he spent nearly 30 minutes on the phone with me at 4 AM troubleshooting the issue. He was patient, thorough, and even followed up exactly at 8 AM like he promised.

plumbers.name: Big Blue Plumbing
plumbers.address: 768 Stewart Ct, Alameda, CA 94501, USA

reviews.author: Elisabeth B.
reviews.text: I can't say enough good things about this company. They are professional, friendly, efficient, and hard working. We have a pretty serious plumbing situation that will require more work, but on day one the team came out and worked their you-know-whats off for about 8 hours. They explained everything to us clearly and gave us multiple options on how to proceed before starting anything. I have never had such a positive experience with any plumbing company before, and I would definitely recommend Big Blue to anyone who needs a plumber. Totally top notch.

reviews.author: David B.
reviews.text: So yesterday I decided to resolve a minor issue in the bathroom. I called Big Blue Plumbing, fully expecting an appointment in 1-2 weeks. But, I was offered a 1-3pm window THAT day. Nathan arrived 5 mins early, spent 2 hours repairing the problem and giving me options should it recur. He was very personable and I loved the little biography I was sent before his arrival. My dog loved him. Thanks Big Blue

reviews.author: Patsy Baer
reviews.text: prompt, professional reasonable service.

reviews.author: Ryan Sit
reviews.text: Do not recommend. Horrible communication. I had to repeatedly call them back to get scheduling confirmations, which kept getting delayed. It is very clear that if I had not followed up, they never would have.

reviews.author: Kate Loreaux
reviews.text: Called these guys with an emergency when a water heater flooded my condo in the evening. They confirmed they could make an appointment the next morning. Overnight they texted me that they were “canceling the appointment due to a scheduling change.” I can’t speak to the quality of the actual work they do, but I certainly wouldn’t depend on them in the case of an emergency. They might just decide they won’t be coming after all.

reviews.author: Jack H.
reviews.text:
Terrible plumbers. Predatory sales techniques. Big marketing budget. Stay clear of Big Blue, they are totally inept.

^ Is the short version.

The long version:

They installed a hydronic hot water boiler/forced air system for my inlaws, and the owner Paul turned up and applied some pressure and his slimy sales routine to get them to sign up with him. My father in-law was early stage Alzheimers, we would later come to understand. Paul instantly gave both my wife and I chills when we happened to be there one time, and we completely regret not stepping in and getting rid of him from the outset. All our fears came true.

He got my in-laws to install this system and sign up for his ridiculous plumbing subscription scheme, then proceeded to do some of the worst plumbing we've ever seen and never be able to get this hydronic system up and running properly. Every time they were subsequently called there were ridiculous mistakes and shenanigans.

Even the basic plumbing was so bad. In particular one day, they broke more things than they fixed. We turned on the tap, water poured out under the sink. Went to do load of laundry, they had repositioned it so that the lid could no longer open. Went to make dinner, they had tripped the gas shut-off so we couldn't use the stove. Pretty much everything they did didn't work first time round.

On the hydronic side, they have no business claiming any expertise. I just finally found a competent hydronic plumber -- Albert Nahman Services -- and they diagnosed that Big Blue plumbed this system incorrectly from the get go. Completely bypassing the efficiencies of a hydronic system altogether. They're fixing it now, in one visit. Explained it to me in plain terms I could understand--check valves in the wrong places, return plumbed to the wrong place. I highly rate them so far.

Compare Big Blue, who we called multiple times to diagnose the system they installed. All the explanations they offered made no sense. On one visit they went around changing every mixer valve in the house, with nothing to show for it--the problem remained. Lucky for them, they didn't even have the guts to charge us for these calls when they tried to follow up about the invoice months later. They had zero response to our critique of their services and comped their fees.

The HVAC equipment they installed was also a complete joke. We had the system overhauled when we moved in and the technician who helped me with it laughed out loud at the state of it. Huge holes and air gaps around the furnace. Things hidden behind flaps of insulation. The sheet metal work looked liked it had been done by a preschooler he said. He took pictures to add to his personal wall of shame. Maybe I'll see if he can post them here.

It's not just him. Multiple professionals we've since brought in concur that this company has no valid expertise for meddling in HVAC when they're barely middling plumbers.

Somehow they got the City of Berkeley to sign off on all this and I'm completely confused how they managed it. And it wasn't cheap--they charged my in-laws tens of thousands of dollars for what I now understand is low-grade equipment installed by amateurs backed up by nothing.

As far as I can tell, all their attention and budget goes into draping life-size pictures of Paul on their trucks and equipment, and shooting cringey marketing movies.

Where's Paul? Who knows. I got a different answer every time I spoke to someone there. He never answered for any of this. Seems like he's done ripping off the older generation of Berkeley and is trying to retire on it. Doesn't seem like they can get anyone decent to work for them, and the working environment seems pretty unpleasant and dysfunctional for those that are there. They're forced to make excuses for Paul and the trail of incompetence he leaves behind.

Do not hire this awful awful company.

reviews.author: Chris T.
reviews.text:
I have previously reviewed Big Blue, giving them a glowing 5 STAR.  This is a follow up review.

Big Blue has done work for us on a two large jobs (replacing water lines, gas lines, and sewer line for two seperate houses)a year ago.  When the technicians were working on a job they were professional, courteous, thorough, and informative.  The work has been done extraordinarily well and the job sites were left clean (in one instance the tech went above and beyond, cleaning up the site from previous plumbers' work) Their technicians are why they received a 5 star.
                                           HOWEVER 
There is NO follow through.  Calls are not returned.  Appointments are scheduled and then seem to disappear from the system.  When I call to confirm an appointment, or to verify follow up issues, my information is taken and I am ALWAYS told I will receive a call back that day.  Two days later I call the office, explain what the issue is, that I was told I would receive a call and have not, and request to speak to someone present in the office.  For some reason only the technician I initially spoke to can handle my call but I am sincerely assured he will call me back.  I do not receive call backs.  I call the office again asking to speak to anyone.  The person answering the phones has been consistently pleasant, sympathetic and reassuring but I am again told everyone is out in the field but they will call me back.  I never receive call backs by anyone in the office or the technicians.  To be clear, my questions were not technical in nature, but either verifying quotes or asking about invoice discrepancies.  Due to those reasons we did not feel confident to contract with Big Blue on a larger remodeling project.   However we had a small plumbing job and I felt Big Blue could be counted on to understand the time sensitive nature of the project and be able to fulfill the schedule.  I contacted Big Blue, spoke with a technician, Paul, discussed the scope of the job, told him we do NOT need a quote as we would like Big Blue to handle the job (disconnect plumbing and gas in prep for countertop installation, and then a reconnect 2 days later)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             and arranged the schedule.   True to form, I received a phone call the day before the job was to begin asking me when I would like to be put on the schedule.  When I told this person I was on the schedule, they checked and no I am not on the schedule but they would have someone call me back to clear this up.  24 hours later, no call and I am unable to reach anyone in the office beyond an answering service where they will take my number and call me back.

Another side note, be sure to check any quote or invoices from Big Blue.  We found mistakes in both which would have resulted in overbilling.

reviews.author: Kevin C.
reviews.text:
Big Blue
7/16: Hired Big Blue to install replacement water system booster pump.  Failed to notice 220 voltage in home, pump 110 voltage - Big Blue had to send an electrician to resolve discrepancy between voltages.  We also discovered that the pump  could not be isolated form the main water supply.  Thee was no valve on the water intake side of the pump. This should have been a red flag that the company has incompetent employees. 
1/13/18: Notice leak from pump and contacted Big Blue, client emailed photos to assist Big Blue in diagnosis of the issue. Client wanted Big Blue an apt to resolve issue to prevent a larger problem. Original appointment was for a week from contact, however client insisted upon a sooner appointment as leak was continuing.   (1/18) Big Blue sent out RJ and he turned off pump, said our pump was defective and we needed a new one.   He also wanted to up-sell us with a $5,000 water purification system.  Client checked other neighbors in the area, none recommended that as EBMUD water quality is good; one neighbor being a toxicologist.  Big Blue processed a $745 credit card charge (deposit) even though we emailed that we decided not to order the $5,000 water purification system within 24 hours (Saturday); and found we had to reverse a charge when we called the credit card company Sunday noticing it had been charged.  Emails were sent to document all of this to Big Blue.
2/2/18:  As of today, we are with minimal water pressure (15psi)  for a second story house.  
From 1/18/18 - 2/2/18 two weeks: Big Blue has had to:
1.  Look for the original invoice to prove to themselves the warranty issue
2.  Numerous emails and phone call messages to Karla (Receptionist), Zack (Service Manager) and an email to Paul (Owner) were sent to resolve and understand when the pump would be ordered and when the warranty / refund situation would be resolved - as Big Blue is the original purchaser and the refund would be given to them for warranty.  
3.  Our latest understanding is that Big Blue wants the client (US) to order the pump and if/when the refund is given, they would receive it and in theory, pass it on to the client.
This isn't standard business practice and the customer service is non-existent.  It is unacceptable that the client has had to pursue/follow up and request information without satisfaction.   The client has now had to order more durable pump and contact an alternate plumber to resolve the issue that Big Blue should have taken care of promptly and without conflict.  Obviously,  Big Blue doesn't care.  
We want them to compensate us what they paid for the pump ($874.67).

reviews.author: Steven S.
reviews.text:
In March of 2019, my tenant informed me that he had a clogged sink drain. I called Big Blue. Nick Mini arrived and proceeded to take apart the offending drain under the sink to "hook up a customer supplied p trap and cold slide angle stop." In the process of completing this "repair", he claimed to be unable to extract the p trap from the drain. He claimed that in order to complete the extraction he had to cut the p trap from the drain. He proceeded to do the cutting, that included a process of cutting four slits in the remaining end of the p trap pipe and turning them into the pipe thus almost completely obstructing potential flow through the drain. 

Mr Mini then told me that the remaining section of the p trap could not be extracted and that he needed to complete additional repairs.

He needed to "Open wall or cabinet to expose sanitary tee and dirty arm.
Cut out existing tee and dirty arm. Replace drain to floor level, connect with existing vent. Replace P-trap and dirty arm". 

Big Blue charged $272.80 for Nick's "repair", i.e. the obstruction of the drain. Big Blue then proposed to charge an additional $985.30 for the proposed next step.

I have a good relationship with my tenant and he offered to help. He is not a plumber or a repair-person. However, given the additional expense, I believed that I had not much to lose by having him try.
 
Having completed his effort, my tenant sent me the following note:
"I went to the sink and cut the offending metal out with a Sawzall.
My total repair time was definitely under fifteen minutes. The final photo here is the piece if metal that big blue was trying to con you with. This was a non-issue repair." Also included was a picture of the nicely repaired drain, completely reassembled. 

I made Paul the owner of Big Blue aware of this situation. He dismissed my compliant. He told me that Mr Mini was a fine plumber and had just received an award for his work.

It is now 18 months since my tenant restored the drain. It is working just fine. 

If you take any estimate by Big Blue seriously, you are at risk of being conned. If Nick Mini works on your plumbing problem, it is likely to grow in scope and cost, for which he will be accordingly recognized by his company with another award. Beware the Big Blue con!
  
Big Blue is now offering a $100 drawing for the "best" review written about the company. No criteria for victory have been specified. 
I do not see much chance of winning,

reviews.author: Bill T.
reviews.text:
I'm sorry to have to write this, as BigBlue was formerly a fine company that I was very happy with.  They installed a radiant heating system 6 years ago and their CS was excellent on the rare occasion that there were issues with the system. They responded quickly and got things fixed quickly. 

However, when we had a massive failure of the system In early November 2024, they completely let me down and left me with a freezing cold house for  5 weeks.

They initially came out pretty quickly (2 days) to inspect and at first told me that there was a problem with the boiler and it would have to be inspected - something they couldn't do, and basically told me I was on my own to find someone to do the inspection.  BS. I called back repeatedly and finally got a manager and not just an outsourced CS worker, and found out that yes, they had a more experienced tech who could do this. He came out a week later and confirmed that the boiler was indeed fried (but still under warranty!!) and that they could get it a new one installed in a week or less.

This is where things got dodgy. They never were able to give me a date for the new boiler installation and they stopped returning my calls or texts. and on the few occasions that I was able to get through to them by phone, they said they would have the new boiler in a day or two and would text to call or email to confirm a repair date later in the day.  Then nothing.  This "We're working on it and will update you by EOD" happened three times in two weeks - with no update or follow through. Basically, they ghosted me. Three weeks total went by with no communication from BigBlue on a repair date.  Meanwhile, my house is a chilly 54 degrees with no hot water for bathing or doing dishes.

I finally got through to the owner (who had been so great in the past) and found out that 4 days prior to my system failing, he had sold his company to some large plumbing company. He was out of the loop an could do nothing. So i was 5 weeks in with zero communications nd. freezing house.

I finally just found another plumbing company that wanted the work and found and installed a new boiler for me in two days. Stay away from BigBlue, it was the worst experience of my life dealing with this slipshod and dishonest company.

reviews.author: Joseph M.
reviews.text:
My neighbors hired these guys to replace a sewer lateral that went under my yard. The initial communication was "We'll have to dig a hole in front of your house to connect the sewer lateral. It won't affect you otherwise." Cool, no problem. 

The first morning of work one of their guys was wandering around in my yard at 8:15am with some kind of beeping surveying equipment. If I had been wearing pants at the time, I would have run out to yell at him. Maybe the neighbors forgot to relay the "we'll have to wander around your yard" info, but no matter what, ring the doorbell first and introduce yourself before you go wandering around someone's yard.

Then two days later, they knock on my door at 9am "Hey we goofed up, we have to dig a hole in your yard today." WTF!? I mean, I let them dig it. I'm not going to prevent my neighbor from having a sewer lateral replacement. But you're supposed to figure these things out ahead of time, so you can let people know a day or two (or preferably, weeks) ahead of time that you are going to be digging a hole in their yard. Or if you're not sure, at least warn that it might be a possibility.

If it was just one act of unprofessionalism I would have let it slide. Two is a pattern, and gets you a one-star review.

*** Response to Big Blue's 12/22 comment:

(1) You did not ring the doorbell before wandering into my backyard at 8:30 am in the morning. It's loud and I can't miss it. You say you knocked; it must have been quietly. 
(2) You did not communicate with me directly before doing the work. The first time I heard anything from you directly was beeping from your detector in my backyard.
(3) I'm not sure what you mean by "We did inform you there was a possibility that there may be some digging in the easement at the back of your property." The notice I got from my neighbors weeks ahead of time, by email, was "neither your nor your neighbor's property will be dug up or affected in any way." The first I heard that digging would happen in my yard, or any possibility thereof, was the morning-of, when your person arrived at my door and said "we made a mistake in measuring, we need to dig a hole in your yard today".

***

reviews.author: Kimberly W.
reviews.text:
I'm someone who leaves reviews -- and most of them are very positive. I believe in acknowledging good service when it's earned. Unfortunately, this is not one of those times.

In early 2020, I contacted Big Blue Plumbing for an emergency plumbing issue involving a leak from the shower pan and drain line. Their website advertises 24-hour emergency response and emphasizes thorough assessment by licensed, insured professionals.

The estimate was $10,147, but the final cost came to $16,215 -- a 60% increase as the scope expanded. I also purchased the required materials myself. The work was completed in March 2020.

A few years later, I had to completely remove the shower installation after discovering that the floor around the shower had become dangerously weak. I realized something was wrong when my foot nearly went through the floor -- you could press with a shoe and see the basement below.

During removal, several serious issues became clear:

The original water damage was never fully assessed

The surrounding subfloor remained rotted and unsafe

The shower installation was crooked, with uneven support planks

Moisture had continued spreading for years, causing further structural damage

These are precisely the kinds of issues an emergency plumbing repair should catch -- and the kind their website claims they thoroughly evaluate.

Considering the amount paid and the urgency of the situation, Big Blue Plumbing should have identified the full extent of the damage before installing a new shower. Instead, the most critical safety and structural problems were ignored. I now face significant additional costs to repair the area and make it safe again.

I've reached out to the company for a resolution and am awaiting their response.

I rarely leave negative reviews -- in fact, most of mine are highly positive -- but consumers deserve transparency. Based on my experience, I urge homeowners to proceed with caution. The lack of proper assessment and the long-term safety hazards that resulted were extremely disappointing and costly.

reviews.author: Roderic M.
reviews.text: I had posted a positive review for Big Blue back in 2020.  This is the first time I have, unfortunately, had to delete the positive review and post a negative one.  Our experience with Big Blue in 2020 was a postive one.  More recently we had a subsequent positive experience, and had for all intents and purposes hoped to have Big Blue as our go to plumbing service going forward.  Unfortunately, that is no longer the case based on our most recent experience.  Big Blue replaced a toilet for us fairly recently.  The toilet began leaking due to the seal under the toilet failing.  This was under warranty since the toilet was recently installed.  The Big Blue Rep came by and initially wanted to charge us for installing a new seal on the toilet until I explained that they recently installed the toilet and we should not be charged for this.  He called the office and this was agreed on.  However, a small angle valve on the back of the toilet was also leaking and replacing this was not part of the initial toilet replacement so we were informed that we would have to be charged for that. After the seal was replaced and the angle valve, we were informed the charge would be over $500.00.  The Angle valve, which sells at home depot for $9.94 and which took just minutes to install, resulted in a bill of over $500.00?  The replacing of the seal and the labor involved in that should not have incurred any labor costs since it was under warranty.   We have tried repeatedly to call and get an answer as to why we were charged over $500.00 for a $9.00 piece to be replaced in just a few minutes, and we have received no reply.  We were attempting to either get a reasonable justification for this or a reimbursement. I paid the amount at the time since my wife was having surgery the following day and the last thing I had to worry about was the toilet leaking.  After multiple attempts to work with Big Blue regarding this, and getting no cooperation at all, I have resorted to posting this and will no longer support this business.

reviews.author: Oscar D.
reviews.text:
We were out of town on vacation and were notified by a house sitter that we had an emergency water leak outside of our home.  We contacted Big Blue Plumbing by telephone due to their Google Reviews and requested an immediate onsite inspection and repair action.  We were initially quoted a flat rate of $3,950 for the repairs without their doing any investigation work of the failed water leak source. The firm tried to talk us out of doing the repair and instead tried to get us to do a complete water line replacement in the amount of $14,000, to which we replied that was out of our available budget.  After much discussion the firm agreed to do the water line repairs, and provided a final cost of $3441.11, (to which they knew we were out of town and were stuck with an ongoing leak, so we were left with little choice but to accept the repairs and pay for it).  Our neighbor and our house sitter notified us that the repair person, who was competent, was onsite a total of approximately 3 hours for the repair action, meaning that we paid a plumber about $1,000 per hour for the repairs. Also, both the repair person and his supervisor verbally inferred to us that since we did not agree to do the $14K water line replacement, they would not warrant these repairs because of the PVC/copper line connection interface possibility of failure (which is clear violation of California Civil Codes 896 and 900, which requires a licensed contractor to provide a minimum warranty of one year for any work completed and paid for).  So, please be advised of this scenario when requesting any work from this contractor.

NOTE:  Regarding the discussed warranty issue above, irrespective of what was verbally discussed between the contractor staff, myself, and my wife regarding a potential lack of warranty (due to the PVC/copper line connection interface possible failure) prior to the completion of the work, the invoice received from the company indicates a two-year warranty for the completed work, which is acceptable.

reviews.author: Krista G.
reviews.text:
Love, love, love Paul and his customer service! Yes, he might be a little bit more than the competitors but it's SO worth it on the customer service side. He knows his stuff inside and out and is willing to take the time necessary to educate you so that you are very clear on all your options. 

I had a unique need because I the previous had installed a radiant floor heating system. Although I didn't use it, I wanted to maintain its integrity for resale purposes. Paul Gerrard (owner extraordinaire!) came out to do the estimate. He spent over two hours explaining everything to me which I really appreciated because I like to be extremely educated on every project I take on. He explained my three options and was very thorough in understanding how the radiant floor unit came into play. He was the ONLY provider that discussed how a tankless water heater would work (or not) in coordination with the radiant floor unit.

After receiving three other bids (some of which were cheaper) I chose Paul and Big Blue because of his knowledge and his customer service. It is so hard to find a contractor for projects these days that you can truly trust that they will do what they say. Yes, I paid more than I would have with another provider but I really needed to know that the project was going to be done right the first time and that I was very clear on how it worked in my current setup. Paul made me feel very comfortable. Hell, he even offered to allow my engineering father who was questioning every part of the project, to call his cell with any questions! He sold me with that. The team was meticulous and Paul was sure to oversee everything from beginning to end. We are happily enjoying a much needed extremely hot shower thanks to the tankless water heater!

synthesis.summary: Big Blue Plumbing shows strong technical expertise and professionalism with excellent individual technician performance, but has a concerning reliability issue for emergency services. While their work quality appears high based on multiple positive experiences, their emergency response commitment may be questionable.
strengths: Thorough explanations of options and work
weaknesses: Emergency appointment reliability issues
Last-minute scheduling cancellations
redFlags: Canceled emergency appointment with short notice via text
synthesis.topQuote: Not only was he able to replace our aging (and old) Glacier Bay fixtures without tearing out the entire wall, he discovered and repaired a unknown major leak under the toilette. We are truly indebted to Harris for his complete professionalism.
synthesis.worstQuote: Overnight they texted me that they were 'canceling the appointment due to a scheduling change.' I can't speak to the quality of the actual work they do, but I certainly wouldn't depend on them in the case of an emergency.

plumbers.name: All Clear Plumbing & Drain Service, LLC
plumbers.address: 154 Lafayette Ave unit i, Laurel, MD 20707, USA

reviews.author: Kim H
reviews.text: Brandon was very knowledgeable and completed to job with ease.

reviews.author: Loretta Polite-Shipman
reviews.text: Installers were prompt, arriving a bit before the appointment time to install two toilets. They were courteous, professional, and efficient. They explained the process and cleaned the areas when they were done.  We are very pleased with their service.

reviews.author: Jay Scott
reviews.text: Allen and Cody provided great service. Their work ethic and personalities were awesome. I would recommend them to my family, neighbors and friends.

reviews.author: Donald White
reviews.text:
Had All Clear out Monday afternoon to snake out a toilet. I had used them before and thought I could trust them. They charged me almost $800. After the fact, I checked with other plumbers and discovered normally the charge would be between $300-400. I called them and they said it was the price in their book. What makes matters worse  is that I am a furloughed federal employee.

All clear tried to hide my post with a positive response.  You suck All Clear!

reviews.author: Jarvis X'avier
reviews.text: Tried calling several times and left messages, but never received a callback. Their website form also failed to submit. Very frustrating when you need service.

reviews.author: Keisha Ross
reviews.text: Amazing Service! Allen Provides Great Knowledge on Water and Plumbing Services!! Allen and Cody are very welcoming and approachable as they are ready to answer any of your questions. They move FAST!!! Allen and Cody were Punctual and the installation was quick. I had my water heater replaced and I am very pleased with the end result.  Allen came to do my initial estimate and inspect the previous water heater. He was very straight forward with pricing and informing me of everything that needed to be done. I didn’t get work done right away, but All Clear Plumbing didn't continuously harass me or pressure me into going forward with their services, which is something I truly appreciate as so many companies do that!!!!  My husband and I waited a while because it’s a large purchase, but when we were ready Allen had such a memorable first impression I knew we HAD  to go with him!! Thank you both, Allen & Cody for providing such amazing service! I Definitely recommend their services and I will be using them again when it comes time to service my water heater in the future.

reviews.author: Aimee Gregory
reviews.text:
I cannot say enough wonderful things about the service I received from All Clear Plumbing, and especially from Juan. When I discovered that my outside garden hose faucet was leaking, I initially considered fixing it myself. However, I quickly encountered a problem: a rusted screw in the handle that I could not remove.
Given that the frost-free faucet was well over 20 years old and that accessing it required going through drywall to reach the pipes, I realized this repair was beyond my capabilities and decided I needed professional help. I contacted All Clear Plumbing, and they sent Juan out the same day.
Juan provided a very reasonable estimate for the work and completed the job within an hour. The new faucet is perfect and has zero leaks. Looking back, I am only upset that I did not replace it years ago.
Juan’s customer service was truly amazing. I would not only use him again for future plumbing needs, but I would also refer him to anyone who requires plumbing services.

reviews.author: Dru Foster
reviews.text:
Edited to Include Service from December 2025: We called All Clear to finish up a vanity installation and replace our kitchen faucet. Brandon came out between Christmas and New Year and did a great job. I was very pleased with how well he explained everything and how quickly he worked! All Clear will remain our go-to for any plumbing needs.

May 2025: Alan and Cody did a great job! From initial quote to installation of our hot water heater, I was impressed by their professionalism and communication. Alan made sure I understood exactly what was needed to do the job, and I never felt like I was being talked down to or treated differently because my husband wasn’t home to oversee the work. We’ll definitely be calling All Clear again for any additional plumbing work!

reviews.author: Deb Carberry
reviews.text:
This company is the greatest! They stuck with me as I called them every day for 11 days straight when I was going through a terrible experience trying to get my water heater replaced through American Home Shield & they were dragging their feet about coverage & outright lying to me & to All Clear as well. I am a 26yr customer of (AHS) so I’m still unclear what their issue was.
The lovely ladies in the office Heather & Adela called (AHS) on my behalf several times to try to get the situation expedited.
Thank you to Jamie, Alan, Cody, Almicor, Heather, Adela & everyone else at All Clear Plumbing & Drain LLC! I am ever so grateful to all of you for sticking with me & finally getting me hot water again!!!!
Here is a photo of my new water heater. Ain’t she a beaut?!

reviews.author: T. Monroe
reviews.text:
I always request All Clear Plumbing for any job covered by American Home Shield (AHS).

For a recent pipe leak requiring a drywall cut and replacement, the team of Juan and Amilcar was great! They were prompt, courteous, and consistently made sure I was satisfied.

Though that repair wasn't covered by my warranty, they noticed an additional issue—a loud water pressure noise—and correctly advised me that that problem was covered by AHS. I immediately requested Juan and Amilcar again for the second job. They arrived on time, quickly fixed the water pressure, and even unclogged two of my bathroom sinks!

Their professionalism, attention to detail, and use of their own shoe covers are why I HIGHLY RECOMMEND this team and All Clear Plumbing!

reviews.author: Melissa Davey- Keller Williams Realty Center
reviews.text: Pros:  On time, professional, answered questions in email Cons:  TOTAL STICKER SHOCK, Would not quote how much a sewer scope would be until after they completed the work.  They charged over $800 for a sewer scope I can get done with Homeland for under $300.  (BTW, the toilet was already out, so very little labor was needed).  The video which was supposed to go from house to street, stopped halfway through the sewer line in the yard.  I made the mistake of letting my dad be the one who was present during the inspection.  My father doesn't ask questions, question why they didn't go all the way to the street,  or why they don't quote before the work, or why they charge over $500 more than market rate.

reviews.author: Cherise Johnson
reviews.text:
I had a great experience with this company. My water heater was acting up and losing hot water, and the technician took the time to properly diagnose the issue instead of rushing me into a full replacement.

He clearly explained what was going on, showed me the condition of the heating elements, and walked me through my options.
