I had issues with my brand new HVAC system, which the installer wasn't willing to resolve anymore. These guys listened to the issue, promptly put me on schedule and a guy arrived at my place on time. He was knowledgeable and friendly. Diagnosed a couple of issues and presented solutions and costs. That's where I took off 1 star.
First issue was that my drain pipe was clogged. The solution, cut the drain pipe and apply pressurized nitrogen to clear clog and cost, $320. Obviously I declined. All I had to do was to plug the end going into the air handler, and apply a little air pressure on the other end with my shop vac, and the drain cleared out in 5 mins of work.
Second issue, my system was low on gas charge. Solution charge it up, but cost was a flat fee of $800 up to 5 lbs. That also I declined, in order to get it covered by my installer. I called a couple of other highly rated places to check, and most had more reasonable charges based on per pound of gas, instead of a flat slab.

reviews.author: David Goodwin
reviews.text:
I recently had the pleasure of receiving plumbing services from Thomas E Clark INC, and I couldn't be more satisfied with the experience. From start to finish, the technician {Alexander Covell} demonstrated a high level of professionalism, skill, and courtesy.

He arrived on time, fully prepared, and immediately assessed the issue with confidence and clarity. His attention to detail and thorough explanation of the problem and proposed solution put me at ease right away. The work was completed efficiently and to a very high standard, leaving everything clean and functioning perfectly.

It’s rare to find technicians who combine technical expertise with outstanding customer service, but Alexander delivered on both fronts. I highly recommend his services to anyone in need of reliable and expert plumbing work. A true professional!

reviews.author: Raquel Morgan
reviews.text: I had called a different plumbing company that was having dispatching issues and tried to send two different plumbers who didn’t show up after waiting several hours. My neighbor had this company over to their house coincidentally fixing a drain issue. I asked the plumber if he could look at my drain when done and he fit me in. I had tried to snake the drain myself but couldn’t get past the block. This plumber was able to snake the drain, and used an apparatus he fixed to my hose to test the drain after snaking it. He was thorough and precise and tested it for a while til he was satisfied with its ability to drain. He also told me my drain is flat under the ground and suggested rebuilding it at an angle if I wanted to prevent the same issue from occurring.

reviews.author: nancy0804
reviews.text: These guys are absolutely great. We've had several different guys  come out over the years. Most recently Efran, installed a submeter connection for us. He was thorough, thoughtful (making sure that the pipes they were installing were placed so that they didn't conflict with our shelving in the storage room), knowledgable and forward thinking. He helped us figure out how to improve our situation in ways we didn't even know we could. Rafael who came and gave the estimate was also very knowledgable and efficient. Theresa was very friendly and efficient on the phone.  It seems these guys are still family owned and not owned by private equity - probably another reason that they were so good. Thanks.

reviews.author: Julie Klingenstein
reviews.text: We had a very complicated plumbing problem: replacing a 100 year-old main water shutoff valve.  This required the team from Thomas E Clark to work carefully and quickly with the D.C. Water employee who had to turn off the water to our home and others in the neighborhood.  Time was of the essence and the job had to be completed successfully so that we had control of our water supply in the event of an emergency.  This team of plumbers stayed calm and focused on solving this difficult problem.  Their plumbing work was A#1 and their attitude throughout was professional.  We are very grateful to the people at Thomas E Clark who REALLY know how to fix things.

reviews.author: Adam Ereli
reviews.text:
you guys are great.  everything, from beginning to end, is personal and professional.

Your phone people are efficient, courteous and helpful.  They’re so unlike the disinterested and ill-informed people on most company call-in lines.

The service techs come on time, communicate in advance and get the job done with a minimum of fuss.  Just love it.

Pricing, invoicing, bill payment is quick easy and reasonable.

What sets Thomas Clark apart from the rest is that it's a good company that focuses on its core business and doesn’t try to do too much. You haven’t grown so big that you lose the ability to service your customers well. I appreciate that.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast, know old DC homes inside and out, and finish jobs right—but they're not cheap and one customer had an awful experience with a technician's personal hygiene.
strengths: 135 years in business with A+ BBB accreditation and zero complaints in 3 years—strongest trust signal available
Emergency response documented in 15+ reviews: same-day service, 2-hour response to 6:30pm calls, multiple 30-minute arrival mentions
Deep expertise in lead pipe replacement and old building systems: 20+ detailed reviews praising work quality on complex 70-100+ year old homes
weaknesses: One 1-star review from April 2024 describes a technician using customer's bathroom without permission and leaving feces unflushed for 3+ days—hygiene/respect violation that contradicts professional claims

One 1-star HVAC sales review (March 2025) documents unprofessional behavior: salesman mocked customer, refused to complete estimate, became angry; suggests internal quality control gaps in non-emergency divisions
2 reviews cite high pricing: one customer declined $320 drain clear (resolved with shop vac in 5 mins) and $800 flat-fee refrigerant charge; another notes 'not cheap' but 'best value long-run'
redFlags: 1 documented case of technician using customer bathroom without permission and leaving waste unflushed for 3+ days (April 2024)—hygiene/respect violation
1 documented case of HVAC salesman unprofessional conduct: mocking customer, refusing to complete estimate, becoming hostile (March 2025)—suggests training/screening gaps in sales division
Pricing disputes in 2 reviews: customer felt charged excessively for routine work ($320 drain clear, $800 refrigerant flat fee); flagged as potential for surprise/high charges
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple same-day emergency calls documented (2-hour response at 6:30pm, Saturday flood assistance); reviews consistently mention prompt dispatch and weekend availability; strong pattern supports emergency reliability.

plumbers.name: Benjamin Franklin Plumbing of Oklahoma City
plumbers.address: 2839 S Portland Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73108, USA

reviews.author: John Hassell
reviews.text:
Garrett came out and did an amazing job ! Got everything I needed done today handled and quoted some work for me that will be done in the future. Great service and a more than reasonable price! 100% recommend. Thanks for the great work Garrett it is truly appreciated.

I had Gus come out to do some work today. Another great experience. He did an amazing job. He was able to make my kitchen sink faucet area look great when I thought there was no chance. He had his work cut out for him with the projects here. He more than excelled. Oh ! And that mustache is even better  than Garrett’s outstanding stache.

reviews.author: Harold Ochs
reviews.text: Chris and Gus did a great job on all the repairs I had done. They were friendly and very knowledgeable.  Did good job of cleaning up and explained all the work they did. Very satisfied.

reviews.author: Sally Dixon
reviews.text: Chris throughly assessed and explained my most pressing plumbing needs and helped me come up with a plan. He and Gus were friendly and efficient.

reviews.author: Brian
reviews.text:
Arren and Chris did great job. They listened to my description of the issue and my concerns. They seemed to be very thorough in their review and gave me a clear assessment of what they found and recommendations for moving forward.

When delivering the quote they gave me options, which was much appreciated. They completed the job the same day, and at the quoted price. They were professional, courteous, friendly, and competent. I couldn’t ask for better. I would highly recommend the company and those two especially

reviews.author: Gang Gang
reviews.text: Gus was amazing and was very understanding to the issue. Listened and gave reassurance. The price was also amazing!!!!! I will always use them for my plumbing repairs.

synthesis.summary: Benjamin Franklin Plumbing consistently delivers professional, thorough service with knowledgeable technicians who explain their work clearly. Multiple customers praise their fair pricing, same-day completion, and courteous approach to problem-solving.
weaknesses: Limited sample size of reviews
No mention of complex emergency scenarios
synthesis.topQuote: They completed the job the same day, and at the quoted price. They were professional, courteous, friendly, and competent. I couldn't ask for better.

plumbers.name: ABC Plumbing, Sewer, Heating, Cooling & Electric
plumbers.address: 828 Davis St UNIT 205, Evanston, IL 60201, USA

reviews.author: anamebanana
reviews.text: Do not trust them they lie do a poor job and leave you hanging on snowstorm days. Monday Nov 10, 2025 - call them for a leak two people came spend the entire day charged 600 dlls now I have more leaks that when I started. Call them again with multiple leaks on Saturday they said they couldn’t bring someone until Monday the same technician calls me and says he will be here Sunday 10 am he LIED to me!!! He never showed up … it is after a snowstorm spend the night worried about my property and myself…couldn’t sleep worried about the leaks …horrible..Monday my property has multiple leaks it is being damaged as we speak..I called again and now waiting for someone to come… I don’t trust them any longer.

reviews.author: Meredith Goodspeed
reviews.text: Juan did an incredible job helping us with our furnace. He was so detailed, provided thorough assessment and ultimately a fix. While waiting for the right part he kept in contact to assure us he was working to locate the part… he personally made me feel confident we were in the best hands. On top of his expertise, he was so kind and lovely. He provided a wonderful customer experience during a time when our furnace went out (usually a high stress moment). I will now have abc as our first call whenever we have a need because of how great this experience was. Thank you Juan!

reviews.author: Robert Feldman
reviews.text:
Tim Leary was the best HVAC tech I have ever dealt with in the over 35 years of home ownership!  He was friendly, knowledgeable, professional, and supportive to me as a homeowner and long time customer.    He diagnosed a major problem with our furnace and immediately took action to have it taken care of ASAP....from ordering the correct parts, to the logistics of the service people who will do the major repair.

I highly recommend Tim Leary!  He makes me happy I am an ABC customer and I will request him to continue to service our furnace and A/C from now on!

reviews.author: Patrick Motherway
reviews.text:
Had a great experience today with Andrey, who not only fixed my HVAC unit , but offered a hand helping me move something. This original review was a 1 star review from an issue a year ago. I was happy to change it and receive the great service I did today.

I will also add that Melissa in the office was very helpful in getting me fast and great service.

reviews.author: Kate Murray
reviews.text: I was so grateful that my unexpected appt need (had 16 hrs b4 I had to leave state) happened and Ronan came eve. Tho it was crazy late - he had a smile, friendly but earnest demeanor- assured me that heating units were fine for me to leave state!  Thanks so much Ronan!!🙏🏻❤️

synthesis.summary: ABC Plumbing has skilled technicians who provide excellent HVAC service and emergency availability, but reliability issues including no-shows and incomplete repairs create significant concerns for emergency situations. The company shows strong technical expertise when they follow through, but trust is undermined by poor follow-up on critical repairs.
strengths: Skilled technicians with excellent technical knowledge
Strong HVAC expertise and repair capabilities
Friendly and professional service when delivered
weaknesses: Reliability issues with scheduled appointments
Poor follow-up on emergency repairs
Incomplete work leading to additional problems
redFlags: Technician failed to show for emergency repair after snowstorm
Initial repair work created more leaks than original problem
Left customer with multiple leaks and property damage
synthesis.topQuote: He was so detailed, provided thorough assessment and ultimately a fix. While waiting for the right part he kept in contact to assure us he was working to locate the part… he personally made me feel confident we were in the best hands.
synthesis.worstQuote: Call them again with multiple leaks on Saturday they said they couldn't bring someone until Monday the same technician calls me and says he will be here Sunday 10 am he LIED to me!!! He never showed up … it is after a snowstorm spend the night worried about my property and myself

plumbers.name: Smooth Saling Plumbing & Water Heater Replacement
plumbers.address: 3511 Gallatin Pike Office 318, Nashville, TN 37216, USA

reviews.author: Wyatt B.
reviews.text:
Saved me $500+ and didn't try to upsell me!

I cannot recommend Morton's Plumbing enough. I needed an emergency water heater replacement in a crawlspace and another company quoted me a massive price. Morton's came out, beat that quote by over $500, and did a fantastic job.

What impressed me most was their honesty. I thought I needed some expensive electrical work (a junction box fix) based on another quote, but the Morton's tech looked at it and confirmed it was actually fine--saving me even more money. They even threw in a new quarter-turn shut-off valve at no extra cost to make things easier for me in the future. If you want a Nashville plumber who is actually honest and does great work, call them.

reviews.author: Mary King
reviews.text: Smooth Saling was fantastic all-around, from the scheduler in the office (Sam) to the on-site plumber (Alex).  We would recommend them to anyone!

reviews.author: Carlos L.
reviews.text:
Not only are they great at Plumbing, they fixed my AC concerns after another company couldn't. 

They're the best and I'm a customer for life! 

Thanks Morton Plumbing...

reviews.author: K R.
reviews.text:
Completely unreliable.  
Pulled a no show on the coldest day of the year.  I made an appointment because heat was not working properly.  They were supposed to come from 12-3.  It's 5pm on Friday now.  They don't answer the phone. Tomorrow is Christmas Eve and now I'm forced to get another company to come out and I'll be charged the emergency/weekend rate...if I can find someone.

reviews.author: Mark T.
reviews.text:
At my realtors suggestion I contacted Morton's to install a water line for an ice maker, when the plumber arrived, on time, my wife showed him the inspection report on  our newly purchased house.  His response was Mr. Morton needed to look at the report and call us with a quote.  No work was done.  After repeated calls to Morton's with no response, we waited a month or two then my wife decided to investigate plumbers again.  After the glowing reviews online, she decided to call Morton's! forgetting they were the company that gave us no response in the first place.  One more time, the plumber showed up on time, said the scope of the work we wanted done would be included in a larger quote and Mr. Morton would have to give us an estimate, charged $50 for the call (probably will be credited towards the bigger job)  Mr. Morton showed up, on time, promised a quote in 2 or maybe 3 working days.  Ten days latter, no quote, woman on the phone does not have anything in our file, same old runaround.

To recap, 2 service calls, $50 paid, one visit by the owner, no work done, no quote given, no phone calls returned.

reviews.author: Jason S.
reviews.text:
What the heck?  I just gave a plumber a 5-star rating.  

We have a kitchen sink that likes to clog.  Doesn't matter what we do or do not put down it, it just sometimes likes to quit on us.  Which is a pretty damn big problem considering that the dishwasher drains into that line and the washing machine is behind it in the drain process.  I've gotten better over the years at fixing it myself; I've got a 25-foot snake that generally fixes the problem, but not when the clog is past 25 feet...

Before I figured out that it really isn't that hard to fix with the right tools, we encountered some really, really bad plumbers: 

http://www.yelp.com/biz/sidewinder-drain-cleaning-plumbing-repair-goodlettsville

http://www.yelp.com/biz/jack-steakley-plumbing-and-showroom-nashville

And some expensive mediocre plumbers:

http://www.yelp.com/biz/hiller-plumbing-nashville

To give you an idea of what happened with the bad guys, it cost us well over $400, the technicians were scummy, took hours and hours because they didn't bring the right equipment and tried to sell my wife $80 Liquid Pumr.  Hiller cost us $250, but they had to come out three times in rapid succession, so the total bill was $750 and they never provided a lasting solution.

So, when I ran into problems recently and couldn't fix it myself, I called these guys.  Their technician arrived when the dispatcher told me he would and he was courteous and wore those little boots to keep from messing stuff up.  He didn't treat me like a jackass when I explained what I had already done to try and fix the problem and he told me up front what the whole affair was likely to cost me.

45 minutes or so later, he had fixed the clog and presented me with a bill for $150, or exactly what he told me that it was going to cost me.  He also explained what he had done and told me that if it reclogged in the short term, he would come back out and remedy the situation for free.

This week I got a thank-you card from the company.  That's some damn good service.

reviews.author: Jill S.
reviews.text:
Well, I would have liked to give a review about the service, because when the owner came by for my estimate, he was friendly, professional, and got the estimate back to me promptly.  It was a reasonable fee.  But I have had so much trouble dealing with the phone staff that I have to pass on them.  I really don't want to be lectured by a phone answerer when I am about to hand over close to a thousand dollars.  I need three jobs done, and I have been getting different answers from different people on the phones about getting the estimate done.  Like I said, the estimate I received was reasonable.   But it was incomplete.  After two weeks of being told "Mr. Morton does the estimates" over and over again, I assumed that this means he would, you know, do the estimates.  I am only today learning that he only does SOME of them, and that other people do other kinds of estimates.  I also got a bit of an attitude that implied I should have known that they run their business this way.  It was a little insulting.  I do not expect my customers to understand the ins and outs of my business.  It is my responsibility to make sure they know what to expect.

All I want to do is pay to have my kitchen and toilets repaired.  I too have a business to run and need to get this done.  I was happy to wait for as long as it has taken, but now I'm told they cannot get anyone out here to finish the estimate until NEXT week.  I cannot wait another week just to finish the estimates.  I would like to begin the work next week.  Regretfully I will be staring the whole process over with a new company.  It's not what I want to do, but I'm left with a sour taste in my mouth after the way I was treated on the phone.

reviews.author: Charles S.
reviews.text:
I found Morton Plumbing here on Yelp, and I am now a very happy new customer. 

I decided that I wanted a new kitchen sink, and considering the hack job the previous homeowner had done on some of the renovations before we bought the place, I decided it would be best done by a professional. 

Pro tip: Call to schedule your appointment, don't use the web form. I initially was a little off put by Morton; I attempted to use the web form to schedule my appointment and I never received a call back. I gave them the benefit of the doubt and called back a bit later; they scheduled me an appointment for the next day. 

Thank god I did, because when I woke up the next morning my hot water heater was doing its best fire hydrant impression. After I managed to cut off the water, I called Morton. They detailed a second plumber to come out and handle the hot water heater install, all on just a few hours' notice. 

I won't go into detail on every interaction, but there were several suggestions made to me about the sink, hot water heater, and a few other plumbing questions I had; Most of them actually saved me money rather than costing more. The few things they suggested that were more expensive were for good reason. It's rare to find an honest plumber it seems these days, so kudos to Morton. 

They don't bill by the hour, they bill by the job, so while they may seem a little more expensive upfront, if the job takes longer than expected you don't have to pay extra. AWESOME!

Also, they warranty most of their work for 6 years; if you develop leaks, they'll come do the repairs free of charge. 

In short, if you have plumbing issues, Morton should be your first call.

reviews.author: Stacey R.
reviews.text: A month ago, our sewer ejector pump overflowed while I was taking a shower. We thought it was covered under our home warranty so we got stuck with whoever they sent out, which was Hers and His Plumbing. Turns out it was not covered, but since they were already there and said it was just the float switch and the pump was working fine, we were like fine fix it. That lasted about a month, and sure enough I was taking a shower, the toilet started bubbling, and the pump overflowed again! We learned our lesson, and called Morton's because we knew you guys had won Best of Nashville and had great reviews on Yelp. Josh and someone else I can't remember (sorry!) came to our house Saturday the 16th and listened to our story, and then went downstairs to check the pump. Long story short, whoever reno'd our basement knew nothing apparently and the pump was not large enough or strong enough to pump everything up and out of our house! They took one look at it and knew that was the problem. And there was also no check valve to prevent back flow! Josh came back on Thursday and installed a whole new pump, vent, and check valve for us and its only been a week, but it's been running great and we can finally use our bathroom! And, this new bigger and more powerful one is quieter than whatever we had in there before! Thank you, thank you, thank you for helping us! Ever since we moved into this house the downstairs bathroom has been the bane of my existence but hopefully we will never have this problem again! If you need any plumbing issues fixed, call Morton's FIRST! We could have saved ourselves some money the first time!

reviews.author: Nickie G.
reviews.text:
You know when you get a plumber you are typically worried that they are going to "lay the pipe" to your backside...and not in a good way. So when I needed some plumbing work I asked around and Morton Plumbing was suggested. 

I have used Morton now for plumbing issues at least 3 or 4 times and for a heating/cooling issue once. They have always shown up during the time window, they have always given me a price quote up front, and they have always made sure the job was completed correctly. Additionally, they treat their repeat customers right and it makes me 100% more likely to continue using them. For example:

My upstairs heating/cooling unit was dripping water, so I had to turn it off. I called and they did not have openings until the next week and it was oh, only 100 degrees outside. What did they do? Made a full effort to work me in the next day because "there's no way you can be in your house without air conditioning".

Another example: my downstairs toilet was clogged just in time for my annual New Year's Eve party. Someone came to look at it and realized he was going to need another person with him in order to fix it and it would possibly be New Year's Eve day before they could do this. Cutting it close! But he told the scheduler that I was having a party and should try and fit me in on Friday so we wouldn't be cutting it so close to the party. So they fit me in and had it fixed well prior to NYE.

It is so relieving to find a plumbing company that is honest, reliable, and provides excellent customer service without screwing you over price-wise!

reviews.author: Brian S.
reviews.text:
These guys are the real deal.  They are honest, straightforward, and do the work without the hard sell of so many other plumbing "contractor/salespeople."  

We have a rather complicated water heater set up consisting of a somewhat unusual atmospheric combustion/power vented exhaust unit. We got quotes all over the very upper end of the price range from 3 other plumbing companies big and small. It became apparent that 2 of the guys had little experience with what they were looking at with our system and so they priced their quotes to cover their inexperience...and big company #3 got it but was so high all I can figure is greed/high overhead. Morton was the only one who "got" what our set up was and didn't want an exorbitant sum to install the system.  I'll note that my research showed that our original unit failed prematurely due to an initial improper installation, and Jimmy Morton immediately recognized this issue during his site survey.  His crew (Jimmy's son and another plumber) showed up on time after a phone call check-in to be sure we were ready for them, and the work was done quickly and with a high level of craftsmanship-clean solder joints, well routed pipes, everything neat and well planned, and a high quality heater (no off brands) properly installed according the the manufacturer's directions.  I hope I don't have plumbing issues in the future, but if I do Morton Plumbing will be who I call for service.

reviews.author: Sam C.
reviews.text: Been a customer for a few years for plumbing needs.  They told me I was ready for a new HVAC system for my single family residence when my AC stopped blowing cold air.  Charged $165 for that visit, which I thought was a little high already, but I figured that if I had them replace it, they would absorb that charge.  Well several days later, I was emailed and estimate for $9238 without any breakdown of charges to show how they came to that number.  I called and talked to the owner to ask about the itemized breakdown and he refused to provide one for me.  When I asked how he arrived at it, he said he just figured it in his head.  I decided to seek out other estimates and got two separate ones for approximately $7700.  Then someone recommended Mountain Air.  They came out, took care of the problem without replacing the system for $200....yes, TWO HUNDRED.  No up-selling or b.s.  I learned that it's worth checking things out and saved thousands.  Sadly, Morton will be losing all my plumbing business because of the owner's arrogance.  He didn't sound like he cared.  If he had been a straight arrow, I might have actually replaced the entire system based on trusting him, but it's his loss and my gain.

reviews.author: Rob H.
reviews.text:
My new go-to guys.

I called Morton Plumbing, for an estimate, after becoming extremely dissatisfied with a very large plumbing/electrical/HVAC company based out of Franklin (white trucks, red lettering) & saw their excellent reviews.   Morton is a smaller, family owned & local company & they did not disappoint. I called during the polar vortex of 2014 & they were overwhelmed with frozen pipes.  It took them a couple days to get to us, but understandable.  When they did come, they were friendly, professional, energetic, and obviously looking out for MY best interest, as a customer. That is something I definitely did not get from the other company.  Their estimates for two 50-gallon water heaters, with rerouted plumbing came in $1300 below my previous plumber's estimate (who was unwilling to re-plumb them - or return my calls).

Today, I smelled a gas leak & thought it was from the new hot water heaters.  Morton came quickly, and diagnosed the problem was not with the water heaters, but with my FURNACE, sitting in closet near the water heaters, & did not charge me for my mistake.  They turned the gas off to the furnace for me.

Now awaiting that "other" company.  If only Morton did HVAC too.

reviews.author: Jane S.
reviews.text: Buyer beware!  Be sure to get multiple quotes before using Morton Plumbing.  Since I had used Morton before with varying degrees of satisfaction...but mainly because I had the number handy when my hot water heater began leaking, I called Morton first for a quote.  Despite a prompt visit, the quote I received was absolutely rediculous.  A 50 lb gas heater (installed) was quoted at about $1,200.   The quote for a 75-gal tank was even crazier...$2,000!  When my jaw dropped, I was quickly informed of  the exciting 6-year "all inclusive" warranty!  (I guess that's okay, as long as they stay in business.) Not having been born yesterday, I decided to take my chances with the leak and keep looking.  My 3 minutes of research informed me that all gas hot water heaters come with at least a 6-year warranty on the tank.   I got a 50 gallon Whirlpool heater (with a 6-year warranty), installation, and even an extra "all inclusive parts and labor" warranty  to go with the manufacturer's tank warranty.....all for less than $800.  Discount Plumbing's quote was similar to Lowes, so Morton was more than 50% higher than its competitors.

reviews.author: C F.
reviews.text: Charges were unreasonable and excessive for services rendered. We thought it was a complicated repair. It was utterly simple & took less than 1/2 hour. Though we were given 2 choices on how to pay, no information was given about how difficult/simple repair would be. So, owner knew we were in the dark. And, obviously, used that opportunity to rook us. Morton refused to adjust amount, giving umpteen excuses. He charged highest repair charge on the scale, and seemed to enjoy rooking us. When speaking to him, he (1) Disparaged the services, accreditation, and experience of other, good plumbers; and ( 2) Retaliated by increasing charges after talking to him. Owner is NOT professional; will rook you by charging the highest rate he possibly can, and thus, lacks character. This is not someone you want in your house. Will return to several other previous plumbers who are reasonable and fair-minded. I have had several great, experienced and kindly plumbers who discuss the work to be done, and make the situation fair for the customer. BROOKS and many others are much better. Do not go to Morton.

synthesis.summary: They show up fast and do solid work, but watch out—some customers got charged way more than expected, and there's a big gap between what Google and Yelp say about them.
strengths: Same-day or next-day response: 12+ Google reviews mention arriving within hours or the very next day after calling
Quality craftsmanship on complex jobs: Multiple reviews (water heater installs, whole-home filtration, pipe replacements) describe thorough, well-executed work with attention to detail
Team members named consistently and praised: Alex, Connor, and Colin appear across dozens of reviews as knowledgeable, friendly, and professional
weaknesses: Significant pricing disputes on Yelp absent from Google: Yelp contains 5+ complaints about surprise charges, overpricing, or refusal to itemize estimates (e.g., $4,600 for moving a spigot 8 feet, $9,238 HVAC quote with no breakdown), while Google reviews rarely mention pricing concerns

One documented refusal to provide itemized quote: A Google reviewer (3/5, Oct 2025) was told they'd be charged $200 for an itemization after receiving a quick quote, sparking a dispute about transparency
redFlags: Pricing transparency concerns: 5 Yelp reviews (dating 2013–2025) report surprise charges, lack of itemized estimates, or refusal to provide cost breakdowns before work; one Google review (3/5) documents a $200 charge just to itemize an estimate
Platform rating gap: Google 5/5 (304 reviews) vs. Yelp 4.1/5 (78 reviews)—a 0.9-point gap suggests different customer demographics or unequal review filtering; Yelp contains more pricing complaints
One documented no-show: Yelp review (1/5, Jan 2022) reports no arrival and no callback for a scheduled appointment, though this is rare in the dataset
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple Google reviews cite same-day emergency response (busted pipe, water heater leaks, urgent repairs); one Yelp reviewer reports a no-show on an emergency heat repair during winter, but this is isolated among 163 total reviews.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Google 5/5 (304 reviews) vs. Yelp 4.1/5 (78 reviews): 0.9-point gap. Yelp contains the majority of pricing complaints and billing disputes; Google reviews focus on speed and quality. This suggests either selective review posting on Google or that dissatisfied customers prefer Yelp. Investigate pricing on complex jobs before committing.

plumbers.name: My Plumber LLC
plumbers.address: 113 Hickory Brook Ln, Acworth, GA 30101, USA

reviews.author: David Hunter
reviews.text: Tim explained everything and did a fantastic job replacing our hot water heater.  He was on-site less than 24hrs after I called.  Thank you!

reviews.author: Peter Thompson
reviews.text: Friendly people, great service, fair price.

reviews.author: Fran Mccrary
reviews.text: Plumber was professional and knowledgeable of my plumbing issues.

reviews.author: Jerry Wright
reviews.text: My house is built on a concrete slab. I developed water leaks in the water lines under the slab. Had to hire a water leak company to try and find the leaks, turned out to be more than one. With out going into a long story, Tim and Jerry from My Plumber LLC, was able to detect the water leaks that I paid a water company to find. After busting through the concrete and fixing one leak they had to re-rout another line to fix the other problem. These guys are extremely good at what they do. Very knowledgeable, very efficient and will offer suggestions on the best way to fix a problem. I recommend My Plumber LLC 100%.

reviews.author: Jim Allen
reviews.text:
I called My Plumber on the Saturday of Mother's Day weekend in hopes of making an appointment for Monday due to having a water leak. Since they were booked full that next week, they asked if they could come today, but we were both not home for most of the day. They told us to call when we got home. They came over at 6:00 that evening, repaired the leak, and found a bigger water problem that we were not aware of. The guys at My Plumber have always gone above and beyond, and this is another perfect example.Thank you once again!

Tim is someone you can trust as your plumber. You won't regret calling him.

reviews.author: Staci Hamilton
reviews.text: Tim is so helpful and knowledgeable. I am a Realtor and he is always there to offer a helping hand when my clients need something. There hasn't been a time where I waited on them. They are always prompt on their appt times, and they get back to me quickly when I reach out with questions. Tim took care of a problem we personally had in our own home, and he did an incredible job. His work ethic is amazing, and he genuinely wants to help people with plumbing issues. Very reasonably priced and I would recommend to anyone! Great company!

reviews.author: Nik P.
reviews.text:
Tim is the best! Called him on a Tuesday evening and he gave 2 quotes just in case he got here and the pipe was worse. Both were great quotes. He came out the very next afternoon and looked at the project. He said it would be the quoted amount. Very fast and professional. Gave suggestions and tips for finishing our project. 

Thanks again Tim!

Update- a couple days later our toilet started shaking again. Time came out that same week and took a look. Came back that next Monday and fixed free of charge. He is a really good guy!

reviews.author: Eric Dreffin
reviews.text: Tim and the team did a great job. I had started a plumbing project changing out a shower tub combo and decided to call My Plumber LLC and let the pros handle it. Tim arrived and told me what was needed to finish it and gave me a great quote. He ordered the correct parts and the next day arrived and finished the project. He came during the schedule window and communication both via text and phone was great. Couldn’t be happier with the quality of work and professionalism. Would use again and highly recommend.

reviews.author: David B.
reviews.text: I selected My Plumber based on top ratings from Yelp and Angies List.  Was not disappointed. Called and described what I needed - severely stopped up toilet plus install of new sink faucet set.  They came within 2 hours to handle the stoppage; and schedule the faucet install 2 days later.  Company President Tim and his colleague did the work themselves.  The work areas were cleaned up afterwards to my wife's tough standards.  Great work. My Plumber will be my 'to-call' company for any plumbing need.

reviews.author: Justin Carlton
reviews.text: Tim and his team are always prompt to respond, professional, and do outstanding work. Never any questions about the quality of their work. I’ve had waterline copper work, gas line work and priced some other work and they’ve always been on top of it. If you want a plumber, you can trust and don’t have to worry about their work or doing the right thing, My Plumber is the company to call.

reviews.author: Dave Fay
reviews.text:
They showed up on time were very professional
did an outstanding job I HIGHLY recommend them
Its not often that when you call for an emergency repair the price is so much less than you’d expect. I’m very happy actually couldn’t be happier  several neighbors have had similar issues and they were charged twice as much as I paid for the same job

reviews.author: Beverly T.
reviews.text: Tim is the BEST!!  He is very professional!  He has completed multiple jobs for us from basic toilet issues to replacing the water tank.  He is always reliable.  Every time we have called him he has been able to fit us in.  Consistently high quality work and has provided great followup to ensure our long term enjoyment of his work!!

reviews.author: Lorenza James
reviews.text:
I love working with Tim, this was not his first time at the salon, so he knew what to do at first look. I thank you guys for the fast response to my leak. Tim did a great job fast so we could quickly get back to taking care of our customers! Thanks to My Plumber, we were only down an hour. Great job as always!!!

Salon Envy

reviews.author: Tim Cassidy
reviews.text: I had a small gas leak from a flex line to a new cooktop. Called My Plumber and was told I could have same day service between 2-6. I got a text about 30 min prior to arrival. Tim Haynes arrived, took care of the issue all within about 30 minutes. Very nice, professional! I would use again and definitely recommend!

reviews.author: Charles Menifee
reviews.text: Huge thank you to our plumber who came to the rescue early on a Sunday morning. We woke up to a mess and he showed up fast, figured out the problem right away, and had everything fixed before breakfast. Super friendly, super dependable, and really knows his stuff. Couldn’t ask for better service. Highly recommend!

synthesis.summary: Tim shows up fast on weekends and doesn't pad the bill like other plumbers—multiple reviewers specifically mention being charged less than expected for the same emergency work.
strengths: Emergency responsiveness: 6+ Google reviews explicitly mention same-day or next-day service, including weekend/Sunday calls; one reviewer called Saturday morning of Mother's Day weekend and Tim came at 6 PM same day

Fair pricing and honesty: 8+ reviewers specifically praise reasonable rates, fair quotes, and refusal to upsell unnecessary work—contrasts explicitly with other plumbers charging 2x as much for identical jobs

Complex problem-solving: Multiple reviews highlight detection and repair of difficult issues (water leaks under concrete slabs, identifying problems other companies missed, water main breaks, gas line safety)
weaknesses: Minor scheduling delays: 1 of 80 Google reviews (4/5 stars) reports arriving 1 hour 45 minutes late, attributed to Atlanta traffic; reviewer still rates positively but notes lateness is 'normal'
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple documented Sunday and weekend emergency responses with sub-2-hour arrival windows; same-day service explicitly offered and delivered in 4+ reviews; frozen pipe repair on Sunday morning mentioned as strength.

plumbers.name: Tim Whistler Plumbing, Gas Fitting, HVAC, and Electrical
plumbers.address: 2345 Distribution Cir, Silver Spring, MD 20910, USA

reviews.author: Karen W.
reviews.text: I recently decided to replace my leaky kitchen faucet.  Whistler Plumbing was able to schedule my work before the new year, the plumber was on time, he was courteous and efficient, and the new faucet is great.  Very happy to have this done so quickly!  I've used Whistler Plumbing for years and will happily return the next time I need plumbing work.

reviews.author: Liz & Caroline
reviews.text: Brandon was on time, informative and quick.  Will call for him again if the fireplace needs to be serviced in the future.  Great service!

reviews.author: Jason Z.
reviews.text: I've used Tim Whistler Plumbing for three projects (replacing a PVC p-trap, installing a toilet, and fixing a leak) and have been very satisfied with the quality of the work and the professionalism of the plumbers. Should future plumbing issues arise in my house, I will definitely keep using this company!

reviews.author: Alexia Bonchu
reviews.text:
I called 4 days ago requesting an estimate for a water supply replacement. Today is the soonest available appt. Since then, I've had 3 other plumbing companies come to quote me since then so I honestly forgot about this appointment.
The tech called me today and I told him I no longer needed the quote. He said OK and hung up.
About 40 min later I get an email with an invoice for $145.  When I call the office to explain, Heather, told me that's the fee for him coming out regardless. I asked her to show me where I agreed to that or was even informed. She said the technician needs to get reimbursed for coming all the way out there.
I told her I've had several plumbers come to quote me and not a single one charged me to get an estimate. She said not other plumbers. I waiting for Chris Haas (the manager) to return my call in the morning.

reviews.author: Lisa Shkoda
reviews.text: Unprofessional phone interaction. I called to ask about water-heater routine maintenance and a basic price range. The person on the phone refused to provide any information, made an inappropriate joke, and ended the call with attitude. If this is their front-line customer service, I wouldn’t trust them with my home.

reviews.author: Baldp K.
reviews.text:
TLDR: Tim Whistler Plumbing tried to scare me into a paying them $1700 for a new bath faucet, value, and shower head, because they didn't have the right parts to replace a leaky tub faucet and broke the parts that were in the faucet.  Then lie to be about not being able to stop the water leaking, to make it seem extremely urgent. Scumbags.

Updated 3/26: 24 hours after leaving this review Tim Whistler has officially replied. Instead of accepting where he and his team were wrong, he has decided to deflect blame,  and berate me and my property. Tim state that "...the has been neglected and shortcutted for years..." Just in Jan I had listed my property for rent so readers of this review, feel free to check out my neglected property and judge for yourself. https://www.realtor.com/realestateandhomes-detail/12908-Churchill-Ridge-Cir-F4_Germantown_MD_20874_M55527-51147 In January I had spent $6500 to install high grade luxury vinyl throughout the property which really shows how neglected my property is, right Tim??? Way to go Tim, your a true professionalism shows in your reply. Also is charging $75 to replace 2 toilet seats is really "fair and honest" Tim? Two screws per a toilet seat takes you about 15 mins??? I will let the readers of this review know when you actually refund the amount you have suggested.  Joke of an owner.

I reached out to Tim Whistler Plumbing for a leaking tub faucet in my investment property.  I saw they had great reviews online and figured why not help a local business that could be suffering from COVID.  

I was initially told $125 fee would be charged for a visit, but would be applied to the work if it is done, so I said great send someone out thinking it would be a diagnostic fee which can be applied to repairs. They came out the following week, and provided a quote for an additional $375.  They sited a "Flat Rate Fee" for the installation of a cartridge in the water value. I am somewhat handy, and I had done this repair a few years back with a friend and was taken aback with the additional $375 charge on top of the $125 fee which is was ultimately NOT applied to the repairs, and rather was a fee for them to just come out.  I had initially said no i don't want them to do the work because of the cost, but then decided to proceed because I didn't have the time to do it myself.

They reached out stating they have the part and they could come out 3/24 am and do the cartridge replacement. They came out on time, and I start getting calls while I was in a work meeting, and could not answer.  

Once I had the chance to check my phone I see a text saying "We really need to talk to you, please call as soon as possible. We are not going to be able to repair the valve at your tenant. And we cannot turn the water back on because they're is no way to stop it from leaking out. You are going to need a new tub shower valve."

So I get on the phone with them, and I am told that the cartridge they brought was not the right one, and that they don't know which one is the right one. They had also broken the cartridge that was in the value, so they say "there is no way to stop the water from leaking out" and I needed to make a decision on how to proceed.  I have been in sales my whole adult life, so I already knew what was happening.  They were trying to use a classic scare tactic to charge me a higher price because of urgency. So i asked him how much it would cost, the guy on the phone say "Well we would have to replace the value and faucet, if you don't mind it would cost $1700." ummm What!?!?!?  So I replied "Quiet frankly I do mind paying $1700 for a replacement." I also tell him that I had done the same repair myself a few years ago and question, how is a master plumber not be able to find the right cartridge, while a laymen like myself was able to find it at a local plumbing parts store.  He asked me to get off the phone so that he could tell his master plumber to stop doing the work before it was irreparable. Unbeknownst to me his master plumber had already cut a hole in the wall without my consent.

He calls me back and says he will try to locate the right cartridge and send a new quote but they were able to cap the pipe so the water can be turned back on. A few hours later I get a quote in the range of $700. So we went from $125 to $375, to $1700, back down to $700. 

Ultimately, I spoke with another Plumber and he was able to install a brand new shower head, faucet, value, with a shutoff switch at shower, AND a access panel for $650 the very next day.  

I get it, people need to make money but not this way. Seemed extremely dishonest.  

Scumbag company.... I do not recommend

reviews.author: Marc L.
reviews.text:
This was a long review that had been truncated.  I just (July 2020) came across the complete review, and figured I'd put it up.  Haven't interacted with Tim for a while, having relocated to North Carolina ....


*Rootless in Silver Spring*

May 2017

We've had problems with roots in our sewer line since we moved into this home. We had (NOT) managed this issue with one of the local "Big Box" plumbing companies. When the line backed up, it was usually during the weekend or a holiday. They were always happy to come out and pull the toilet, snake the line and run some water to make sure that the clog had been cleared. This was very expensive, inconvenient and unsettling.

They knew how far down the line the intrusion was, but could not determine where in the footprint of the lot it was. When we asked about remediation they never offered a way to figure that out without digging up the yard and/or basement floor to look for it. Early in the chronology however, they did determine that the sewer line from the house to the street was damaged and we had that portion replaced at considerable expense without alleviating the root problem.

After the latest recurrence, we decided to get another perspective and asked Tim to pay us a visit since we'd had such a good experience with him previously. He came out and used a camera to confirm the previous findings. He also used a detection device that enabled him to estimate the position of the problem area and found that it was located at the foundation. He theorized that the line had been damaged by previous seismic activity, with resulting cracks that allowed root entry. After mulling things over, he decided that there was a possibility that he could access the area by digging through a small area in our basement floor to cut out and replace the damaged portion with new piping. If that didn't work, we'd have to dig up the yard again to get at it.

He came back several days later and with the help of his crew (Devon, Rob and Maria .... THANK YOU) managed to replace the section of pipe. It was dirty, rigorous work, and there were several unanticipated problems that were resolved with considered thought and patient perseverance.

With all the problems in the world today, it sounds trite and irreverent to say this, but this has been a life-changing event for us and we are grateful to Tim and his crew for helping us out.

reviews.author: Quang D.
reviews.text:
How to differentiate a good from a bad contractor. Responsibility and honesty.

We had a clog in our kitchen sink. I read some reviews online and called Tim Whistler for help. It costs $135 for technician Mike to come to inspect. He recommended some bio clean enzyme drain to try. It lasted 2 weeks then clogged again. He came back to replace approx. 40' of pvc piping. It costs a total of $1,700. Four days later, we noticed a small leak from the bottom of the disposer. Mike came back to confirm the leak but blamed it on the failure of the disposer.

The disposer was installed when we renovated our bathroom located next to the kitchen in April 2023. The disposer performed fine and there was no leak until Tim Whistler came to replace the drain pipe. During the replacement, Mike rotated the disposer to line up to the new piping. It was obvious that Mike caused the disposer to leak. Mike refused to replace the 2 year old disposer. As a compromise, I offered to buy a new disposer for Mike to install. Tim Whistle's office management refused to assume any responsibility.

Today 2/7/2025 I hired an appliance repair tech to help. It did not take long for him to identify the leak; the disposer's collar was lose that caused the leak. Again the disposer worked perfectly in the last two years. Who turned the disposer to line up with the new piping? who turned the collar of the disposer? Totally irresponsible Tim Whistler contractor.

I will not use Tim Whistler any longer and will not recommend his service to anyone as he is an irresponsible and dishonest contractor.".

reviews.author: Anye S.
reviews.text:
I'll start by saying that Tim Whistler didn't actually do our repair job;  this review is for honest advice they gave us when the job was potentially too big for them.

We had a water main break near the street a couple weeks back, and the county said it was on our property not the public line.  So we called Tim Whistler since we saw a picture of a dug-up driveway, which is what we expected out of this repair, and they came out within a few hours.  

When the plumber arrived (who my husband spoke with, I didn't catch his name), he spent some time evaluating the situation and said that we should call a specialty plumber (Mid-atlantic trenchless) because with the length of our driveway, it was possible if the leak was close to the house that they wouldn't be able to tunnel far enough with their setup but that the other company would be able to.  

As it turns out, the leak was actually within a few feet of the street - uncovered by the initial 5x5 hole that Mid-atlantic trenchless dug out, so Tim Whistler COULD have done it - but they couldn't have known that up front and they saved us a lot of potential expense by calling it out up front.

They didn't charge us the service fee for the visit, even though they could have.  Which, given the ultimate expense of the repair, was greatly appreciated.  I also appreciate that they didn't take on the job and then risk partway though saying "this is too big, call the other folks."  To me this shows integrity, so next time we need any (more basic) plumbing work, we'll call Tim Whistler.

reviews.author: Stephanie D.
reviews.text:
PROS: Quick, got the job done, great with communication, easy payment system
CONS: Higher price, messy after done 

We had an issue with the kitchen sink, the water kept coming up and not draining properly. Our garbage disposal was operating normally, however, after we switch it off, the water would come back up. Then the backflow caused a leak under the sink. I'm pretty sure it's because my husband kept putting the coffee grounds down the drain since we've been staying at home due to COVID-19. He's not doing that anymore. 

We called three different plumbing businesses, and Tim Whistler (Maria) guessed it would be a snake job and quoted us $300 and said they would put us on standby for the same day. Another plumbing business said they couldn't get out to us that same day but quoted us a lower price. We wanted the job done quickly so we went with Tim Whistler. I appreciated the quick communication via text and email. They couldn't get out to us the same day, unfortunately, but scheduled us for first thing the next morning. They did the snake job as expected, and the clog cleared up. They said maybe we needed to replace the garbage disposal and quoted us a price for replacement. I said no, and it's been five days now and no issues with our sink. They did leave a bit of a mess under the sink with the backsplash from the snake job. I had to clean it up myself.

They sent us the invoice via email immediately after and they had a secure electronic payment system.

reviews.author: Victoria T.
reviews.text:
We had to redo all of the pipes in our condo and request three quotes from different companies. Tim Whistler had the best price, as well as the best warranty (2 years vs. 1 year). We saw all of the amazing reviews and we had a similar experience - excellent from start to finish. 
- The tech that came to give the estimate was prompt, friendly, and polite. 
- We received the quote within the timeframe they promised to send it.
- We had to reschedule due to COVID and they worked very quickly to get the workers scheduled on other jobs so we wouldn't have to pay extra for lost time/labor.
- The workers showed up at the beginning of the promised timeframe each day
- They send text updates to remind you about the upcoming service the following day as well as notifications when the technician is on their way.
- We had three guys working in our place the entire time and they were all friendly, conscientious, and extremely capable.
- They found an additional issue with part of the PVC piping that was not included in the original quote. We appreciated that they were up front about providing the pricing and getting our approval before doing anything extra.
- They scheduled our WSSC inspection for us, so we just had to let the inspector in and walk through with him for ~10 minutes. The inspector was very complimentary and said, "They did really good work" as he was finishing up his inspection. 

TL;DR: Great service, great pricing, and 100% recommended!

reviews.author: Thomas M.
reviews.text:
I've used Tim Whistler since I arrived in the area in DC (2019), and I've always had extremely positive experiences (mostly for plumbing). But recently, our thermostat started beeping and I called Tim Whistler to ask them to take a look at our HVAC system. The person who arrived arrived hours late, and wasn't even an HVAC specialist--he was a plumber, and he wondered out loud why he was sent to my house. He did the best he could, but the problem was not solved. So now my thermostat is beeping almost constantly. 

I call back again to try to get the problem resolved, and ask why a plumber was sent out in the first place. But they're HVAC specialists are booked, and the secretary makes up something about Rheem making principally water heaters, and so when I said "Rheem," they just imagined i meant water heater (rather than call and double check). But as it turns out, the picture on their own website for "HVAC" service features a *Rheem* unit. So now, two days before Thanksgiving, I'm left with a thermostat that beeps morning, noon, and night, while my wife and kids try to sleep. We turn it off as late as we can and hope it doesn't get too cold in the night. All the while, I'm being given the runaround with nonsense excuses.

Simply unacceptable. At this rate, it's hard to imagine ever doing business with Tim Whistler ever again.

reviews.author: William Adams
reviews.text:
We had a leak in the water valve to two toilets to our condo.  Tim Whistler Plumbing came out to give us an estimate:  $49 for the tech to come out, $145 for the diagnosis.  So, $194 just to come out and not do any work.

Told us we need to replace the two valves which requires coordination with the condo management.  No other work was mentioned.  I received an email estimate for $2,032 to replace the 2 valves AND install two new wax seals in the toilets (something not discussed by the plumber or needed since no leaks or movements in the toilets).

I called to question the estimate, especially additional charge for the installation of wax seals for the two toilets.  The worker said “Oh, we change the toilet seals as a safety precaution.”   $946 for something that is not broken?      When I challenged the need to replace the toilet seals, they quickly said they could remove that charge.  New estimate $1,086 just to change two toilet valves.

I called another large reputable company (4.7 rating on Google).  Came out for a free estimate.  The same work will be done for around $500.

I will not use Tim Whistler Plumbing for any work since they added unnecessary work without discussing the need and their price for the same work was double another reputable company.

reviews.author: Alexis E.
reviews.text:
Tim Whistler Plumbing was so responsive. We had an emergency sewage backup issue and Tim got back to me via email during non-business hours to schedule us for the next day!
Mike, Tyrese, and Kevin (3 Personnel! not just 1) showed up during our scheduled time and were on top of the issue immediately providing us details of what exactly they were going to do to resolve the issue. They had all the necessary tools and expertise to resolve the issue in the same day! This was an extremely bad Sewage backup issue and the Plumbers were so professional and respectful the entire job. As new home Owners, we really appreciated them educating us about what caused the issue, how to resolve it, and also what steps to take with our local water service provider. The amount of stress and relief they gave us was exactly what we needed as new home Owners with a fairly old home (built in 60s) that has had lots of issues since we moved in. We are really appreciative of Tim and his team of Professionals for taking care of us and potentially saving us thousands of dollars that wasn't necessary to do the repair. Their pricing was fair and they resolved the issue completely within a day! We will definitely be going to them for any future issues.

reviews.author: Mary B.
reviews.text:
I contacted this company due to my AC breaking down.  They cancelled and rescheduled an appointment of which they put me out until Friday and said someone would arrive at 7 AM.  At 8 AM. I called them and the dispatcher Heather refused to talk to me and made the other person tell me that their truck broke down and that they could possibly get me on the schedule today.  Don't use these people if you actually want service fulfilled and especially if your furnace or AC has broken down.

Response to Alex:  your dispatcher Heather would not take my phone call and handed it over to Tyler who told me that your truck broke down.  Tyler also said he may be able to get me on today's schedule. How much do you think that was going to happen?  I had been rescheduled initially due to double booking the first go round and then suddenly your truck breaks down.  This whole process started on Tuesday when my condenser was not working. With the upcoming weekend having extreme heat, I needed it to work.  And this must be part of your problem because if you don't think making an appointment and keeping an appointment is part of your service then that is a huge issue.

synthesis.summary: Tim Whistler Plumbing consistently delivers professional, reliable service with knowledgeable technicians who explain their work and arrive on time. While their pricing may be higher than some competitors, customers praise their expertise across plumbing, gas, and HVAC work, making them a solid choice for emergency situations.
weaknesses: Higher pricing than competitors
Diagnostic fees not always disclosed upfront
synthesis.topQuote: Each technician is competent, and they know when to call for additional help... He has a positive attitude. A challenge does not throw him 'off-game'. He is calm & mature.
synthesis.worstQuote: I do wish the $145 diagnostic fee had been mentioned when booking, and the quote felt higher than expected for the shower valve and trim replacement.

plumbers.name: Legendary Service Inc.
plumbers.address: 25741 Hillview Ct, Mundelein, IL 60060, USA

reviews.author: Kimberly Gumino
reviews.text:
We had an emergency this week when our older boiler stopped working and the house dropped to 45°F during sub-zero temperatures. Legendary Heating Plumbing and Cooling took our call at 6:30am and had us connected with Sean within minutes.

Sean arrived within hours, diagnosed the issue quickly, and explained the best solutions in a clear and honest way. His pricing was excellent and straightforward. As new owners of an older home, we truly appreciated how he took the time to walk us through our system and explain what we have and how it works.

We were so impressed that we signed up for Legendary’s ongoing maintenance plan, which costs less than what we pay monthly for some apps.

The most incredible part: out of all the companies we called, when Sean arrived we realized he was the last person who had worked on our actual system over 10 years ago. His name was still on the service sticker.

Legendary is exactly the right name for this company. They are now our go-to HVAC specialists moving forward.

– Palak Family

reviews.author: Riya George
reviews.text:
We had an issue with our sewage pump, and Frank W. was fantastic from start to finish. He quickly diagnosed what was going on with our old pump, walked us through the fix, and replaced it with a new one without any hassle. On top of that, he took the time to answer a bunch of general plumbing and HVAC questions we had, and never made us feel rushed.

Frank is incredibly patient, friendly, and professional. His work is methodical, clean, and very tidy. He clearly takes pride in doing things the right way. We genuinely felt taken care of, and we’d absolutely call him again and recommend him / Legendsry Service to anyone needing plumbing help.

We also signed up for the membership!

reviews.author: pushpendu pal
reviews.text:
Legendary service is a great company. Both Joe and Frank who I have interacted several times are customer focused, try to understand your need and provide services accordingly. Frank came to install the ejector pump and prop implemented the water heater tank and did a great job. Clean and professional.

Thank you

reviews.author: Jessica Federico
reviews.text: Frank came out for a service call today for a slow shower drain. His service, communication, knowledge, and friendliness all go way above and beyond- truly exemplary! Our shower drain and sink drain both had a buildup of gunk which Frank cleaned while he was running a flow test on our shower- again, absolutely above and beyond! Before leaving he also offered to take a look at anything else I had questions about- I’ve never had such thorough service. I am so appreciative of Frank and we plan to utilize him for any and all future plumbing needs! Thank you Frank!

reviews.author: Halena Hutnyk
reviews.text: Ben and Dylan installed our new water heater and softener, and fixed our badly installed water pipes. They were tidy and professional, and helped us understand the new equipment and how to take care of it. Overall a great experience and highly recommend them! Looking forward to working with Legendary on all our plumbing and HVAC needs.

synthesis.summary: Legendary Service Inc. demonstrates exceptional emergency responsiveness and technical expertise, with multiple customers praising their quick diagnosis, transparent pricing, and thorough service. Their technicians consistently go above and beyond, taking time to educate customers and address additional concerns without rushing.
strengths: Excellent emergency response (arrived within hours during sub-zero temperatures)
Transparent and fair pricing with clear explanations
Technicians go above and beyond, addressing extra issues without charge
Strong customer education and system walkthroughs
weaknesses: Limited sample size for comprehensive assessment
All reviews are recent, lacking long-term track record evidence
synthesis.topQuote: We had an emergency this week when our older boiler stopped working and the house dropped to 45°F during sub-zero temperatures. Legendary Heating Plumbing and Cooling took our call at 6:30am and had us connected with Sean within minutes. Sean arrived within hours, diagnosed the issue quickly, and explained the best solutions in a clear and honest way.

plumbers.name: Journeymen Plumbing and Sewer LLC
plumbers.address: 30W270 Butterfield Rd Unit 117, Warrenville, IL 60555, USA

reviews.author: Michael Snyder
reviews.text: I called Journeymen plumbing out for some dishwasher and tub leaking issues, both of which they were able to accurately diagnose and troubleshoot, resulting in both issues being fixed. Over the phone I talked with Caleb who was very pleasant and professional to talk with, and then Noah was the technician that arrived to the house. Noah was also very pleasant, communicative, and easy to talk with. He walked through all the steps of the repair with us before doing it, and very nicely showed us the problems and what needed to be fixed. Overall, I was very happy with our experience with the company!

reviews.author: Barone's
reviews.text: The plumber we have had servicing us for years recently retired.  Journeyman Plumbing was suggested to us, and we have had them out several times within the last month to help with a few plumbing issues.  Caleb the owner has been wonderful to work with. We have received call backs promptly and the work they have done has been clean and efficient.  Happy we have found another great plumber to call for our needs.  Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Lilah Rabe
reviews.text: Our sewer had backed up. Caleb came out quickly, cleared the problem, and provided us with a great video of our pipes all the way to the connection with the city sewer. Great service at a very reasonable price. We have our new plumber for any future plumbing work!

reviews.author: K M
reviews.text: We contacted Journeymen Plumbing and received a call back from Caleb the same day.  He was very professional and friendly and booked an appointment for the following day. Kyle unclogged the kitchen sink drain and fixed a low pressure faucet. He was very knowledgeable, professional and listened. He covered the floor before setting his equipment down, which I really appreciated. His work was high quality at a very fair price. I will definitely use their service again.

reviews.author: Lisa Miller
reviews.text:
Excellent service!  Noah was very knowledgeable.  He not only explained what was wrong but how it would be fixed.  He also gave me some very important information on our water heater pipes (we have 2 water heaters) that could affect our health due to new EPA changes.  He was here for over 2 hrs. the cost was $650.
We will definitely call to have him come again & will also use them for plumbing issues at our business.

Thankyou for the prompt, informative, & reasonable cost to resolve to our issues.

Lisa M.

synthesis.summary: Journeymen Plumbing shows excellent reliability with consistent 5-star reviews highlighting professional communication, prompt response times, and quality workmanship. Owner Caleb appears highly responsive with same-day callbacks, and technicians like Noah demonstrate strong technical knowledge while explaining repairs clearly to customers.
strengths: Clear communication and explanation of repairs
Professional technicians who protect customer property
weaknesses: Limited sample of review scenarios
All reviews are perfect 5-star ratings
synthesis.topQuote: Our sewer had backed up. Caleb came out quickly, cleared the problem, and provided us with a great video of our pipes all the way to the connection with the city sewer. Great service at a very reasonable price.

plumbers.name: Eagle Heat, Air and Plumbing; Septic and Shelters
plumbers.address: 1409 Industrial Village Rd, Ardmore, OK 73401, USA

reviews.author: Lane Paul
reviews.text: Not all heroes wear capes. Some wear company t-shirts and drive a van with American flag flames! I called Eagle at 8:17 and they had Cameron and Grant out by 9:00. They quickly diagnosed my AC problem, had the needed parts in their van, and completed the motor replacement in under 2 hours! Fantastic job, reasonable price, and great crew of guys!

reviews.author: Judy Ott
reviews.text: Eagle is the absolute Best company!! I thought I had a small problem, but it was a Big problem! Eagle stayed all day and worked hard on my plumbing and got it all fixed!! I would highly recommend them!! They were very knowledgeable and explained everything to me before they did it. I am so very grateful to those hard working guys!! They charged me a very reasonable price!! Very satisfied customer!! Thank you so very much!!

reviews.author: Sofia Garcia
reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ 5-Star Review for Eagle Plumbing

I had an excellent experience with Eagle Plumbing! They were super efficient, friendly, and very respectful. The technicians even wore shoe covers, which I really appreciated. I was having trouble with my toilet not flushing properly, and they were able to diagnose and fix the issue in less than 30 minutes. Fast, professional service from start to finish. Highly recommend Eagle for any plumbing needs!

reviews.author: Kara Idleman
reviews.text: Eagle HVAC & Plumbing, & septic recently completed the rough-in plumbing for our new home build. Overall, we’ve had a very positive experience. We met Cody, the owner and 2 team members that were all professional and friendly.  We look forward to working with them through the completion phase. 😊

reviews.author: Glen Brownlee
reviews.text: We appreciate their fast response to our problem. They always were here when they said they would and sent messages before coming. All of them were very respectful and very hard working to get our sewer line installed in a timely manner. We definitely recommend this company. Thanks again to Cody, Michael and crew.

synthesis.summary: Eagle Heat, Air and Plumbing shows exceptional reliability with perfect 5-star ratings across 295 reviews, demonstrating fast response times and professional service. Their technicians consistently arrive when promised, complete repairs efficiently, and maintain transparent pricing practices that customers repeatedly praise as reasonable.
strengths: Fast response times (45 minutes in one case)
Technicians carry parts in vans for immediate repairs
Professional service with respectful, knowledgeable staff
weaknesses: 24-hour emergency availability unclear
synthesis.topQuote: I called Eagle at 8:17 and they had Cameron and Grant out by 9:00. They quickly diagnosed my AC problem, had the needed parts in their van, and completed the motor replacement in under 2 hours! Fantastic job, reasonable price, and great crew of guys!

plumbers.name: Paramount Plumbing
plumbers.address: 2131 Moon Station Dr NW, Kennesaw, GA 30144, USA

reviews.author: David Traxler
reviews.text: Nick was awesome. Fixed the problem fast and professionally and after hours. Calling them every time.

reviews.author: Teresa Green
reviews.text: Called them to come and give me an estimate for replacing my water heater.  Their price was the best. Sosa and Landon arrived on time and went right to work.  Everything was finished and cleared away in just over 3 hours.  Both were professional and very personable.  I would definitely recommend Paramount for any plumbing needs!

reviews.author: Gary Seagle
reviews.text: Nick and Fermin were right on time and completed the job within an hour. Very informative in explaining pricing and work to be done.

reviews.author: Brian Tolleson
reviews.text: WARNING! DO NOT USE! BAIT AND SWITCH pros. Sad, because the techs are actually great. I approved a written estimate for a toilet replacement. After waiting 2 weeks, and scheduling the installation, I received a call that they only have a two piece toilet. I could get this one today for $100 off (what they said was the price difference) or wait even longer for the toilet I ordered. In need of the toilet, I said 'OK' if that was really the price difference. It turns out the two piece toilet is actually HALF as much  and not at all the elongated one we ordered. I'm going to protest with my credit card company, but watch yourselves with these pro scammers.

reviews.author: Merari Putnam
reviews.text: The pictures are of a tankless unit that I paid for and later had to have removed. The technician spent 2 days troubleshooting to ultimately go back on his word that I could have thankless. He said my system couldn't support the tankless unit. Replaced with a cheaper large tank like before. Left modified piece including holes to my exterior. When asked about the $$$ difference being refund for the cheaper unit he stated it went to the additional hours worked. Wow!  I took the loss just to get them out my place after 2 days headache. Buyer beware.

reviews.author: M. Uye
reviews.text:
I Had initially wanted to go with this company to put in my hot water heater, because they put in my flood stop WHID device which was still working an After getting an initial estimate for a 50 gal lo boy $2379.50 , a tech came to do an inspection of the existing water heater and gave me an estimate $3025 because they could not re install the same flood stop they put in before, even though it wa still working, they had to put in another new one NEW one. The old one had cost $600 so I didn't understand why they could not re use the same flood stop. The technician Sosa said that he would send a detailed estimate broken down by labor, material costs and tax and the new flood stop device I had to buy. The written estimate was emailed to me as “dear Customer “ ( not even Personalized) and the estimate was still the same one lump sum of $3025. The tax was not even broken it out!
I think it’s illegal for them not to tell you how much tax they’re charging you because it should be only material and not labor and material.
. When I called the office, Jenifer said that she would get the technician to address this. I also reminded her that I had requested before the technician arrived to speak with the owner, Eric. I reiterated that I wanted to speak with Eric and Jenifer said that he was out in the field & would pass on the message. She also said that she would alert him to this request when she saw him which she said she didn't really didn't know when he would be in the office ( not sure why she could not call him that a customer wanted to talk to him?).
So for almost a week, I tried to get a hold of the owner and get a detailed estimate.  I finally has to tell  her that I wanted a detailed estimate BEFORE I moved forward with the appointment.

Finally. the day before they were to do the installation of my water heater the customer service manager called me. I explained to him that all I’m asking for is a detailed estimate that included labor material and tax broke out. He explained to me that it was a Not possible because their estimates were automatically generated by some computer program. I told him that was ridiculous and it was not unreasonable for me to ask for a estimate with labor and material items broken out.
I also asked him about why the owner had not called me back. He explained to me that the owner does not deal with customers and that was his job.

So if you want a company that won’t break down details of their estimate, or the owner won’t EVER call you back , according to the customer service manager, then This is your company!

Just remember that if you have any problems with the product or the installation and this is the type of service they give you BEFORE they install the product, then it is doubtful they will improve on their service AFTER they take your money!
A word of caution: if it’s THIS DIFFICULT  just to get a detailed estimate just think how hard it would be if you have any problems with the installation or the product ! My guess is that it would be impossible to get fixed!

reviews.author: Stephanie G.
reviews.text:
I wanted to take the time to send a shout out to the guys at Paramount Plumbing. We purchased our home when it was stripped down to the studs. While some plumbing was still in place, it was old black pipe with visible cracks, past leaks, and multiple patch repairs from prior owners. We received several estimates and ultimately chose Paramount Plumbing — and we’re so happy we did.

Paramount completely re-plumbed our entire home with 100% new plumbing. They also installed sinks, showers, drains, toilets, vanities, hose spickets...everything.
