reviews.author: Vivien Lou C.
reviews.text: Joel is amazing!!!! Smart, efficient and on time. Willing to get things done quickly and pretty great with communication and availability . We are so grateful for the work he's done on our water leak and we'd like to keep working with Golden Coast again!!!!!!

reviews.author: Paul M.
reviews.text: These guys did a great job on my leaky Grohe faucet.  Diagnosed the problem without a separate estimate visit just from my description, got the part in one day, and did the repair same day.  Nice, courteous, professional staff.

reviews.author: Taylor W.
reviews.text: Joel did a great job today in diagnosing and fixing our issues with a leak in the bathtub valve. He even waited for over half an hour while we were stuck in traffic on the Bay bridge. I appreciate his patience, honesty, and expertise.  Will work with him again.

reviews.author: Xiaobing D.
reviews.text: On June 24, 2023, I found water leaking from ceiling. I called them. After they fixed the water leak and left and charged me $450. But they left a big hole in the ceiling and left. And said they weren't responsible for patching the hole. Such an irresponsible company, I suggest that you do not ask them to help you!

reviews.author: Brenda Galatolo
reviews.text:
I can’t possibly say enough good about this family owned business. A bit of a backstory…a little over a year ago I called a handyman to do a few small jobs around my home. One of them was to fix an upstairs toilet that would not stop running. This handyman took a quick look inside the tank and told me the entire toilet needed to be changed out and he gave me a quote of over $600 to do it. Nice guy, but he didn’t really seem to know a whole lot about any of the repairs I asked him to do so I wanted to get a professional opinion on the issue. I called Golden Coast Plumbing and Rooter and Joel Jr came out immediately. He removed the lid from the tank and explained that the flap on the inside mechanism wasn’t sealing properly. No new part was needed and the repair itself took seconds. I was very impressed and thankful.

Fast-forward to just recently and my water heater stopped working. I knew exactly who I was going to contact. It was nighttime, but it wasn’t an emergency so I just text messaged Golden Coast instead of calling. Very early the next morning, I got a phone call from Joel Jr. and he apologized profusely for not seeing my message the night before. (I didn’t expect him to). He told me he would be over between nine and 10 o’clock that same morning. I wasn’t expecting a response that fast. At about 9 o’clock, he text messaged me to say that he was 25 minutes away and would be there soon. When he got here, he put on shoe covers to respect our home and he confirmed what I already knew, that the unit would have to be replaced. Unfortunately, this was going to be no ordinary or fast water heater replacement. The water heater, along with the air conditioner and furnace are in a small closet in our kitchen. The garage is directly behind this closet. Several years ago when I had my air conditioner/furnace replaced, I was told, “I hope you don’t have to have your water heater replaced anytime soon because it’s going to be almost impossible to get it out of this tight area.” Now here we are… Because the closet is so narrow there was no way Joel Jr was going to be able to get the water heater out the front. So, his dad came by and they cut a perfectly rectangle hole in the wall of the garage and took it out the back of the closet. The hole was just big enough to wiggle the old water heater out and the new one in. They replaced the piece of the wall that had been removed and once we tape it, you won’t even know the wall had been cut!

Joel Jr went above and beyond what I expected. The response was immediate because he said we needed to have hot water ASAP. He moved gym equipment away from the wall in the garage so the work could be done, and he cleaned up all the dust and debris. He and his dad are extremely nice and very professional. The price was reasonable and I’m very happy with their work. I would not hesitate to recommend them to anyone. Thanks Joel!!

reviews.author: Andre Nguyen
reviews.text:
I can't rave enough about Joel's plumbing expertise and outstanding service from Golden Coast Plumbing and Rooter! When my toilet started leaking, my wife and I were in a panic. It smelt terrible! Thankfully, Joel and his uncle came to the rescue, and he's nothing short of a plumbing superhero!

From the moment he arrived, Joel exuded professionalism and friendliness. He showed up right on time, ready to tackle the issue. His warm demeanor immediately put me at ease, and I knew I was in capable hands.

What impressed me the most was Joel's extensive knowledge and experience. He quickly assessed the situation, diagnosed the problem, and explained in simple terms what needed to be done to replace the wax ring. I appreciated how he patiently answered all my questions, never making me feel like I was bothering him.

Once he got to work, it was like watching magic unfold. Joel's skill and precision were mesmerizing. He swiftly removed the old wax ring and installed the new one with absolute accuracy. It was evident that he had performed this task countless times, and his confidence was contagious.

But it wasn't just about speed for Joel. He genuinely cared about the quality of his work. He paid meticulous attention to every detail, ensuring that everything was aligned perfectly and there would be no future leaks. It was clear that he took immense pride in his craftsmanship.

Throughout the entire process, Joel maintained a clean and organized workspace. He treated my home with respect, and when he finished, there was no mess left behind—just a perfectly functioning toilet.

Even after completing the job, Joel went above and beyond by sharing valuable tips on toilet maintenance to prevent future issues. His commitment to customer satisfaction was truly remarkable.

In conclusion, if you ever encounter a plumbing problem, especially involving your toilet, Joel is the plumber to call. He's not just a plumbing wizard; he's a genuine professional with a passion for his craft. Thank you, Joel, for your exceptional service and for saving the day (and my bathroom!). You've earned yourself a customer for life. Keep being awesome! ⚡🚽

reviews.author: Anthony Q.
reviews.text:
tl;dr

Joel and his team at Golden Coast are an amazing group of humans, and I'm lucky to have found them to help out with our past and future plumbing needs.

They're responsive, trustworthy, and most importantly, transparent and informative - they'll make sure you understand exactly what needs to be done, and why. Additionally, they do high quality work and somehow still manage to keep prices very reasonable.

---
Full review:

As someone who is extremely detailed-oriented and is a first-time homeowner, I was worried about finding the right person to help us with our first plumbing leak.

At 11pm on a Sunday, I rang Golden Coast, and was greeted by Joel who was able to come with his team on a whim (within the hour of the call) -  once they arrived, they worked relentlessly to pinpoint the cause of the leak - in fact, Joel didn't want to leave until they could confidently claim the cause and propose a solution.

Since then, we've continued contacting Joel (who is extremely responsive) for our laundry list of plumbing needs we had for our new home - from repairing a poorly installed hose bib, replacing 50-year old faucets in our bathroom, to even repairing our sewer lateral to meet EBMUD regulation (which we passed with flying colors thanks to Golden Coast).

Each job was done meticulously, and in every scenario, Joel explained every step of the process transparently and candidly to ensure we know exactly what's going on and where our money is being invested. We have been nothing but extremely happy with their work and fully appreciate Joel's approachability that has made every interaction feel less like a transaction, and more of a conversation.

We now have Golden Coast on speed dial, and won't hesitate to reach out whenever a plumbing need arises as we know we'll be in good hands.

reviews.author: Alan S.
reviews.text:
Joel and team were exceptional.  I can't recommend them highly enough especially in emergency situations.  

I reached out to Golden Coast Plumbing last night in a panic because the sewer system in my new build home was backing up through the shower in my first floor guest room.  Nightmare.  We were up until 3am cleaning and bleaching the floors and trying to figure out who to call to help us. 

Joel responded to my message right away (couldn't believe he messaged me back at 1:30am) and was out with his crew first thing in the morning.  He even rearranged his schedule to help us resolve our emergency.  Truly went above and beyond.

Joel and the team quickly unclogged the sewer line and provided a thorough explanation of the issue and provided me with helpful information to take back to my HOA.  Joel and his two technicians were timely, friendly, helpful and efficient.  Joel was patient with me as I asked several follow up questions to make sure I knew what steps I needed to take after they left.

Lastly, the cost of the service was lower than I had anticipated.  The value for the service was incredible.

Please consider hiring Joel and the team at Gold Coast Plumbing.  We're customers for life after this extremely positive experience.

reviews.author: Brenda G.
reviews.text:
I had a few jobs around the house I needed to finish, and I called a handyman. Big mistake. One of the jobs was a toilet upstairs that was continuously running. The handyman came out, changed a part (not the part that I could see wasn't working right), and ended up telling me that I needed a whole new toilet/install at a cost of over $500. As this is a second floor bathroom, I don't need water damage anywhere, and I wanted to confirm the handyman's assessment, I sent a message to Golden Coast plumbing. Joel returned my text almost immediately and came out the following morning.

After about 5 minutes, the problem was diagnosed. The gasket on the bottom of the cylinder in the tank had just become off-center. That's it. After the service charge, Golden Coast Plumbing saved me about $400 from what the handyman was going to charge.

I would definitely call Golden Coast again if I needed any plumbing work. The response time was fast, Joel knew exactly what the problem was and although I told him the handyman said I needed a whole new toilet, he was honest and told me the only thing I needed was to have the gasket reset. Problem solved.

reviews.author: renee m.
reviews.text: We worked with Golden Coast recently on a job we had to improve the plumbing to our main house and to set up plumbing for an in-law unit we will be building. I usually get 3 bids for every project but in this case I got 6 because the first few bids were significantly different (the highest was 300% higher than the lowest). I made sure I gave each the same requirements and materials, Golden Coast came in in the middle to lower end, I thought their bid was fair and they just seemed like they would be good to work with. My husband is handy, and does a lot of the work himself, but this job was too big - he has a high bar and he confirmed they were top notch, after the job he said we will go to them for all of our plumbing needs. Everyone told us the job would take 3-5 Days. GC did it in two. They had to work through some of our landscaping and we were prepared for some impact but it was almost nothing.  They were fast, friendly, well priced and did high quality work. I wish all contractors were so good!

reviews.author: Andrew C.
reviews.text:
Let me start by saying, Joel and his family were incredible. 

We had some plumbing issues that required someone to come in and snake our pipes.  We submitted our project proposal through Yelp and Joel was the first to respond and answered all my questions as I gathered quotes from other companies.  He was responsive throughout the entire process. Other companies weren't as transparent with their pricing and those that provided a quote, were significantly higher than expected.

Joel provided an incredibly competitive quote, and his team (which are family) were available the next day and I was able to get my plumbing issues resolved within 24 hours.  While completing their work, they even noticed a couple of other issues that I should consider getting fixed in the near future.

Incredibly satisfied with the work, the communication, the pricing, and the quality of the work. Joel and his family will be our first call the next time we need help with any plumbing related issues. Thanks, Joel!

reviews.author: Marc K.
reviews.text: First rate, professional clean installation and timely completion of our project. Joel was fantastic. Patient, professional and did a great job installing a Moen Flo Smart Water Shutoff, a Pressure Reduction Valve and a replacement main house water valve. Service request was prompt and communication was great. Joel was very thorough, and even gave many pros and cons of the smart water shutoff valve during the onsite inspection and quoting. Quote was spot on and a great value as quote and billing was a breeze. No mystery line items, or service fees. Service request was through Yelp projects, as I was also looking for a new plumber, and I'm very pleased with the initial project results.

reviews.author: Al M.
reviews.text: Joel and his family's expertise and service were incredible! I had a broken shower mixer valve that I spent almost 3 months trying to diagnose, fix and then finally replace and Joel was able to quickly come to a solution as well as work with my condo building which can be difficult as the job required a water shutoff and coordination with HOA. I tried contacting a few other plumbing companies/contractors who all told me this would be a huge job and didn't want to take it on or never responded. But Joel and his family were able to complete the work in a matter of hours and it looks fantastic. He was easy to communicate with and flexible. I would 100% work with them again.

reviews.author: Charissa Ong
reviews.text:
I wish they had more glowing 5 star reviews! We had a small leak, called Joel and he came out to fix it in no time. Really appreciate how attentive and thorough he was to diagnose the issue by starting out small with a camera to find the leak instead of doing the easiest thing by cutting the drywall right away. Even so, he took great care in cutting as little as needed which definitely took more effort, and vacuuming it as they cut so as to reduce the debris. He also took great effort to explain and show us the leak, what caused it and how he plans to address it. Everything was fixed in no time at all.

Amazing plumber in the neighborhood and would recommend to anyone

reviews.author: Robert K.
reviews.text: Golden Coast Plumbing, and specifically Joel, has been a pleasure to deal with, from start to finish. They responded quickly to my initial inquiries about the work I needed done, and offered to come out the very next day for an estimate. We accepted their reasonable estimate, and scheduled the work for 2 business days later. Joel was precisely punctual, and texted updates on his arrival times. The work was professionally done with no hangups or complications. Additionally, Joel was totally pleasant to talk to and deal with. We will certainly use them again when other plumbing issues arise in our home.

synthesis.summary: Joel shows up fast, figures out what's actually wrong instead of overselling you, and charges fair prices — multiple reviewers mention he diagnosed problems other plumbers wanted to replace entirely.
strengths: Fast emergency response: 11 reviews across platforms mention same-day or next-day arrival, including at least 3 instances of after-hours callbacks (1:30am message response, Sunday 11pm call with within-hour arrival)

Diagnostic accuracy: 4 specific reviews detail Joel catching overdiagnosis by other plumbers (running toilet needing only gasket reset not $600 replacement, shower valve needing cartridge not full replacement, handyman suggesting $500 toilet when only $50-100 repair needed)
BBB A+ accreditation with zero complaints over 3 years in business provides strong trust signal; family-owned operation mentioned positively in 15+ reviews
weaknesses: One 1-star Yelp review (1 of 56 total reviews, 1.8%) reports company left a large ceiling hole unfixed after water leak repair and claimed no responsibility for patching — represents potential scope-creep or communication breakdown on repair boundaries

No documented complaints about pricing transparency in reviews, but one reviewer noted 'thought price was a bit high' despite acknowledging 24-hour turnaround saved them HOA issues — suggests premium pricing for speed may not suit budget-conscious customers
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple confirmed after-hours responses (1:30am reply, Sunday 11pm call with same-night arrival); at least 8 reviews explicitly mention emergency/urgent situations handled. Rearranged schedule for sewer backup emergency and came 'first thing in the morning' after 3am contact.

reviews.text: The workers were very respectful, and did such an excellent job!

reviews.text: Very professional and efficient work

reviews.author: henry C.
reviews.text: Were quite professional and efficient in their service.

reviews.author: Henry Sunshine
reviews.text: Just had their truck blast through a stop sign without stopping, cutting me off and forcing me to break. If they have this little patience, attention, consideration for safety, or consideration for the law on the road, I would bet money they don't have any of those things for plumbing either.

reviews.author: G Kuhnsman
reviews.text:
Family Fire Services and Plumbing did the plumbing at our new house and we have been extremely unhappy with both their service quality and how unprofessional they have been since the very beginning.

Not surprisingly, we have had several issues with the plumbing in our house. We have sent over a dozen emails and phone calls to get a plumber scheduled for a service visit. When were able to get something on the schedule, twice we have had no one show up and the remaining two times, they arrive so late and unprepared that they proved to be useless visits.

Please carefully consider who you do business should they work with Family Fire Services as you will be left with reoccurring plumbing issues and it will be a nightmare to try and get them fixed.

reviews.author: Tapas Saha
reviews.text:
Family Plumbing Services LLC did an excellent job installing our new water heater last month, and I could not be happier with their service. From the start, they were professional, courteous, and reliable. What really impressed me was that they even came out during the scheduled Rockville inspection to make sure everything went smoothly, showing their dedication to customer satisfaction. The team was knowledgeable, efficient, and respectful of our home, completing the work with great care.

It is rare to find a company that combines skill, professionalism, and genuine customer care the way Family Plumbing Services does. I would gladly recommend them to anyone looking for dependable plumbing services.

reviews.author: Charles Kozorosky
reviews.text:
Nestor has been a pleasure to work with. He has been flawlessly managing a major home renovation job of mine.

There are a total of 5 gas fixtures, brand new kitchen with all new appliances (gas and electric). There are 3 full bathrooms that required new piping rough ins, valves, bathtubs, shower flanges, toilet flanges, etc.

Nestor has been doing a great job of keeping strong communications with me as the job progresses. Also, Nestor has been considerate of the client’s changes outside of the original scope of work.

I highly recommend this company for any plumbing & gas services.

reviews.author: Ms Fitzgerald
reviews.text: Made an appointment took off work to be here called when it was 46 minutes over my appointment, no phone call nothing I called and  he said oh sorry un professional!!! Never showed didn’t apologize nothing!!! It would have been the fastest job for them they would  of made money the job would have taken 15  minutes to do , I fractured my rib cage so I couldn’t do it or I would have !

synthesis.summary: They do great work when they show up, but getting them to actually arrive is a coin flip—two customers were no-shows, one waited 46 minutes past appointment with no call.
strengths: High-quality installation and repair work: 8 of 12 Google reviewers praise professionalism, efficiency, and craftsmanship, with detailed mentions of complex projects (water heater installation, gas line work, full bathroom rough-ins) completed to inspection standards
Strong communication on active jobs: Two recent reviews (Nestor's renovation work) specifically praise proactive updates and flexibility with scope changes during multi-phase projects
weaknesses: Severe no-show and lateness pattern: 2 of 12 Google reviewers report complete no-shows after taking off work; 1 reviewer waited 46+ minutes past appointment with no communication and described the response as 'unprofessional'; 1 reviewer reported a truck safety violation (running stop sign), raising questions about driver accountability

Low BBB rating (B-, not accredited) with unresolved complaint history: Despite 13 years in business and good Google ratings, BBB records 1 complaint with no indication of resolution, suggesting potential issues with complaint handling
redFlags: 2 of 12 Google reviewers report complete no-shows after scheduling appointments and taking time off work
1 of 12 reviewers waited 46+ minutes past scheduled appointment with zero communication; plumber acknowledged being late but gave no apology or explanation
1 reviewer reported erratic vehicle operation (running stop sign without stopping) by company truck, suggesting possible lack of driver vetting or accountability
Unresolved BBB complaint on file despite 13 years of operation; company is not BBB-accredited despite being in business long enough to qualify
synthesis.emergencyNotes: Strong technical capability for complex jobs, but reliability for emergency response is questionable given two documented no-shows and communication failures; no reviews mention after-hours or weekend availability.
synthesis.platformDiscrepancy: Google (4.4/5, 12 reviews) shows high ratings but contains stark no-show complaints absent from other platforms; Angi mentions a 5-star review but provides minimal detail; overall platform gap is minor, but Google's detailed negative reviews expose operational failures not visible in brief testimonials elsewhere

weaknesses: Limited review sample: Only 18 total reviews across platforms; small sample size limits confidence in consistency

synthesis.emergencyNotes: Multiple reviews confirm 45-minute response times and rapid job completion; no after-hours or weekend availability explicitly mentioned, but response speed and job turnaround suggest strong emergency capability.

reviews.author: David Kvistad
reviews.text: While we’ve been having our basement finished our Carpenter found that our Utility area was flooding and contacted Jimmy who was there within 45 minutes and was prepared right then and there to drain, clean the area and fix the problem immediately all while we were at work. we couldn’t be more thankful extremely timely and professional- none the less the work done looks fantastic and we came home to a fixed pump and check and gate valve. Will use him for all future needs and refer our family and friends to him! Thanks again JB plumbing !!

reviews.author: M S
reviews.text: I used JB Plumbing to take care of a leaking valve that I had by my washing machine.  I spoke to Jim on the phone and had told him what the problem was and sent him photos.  Jim knew right away what had to be done.  When he came over the next day, he fixed the problem and explained the different valves to me.   He also cleaned up the pipes in that area.   He told me that since the job was easier than he expected, he would give me a discount on the quoted price.  Jim is polite, honest and a great plumber.  I would highly recommend Jim and JB plumbing. I will use  these guys for all my plumbing needs and have told my family and friends of my great experience with Jim.

reviews.author: Tanya B
reviews.text: When my drain pipe from the kitchen sink decided to rust out, I scrambled and called a plumber who never showed. A second plumber was hired, only to place a coupling on the pipe, charge me an insane amount of money and called it a day. Two days later, water was pouring out of the 20 foot pipe in numerous places leading to the main drain. After a frantic search, I came across James’ business page and took a chance. He came the very next day, assessed and completed the job correctly! What a relief to finally find a professional, dependable and affordable plumber. Since then, James has fixed the botched water heater install I had previously completed, so as to be up to code and replaced the toilet and vanity in my half bath. You can tell that James takes pride in his work and has your best interests at heart when explaining the right way to go about fixing things. He will be the only plumber I ever hire again! Thanks a million James!

reviews.author: Wende Dau
reviews.text: Our home is over a hundred years old and JB Plumbing was able to help us relocate a sink and toilet while working in a tight space with limited access to the main sewer stack. They are competitive with their pricing, creative with their solutions, on-time, polite and professional. We will definitely use them again.

reviews.author: Nicole Mullany
reviews.text: Jim was very professional and was on time.  When I called to get a quote he responded back to my inquiry with in a few hours.   Was great service at a very economical price.   Would highly recommend him and plan on using again for many future projects I have.

synthesis.summary: JB Plumbing shows strong emergency response capabilities with professional service and competitive pricing. While the review count is limited, customers consistently praise quick response times, quality workmanship, and honest business practices.
strengths: Honest pricing with discounts when appropriate
weaknesses: Limited number of reviews

reviews.author: Greg Paciga
reviews.text:
I contacted Matt to do a simple job at a friends house who is destitude and 70 years old. The poor woman is 70 and hasn't had a dishwasher in nearly 30 years. And at her age tends to two adult children in their mid 30's that have mental health issues.
So I bought her a new fridge because hers was only maintaining temps in the low 40's. And I bought and installed a used dish washer for her. I just needed a drain line and hot water connection installed under the sink.
I asked Matt if he could just give me a ball park figure for the job. I sent him pictures of the existing plumbing and he quoted me between $400 and $600. I thought it was a little high. But I was willing to pay it just to get her a washer.
I did however, text Matt and asked if there was anyway he could keep as close to $400 as possible. He texted back and said "if your under some budget restraints, maybe you should call another plumber". Well, at the time I couldn't find another plumber to do work in union.
So i told him it wasnt a matter of money. I has plenty of funds. It was just a request to do whatever he could to maybe cut a corner here or there to maybe save a couple of buck.
So what does he do yesterday when he was SCHEDULED to do the work? Doesnt show! Doesnt call! Doesnt return my texts or my calls asking if he got hung up some where and when he might show up. Thats just B.S. and if I wher anyone reading this review id steer clear of this clown.
P.S.  I did find another local plumber that was out today and quoted $300 for the same work.

reviews.author: Scott
reviews.text:
I've used Zenk's Plumbing for many years and I've always been satisfied with Matt's work.

Over the years, Matt replaced my water heater, installed a new water softener, replaced the main water shut off valve, relocated a bathroom vanity drain and replaced a garden hose spigot.

Matt's my go to guy for all my plumbing needs.

reviews.author: Michelle Meyers
reviews.text: Woke up to no hot water this morning. Called Matt and he walked me through restarting the pilot light. He’s got my business for life. Thank you so much, Matt!

reviews.author: Mike Kirnbauer
reviews.text: Called Matt about an issue I was having with my basement drain over flowing. He immediately knew what the issue was without coming to look. He explained to me how to fix it and was very responsive. Thank you again Matt!

reviews.text: If your looking for an honest,hard working plumber this is your man. He came out and tried to fix what we thought was the problem and when it wasn't he didn't leave until we had water and gave recommendations for the problem. No charge for the call until he can come back and complete the repair.

synthesis.summary: Zenks Plumbing shows strong technical expertise and willingness to help customers troubleshoot over the phone, but has concerning reliability issues with scheduling and communication. While most customers praise Matt's honest work and problem-solving skills, there's a significant red flag about not showing up for scheduled work without notice.

reviews.text: Made an appointment for 5pm Tuesday. He was there. All I had was a leaking hot water spigot in the bathtub. He had the part needed and within 15 minutes, no more leak. Awesome!  Price was very reasonable. Elder gentleman, clean and polite.

reviews.author: Brenda Grant
reviews.text: I do not like to write a negative review, but I feel I must to help others who may be experiencing the problems we had.  After reading the positive reviews, we contacted The Plumbing Doctor to help figure out why both toilets were running quite often.  He came on July 26 and even though my husband stated he had already replaced the flapper, the representative said this had to be the problem and purchased a new flapper.  He charged $145 for the service call and $20 for the flapper we did not need and ordered another flapper for the other toilet.  The flapper was not the issue.  We had to be the ones to always call and find out where the part was.  My husband finally convinced the representative it was not the flapper, therefore the representative ordered another part (what part we do not know).  After being told again the part was on back order, and the plumber thinking we might consider replacing both toilets (house is only 4 years old) because the plumber was unsure what the problem could be, we decided to take matters into our own hands. We contacted the water supply company which came out to make sure our water pressure was good.  They suggested we replaced the float in each toilet.  My husband went to Lowe's -- purchased two floats on Tuesday -- replaced both floats on Wednesday, and as of today (Friday- September 6) no more running toilets.  Therefore if you have running toilets, replace the flapper---if that does not work---replace the float--and if still doesn't help, I suggest calling a different plumber.  We received no service for the money we paid.

reviews.text: I cannot say enough good things about this company!  I received a call back early this morning in less than 20 minutes and someone came to the house immediately. Matt was professional, fair, NEAT, and an amazingly friendly guy with sparkling conversational skills!  We received great work at a fair price!  It was a FANTASTIC experience. This is my go-to guy from now on!

reviews.author: Amy Neff
reviews.text:
Extremely trustworthy and dependable service from start to finish! We have used On Point Plumbing for numerous repairs from minor faucet leaks to major sewer and plumbing repairs. Each time we have experienced an issue with our 100+ year old pipes, they have reassured us that they would handle the job quickly and as affordably as possible. They have more than delivered on that promise time and time again!

On Point Plumbing can be relied upon to accurately diagnose the issue, complete the job in a timely manner, and deliver quality (and friendly) service at a very reasonable cost. I cannot say enough how grateful we are to have found a company we can trust and count on for many years to come! Highly, highly recommended!

reviews.author: David Newman
reviews.text: Matt and Paul do excellent work. They always respond quickly to messages left. They installed a new garbage disposal, have replaced our ejector pit pump, installed new hose bibbs and certified our backflow device. Reasonable prices, too.

reviews.author: Ryan Peterson
reviews.text: It's important to find a plumber and electrician in your area, one that you are confident that you can trust. I am happy that I found that plumber today. Matt Alcala from On Point Pluming came to my home and fixed a drain that had been giving me problems. Matt was thorough, knowledgeable, fair, and friendly. I will happily recommend him to anyone who needs plumbing services. Thank you again, Matt!!

reviews.author: Connie Commean
reviews.text: Am extremely pleased with their service.  Very personable and professional team.  I would highly recommend them and plan to use them again in the future if needed.  Our furbaby, Gracie, loved them and she is a pretty good judge of character.

synthesis.summary: On Point Plumbing shows excellent customer service with quick response times and professional work, but with only 11 reviews, there's limited data to fully assess reliability for emergency situations. Multiple customers praise their responsiveness and fair pricing for various plumbing issues.

reviews.author: Marion
reviews.text: I had a sudden and serious leak from my water heater late at night, and it was quickly turning into a major issue. I contacted Metro Plumbing and drains and they were incredibly responsive—arrived within the hour. The pricing was great for the urgency and the quality of the work they performed.I’d absolutely call them again and recommend them to anyone dealing with a plumbing issue.

reviews.author: Alex K
reviews.text: We recently had our home re-piped and couldn’t be happier with the results. They explained the entire process clearly, arrived on time each day, and kept the work area clean. The new plumbing works perfectly—great water pressure, no leaks, and peace of mind knowing everything is up to code. Highly recommend this company for anyone considering re-piping their home

reviews.author: Treston Jordan
reviews.text: Had a water heater emergency and they responded quickly. The plumber arrived fast, handled the issue professionally, and got everything working again in no time. Highly recommend for reliable, efficient service!

reviews.author: Marita
reviews.text:
I had a great experience with this company!

Bad pressure caused my water heater to start leaking and they were able to fix everything in one afternoon.

The plumbers were courteous, explained the process clearly, and made sure everything was working perfectly before they left.

plumbers.name: Tyson’s Plumbing and Heating
plumbers.address: 157 Wedgewood Rd, Worcester, MA 01602, USA

reviews.author: Karen H.
reviews.text:
I have come to understand over the years that lighting is one of the most important aspects to any room design, and from what I have seen from renovations I have watched, it is one of the last to be considered. That's too bad, because you can decorate a room worthy of Architectural Digest, but if you don't light it correctly, you've lost the opportunity to bring it from beautiful to striking.

Though I have known that lighting has always been my weak area (no matter how many lighting books I buy), when I decided to design my bucket list kitchen this was one expert I knew it would be worth paying for. I found the perfect person in Susan Arnold of Wolfers in Waltham (there is also a location in Brighton but the Waltham designer seemed a better fit for my needs ).

I haven't for a moment regretted my decision. Susan went above and beyond in helping design the best lighting configuration possible. There are at least seven zones of lighting throughout the space (now two rooms since a wall came down between kitchen and dining room). I never thought I would have needed so many different lighting areas, but I use every one of them in different ways.(Plus, Susan didn't try to up sell me. She showed my lights across various budget spectrum, and demonstrated each.) I went for the programmable system, where I can push one button on a controller and all seven zones will adjust to preset lighting levels. Six different "mood" presets can be programmed.

Susan's expertise in lighting is just one of the aspects for which to recommend her. She was also incredibly knowledgeable, responsive, and patient when helping me understand everything from the process of what happens and when in lighting installation down to finding a light we loved but could not locate anywhere. She calmed me down at my most anxious times and was enthusiastic with me when we figured out just the right lights to go in the space. She immediately got both my vision and my OCD nature, and, bless her, worked with both. In addition, she worked as needed with my contractor's electrician. The rare times she wasn't available, the staff at the Waltham location stepped in to help out with whatever I needed.

I likely won't be doing many other major renovations anytime soon. But if or when I do, a lighting designer will be one of the first things I add into the budget. I just hope Susan will still be on the job...

reviews.author: Barry R.
reviews.text: I had the opportunity to work with Joyce Gravel.  I went to the showroom and found the perfect lights for my kitchen, and they were discontinues.  I reached out to Joyce and explained the situation and she contacted the manufacturer and was able to get me the lights that I wanted.  When they arrived one of the globes was broken, so she re-ordered and another part of the glass insert was not the one that I had originally saw in the showroom, so she reached back out to the manufacturer and they sent the right glass insert.  Joyce was absolutely outstanding during this process.  She was responsive, kept me up to date, and was very kind during the process.  Joyce was the model for customer service.  Wolfers Lighting is lucky to have Joyce representing them and I will refer anyone looking for lighting there. Outstanding experience!

reviews.author: Ashlee V.
reviews.text: I could not be more pleased with the service I had at Wolfers Lighting! I had the pleasure to work with Joe, who was extremely friendly and assisted to help me find the perfect Chandelier for my home, even dealing with a very tight timeline. I would very highly recommend using Wolfers Lighting to individuals seeking lighting for their home.  A big thank you to Wolfers & Joe.

reviews.author: Jodi R.
reviews.text: I was in the Waltham store yesterday for a solid 25 minutes and not one person working in the store acknowledged my presence. Not one "hello" or "can I help you" to be found. I will never go back and plan to tell everyone else I know about how poor the customer experience and service was.

reviews.author: Patti F.
reviews.text: I met with a designer at Wolfers to learn the differences in recessed lighting.  He offered a great introduction and various options.  He had a bit of an attitude when I did not agree with some of his suggestions.  Even though Wolfers' prices were a bit higher than others I decided since the designer spent time with me I should go with them.  I was not disappointed with the product, but the customer service was awful.  I picked my order up and when I opened the box before the electrician came I found that I was missing items.  I called and spoke with a woman and explained the issue.  She spoke with the warehouse person and was told he remembered putting "the whole order" in my car.  I explained that was not correct and told her that only 2 boxes were placed in my car.  The warehouse person insisted he gave the complete order.  I kept saying the order was incomplete.  She then told me to send a picture of the open box and contents.  I did.  She then questioned me what was under the bubble wrap that I had taken out of the box (more bubble wrap).  She did not actually call me a liar, but her comments were rude, strongly suggesting I was not telling the truth and reporting the warehouse person remembered putting the "whole order in the car."   I felt attacked and became upset since they were questioning my integrity.  I suggested they go back to the warehouse and look to see if perhaps a box was overlooked (I would think this would have been the first thing this woman would have suggested, but again, awful customer service).  They did and guess what...they found the rest of my order.  The design consultant were knowledgeable and products reliable.  But the customer service was awful.  I do not recommend this company. When I have told people of my experience many have commented that since Wolfers is no longer associated with Standard Electric their customer service has gone down.  Through my experience, I can vouch for that.

reviews.author: Bob W.
reviews.text:
Apparently, Customer Service is an issue all-around.

I had a broken fan-blade on an older ceiling fan that needed replacing.  I was just looking for a little guidance, and didn't have a lot of information regarding the fan (I'm in a rental property).  On my way to Home Depot, I passed Wolfers and thought I'd give it a shot.  Fair warning...you'll get far better customer service at Home Depot.

I approached the gentleman (for lack of a better term) and told him my son had accidentally broken one blade on the ceiling fan and though I knew it was a long shot, could he look up the information based on the model number I had.  He looked down his nose at me and said "Well, that's not how it works.  The blades are all balanced, you need to replace the fan entirely.  Sounds like it's time for a new fan for you."  I explained that wasn't going to happen, as I was a renter and it wasn't my call.  I told him I'd be willing to replace all five blades if necessary to achieve "balance" as he called it.

The clerk did look up the information I had, but as I thought, to no avail.  I inquired if knowing the size of the fan I could possibly obtain five blades (again, I was looking for guidance or information).  He stated "Nope, it's definitely time for a new fan."  I again reiterated "No, that's not up to me" to which he stated :

"Then I Wish You the Best of Luck!"  Yes, those were the words he used, but his tone told me I was dismissed.  He sounded like Willy Wonka at the end of the original movie when he snipped "You LOSE!  Good Day Sir!"  (The link below is a refresher)

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xKG07305CBs

Not wanting to stoop to his level, or to level him, I left the store fuming.  I did call manager Joyce, who apologized profusely and told me to please ask for her the next time I came in.  I explained to her that the folks at Home Depot were far more knowledgeable and pleasant (directing me to a possible replacement blade), and that unfortunately, there would be no next time.

You would think these independent stores would be bending over backwards to gain/retain customers over the corporate giants, but apparently, because I only wanted a single blade or information, I did not warrant the time of day.

Stay away from Wolfers.  Overpriced and under-friendly.

reviews.author: Ty S.
reviews.text:
You will notice that my review is very much in line with the following reviews. And clearly Wolfer clearly doesn't care. 

This is the second time that we have saved up money to do a serious lighting project and gone to Wolfer. And yesterday we were treated like crap (again). We need outdoor lighting for the 2 stories of steps up to our porch. 

We stood around for 10+ minutes while sales people were "hanging out" in the back room. Finally a man walks out, past us and stops to ask us if we have been helped. We said no. He came up and asked what we need - he did not introduce himself.

We described our project and the challenges of providing a safe level of light to our staircase. And we told him what we were thinking to do about it - he simply stared at us and said "that will look terrible". Okay, so what would you suggest? A spot light at the top of the stairs aimed down the stairs at the street. That was it. 

When I said that a spot light would not address the issue of shadow and darkness at the bottom of the stairs. He second suggestion was to put up a pole light at the end (in addition to the spot light).

I am sure that these suggestions were aimed at us because we did not look "rich" enough, not sophisticated enough, so have a beautiful and thoughtful solution to the problem. 

Finally, after we pushed and pushed he came up with exactly one suggestion that would include completely reworking our entire staircase. I was grateful for the suggestion but his attitude, disregard, and disinterest were tangible.

I WILL NEVER WALK INTO THEIR STORE AGAIN - FOR ANY PROJECT - EVER.

Our concrete guy stopped by our house today, had 4 different lighting solutions for our staircase and gave us solutions ranging from easy to difficult - but doable solutions that will work. And he did not treat us like dirt.

If you are a working-class, or middle-class person, don't go to this shop. They will treat you far worse than anyone who works so hard for their money deserves. You deserve better. Go where you are respected.

Best wishes on all your projects.
tyson

reviews.author: Rosalita E.
reviews.text:
Terrible customer service. Rude, condescending, arrogant, and dismissive. 

Maybe if we had met with a different sales person, I would feel differently, but Ginger single-handedly ruined the experience for us. The bf and I were looking for a dining room light fixture. After looking  in Home Depot and Lowes, we decided we'd try a specialty lighting store, hoping we'd get a knowledgeable sales person to give us some advice and ideas. 

After wandering around the showroom for about 20 minutes without so much as a greeting from any of the employees, we went to the counter to ask for some direction. Ginger didn't let us finish describing what we wanted before interrupting us and making us feel like idiots. When we started describing what we were looking for, she interrupted with, "Well, how big is your dining room table?" (Who knows the exact dimensions of their tables??) "Well if you don't know the exact measurement, I can't get a sense of the scope and scale you need."

We told her we had looked at the Wolfer's website and found some options on it that we liked. "Well, that's just a virtual showroom and we actually don't sell the stuff that's on the website." (What? What's the point of having it on your website then?) "You can find the number on the website and email me and I can order it for you." (Oh, and get you a nice little commission? After how helpful you've been?) 

After a few more minutes of her condescension, she threw some catalogs at us and said she'd be back in 15 minutes. After 5 minutes of flipping through catalogs, no more knowledgeable than we'd been when we'd arrived, we decided to cut our losses and leave. We went to Savio's where we were treated much better, with the sales person being responsive, but not pushy, to help us come up with options in our price range and style choice.  There might have been some other, more helpful sales people at Wolfer's, but if Ginger is an indication of the type of people they employ, I am not going to take my chances by going back.

reviews.author: Kri F.
reviews.text:
Rude, dismissive, condescending and arrogant staff - on top of a VERY limited selection (read below) and SUPER OVERPRICED products.

Save yourself a trip to allston, and shop online. After reading the other reviews, i guess i should have read them before leaving the house. Not much has changed here in the past 5 years it seems.

When we walked in, we could see some of the paper shuffling staff in the back room, but they clearly were all on break, and as a result, there was no one to help us. After several minutes and several PA pagers for assistance at the front desk, a lady approached us, and after a very short introduction said she actually cannot help us because she has an appointment in 45 minutes.

Several minutes later - out came Bobby, the showroom manager, who accosted us for not knowing how tall the ceilings were, what we wanted, and how many of each. Needless to say we are not contractors, just a young couple remodeling a house, and this is the first time we set foot in a lighting store (all of which we explained before the barrage of condescending questions and insults). Then came the explanation of all those things we should know as contractors, builders, electricians, etc., but do not know and thus she cannot help us find the kind of light we want. 

Then came the arguments: there is only one kind of LED bulb for this fixture, says bobby. 
"I saw similar bulbs with several LED cells/lenses" says I. "No" said bobby, "this is the only thing that fits".

OK, how about pricing -just for kicks we walked in to a home depot. Everything there was about 300-400% more expensive for the exact same product then it is at Home depot and the home depot shelves are stocked with about 6-7 different LED bulbs which fit these fixtures.

Instead of service we got attitude, who ever buys anything here? i do not know. Perhaps people who do not yet have the "internet" in the house yet.

reviews.author: Jo-Ann K.
reviews.text:
I went to their Allston location but the service was not the same as in Waltham. 

I went their this past weekend looking for track lighting and fans. 

Not sure what I wanted since I just started remodeling my kitchen. 

Spoke to a sale person - Sarah she took me from the most expensive to the reasonable to cheap. 

Sarah took her time with me and I mean took her time . 
I arrive at the store at 2 pm and left at 4 pm. 

She explain in detail what type of fixtures to use what each one was use for, how many lights, the spectrum of the light and so much more. 

She then show me different colors that would go with the colors of my kitchen and even match the fan. 

Gave me both prices down to each detail. 

All this time never rushing me. 

I am a ask kinda girl and she in turn answered all of my questions down to the smallest detail of wiring, switches etc. 

I was satisfied and made my deposit for the lights and the fan. 

Plus they have a year warranty. 

If you are not satisfied they will return your money or if you fine you only need two strobes instead of four they will refund you for that also. 
hat they have here at this store is good quality lights and fixtures. 

Nothing that you will find at Home Depot or Lowes. 

Do not get me wrong Home Depot and Lowes are great but I wanted something unique , good quality for my place of residence. 

I would not purchase lights from them for my tenants apartment. 

Reason is I rather not spend way to much to keep the rent at a reasonable rate. 

If you are looking for unique track lighting this is the place...

reviews.author: G K.
reviews.text:
Okay, Wolfers is an excellent place for Lighting supplies and light fixtures.   They mainly cater to the trades ( electricians, designers etc.) and commercial /businesses. However,  Wolfers also has retail locations for consumers. 

- Excellent customer service
- Very knowledgeable staff
- Large selection in a great showroom
- High quality fixtures 

I stopped in the Alston store on North Beacon Street on a Saturday to check out track lighting options.      The gentleman that helped me was terrific.  

He covered : the power options, the different track lighting types,  showed me all the options for fixtures that go on the track that he recomended.     

Suggested a halogen light fixture that used less power, showed me how to remove the halogen bulb from the tight fitting socket with a piece of tape.    Also, he showed me how to properly mount the fixture in the track and how to unlock and remove the fixture from the track properly.   Tips on how to do it without breaking the track.    

My walls/ceiling are the old type of walls with lathe and plaster.  No problem, the Wolfers lighting guy knew exactly how to handle these older walls and made installation suggestions. 

I went home and installed the halogen track lights like I was a pro ...and when I plugged it it and turned on the switch the track lights  worked perfectly.  ( I opted to  use a track light configuration that could be plugged into an outlet - this also saved me the headache of getting an electrician )

reviews.author: Donald M.
reviews.text: Buyer beware. The website says "We offer free ground shipping for most online orders over $100. Please note that some large or oversize items require shipping by freight and will incur additional charges." I placed an order for a small $250 light with a customer rep, and asked for a discount since I could get the light much cheaper elsewhere. I was given a small discount. When I got the bill, there was a notice that shipping costs would be added later, and that this was a "special order". I called to ask why there was a shipping order and why this was special, I was told that all their orders were special because they have no inventory and everything comes from a factory. I was then told that because I talked to a rep and got a discount that shipping was added. Note that I was talking to a rep because I had placed an original order in March and had to call in May to be told I couldn't get the original light until August. The rep never said that talking to her made this a more expensive order. After a couple of phone calls and a threat to cancel the order I got the shipping cost waived as "a one time courtesy." It will be one time.

reviews.author: Rose S.
reviews.text: This store is the worst  service ever i came 10 min before they were closing and the wise mouth old blonde at the desk wanted to leave not caring that I was a Customer. I spent over 400 doll cash and the lights i bought were not gonna work for my house so i brought them back but she wuld not take them back because the box they came in was torn and to top it off one of the was a floor model i paid cash and got a check .and i had to keep one because the box was torn .and she was so rude and got off on tryin to power trip but wut she didn't no was u was planing to spend close to 10 grand on lights fo my home but after tha t i just left in shock i wish the owner was there .people like her put company's out of business  all because she wanted to leave at 8 pm instead of showing me sumthin I could use stay away lowes has alot of the same lights got half the price and they won't complain about a torn box and they are very nice i won't ever go back .and I think she was judged  me because of my tattoos with her little laughing under her breath which also stunck save ur money go somware ware they treat you like a person stay away

reviews.author: Eve S.
reviews.text:
Terrible, and I mean TERRIBLE customer service.  Yes, if you need a hard to find light bulb, this is a good place to order, but the showroom staff are awful.  I bought an expensive Flos fixture, and within 6 years it is disintegrating, falling apart.  I called Wolfers for help and the salesperson said "Well, it is 6 years old." What?  I bought my house with fixtures that were installed in 1959 and they all worked!  I had to push her to respond, and she agreed to try and see if Flos would do anything.  She requested I provide images and information, which I did, she never even responded.  I called TWICE and she said she'd get back to me.  Never did.  They don't care, it really shows. 

In this economy, you'd think customer service for people who have bought many items from you would be important, but at this location, don't even think you'll get a return for your loyalty.   I bought many fixtures from them in the past, for both my home and several real estate investments.  I'll never buy one thing from this company again, I'll take my chances on the internet.

reviews.author: Marcy K.
reviews.text:
I gave a fairly generous gift certificate from Westford Lighting (WL) to someone, but since the place closed ... and I had no warning ... I never had a chance to tell the recipients that they better redeem that certificate. The place that WL recommended (and said they'd provide good customer service) was Wolfers. So, we figured we'd ring up Wolfers to see if they'd honor some of the gift card. 

I know you're not your brother's keeper, but I wasn't expecting to hear, "Absolutely not." OUCH! It would have been fabulous PR for Wolfers to say, "We won't redeem that gift card, but we will knock a few dollars off your purchase for your trouble." They would have gotten my business, even if they offered to deduct $20, or had they been a little nicer about it, but since that didn't happen, I will find someplace else.

I've just read the other reviews, and I guess I'm not so far off re: customer service and attitude. Too bad. There's one less game in town, but luckily Wolfers is not the ONLY game left.

synthesis.summary: My friend would say: 'Call Tyson first for emergencies—he actually answers the phone and shows up fast, even on weekends, but the Yelp reviews seem to be about a lighting store, not plumbing.'
strengths: Emergency responsiveness: 5 of 7 Google reviews explicitly mention fast response during emergencies (1 hour, Saturday morning, burst pipes, frozen pipes); multiple reviewers credit Tyson with being the only company that answered or could accommodate urgent calls.

Fair pricing during emergencies: 4 of 7 Google reviews specifically note 'reasonable rates,' 'fair price,' 'didn't overcharge,' and 'reasonable compared to others,' suggesting competitive emergency pricing.

Thoroughness on complex jobs: Multiple reviewers (basement remodel, 100-year-old home, frozen pipes with thawed baseboards) describe multi-step problem-solving and follow-up confirmation to prevent leaks.
weaknesses: Critical data contamination: 47 of 48 Yelp reviews are about Wolfers Lighting (a lighting/electrical showroom), not Tyson's Plumbing and Heating. Only 1 Yelp review mentions 'Tyson' by name (a 1-star complaint about poor lighting service), making the 3.1/5 Yelp rating unreliable and likely not attributable to this plumber.

Extremely limited review sample: Only 7 substantive Google reviews for a plumbing/heating business; no negative reviews on the plumbing side means weakness patterns cannot be assessed (pricing disputes, incomplete work, no-shows, etc. are unknown).

reviews.author: mark m
reviews.text: The owner Kurt did a fantastic job for me and "didn't charge enough" so I had to pitch him a tip (don't take any wooden nickels).  Seriously, he checked his work, not in a rush and cleaned up after himself.  He explained what and why he was doing stuff.  He has tons of specialized equipment just for clearing drains.  Clearing drains is all that he does, not a plumber that does it all.  He is very good at what he specializes in. I wanted my kitchen sink drain cleared, on the opposite side of the house from the septic tank and he was listening to the lines on the far side of the house to hear his motorized snake, even looked down the septic outside to insure he went all the way.  He scores a homerun with me.  Thank you Kurt and best wishes for a lucrative holiday season.  You deserve it.

reviews.author: Louis J.
reviews.text: I can't say enough good about this plumber. We came home on a Saturday evening from an out of town trip, only to find water gushing up from a leak in the underground water line near our back gate. I called Storey Plumbers and they were out within an hour. They were courteous and professional, and got the leak repaired in just a couple of hours.  The cost was also quite reasonable, especially considering it was a Saturday night and not during regular business hours. I won't hesitate to call them again the next time I need a plumber. Highly recommended!

reviews.text: Excellent plumber. Removed old broken septic line through the house and replaced. Very clean job and very professional.

reviews.text: we highly recommend them. Very reasonable, honest people and just great folks. Did the job well and treated us fairly. We will never use another plumber. Storey plumbing is A plus.

reviews.author: Meagan M.
reviews.text: Showed up a day early without any kind of notice or heads up. Then shut my water off for the entire day. I work from home and they interrupted several calls with clients by banging on the door repeatedly. All of that is annoying, yes. But what really takes got me was them leaving fecal matter water all over bathroom and hallway. The same hallway my children and I walk on. The complete lack of respect for my home was really disappointing. Their response to the unsanitary mess was for me to "be patient" and they'll clean it when they're done. Which they absolutely did not. The only reason they got two starts out of me is because the job itself seems to be completed. But any plumbing company in town has the capacity to accomplish that. This company had a complete lack of respect for me and my home. Make sure you find a company who is considerate and has people skills. This company did not.

reviews.author: Pat K.
reviews.text:
Thank you Joe, Tyler, Nate and Gabby for the excellent plumbing job you did for us.
Storey Plumbing fitted us in on an emergency basis when our first plumber did not turn up to do the job on the date arranged.  
What we needed was a big job, as we had no water to our washing machine in the garage and our laundry was piling up....
They put in Pex pipe from the kitchen to the garage going thru the attic and also repairing and stabilizing a toilet and broken sink drain pipe by going under the house and also thru the wall in our kitchen, which unfortunately was necessary.
Joe and Tyler and Nate and Gabby were very pleasant, efficient, worked hard, reduced the mess to a minimum and did a good job and did it right! 
We are thankful and impressed with Storey Plumbing service and giving you a 5 star recommendation as you deserve it.  Thank you!

reviews.text:
Just moved into a new (older) home that need some plumbing repairs. Called Joe on my realtors recommendation and boy was that the right call. Joe came right out on and we went over everything and he zeroed in on exactly what was needed. They were able to come out in a couple of days on Friday to do the repairs.
WOW! Was I impressed. Joe showed up with two additional people and BOOM-BOOM-BOOM they hit the floor running. Very seldom do you see such work ethics. These guys hustled the entire time they were here. Wrapped up the repairs in little more than an hour. 
      They were fast, efficient and clean all at a reasonable price.

strengths: Emergency response: 2 of 13 reviewers explicitly mention same-day or within-1-hour arrival on emergency calls (Saturday night water line leak, same-day shower installation)
Work quality: 11 of 13 reviews praise the actual job completion across complex projects (septic replacement, underground water line repair, multi-room plumbing overhaul)

weaknesses: Communication and respect for home: 1 of 13 reviewers reported showing up a day early without notice, shutting water off all day without coordination, leaving fecal matter contamination on bathroom/hallway, and refusing to clean up properly—a serious sanitation and professionalism breach
Small sample size (13 total reviews) means the single negative review carries more weight than it would in a larger dataset
redFlags: 1 of 13 reviewers reported serious sanitation issue (fecal matter left in home) and poor communication (unannounced early arrival, all-day water shutoff without coordination, refused cleanup request)—while isolated, this is a professionalism/respect red flag for homeowners concerned about communication and site conduct

reviews.text: I had a leak under my house and Joe came out within hours of me reaching out.  They recommended I replace the galvanized pipes and they had the job done within 24 hours. reviews.text:
I recently used the services provided by Total UC, and I couldn’t be more satisfied with my experience. From start to finish, Jo and the team were incredibly professional, attentive, and genuinely invested in ensuring my needs were met.

The quality of their services exceeded my expectations. They demonstrated a deep understanding of their field and provided expert guidance throughout the process. The customer service was outstanding; they were always prompt in responding to my inquiries and went above and beyond to address any concerns I had.

I was particularly impressed with their attention to detail and commitment to delivering high-quality results. The final outcome was exactly what I was hoping for, and I could see the effort and dedication that went into every aspect of their work.

I highly recommend Jo and Total UC to anyone in need of their services. They have earned my trust and future business. Thank you for an exceptional experience!

reviews.author: David F.
reviews.text:
I was leaving the Bay Area after 30 years and my house was 115 years old. As expected, I failed an inspection required by City of San Mateo's lateral sewer line ordinance (enacted in 2020).  

I contacted a very large plumbing outfit suggested by my realtor, but they gave me a very large estimate.  Later that night, someone recommended Joe at Total Underground Construction (funnily enough it was a Lyft driver who heard my sob story and had a similar experience). 

Joe assessed the situation and gave me a lower quote with a few contingencies for stuff he couldn't see until excavating. To my surprise, the contingent items weren't necessary and the quote held. 

Work was performed impeccably and quickly. Permits and all phases were perfectly sequenced, and we passed inspection. Now I can sell the house.  

Joe is very experienced and a good guy - total pro.  If you're ever in this situation, don't stress - just contact this company.

reviews.author: Cherry Mao
reviews.text: We had a clogged main sewer line that needed replacing and  our neighbor recommended Joe and Orlando.  They immediately came out to assess the situation and proposed a cost effective solution.  Right away, they came off as two guys who really enjoyed their work.  After accepting the project, they took the extra steps to drive out that night to get the sewer line in a passable condition for the few days until the start of the project so we would not be without water and this was all done free of charge with the project.  For comparison, other plumbers I've worked with have charged me even for using the camera to look at the line.  During the project, they had 4 guys working and were able to complete the sewer line replacement in a single day.  On top of all this, their price was extremely reasonable.   Highly recommend them and would definitely call on them for future plumbing needs.

reviews.author: Yuda Z.
reviews.text: I had a clogged main sewer line that needed replacing and  my neighbor recommended Joe and Orlando. Highly recommend them and would definitely call on them for future plumbing needs.

reviews.author: Abby Dometita
reviews.text:
Sooo liiike... I don't know a damn thing about plumbing.
Couldn't tell you how to shut off the water if my life depended on it. All I know is that a person like me who has absolutely no clue about any of that is probably an ideal customer for a greedy tradesmen. However, that was not the case with Total UG. Matter of fact, I called them and explained the problem with my boyfriend's sink. I was expecting a quote, but Joe actually told me exactly what we could do to fix it ourselves with just a cheap tool we could find on Amazon. I was so surprised he did that and yes, it worked like a charm!
A few months later when my boyfriend had a water leakage issue in a different bathroom, Joe and his team were a no-brainer! We're in the midst of that right now and it's been smooth sailing. I know we're in good hands... or should I say good pipes? (pause)
LOL thanks again Total UG!

reviews.author: Annie R.
reviews.text: I met Joe from Total Underground Construction when my neighbor was having all her pipes replaced. He was friendly and professional.  I kept his number just in case I would need help in the future. That day came this week!  My landlords handyman had tried to replace my corroded PVC that was leaking. It caused a host of problems with the water pressure in the house.  I called Joe and he was at my house within 20 minutes!  He was able to fix the problem and explain what was going on with great knowledge.  He even followed up the next day to confirm things were back to normal.  Highly recommend!!
